title: Sponsored by: Genpact | Enabling AI-Ready Data
abstract: Discover how Genpact and Baker Hughes are revolutionizing data platforms with an AI-driven Lakehouse designed for seamless human and agent interactions. This session will explore a four-layer approach: integrating master data for a single, trusted foundation; building ontologies to connect jobs, cash, and invoices; enabling natural language queries through a metrics layer—no SQL or analysts required; and leveraging the Agent Bricks multi-agent framework to orchestrate workflows end-to-end. Attendees will gain actionable insights into creating a unified, intelligent platform that transforms data into decisions and connects every step from job to cash.

This session will explore a four-layer approach: integrating master data for a single, trusted foundation; building ontologies to connect jobs, cash, and invoices; enabling natural language queries through a metrics layer—no SQL or analysts required; and leveraging the Agent Bricks multi-agent framework to orchestrate workflows end-to-end. Attendees will gain actionable insights into creating a unified, intelligent platform that transforms data into decisions and connects every step from job to cash.

speakers.name: Matthew Arellano
speakers.bio: Matthew Arellano is Senior Vice President at Genpact, where he leads the Databricks Business Groups, shaping the company's global strategy across data modernization, agentic AI, and enterprise platform transformation for Fortune 500 clients. Before Genpact, he co-founded XponentL Data, building next-generation data products and AI-powered accelerators that are now embedded in Genpact's Data & AI practice, and prior to that led the Global Data, Analytics and AI Strategy at Accenture. With nearly 20 years at the intersection of data, cloud, AI and enterprise transformation, Matthew brings a practitioner's perspective on how organizations move from data and AI strategy to measurable business outcomes, at scale.

title: “Make Me a Map”: Building a GIS Agent with AgentBricks, MCP, and Lakebase
abstract: Geospatial workflows are complex—querying spatial databases and generating visualizations requires specialized expertise. We built an AI agent that takes a Slack message and returns a fully functional map application. What started as an internal tool for Solution Architects—replacing hours of manual data prep—is now used by customers too. The architecture: Lakebase serves as the spatial backend (Postgres-compatible with PostGIS), while our MCP server exposes Felt's mapping capabilities to AgentBricks. Users ask for a map in Slack, the agent queries Lakebase, connects to Felt, and posts back a working map. This talk covers: Lakebase as a geospatial engine—Postgres compatibility in a serverless lakehouse MCP tools for live connections—wiring Lakebase → Felt directly AgentBricks orchestration—multi-step GIS workflows from Slack Attendees leave with a pattern for building agents that produce working applications, not just answers.
description_raw:
Geospatial workflows are complex—querying spatial databases and generating visualizations requires specialized expertise. We built an AI agent that takes a Slack message and returns a fully functional map application.

What started as an internal tool for Solution Architects—replacing hours of manual data prep—is now used by customers too.

The architecture: Lakebase serves as the spatial backend (Postgres-compatible with PostGIS), while our MCP server exposes Felt's mapping capabilities to AgentBricks. Users ask for a map in Slack, the agent queries Lakebase, connects to Felt, and posts back a working map.

This talk covers:

  • Lakebase as a geospatial engine—Postgres compatibility in a serverless lakehouse
  • MCP tools for live connections—wiring Lakebase → Felt directly
  • AgentBricks orchestration—multi-step GIS workflows from Slack

Attendees leave with a pattern for building agents that produce working applications, not just answers.

title: 0 to 100 AI/BI Apps in 120 Days: Building an Enterprise Data and AI Platform From Scratch @ Alpura
abstract: In early 2025, Alpura faced fragmented data, multiple BI tools, weak governance and no scalable AI foundation. In just 120 days, we rebuilt the entire data platform on Databricks. Using the Medallion Architecture (Bronze, Silver, Gold), Unity Catalog for enterprise governance and Databricks Metrics for a centralized semantic layer, we created a governed, scalable lakehouse foundation. We consolidated more than five legacy platforms without disrupting operations and eliminated KPI inconsistencies by standardizing business logic. On top of this foundation, we rapidly delivered 100+ AI/BI assets, including 10+ production-grade applications such as a Walmart Scintilla analytics app and an Ambassador Program app — built in days, not months. Leveraging Genie AI, we enabled conversational BI to accelerate adoption. This session shares a practical blueprint for building an AI/BI factory at enterprise speed.

title: 1000× Faster Retrieval: Indexed and Full-Text Search in the Lakehouse
abstract: Delta Lake and Iceberg excel at large-scale analytics, but they are not optimized for sub-second point lookups or full-text search. In operational workloads—such as security analytics, log investigation, and telemetry—retrieval queries that require full scans or high-cardinality filtering can take minutes to hours, when seconds or milliseconds are required. I built IndexTables, an embedded, Tantivy-based indexing layer that runs directly inside Spark executors, enabling up to 1000× faster query performance while preserving the Lakehouse model. This session explores the architecture: object-storage-hosted indexes with ACID transactions, millisecond-latency aggregations over billions of rows, native time-series bucketing for efficient GROUP BY analytics, and NVMe-backed caching with proactive pre-warming. Attendees will learn how Capital One is using IndexTables to complement Delta and Iceberg, creating Lakehouse architectures that support both analytical and retrieval-heavy workloads.

title: 3 Steps to Red Team Your LLMs on Databricks (Using Tools You Didn't Know You Had)
abstract: You added guardrails, PII filters, and safety controls. Then someone Base64-encodes a prompt injection and your model hands over data like it was asked nicely.This session is a live walkthrough of how to red team your LLMs on Databricks using BlackIce, Databricks' own open-source containerised toolkit. 14 security tools. One Docker image. Five minutes to deploy.In 3 steps we will spin up a model serving endpoint with AI Gateway guardrails, launch BlackIce on a single-node cluster with NVIDIA's Garak, Microsoft's PyRIT, Meta's CyberSecEval and 11 more tools ready to go, then point Garak at the endpoint and run attack probes, encoding attacks, jailbreaks, prompt injection, and system prompt extraction — to get an Attack Success Rate in minutes.You'll leave fully understanding how to use BlackIce to secure your LLMs, whilst feeling slightly more paranoid about just how easy they are to break. Better you find out here than in production.

title: 800TB, Zero Pipelines: How Ensemble Health Partners Built an AI-Ready Revenue Cycle on Lakebase
abstract: Ensemble Health Partners manages revenue cycle operations for 30+ health systems, processing 25 billion+ transactions across 800TB of healthcare financial data. Legacy SQL Server sprawl —1 5 individual databases with separate schemas —c reated silos that blocked cross-client insights and slowed AI innovation in an industry where providers operate on 1% margins. This session shows how Ensemble consolidated onto Databricks Lakebase as their AI-ready transactional layer, eliminating brittle pipelines and enabling self-service pods to materialize views directly from harmonized Delta tables. The results: 3–5% net revenue yield improvement ($30M+ per $1B health system), 20% organizational efficiency gain, and 10x reduction in failure rates — while powering agentic AI workflows for denial prevention, AR follow-up, and payer-behavior prediction at scale.

title: A Unified Future for Delta and Apache Iceberg™
abstract: Delta Lake and Apache Iceberg™ have converged on similar ideas: columnar metadata for efficient pruning, manifest trees for scalability, and deletion vectors for fast updates. Yet today, we maintain two separate metadata structures, duplicating work and diverging in capabilities.The next major evolution of Delta Lake reimagines metadata from the ground up. This talk introduces a unified metadata architecture where Delta Lake commits store content metadata directly in Iceberg v4's adaptive metadata tree. Delta Lake gains efficient tree-structured manifests and Iceberg interoperability while preserving the transactional guarantees that Delta users depend on.Join us to see the future of lakehouse metadata.

title: Accelerating AI From Model to Impact at Workday With Databricks Apps
abstract: Workday needed to put trusted ML insights directly in the hands of sales, customer success and finance—fast. Before, delivering a single internal app required a bespoke full‑stack build and repeated reviews, stretching timelines to nearly a year. By shifting to Databricks Apps and building where the data already lives, Workday compressed delivery to weeks, launched three production AI applications in a single year, and scaled its flagship Sales Companion to 4,500+ active users. This session details the architecture and adoption playbook: Databricks Apps as the UI/middleware, MLflow‑powered components, retrieval‑augmented workflows for unstructured content, governed access with Unity Catalog and fast analytics via SQL Serverless—enabling conversational experiences that let business users self‑serve sentiment analysis, forecasting and KPI insights without waiting on the data science team or adding headcount.

title: Accelerating Corteva’s Data Source Onboarding With Lakeflow
abstract: Onboarding a new data source at Corteva used to take 30–45 days. Now it’s 4–7 days, about 85% faster. The Lakeflow-first strategy and Unity Catalog helped standardize the ingestion‑to‑consumption pathway across 6 domains. Lakeflow Connect, Spark Declarative Pipelines and Jobs make pipeline runs repeatable and config‑driven. A lightweight metadata layer centralizes data cleansing, and an incremental DDL tool pushes schema changes safely through each environment.This pattern now underpins Corteva’s operational analytics. It accelerates root‑cause analysis linking field variability to dryer‑bin stabilization, improves throughput across 35 seed sites, and strengthens seed quality. Today, the setup covers 5,000+ tables from 15+ sources, shortening time‑to‑insight and speeding partner onboarding. Attendees will leave with a reusable Lakeflow‑first ingestion pattern, a metadata‑driven data quality blueprint and a safe schema‑evolution workflow across environments.

title: Accelerating Data Utility at Toyota: How vista Unifies Data Access and Governance via Data Mesh
abstract: Toyota Motor Corporation, with over 70,000 employees, initially centralized corporate, vehicle and customer data into a data library. This caused scalability issues due to a central team lacking domain knowledge, resulting in delays of several months. To solve this information disparity, Toyota created “vista,” a platform offering a company-wide data view. Vista shifts from a centralized system to a decentralized data mesh, enabling domain experts to manage their own data. It uses a data fabric on Databricks Unity Catalog for centralized metadata governance and access control while keeping data physically owned by each domain. This zero-copy setup avoids duplication.Using the vista marketplace for search and Data Atelier for processing, Toyota adopted a self-service model that cut data wait times from months to weeks. This supports AI use cases like inspections via the AI Garage, preparing Toyota for future autonomous optimization.

title: Accelerating the Monthly Financial Closing with Genie
abstract: Closing the books every month is still slow and manual for many finance teams. In this session, we will walk through a monthly financial closing for a mid‑size retailer and show how we tackle two of the most painful steps with Databricks One: reconciling gaps between CRM invoices and accounting numbers, and running variance analysis to explain differences between actual results and budget. We will follow a finance controller using an AI/BI dashboard to spot issues, Genie in natural language to investigate root causes and draft variance commentary, and a Databricks app to apply the corrections safely, update the numbers, and generate the final reporting deck. Attendees will see how business users can make financial closing faster, simplify investigations, and produce more reliable reports with less manual effort - all from a single, unified workspace.

title: Accelerating the Speed to Value of Analytics with Databricks AI/BI & Agents
abstract: After migrating from Snowflake, Addepar ventured deep into Databricks' native AI/BI suite. This talk shares our practitioner's roadmap for accelerating "speed to value" by building analytics directly where data lives. We explore why we chose AI/BI over external tools and how we serve as early adopters pushing the platform's limits.Key takeaways:- The AI-Assisted Analyst: Leveraging Databricks Assistant to chat through notebook discovery, bypass syntax struggles, and cure "analyst's block" with AI-suggested Dashboard visuals.- Metadata as Code: Why rich Unity Catalog metadata is the critical UI for accurate GenAI.- Metric Views > Tables: Defining semantics once for both Dashboards and Genie to ensure consistent answers, rather than relying on static tables or views.- Genie & Agents: Tips for "teaching" Genie spaces and early experiments using Agent Bricks to orchestrate structured analytics alongside unstructured knowledge agents.

title: Activating AI With Lakeflow and Agent Bricks
abstract: Enterprise knowledge is trapped in PDFs, images, and office docs—far from the reach of AI. This session demonstrates how to unleash that data by ingesting it with Lakeflow Connect and scaling it with Agent Bricks and Lakeflow Jobs.We’ll cover: Accessing with Lakeflow Connect: How to efficiently ingest from common AI sources, including email, cloud storage, and popular applications—as well as how to preserve the file permissions, metadata, and lineage that’s critical for secure AI. Scaling with Agent Bricks and Lakeflow Jobs: How to turn AI models into production-grade ETL automation using AI functions and Databricks’ built-in orchestrator, with end-to-end patterns for search, data transformation, summarization, fuzzy matching and more. Built-in governance: How to ensure these workflows are production-ready, with robust governance, observability, data quality, pipeline management, and more. Stop letting your most valuable data sit idle. Join us to transform fragmented documents into a governed foundation for data intelligence.

title: ADBC and the Future of Universal Data Connectivity for Agentic Systems
abstract: Agents are becoming first-class users of the data stack, but they inherit 1990s-era connectivity: slow row-oriented APIs, heavy SDKs, and brittle system-specific connectors. For AI-driven workflows making fast tool calls across systems, this isn’t just slow—it’s terrible agent ergonomics, leading to failures, retries, and need for human intervention.This talk explains why ADBC (Arrow Database Connectivity) is emerging as the universal connector for agentic data workflows: a vendor-neutral API and driver standard that ships Arrow columnar results, enabling fast data access across languages and engines. We’ll show why this matters for agents: frequent queries, tight latency/cost budgets, and the need for safe, reliable access to the tabular truth.You’ll leave with a clear model for where ADBC fits in lakehouse architectures, why it aligns with Databricks and open standards, and a how to move from framework-heavy integrations to lean, CLI-first building blocks guided by agent skills.

speakers.name: Ian Cook
speakers.bio: Ian Cook is an Apache Arrow PMC member and co-founder and CEO of Columnar, a data infrastructure company enabling radically faster, simpler, and more secure database access for humans and agents.

title: Agent Evaluations
abstract: This hands-on course introduces learners to evaluating, governing, and securing agentic AI systems on Databricks. You'll begin by exploring the motivation for evaluation and governance frameworks and how they connect to the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform. Next, you'll learn how to apply MLflow evaluation metrics and a variety of evaluation techniques, including online evaluation, synthetic evaluation, and building evaluation datasets from MLflow traces. The course then examines how Unity Catalog governance extends to agents' functions, models, and tools, ensuring proper access control, auditability, and compliance. Finally, you'll learn Databricks AI Security Framework (DASF) and how to secure agents with Mosaic AI Gateway, applying guardrails, rate limits, and policy filters to enforce safe and reliable usage. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, including Unity Catalog for governance and model management Intermediate Python programming experience Familiarity with Generative AI fundamentals, including RAG architectures and their components (chunking, embeddings, vector stores) Basic knowledge of MLflow for experiment tracking and model lifecycle management General awareness of data governance principles, including access control, data lineage, and data privacy
description_raw:
This hands-on course introduces learners to evaluating, governing, and securing agentic AI systems on Databricks. You'll begin by exploring the motivation for evaluation and governance frameworks and how they connect to the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform. Next, you'll learn how to apply MLflow evaluation metrics and a variety of evaluation techniques, including online evaluation, synthetic evaluation, and building evaluation datasets from MLflow traces. The course then examines how Unity Catalog governance extends to agents' functions, models, and tools, ensuring proper access control, auditability, and compliance. Finally, you'll learn Databricks AI Security Framework (DASF) and how to secure agents with Mosaic AI Gateway, applying guardrails, rate limits, and policy filters to enforce safe and reliable usage.

Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. 

Prerequisites

  • Familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, including Unity Catalog for governance and model management
  • Intermediate Python programming experience
  • Familiarity with Generative AI fundamentals, including RAG architectures and their components (chunking, embeddings, vector stores)
  • Basic knowledge of MLflow for experiment tracking and model lifecycle management
  • General awareness of data governance principles, including access control, data lineage, and data privacy

title: Agentic Admins: Managing Databricks @ Databricks
abstract: If we are building a future with 100,000 agents accessing our platform, relying on human administrators to click buttons and approve tickets is a non-starter. The math simply doesn’t work; human admin teams cannot scale linearly with an exponential agent workforce. This session explores how the Data Platform team is solving this by building "Agentic Admins"—autonomous systems capable of managing the platform for us. We will discuss the shift from manual provisioning to agent-led resource management, permissions granting, and configuration tuning. Learn how we are architecting the control plane that allows a small team of humans to govern an army of 100,000 digital users without burning out.

The math simply doesn’t work; human admin teams cannot scale linearly with an exponential agent workforce. This session explores how the Data Platform team is solving this by building "Agentic Admins"—autonomous systems capable of managing the platform for us. We will discuss the shift from manual provisioning to agent-led resource management, permissions granting, and configuration tuning. Learn how we are architecting the control plane that allows a small team of humans to govern an army of 100,000 digital users without burning out.

speakers.name: James Burns
speakers.bio: James has spent more than two decades working at every layer of technology from ASICs to large scale distributed systems. Recently at Twilio, StitchFix, LightStep, and Snowflake, James has worked on secure and scalable observability and data handling. At Databricks, James leads initiatives related to security, privacy, and operational excellence within the Data Platform team handling petabytes of data daily across the globe.

title: Agentic Data Engineering with Lakeflow, Genie Code, and IDEs
abstract: AI agents are changing how data engineering gets done. They can reduce manual work across the lifecycle of building and operating production pipelines, including authoring jobs and pipelines, testing changes, troubleshooting failures, and resolving incidents.In this session, we show how Genie Code can be used with Lakeflow for data engineering. We demonstrate how builders can iterate on Spark Declarative Pipelines (SDP) and Jobs with Genie Code, accelerate authoring, and use agents to investigate failures by tracing logs, dependencies, and candidate fixes.We also cover how local development tools can be used with Lakeflow, including IDEs and tools like Cursor, Claude Code, and Codex. This lets teams use the tools they already have for Lakeflow development and troubleshooting.Finally, we show how to safely adopt agents in real environments through sandboxing and guardrails to protect production data, as well as standard engineering practices such as source control, code review, testing, and CI/CD.

speakers.name: Gal Oshri
speakers.bio: Gal has been building AI products for the past decade. At Databricks, he works on the Databricks Assistant, helping make it a useful tool for all users across the platform. Previously, he worked on machine learning products at AWS (SageMaker Training), Google (TensorFlow), and Microsoft (ML.NET), contributing to tools used by developers and data scientists around the world.

title: Agentic Feature Engineering: How McAfee Drives Personalization With Agent Bricks
abstract: McAfee protects millions of users through products like Scam Detector and Social Privacy Manager. Iteration on adaptive pricing models was bottlenecked by slow, manual feature engineering. To unlock value from massive user signals, we needed to move from ideation to production fast. In this talk, we show how we built an autonomous feature engineering agent on Agent Bricks that cut cycles by 60% and drove $54M+ in revenue—replacing manual SQL with an agent that reasons, iterates and converges.Attendees will see a live blueprint of our agentic pipeline: Analyze & ideate: Agent loads domain context and playbook from Unity Catalog, analyzes baseline and proposes features via LLM reasoning Generate & evaluate: Agent writes code, executes in sandbox, evaluates against baseline and logs every iteration to MLflow. Converge & deploy: Best features publish directly to Unity Catalog feature store. Walk away with a reproducible blueprint for agentic feature engineering on Databricks.
description_raw:
McAfee protects millions of users through products like Scam Detector and Social Privacy Manager. Iteration on adaptive pricing models was bottlenecked by slow, manual feature engineering. To unlock value from massive user signals, we needed to move from ideation to production fast. In this talk, we show how we built an autonomous feature engineering agent on Agent Bricks that cut cycles by 60% and drove $54M+ in revenue—replacing manual SQL with an agent that reasons, iterates and converges.Attendees will see a live blueprint of our agentic pipeline:

  • Analyze & ideate: Agent loads domain context and playbook from Unity Catalog, analyzes baseline and proposes features via LLM reasoning
  • Generate & evaluate: Agent writes code, executes in sandbox, evaluates against baseline and logs every iteration to MLflow.
  • Converge & deploy: Best features publish directly to Unity Catalog feature store.

Walk away with a reproducible blueprint for agentic feature engineering on Databricks.

speakers.name: Arul Bharathi
speakers.bio: Arul Bharathi is currently spearheading the ML Personalization team at McAfee, where he drives top-of-the-funnel revenue optimization initiatives using personalized, dynamic pricing strategies. His journey into machine learning was sparked by an unwavering fascination with how data can enhance user experiences at scale. Over the years, he has channelled that passion into architecting robust ML solutions that deliver real-time personalization and actionable insights for diverse stakeholders. From implementing large-scale data pipelines to fine-tuning algorithms for maximum precision, his expertise spans the end-to-end lifecycle of AI projects. Whether it’s building feature stores to streamline predictive modelling or orchestrating champion-vs.-challenger deployments, he thrives on pushing boundaries to create impactful results. Above all, I believe that well-crafted ML systems can elevate digital experiences, unlocking new dimensions of value for both businesses and their customers.

title: Agentic workflows: Transforming, structuring, enriching data at scale with AI
abstract: Every enterprise unfathomable volumes of unstructured data — documents, emails, transcripts, contracts, support tickets, web content — growing faster than anyone can use it. Agentic workflows promise to turn this data into insights and automations, but they're only as good as the data feeding them.AI Functions bridge that gap at scale: research-backed, composable building blocks optimized for batch processing millions of records. From SQL, they parse documents, classify text, extract structured insights, and resolve unstructured data into clean, governed inputs — one pipeline that feeds every downstream consumer. This session shows how to optimize running agents on your data at scale. We'll walk through how to compose AI Functions into end-to-end agentic workflows, and reveal new AI Functions that push batch enrichment and retrieval further than ever before.

speakers.name: Archika Dogra
speakers.bio: Archika is a Product Manager on the Mosaic AI Platform, focusing on AI governance, observability, and domain-specialized agents.

title: AI Agents for the Frontline: 7-Eleven’s GenAI Maintenance Assistant
abstract: Maintenance technicians at 7-Eleven rely on preventive maintenance, catalogue and parts documents for equipment like HVACs and ovens — often using only their phones. These documents span thousands of files in formats like PDFs and spreadsheets stored in shared drives, making it hard to find the right information quickly. High attrition and missed training sessions compounds knowledge gaps. To address this, 7-Eleven developed a GenAI agentic system using Mosaic AI, Agent Bricks and Databricks Hybrid Search that enables technicians to 1) Retrieve relevant maintenance documents with concise answers 2) Capture equipment images and receive troubleshooting solutions 3) Access parts information by equipment brand 4) Get solutions from the internet. This chatbot bridges knowledge gaps with instant access to institutional knowledge. We will walk through how we built this system, our evaluation methodologies and the three-step prompt framework including guardrails for safe internet searches.

title: AI Will Go Wrong and the Blueprint to Get It Right
abstract: Most AI failures aren't technology problems — they're people, process, and architecture problems. In this hands-on session, you'll witness a live AI trainwreck: an agent operating without guardrails, governance, or the right organizational design. Then, in a real-time post-mortem, we will collectively dissect what broke and why. You'll map the missing middle layer: the intersection of roles, accountability structures, and platform architecture. And you'll work alongside peers to design practical fixes. The session closes with an exercise where attendees act as domain experts to train an AI Judge capable of scaling oversight across enterprise deployments. Expect minimal slides, maximum doing, and a concrete blueprint you can apply within 30 days. This session is built for executives who are tired of theoretical AI frameworks and want proven patterns that make AI projects safer, faster, and genuinely business-ready.

speakers.name: Maria Zervou
speakers.bio: Maria Zervou is a Greek-expat experiencing the London life… and weather! With a Data Science degree and a lot of curiosity about how coding and data can unlock new ways of solving problems, her journey to the data world started. Maria has worked as a Data Scientist and an ML engineer for the past 7 years, where she experienced how data actually challenges and transforms the way we think and operate. Today, Maria is working as a Senior Specialist Solution Architect @Databricks where she helps businesses leverage data and AI to solve their business challenges at scale. Her enthusiasm for emerging technologies aligns her trajectory with the LLM and GenAI wave, exploring how to harness these technologies for tangible, meaningful impact. Fun Fact: Fitness geek, I workout more than I actually work! and as Juvenal once said: ‘Mens sana in corpore sano’ or in Mark Wahlberg’s words “No pain no gain” !

speakers.name: Lexy Kassan
speakers.bio: Lexy Kassan is a Lead Data and AI Strategist at Databricks, overseeing and guiding customer organizations through holistic transformation programmes built on industry trends and best practices. As an Ethicist, Lexy coaches companies on implementing responsible AI and works with public affairs to shape Databricks’ messaging to regulators and lobbyists. Outside of work, Lexy mentors for multiple initiatives supporting underrepresented groups in STEM.

title: AI_DIAGNOSE: Automating Spark Job Debugging with LLM Agents
abstract: Debugging distributed Spark jobs is notoriously difficult, often requiring hours of sifting through massive logs to find root causes. We introduce AI_DIAGNOSE, a new Spark SQL procedure in Databricks Runtime that automates this process using Large Language Models (LLMs).This session explores the architecture of AI_DIAGNOSE, detailing how we implemented a ReAct-style agent within the Spark engine. We will demonstrate how the agent autonomously gathers context, retrieves distributed logs, and analyzes job failures. We'll cover technical challenges like managing LLM context windows with large logs, ensuring structured actionable outputs, and leveraging Databricks Model Serving for secure inference. Join us to learn how AI_DIAGNOSE works under the hood to drastically reduce Mean Time To Resolution (MTTR) for data pipelines.

speakers.name: Allison Wang
speakers.bio: Allison is a software engineer at Databricks, working on Spark SQL and PySpark. She holds a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science from Carnegie Mellon University.

title: AiChemy: How MCP-Powered Agents Cut Drug Discovery Research from Weeks to Minutes
abstract: Drug discovery demands sifting through millions of compounds, thousands of papers, and dozens of databases—a process that costs the industry an average of $2.6B and over 10 years per approved drug. Researchers spend days cross-referencing OpenTargets for genetic evidence, PubChem for chemical properties, and PubMed for supporting literature. What if agents could do it in minutes?Meet AiChemy, a multi-agent assistant that combines external MCP servers like OpenTargets, PubChem, and PubMed with your own chemical libraries on Databricks. Add Agent Skills to find druggable targets, drug candidates and their chemical properties and safety profiles, complete with supporting evidence.You can build your own multi-agent assistant in just 5 minutes using our no-code Agent Bricks. Plus, Databricks assets like Genie, Vector Search, and Functions are automatically turned into MCP servers for seamless integration. Stop scrolling through endless articles and databases—let your agents do it for you!

speakers.name: Yen Low
speakers.bio: Yen Low brings 20 years of Health AI experience translating cutting-edge science into practical real-world products across Pharma (GSK), Payers (Optum, Aetna), Providers (Stanford), Regulators (WHO), and startups (Atropos Health). She received her PhD in Biomedical Informatics and has built teams and AI products from the ground up, spanning drug discovery, RWE, and clinical foundation models. Yen specializes in helping healthcare and life sciences organizations identify AI opportunities and designing systems to capture it. Her latest creation, AiChemy, is a scientific AI agent built to accelerate drug discovery and real-world evidence by connecting custom chemical libraries and knowledge bases via MCP on Databricks.

title: Aladdin and Genie — the Ultimate Engineering and Insights Duo
abstract: In Aladdin, the Genie can grant any wish — but only within clear rules. Databricks Genie brings similar magic to data: users ask questions in plain language and instantly receive insights. But as with any powerful tool, the real challenge is not what it can do, but how it should be used. This session explores real-world use cases for Databricks Genie and the governance controls that keep its magic safe, reliable and compliant. Through the lens of Aladdin and Genie, we show how organizations can unlock fast, self-service analytics while protecting data quality, security, and trust — turning raw wishes into responsible decisions.
description_raw:

But as with any powerful tool, the real challenge is not what it can do, but how it should be used. This session explores real-world use cases for Databricks Genie and the governance controls that keep its magic safe, reliable and compliant.

Through the lens of Aladdin and Genie, we show how organizations can unlock fast, self-service analytics while protecting data quality, security, and trust — turning raw wishes into responsible decisions.

title: Alinta Energy’s Data Platform: From Platform Migration to Enterprise Ready AI-Driven Energy Ops
abstract: Alinta Energy has modernized its enterprise data estate by standardizing on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform. Unifying over 90 systems and 2.4K objects, ADH now supports 36,000+ daily queries via an automated, reliable release pattern. This governed, cost-efficient foundation powers analytics and AI across generation, trading and retail.Key session insights: Business leaders: Learn how a single platform and operating model reduce risk and cost while accelerating time-to-insight through enterprise-scale adoption and repeatable savings. Tech teams: Explore “Pilot for Teams” for scalable production, the ingestion of OT/generation telemetry for near-real-time ML and standardized patterns for lineage and auditability. AI innovators: See how ML forecasts energy demand and how the Databricks agent framework enables agentic workflows for pricing and finance with responsible enterprise controls. Walk away with a pragmatic roadmap to land governed, AI-ready capabilities in energy.

title: An Intro to Building and Scaling Agentic Apps
abstract: Agents have promised a lot lately, but most people just have a job they want done and don't want to spend time on endless science experiments. Teams stall on integration, struggle with scale, and wrestle with how to deliver agents to users safely and reliably inside a real application.This intro session shows how to build and scale agentic apps end-to-end on Databricks. You'll see how Agent Bricks lets you create managed, production-ready agents; how Databricks Apps delivers them to users with governance and observability built in; and how Lakebase powers the transactional backend so your app can act — not just answer.You'll walk away with a clear blueprint for turning your first agent into a production app your whole company can use, and the confidence to move from prototype to scaled deployment without rebuilding your stack.

title: Anthropic + Adidas + Databricks: Unlocking 400 Hours of Productivity Weekly with Conversational AI
abstract: LLMs are transforming how enterprises explore data and make decisions. But how do you integrate conversational AI with existing analytics and data ecosystems?In this joint session, we'll explore the common use cases and design patterns that leading enterprises are adopting. Anthropic will share insights from across their customer use cases on model selection, serving infrastructure, governance and cost optimization, and integration with downstream BI & applications. Then, Adidas will walk through their production implementation: how they deployed Claude via a multi-agent orchestrator to enable 200+ marketing and CRM users to explore data conversationally, compress insight-to-action cycles, and unlock productivity gains. Adidas unlocked up to 400 hours of productivity weekly and accelerated insight-to-action cycles from weeks to daysLearn how Claude, Agent Bricks, AI Gateway and Databricks Genie come together to transform analytics from a bottleneck into a competitive advantage.

title: Apache Iceberg™ Interoperability: First-Class Open Support in Databricks Delta Sharing
abstract: Open data sharing across platform boundaries is now a reality. As organizations move toward multi-engine environments, collaborating across different catalogs and ecosystems is a strategic necessity. With the launch of first-class Iceberg support in Databricks Delta Sharing, you can maintain a single governance layer while supporting a diverse, multi-platform data estate.Key Highlights: First-Class Iceberg Support: Share Delta tables as native Iceberg tables via the Iceberg REST Catalog API for zero-copy sharing to Snowflake, Trino, and Spark. Bi-Directional Interoperability: Bring ""foreign"" Iceberg tables from AWS Glue or Snowflake into Unity Catalog and share them to any recipient. Unified Governance: Share fine-grained Views via secretless authentication, even to non-Databricks platforms. Open Standards, No Lock-In: Shift from closed sharing models to a transparent protocol that supports multiple clouds, formats, and platforms simultaneously.
description_raw:
Open data sharing across platform boundaries is now a reality. As organizations move toward multi-engine environments, collaborating across different catalogs and ecosystems is a strategic necessity. With the launch of first-class Iceberg support in Databricks Delta Sharing, you can maintain a single governance layer while supporting a diverse, multi-platform data estate.Key Highlights:

  • First-Class Iceberg Support: Share Delta tables as native Iceberg tables via the Iceberg REST Catalog API for zero-copy sharing to Snowflake, Trino, and Spark.
  • Bi-Directional Interoperability: Bring ""foreign"" Iceberg tables from AWS Glue or Snowflake into Unity Catalog and share them to any recipient.
  • Unified Governance: Share fine-grained Views via secretless authentication, even to non-Databricks platforms.
  • Open Standards, No Lock-In: Shift from closed sharing models to a transparent protocol that supports multiple clouds, formats, and platforms simultaneously.

speakers.name: Balaji Swamynathan
speakers.bio: Balaji Swamynathan is a leader at Foot Locker, where he leads enterprise data and analytics initiatives focused on modernizing retail data platforms. With deep expertise in Databricks, cloud data architectures, and large‑scale analytics, Balaji partners closely with engineering and business teams to deliver scalable, high‑impact solutions across customer, merchandising, and operations domains. He is passionate about leveraging the lakehouse architecture, AI‑driven analytics, and real‑time insights to enable faster decision‑making and measurable business outcomes in retail.

speakers.name: Mario Cadenas
speakers.bio: Mario Cadenas is a Senior Software Engineer at Databricks, where he builds AppKit — the Node.js and React SDK for Databricks Apps. He focuses on developer tools, test infrastructure, and CLI workflows.

speakers.name: Victor Ditadi
speakers.bio: Creating products for more than one decade.

title: Apps Factory on Databricks​: Provision, Standardize, Observe and Govern 100+ Apps​
abstract: Databricks Apps makes it easy to publish the first app. We address the compounding challenges organizations face when scaling to 10, 50, or 100+ Databricks Apps: inconsistent architecture, unclear access control, missing standards, ungoverned AI components and runaway costs. We will walk through a comprehensive methodology, including solutions and principles to provision, govern and observe dozens of applications at enterprise scale. The session includes reference architecture, frameworks, templates, a production readiness checklist, CI/CD setup, security & access control, unified logging and cost tracking. Attendees receive a comprehensive technical governance and adaptation playbook to effectively manage and scale app ecosystems.

speakers.name: Domonkos Pal
speakers.bio: Domonkos Pal is Head of Digital Products at Hiflylabs, where he helps enterprises design and build data and AI applications on Databricks for clients across Europe and the US. His work centers on Databricks Apps, Lakebase, Genie, agents and the broader app ecosystem — particularly how to make AI applications scalable, secure, and maintainable in production, with a strong focus on DevEx and agentic engineering workflows. As the world's first Databricks Apps MVP (2025–2026), Domonkos returns to the Data + AI Summit stage in 2026.

title: Architecting Agentic Security: Scaling Threat Detection with LLM and Lake house Architecture
abstract: When new threat intelligence lands, attackers are already moving. Adobe’s security team needed more than automation, so we built an agentic security platform that ingests data, orchestrates hunts, and surfaces detections with minimal human intervention. Using reasoning, agents analyze threats, validate attack paths, and execute fleet sweeps, reducing threat detection timelines from days to minutes. In this talk, we’ll walk through how we built this system on a lakehouse architecture. Databricks Workflows orchestrate parallel hunt campaigns across various security logs, autonomously triggering jobs and aggregating results. You’ll learn how we designed for agentic behavior, including multi-source ingestion into a single pipeline, workflow orchestration logic that decides when and what to hunt, and production patterns that keep the system reliable at scale. We’ll share architecture and code so you can adapt these agentic security patterns in your own environment.

speakers.name: Sai Kiran Uppu
speakers.bio: Sai Kiran Uppu is a Senior Security Researcher on Adobe's Cyber Threat Research and Intelligence team, where he architects Adobe's agentic AI threat intelligence and fleet sweep platform protecting over a billion users. He is the 2026 Gold winner for AI Security Innovator of the Year at the Cybersecurity Excellence Awards and a Recorded Future RSA CTF champion. Kiran has delivered the keynote at ToroHack 10.0, served as an invited panelist at Johns Hopkins' Security Metamorphosis 2026, and spoken at IEEE SVCC, The Diana Initiative, BSides SLC, Penn State, and the UMBC Cyber Defense Lab. He judges Atlantic Council's Cyber 9/12 Strategy Challenge and student hackathons, and is CISSP, GCTI, and AWS Security - Specialty certified.

title: Ask Astro: How CAVA Built an Enterprise scale Agentic Data Copilot on Databricks
abstract: At CAVA, teams across all business domains rely on governed data in Databricks to drive decisions across the enterprise. This includes datasets used by Investor Relations to prepare earnings materials, support board reporting, and inform market-facing communications.To enable users seamlessly access and act on data across through a single interface, we built Ask Astro, an enterprise-scale agentic data copilot on Databricks that consolidates analytics, institutional knowledge, and operational workflows into one governed conversational experience.Ask Astro is powered by a Multi-Agent Supervisor architecture that intelligently routes user requests to specialized agents and governed tools. The system is deployed in production through databricks apps and as a Slack bot.In this session, we will walk through the end-to-end architecture of Ask Astro, including the Supervisor Agent, MCP setup, internet tool governance, and production hardening practices that enabled safe enterprise scale.

The system is deployed in production through databricks apps and as a Slack bot.In this session, we will walk through the end-to-end architecture of Ask Astro, including the Supervisor Agent, MCP setup, internet tool governance, and production hardening practices that enabled safe enterprise scale.

title: AskPorto - a unified Agentic AI solution for data analytics and discovery
abstract: The Central Provident Fund Baord (CPFB), Singapore's social security agency, manages complex data across multiple business lines with unique analytical requirements despite centralising on Databricks Lakehouse. Users struggled with traditional querying methods and fragmented governance documentation across multiple sources, creating analytical silos. Employees needed intelligent systems capable of querying multiple sources without technical expertise, requiring contextualised responses understanding domain-specific terminology and automatic validation. Solution: unified conversational interface with multi-agent orchestration built on Databricks architecture. Orchestrating AI routes queries to specialised agents—Databricks Genie for contextualised data answers, Purview for metadata discovery, RAG-powered agents for governance knowledge—whilst maintaining audit trails and compliance. Stakeholders access comprehensive insights via conversational queries with automated accuracy checks.

Users struggled with traditional querying methods and fragmented governance documentation across multiple sources, creating analytical silos. Employees needed intelligent systems capable of querying multiple sources without technical expertise, requiring contextualised responses understanding domain-specific terminology and automatic validation. Solution: unified conversational interface with multi-agent orchestration built on Databricks architecture. Orchestrating AI routes queries to specialised agents—Databricks Genie for contextualised data answers, Purview for metadata discovery, RAG-powered agents for governance knowledge—whilst maintaining audit trails and compliance.  Stakeholders access comprehensive insights via conversational queries with automated accuracy checks.

title: AstraZeneca's Multi-Agent System: Lessons Scaling Agents by 10x With Agent Bricks
abstract: AstraZeneca built a multi-agent system using Agent Bricks to transform how commercial teams access pharmaceutical data. The Brand Assistant uses a supervisor agent coordinating specialized sub-agents across therapeutic areas, combining structured data via Genie Spaces with unstructured documents via Knowledge Assistant. Unity Catalog integration with Entra ID enforces strict permission boundaries. MCP unlocks third-party tool integration.The architecture scaled from 5-agent POC to 20+ agents in production, designed for 50+. Key components: Multi-Agent Supervisor for orchestration Genie Spaces for NL-to-SQL Knowledge Assistant for documents Unity Catalog for row/column-level security MLflow for tracing. This session covers: Multi-agent architecture patterns Permission-preserving design When to split supervisors vs. add agents Human-in-the-loop testing Why bad data breaks agents regardless of tech Organizational challenges of agent ownership Lessons from scaling
description_raw:
AstraZeneca built a multi-agent system using Agent Bricks to transform how commercial teams access pharmaceutical data. The Brand Assistant uses a supervisor agent coordinating specialized sub-agents across therapeutic areas, combining structured data via Genie Spaces with unstructured documents via Knowledge Assistant.

Unity Catalog integration with Entra ID enforces strict permission boundaries. MCP unlocks third-party tool integration.The architecture scaled from 5-agent POC to 20+ agents in production, designed for 50+.

Key components:

  • Multi-Agent Supervisor for orchestration
  • Genie Spaces for NL-to-SQL
  • Knowledge Assistant for documents
  • Unity Catalog for row/column-level security
  • MLflow for tracing.

This session covers:

  • Multi-agent architecture patterns
  • Permission-preserving design
  • When to split supervisors vs. add agents
  • Human-in-the-loop testing
  • Why bad data breaks agents regardless of tech
  • Organizational challenges of agent ownership
  • Lessons from scaling

speakers.name: Brian Burke

speakers.name: Homayoon Moradi

title: Atlassian's Journey of Using AI/BI Genie From Pilot to Production and What's Next?
abstract: This session is not a generic “AI is the future” talk. It is a practical, experience‑based guide to taking Databricks AI/BI Genie from a promising pilot to a trusted, production‑grade capability. Dashboards answer “what,” but not “why” or “what if.” Here is where Genie comes in.We’ll walk through our journey at Atlassian from pilot to production—designing Genie spaces on curated tables and metrics, building an evaluation framework for accuracy and coverage, and integrating Genie into our everyday tools and Rovo AI agent. We will touch upon how Databricks and Atlassian partnered to bring the power of Genie to our Rovo Marketplace and built the ability to route a user question to the right Genie space.In the end attendees will have a concrete “0‑to‑95*” playbook for unleashing their data with Genie to reach a point where roughly 95% of everyday questions can be answered accurately, safely, and self‑service freeing data teams to focus on higher‑value work and empowering faster decisions.

title: Automate Deployment with Databricks Asset Bundles
abstract: This hands-on course covers DevOps principles, including CI/CD and testing, applied to Databricks data engineering pipelines. It focuses on continuous deployment using the Databricks REST API, SDK, and CLI, specifically incorporating Databricks Asset Bundles (DABs) for streamlined deployment across environments. You will learn to modify, validate, deploy, and execute DABs for multiple configurations using the Databricks CLI. The course also introduces using Visual Studio Code as an IDE for local development and concludes with automating deployment using GitHub Actions and DABs. By the end, you'll be able to automate Databricks project deployments with DABs, improving efficiency through DevOps practices. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Strong familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform Intermediate Python programming experience, including designing and implementing functions and classes Intermediate SQL experience for data analysis and transformation Experience with data ingestion and transformation using PySpark Familiarity with DevOps practices and basic Git version control
description_raw:
This hands-on course covers DevOps principles, including CI/CD and testing, applied to Databricks data engineering pipelines. It focuses on continuous deployment using the Databricks REST API, SDK, and CLI, specifically incorporating Databricks Asset Bundles (DABs) for streamlined deployment across environments. You will learn to modify, validate, deploy, and execute DABs for multiple configurations using the Databricks CLI. The course also introduces using Visual Studio Code as an IDE for local development and concludes with automating deployment using GitHub Actions and DABs. By the end, you'll be able to automate Databricks project deployments with DABs, improving efficiency through DevOps practices.

Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. 

Prerequisites

  • Strong familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform
  • Intermediate Python programming experience, including designing and implementing functions and classes
  • Intermediate SQL experience for data analysis and transformation
  • Experience with data ingestion and transformation using PySpark
  • Familiarity with DevOps practices and basic Git version control

title: Automating KPI Reasoning and Narrative Generation With Agent Bricks and Databricks Apps
abstract: Monthly performance reporting previously required approximately 7 person-days each month, as analysts manually wrote queries, reviewed distributed datasets, interpreted metric relationships and produced presentation-ready reports. This repetitive process tied up analytical resources and slowed decision-making. To address this, I built an app-based automation pipeline using multi-supervised agents with Agent Bricks and Databricks Apps. The agent operates as an analyst, systematically analyzing relevant data sources through a structured, table-driven representation to identify key drivers, evaluate metric relationships, and generate narrative insights without manual querying. Outputs are delivered through Databricks Apps as a self-serve analytics platform with automatically refreshed metrics and visualizations, eliminating repetitive reporting work, reducing monthly effort by approximately 7 person-days, and enabling agent-authored reporting for both analytics and non-analytics stakeholders.

I am particularly interested in building analytical systems that make AI and data more actionable, measurable, and impactful in real-world environments.

title: Automating Phishing Triage With Agentic AI and Knowledge Graph Memory on Databricks
abstract: Phishing attacks remain a persistent threat, exploiting human trust through sophisticated impersonation. In this session, we present a Databricks-based compound agentic system using knowledge graphs, multiple agents, and MLflow to automate triage of reported phishing emails at scale, reducing analyst workload and accelerating threat response. We walk through scalable pipelines built on Spark Structured Streaming and Delta Lake, then describe how our knowledge graph provides long-term memory, storing historical emails to enable reasoning across campaigns. We demonstrate agentic orchestration with LangGraph where specialized agents use tools to analyze headers, sender reputation, body content, URLs, and behaviors before producing triage decisions with reasoning traces. We cover evaluations, observability, including hallucination detection, metrics, and tracking with MLflow. Join us to learn practical patterns that combine multi-agent reasoning with production workflows on Databricks.

Join us to learn practical patterns that combine multi-agent reasoning with production workflows on Databricks.

speakers.name: andrew HintonA
speakers.bio: Andrew Hinton is a Principal Data Scientist and AI strategist with 15 years turning research breakthroughs into enterprise‑scale solutions. At one of the world’s largest healthcare companies he builds GPU‑accelerated, AI and streaming systems on Databricks and AWS that convert security telemetry into real‑time insight from billions of events each day. A published author, mentor, and inventor with multiple AI‑security patents pending, Andrew blends rigorous data science with modern MLOps to operationalize generative and predictive models at cloud scale. At Data + AI Summit 2025 he will unveil his patent‑pending bot‑detection architecture and share field‑tested lessons on securely scaling deep‑learning workloads.

title: BASF Coatings: Scaling Enterprise Multi-Agent Systems with Federated Governance on Databricks
abstract: As enterprises evolve from isolated AI assistants to domain-specific multi-agent systems, scaling innovation without overloading central teams becomes critical. BASF Coatings addresses this with MAUI (Multi-Agent Universal Interface), a Databricks-native platform that enables business to build, operate, and scale multi-agent solutions within a unified enterprise framework.MAUI expands the success of the MarketMind project across supply chain, marketing and sales. By separating responsibilities among domain experts, data stewards, and users, it accelerates deployment while maintaining security and governance. Technically, MAUI runs as Databricks Apps, using Lakebase for context store and UC for secure data access. Deployed across three units with 1000+ users, MAUI achieves 95% accuracy in cross-domain data retrieval, reducing query times by 50–80%. Its federated governance allows stewards to improve intelligence and expand usecases, with full enterprise rollout planned.

title: Beacon: Revolutionizing Media Analytics With Near Real-Time Declarative Pipelines
abstract: CondéNast operates in a digital first media landscape where editorial and business decisions are defined in minutes. To meet this need, we built Beacon, a near real time media analytics platform that transformed a fragmented, high latency analytics stack into a unified architecture powered by the Databricks Lakehouse and Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines.Having InfluxDB and Qlik solution, introduced a 15 minutes data lag and inconsistent KPIs. It was re-architected by streaming data directly from web collectors into Databricks, unifying streaming and batch processing through declarative pipelines. A Redis-based caching layer enables sub second access while preserving the Lakehouse as the system of record.This approach reduced end-to-end latency to under 10 seconds, enabling editorial, revenue and growth teams to monitor article performance, live events and monetisation signals in near real time through a single analytics experience. This will be enabled with Genie LLM in future roadmap.

He is passionate about building high-performing teams and driving innovation in data and AI.

title: Behind the Curtain: How We Do Eval in Genie
abstract: Evaluating AI systems is notoriously challenging—especially when correctness isn’t binary. In this talk, we’ll walk through how the Genie engineering team approaches evaluation at scale, from defining what “good” looks like to building reliable, automated eval pipelines. We’ll cover our mix of offline benchmarks, human-in-the-loop validation, and production feedback loops, along with the tradeoffs we’ve encountered. The goal is to share practical patterns and lessons learned that can help teams move faster while maintaining trust in their models.
description_raw: Evaluating AI systems is notoriously challenging—especially when correctness isn’t binary. In this talk, we’ll walk through how the Genie engineering team approaches evaluation at scale, from defining what “good” looks like to building reliable, automated eval pipelines. We’ll cover our mix of offline benchmarks, human-in-the-loop validation, and production feedback loops, along with the tradeoffs we’ve encountered. The goal is to share practical patterns and lessons learned that can help teams move faster while maintaining trust in their models.

title: Behind the curtain: Key learnings from building Genie
abstract: As organizations scale, the real challenge isn't just single talk AI — but designing a system like Genie that can coordinate multiple agents, domains, MCP connections, structured data, unstructured data, etc. while keeping the user experience effortless.We've introduces a unified, multi-agent interface that allows anyone to ask complex questions across their entire data and agent estate and get answers without needing to understand how the system works underneath.In this session, we’ll pull back the curtain on how Genie was designed and share the best practices we developed to make multi-agent analytics usable, reliable, and scalable.In this session, we’ll cover:- How to design a unified experience across multiple agents and data domains- How to route, orchestrate, and combine results from different agents- How to balance flexibility with simplicity for business users- Key lessons learned building and deploying multi-agent systems in production

title: Benchmarks Don't Lie: Inside Lakebase Performance Engineering
abstract: Get an inside look at how the Lakebase engineering team designs benchmarks that reflect real-world workload patterns rather than synthetic best-case scenarios, and how we isolate variables across the storage, compute, and network layers to understand where time is actually spent. From there, we'll walk through results from our latest engineering work, covering throughput under concurrent load, latency across workload types, and the optimizations that moved the needle most.This is not a marketing benchmarks talk. It's an engineering post-mortem on what we tested, what surprised us, and what the numbers mean for how you architect and tune your own Lakebase workloads.

speakers.name: David Wein
speakers.bio: David Wein is a Principal Software Engineer on Databricks Lakebase, focused on performance and scalability for cloud-native transactional workloads. With nearly three decades of experience building enterprise OLTP databases at Sybase, SAP, AWS Aurora PostgreSQL, and now Databricks, he has designed core kernels, logging, and durability systems, holds multiple patents in database internals, and is a frequent speaker on running mission-critical applications in the cloud.

title: Best Practices for Lakebase
abstract: This deep-dive session covers architectural and operational best practices for building production-grade applications on Lakebase. You'll learn how to design schemas and connection patterns for performance, how to leverage autoscaling and branching for cost-efficient development workflows, and how to apply governance controls at scale using Unity Catalog. Whether you're migrating an existing PostgreSQL workload or building something new, you'll leave with actionable guidance for running Lakebase reliably in production.

title: Beyond Batch: Engineering Self-Evolving Ingestion with Databricks Auto Loader
abstract: Is your data engineering team trapped in brittle schema fixes and rising costs? As data sources multiply, traditional ingestion stalls enterprise insights. At Capital One Software, we’ve moved beyond static ETL to a “self-evolving” framework that treats data as a dynamic stream. This session reveals our modular architecture using Databricks Auto Loader to bridge multi-platform data into S3 with zero manual overhead. We’ll dive into how Schema Evolution and Rescue Columns handle upstream drift automatically, keeping pipelines live when source systems change. Key takeaways: Modular design: Decouple ingestion from transformation for reusable, enterprise-scale patterns Dynamic schema management: Strategies to detect and adapt to drift without breaking dependencies Cost optimization: Real-world tactics for balancing trigger intervals and compute for peak efficiency. Transition from reactive maintenance to automated data empowerment

title: Beyond Dashboards: How Grupo Bimbo Delivers Insights with Databricks AgentBricks
abstract: Commercial operations at Grupo Bimbo generate thousands of transactions every day across sales routes. While dashboards highlight valued insights, commercial team are constantly on the move and don’t have time to explore BI tools.Grupo Bimbo rethought insight delivery using Databricks Agent Bricks — moving from “look at dashboards” to “ask and receive answers.” For supervisors, we implemented AI/BI Genie as a conversational AI layer over sales data, enabling natural-language questions like “Which clients were missed this week?”— no dashboards required.For executives, we built an AI query agent powered by Claude that automatically reads multiple KPIs and summarizes insights, generating reports delivered directly by email.All agents run on Databricks with Unity Catalog ensuring secure data access.We’ll share the real architecture, prompt design choices and lessons learned and how this approach accelerated decisions, expanded insight adoption and improved sales process efficiency.

title: Beyond Medallion: Architecting Disney’s DATOS for Complex, Real-Time Data Streams With Databricks
abstract: Disney’s Medallion architecture laid a strong foundation for enterprise data governance, but emerging needs—ranging from personalized user experiences to real-time financial insights—required a richer, near real-time view across domains such as subscriptions, billing and payments. DATOS addresses this by serving as a contextual aggregation and abstraction layer built on the Databricks Lakehouse Platform. Using Structured Streaming and Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines, DATOS ingests diverse sources like Kafka, Kinesis, and data lakes while absorbing upstream schema and delivery variability to provide stable, reusable data products. Liquid Clustering enhances performance on high‑volume tables, and Unity Catalog ensures unified governance and security. This session explores how DATOS reduces operational overhead, decouples producers and consumers, accelerates onboarding, and delivers a context-aware source of truth that powers real-time analytics, Unity Dashboards, and advanced AI/ML workloads.

title: Beyond Monitoring: Lakeflow Observability for Operational Health
abstract: It is 3 AM. A dashboard is stale, an SLA slipped, and everyone is guessing which job, pipeline, or dependency broke first. That is when surface-level monitoring stops being useful. You need observability that shows the health of your workflows and helps you find the cause fast.In this session, we will show how Lakeflow Observability helps data engineering teams move from reactive monitoring to real operational health. You will see how to monitor runs, tasks, dependencies, alerts, and health metrics across jobs and pipelines, and how Databricks system tables provide the historical depth needed for root-cause analysis, performance analysis, cost tracking, and reliability reporting at scale.We will also cover practical patterns for tracking streaming backlog, freshness, SLA risk, and failure trends, and show how Genie Code can help teams troubleshoot faster using natural language. Your 3 AM self will thank you.

title: Beyond the Trace: adidas’ Agent Digital Twin for Governance, Cost and ROI
abstract: Your agent answered but can you prove why, what it cost, and what breaks when a tool or prompt changes? AI spend is forecast near $1.5T in 2025 and GenAI at $644B, yet Gartner predicts >40% of agentic AI projects will be canceled by end-2027 as costs rise and controls lag. At adidas (200+ serving endpoints, 300+ data and AI products, 6k+ registered models, 600k+ pipeline runs), we go beyond per-run traces to an agent digital twin: A lakehouse control plane mapping agent → tool → prompt → retrieval → model call → post-processing, then rolling up risk, quality and unit economics across a fleet. You’ll see a Databricks pattern with MLflow Tracing, Unity Catalog + system tables for audit evidence and DBU/cost signals, plus eval/optimization loops with governance guardrails. Key highlights: Digital-twin control plane for fleets Per-hop unit economics + cost leak forensics Continuous audit evidence by design ROI attribution by agent behavior

speakers.name: Pandey, Rahul
speakers.bio: Rahul is a Senior Solution Architect at Adidas, where he focuses on constructing ML architecture designs that are both cost-effective and efficient. Currently, he is taking responsibility as a Machine Learning Journey Owner in the Lakehouse platform, taking charge of advancing the field of machine learning. One of his primary motivations is to promote awareness of sustainability in AI among Data Science teams

speakers.name: Mahavir Teraiya
speakers.bio: Mahavir Teraiya is a Senior Resident Solutions Architect at Databricks with 15+ years of experience in Data, ML, and large-scale AI platform design. Formerly with AWS and Zalando, he now focuses on helping enterprise customers scale GenAI through agentic workflows. At DAIS, Mahavir will present the groundbreaking GenAI platform built with Adidas the first of its kind in EMEA driving 20% productivity gains and up to 91% cost savings. He is currently pursuing a PhD in Federated Learning and an MBA in IT. A passionate technologist and frequent speaker at AWS events and GopherCon, Mahavir thrives at the intersection of cutting-edge tech and business value.

title: Bionic Supply Chain: Architecting a Unified Agentic Framework for Risk & Cost Recovery
abstract: The modern supply chain is fractured, stuck in a “visibility vacuum” of cost asymmetry and a “triage trap” where material and production planners miss critical risks and purchasing managers miss cost targets due to cognitive overload. To bridge this gap, we present a unified vision for the AI-enabled supply chain ecosystem, moving beyond disparate tools to a cohesive intelligent platform. We introduce an agentic architecture built entirely on the lakehouse, utilizing Databricks Apps and Unity Catalog to serve distinct personas. For purchasing managers, we deploy NOVAA, an engine using MLflow to benchmark “should-costs” and Mosaic AI to arrest margin leakage and generate negotiation playbooks. Simultaneously, SONAR, our supply planners’ control tower, empowers them by aggregating SAP/TMS data into a unified truth. Here, a hierarchical agentic workflow, powered by Agent Bricks, bridges classical ML with GenAI to predict disruptions and orchestrate sub-agents for autonomous resolution.
description_raw:
The modern supply chain is fractured, stuck in a “visibility vacuum” of cost asymmetry and a “triage trap” where material and production planners miss critical risks and purchasing managers miss cost targets due to cognitive overload. To bridge this gap, we present a unified vision for the AI-enabled supply chain ecosystem, moving beyond disparate tools to a cohesive intelligent platform.

We introduce an agentic architecture built entirely on the lakehouse, utilizing Databricks Apps and Unity Catalog to serve distinct personas. For purchasing managers, we deploy NOVAA, an engine using MLflow to benchmark “should-costs” and Mosaic AI to arrest margin leakage and generate negotiation playbooks. Simultaneously, SONAR, our supply planners’ control tower, empowers them by aggregating SAP/TMS data into a unified truth. Here, a hierarchical agentic workflow, powered by Agent Bricks, bridges classical ML with GenAI to predict disruptions and orchestrate sub-agents for autonomous resolution.

speakers.name: Nikhil Pande
speakers.bio: Nikhil Pande is a distinguished data leader and Staff Data Architect at Rivian, specializing in the design of scalable data ecosystems and the orchestration of agentic AI. Having played an instrumental role in migrating Rivian’s data landscape to Databricks, he holds registered intellectual property for his innovations in real-time PLM ingestion pipelines. Currently, Nikhil champions AI adoption by architecting agentic frameworks; he recently pioneered a Procurement Negotiations App utilizing a master-agent architecture and human-in-the-loop feedback. With over a decade of expertise and a Master’s in Data Science from the University of Illinois, Nikhil balances technical rigor with a lifelong passion for scuba diving and hiking.

speakers.name: Collin Brosko
speakers.bio: Collin Brosko is an experienced data and analytics leader currently serving as the Manager of Data & Analytics at Rivian Automotive LLC. He specializes in building scalable data architectures, driving AI and machine learning implementations, and leading engineering teams to deliver end-to-end solutions that optimize complex operations. Prior to his current role, Collin held key data science and architecture positions at industry-leading organizations, including IBM and ZF Group. Throughout his career, he has focused on modernizing data platforms, deploying advanced analytics, and transforming raw data into strategic, actionable business value. Collin holds a Master of Applied Data Science from the University of Michigan

title: Blue Origin — Using Data to Build a Road to Space
abstract: At Blue Origin, Databricks is the central data lakehouse for design, manufacturing, and launch operations. Scaling to thousands of pipelines without scaling headcount required rethinking how we manage data in the first place. We will share how we use AI and automation to build pipelines and tables with no human intervention required and how we automated data quality and metadata management to make our data AI ready. This enables federated data teams across the company to use trustworthy data and AI to build a road to space! We will walk through a multi-agent system built on this foundation to manage satellite telemetry. An orchestrator queries a knowledge graph of prior episodes and a vector store of resolution procedures, then routes tasks to specialist agents for anomaly classification, forecasting, resolution, and command safety validation. Each execution is captured, clustered, and distilled back into procedural memory, so the system improves from its own operational history.

speakers.name: Theodor Talvac
speakers.bio: Theo Talvac is a Senior Manager of Ground Software at Blue Origin, where he leads an engineering team building the satellite ground system for the Blue Ring program. He serves as single-threaded leader for a multi-agent AI platform for autonomous satellite operations and is architecting Blue Ring's Mission Planning System to support the platform's various missions across the commercial, civil, and government space. . Previously, Theo held principal engineering and product management roles at York Space Systems, AST SpaceMobile, and Kratos, spanning Command & Control, Flight Dynamics, and Mission Planning across various programs in GEO and LEO orbit regimes. He holds a B.S. & M.S. in Aerospace Engineering from the University of Maryland.

title: Blueprint to Breakthrough: Activating Banking Analytics, AI, and Agentic Use Cases
abstract: This session continues the story of how a Regional Bank is modernizing its AI and analytics journey, evolving from building the foundation to activating value through measurable business outcomes. Powered by Databricks and Deloitte’s industry experience, the Enterprise Data and Analytics Hub is a one-stop shop for line-of-business and enterprise consumption, anchored by a governed Data Marketplace that simplifies discovery and reuse of trusted data products. As part of this evolution, Databricks serves as the knowledge base for enterprise agentic and AI capabilities, and Databricks Genie is being leveraged to accelerate development and delivery of business-ready solutions through conversational and developer-enabled experiences. The session will highlight how the bank is operationalizing these capabilities through a prioritized pipeline of high-impact use cases by accelerating speed to insight, improving consistency across reporting and analytics, and realizing value at scale. Discover how the bank is embedding advanced analytics and AI, including agentic and multi-agent systems, into decisioning and workflows with strong governance, security, and controls.

speakers.name: Shailender Sidhu
speakers.bio: Shailender is a leader in Deloitte Consulting's Financial Services Industry AI & Engineering practice. He works with US and Global financial services institutions to deliver and advise on large scale enterprise AI & Data driven transformations and change agenda. He supports line of business leaders across the financial services industry segments to drive growth, regulatory, compliance and efficiency agenda.

speakers.name: Mohan Sankararaman
speakers.bio: Mohan Sankararaman is executive vice president and chief information officer for First Horizon Corporation. Sankararaman joined the company in 2009 and has served in his current role since 2020. Prior to this role, he was director of strategic initiatives, leading efforts with a focus on executional excellence and managing the day-to-day activities for several mergers and acquisitions to ensure a smooth integration, conversion, and transition for the bank’s clients and associates.

title: BP Accelerates Maintenance Decisions With Conversational Analytics With Genie
abstract: BP, a global energy company, operates complex, asset-intensive environments where maintenance, reliability, safety and logistics decisions must be made quickly and with confidence. Across refineries and sites, teams rely on data from dozens of systems, including SAP and multiple operational tools, to plan maintenance work, manage materials and ensure equipment safety. Before Databricks AI/BI Genie, accessing this information required deep system knowledge, manual coordination across teams, long request queues and weeks of engineering effort to produce custom reports. With Genie, BP introduced a simple, natural language interface to its maintenance data, giving a large number of business users an intuitive way to access insights without bottlenecking on a small number of colleagues with deep technical expertise.

title: BP Cut Cloud Spend by 43% and Speed Critical Jobs From Days to ~8 Hours With Databricks SQL
abstract: BP is a 117-year-old global energy company spanning upstream exploration and production, refining, trading, retail, and growing low-carbon businesses. Its data estate evolved across classic enterprise warehouse patterns and cloud stacks split by business unit—upstream on Azure and retail on AWS. Hadoop-era systems became too slow at scale, and Redshift-based serving was costly and hard to right-size. BP standardized on Databricks SQL as its warehouse and BI serving layer across clouds, using Unity Catalog and Delta Sharing to reuse governed data without copying it. The team reduced cloud cost 43% and accelerated key processing from days to ~8 hours, while powering Power BI and other tools through Databricks SQL Serverless warehouses.

title: Breaking Down Data Silos: Mastercard’s Path to Comprehensive, Privacy‑Safe Marketing Intelligence
abstract: In today’s rapidly evolving marketing landscape, global and multi market teams in companies like Mastercard face unprecedented complexity - fragmented Martech tools, siloed data, proliferating channels, and stricter privacy regulations. The results are shallow insights, weaker personalization, and difficulty measuring ROI across channels. A unified data platform built on a governed, scalable data lake addresses these challenges by integrating global data into a single source of truth, enabling identity resolution, advanced segmentation, real time activation, and consistent full funnel insights. We will explore how Mastercard is building a CDP in Databricks with marketing data sources powered by Unity Catalog. Using Data Vault modeling within the CDP allows data to be onboarded easily and enables teams to ingest data quickly. Finally, we will cover how Unity Catalog enables governance, ensuring each team has access to only the data they need, while sensitive attributes remain protected.
