speakers.name: Aaron Beyer
speakers.bio: Aaron Beyer is a Senior Data Engineer at Rearc with more than eight years of experience designing, building, and optimizing modern data platforms. He specializes in architecting and maintaining scalable data pipelines and services in Databricks, and he holds the Databricks Solution Architect Champion certification.

title: Breaking the Silos: Data Fusion for Financial Crime and Cyber Security
abstract: Traditional silos between cybersecurity, payments and fraud detection often hide the "connective tissue" of modern threats. By adopting a Databricks-based SIEM 2.0 and an Instrumentation Data Lake, NAB has enabled "Data Fusion"—the ability to instantly correlate a spike in payment latency with specific security threat vectors. This talk outlines the "hub-and-spoke" governance model used to map common entities across 50+ distinct sources, enabling a unified security posture that enhances the firm’s safety without stifling innovation.

title: Bridging Transactional Speed and Analytical Depth with AI/BI, Genies Lakebase & Databricks Apps
abstract: Discover how Databricks transformed a basic Gradio app into GTM Hub 2.0, an enterprise-grade platform serving 10,000+ global users (Sales, Marketing, XDR) with personalized insights.Core Architecture & Achievements:Databricks Apps (Enterprise Scale): Built a React/FastAPI application serving 10k+ concurrent users with <3s load times. Features include RBAC, multi-persona views, and seamless cross-origin embedding.AI/BI Dashboards: Embedded dynamic dashboards with global filters, bookmarkable states, and usage tracking for optimized analytics.Genie (Conversational AI): Integrated multi-agent support. A supervisor agent (via MLFlow) routes natural language queries across specialized spaces, eliminating the need for SQL.Lakebase (State Management): Leveraged PostgreSQL on Databricks to handle user preferences, session history, and caching for real-time personalization.The Result: A unified, modular analytics experience with robust telemetry and a future-proof architecture.

speakers.name: Abhinav Bhatnagar
speakers.bio: I am a technical leader specializing in building data science and AI capabilities that drive business value. At Databricks, I lead teams to deliver scalable, enterprise-grade Data & AI solutions, fostering innovation, mentorship, and inclusivity. I excel at aligning technical vision with business goals to deliver impactful results.

speakers.name: Wenwen Xie
speakers.bio: Wenwen Xie is an experienced Machine Learning and Generative AI Solutions Architect at Databricks, based in Jersey City, New Jersey. At Databricks, Wenwen specializes in the design and deployment of advanced ML systems, focusing heavily on model evaluation and validation frameworks. She is particularly passionate about Evaluation-Driven Development, advocating strongly for integrating robust model evaluation as a foundational practice in ML system design. Prior to joining Databricks, Wenwen served briefly as a Data Scientist Lead Vice President at J.P. Morgan. Her earlier role as Senior Data Scientist and Machine Learning Engineer at LexisNexis significantly shaped her expertise in generative AI and natural language processing (NLP).

title: Bringing Agents to Data: How Workday Architects Governed Agentic AI with Iceberg and Unity Catalog
abstract: Traditional “data-to-compute” models create latency and fragmented governance that hinder Agentic AI. To solve this, Workday and Databricks advocate “bringing agents to data” via the Databricks Lakehouse. By operating on data in situ, autonomous agents maintain real-time consistency without the risks of replication. This architecture enables reliable multi-agent coordination at scale while ensuring unified data integrity and reduced operational costs across the enterprise. The technical core uses Apache Iceberg for ACID transactions and snapshot isolation, providing a stable foundation for agent reasoning. Databricks Unity Catalog adds a unified governance layer, enforcing fine-grained security, lineage, and auditability. This production-grade approach significantly cuts query latency and storage expenses, allowing organizations to scale from single-agent pilots to hundreds of coordinated agents across multiple departments like Customer Experience, RevOps and Analytics.

speakers.name: Jason Reid
speakers.bio: Jason Reid is a Director of Product Management at Databricks and co-founder of Tabular. He's currently working on enhancing data compatibility and interoperability between lakehouse formats.

title: Build and Deploy Databricks Projects Using Automation Bundles and Agents
abstract: Modern data engineering teams struggle with environment drift, manual deployment errors, and fragmented assets that make it difficult to reliably scale, govern, and productionize complex data and ML workflows. With Declarative Automation Bundles (DABs), engineers can easily build, deploy, and operate data and AI projects on Databricks. By defining your entire project as a declarative bundle of assets, DABs help you apply proven software engineering practices like source control, testing, and CI/CD consistently and at scale. In this session, you’ll walk through structuring real DABs projects, handling dev vs. prod differences, integrating with GitHub Actions or Azure DevOps, and using new workspace authoring and debugging capabilities to keep deployments safe and repeatable. We’ll close with a first look at BrickOps, Databricks’ new operating model for data engineering in the age of AI, showing how DABs‑powered blueprints, governed deployments, monitoring, and agents work together to reduce toil, detect and troubleshoot pipeline and job issues, and give teams an auditable, AI‑ready foundation for production data products.
description_raw:
Modern data engineering teams struggle with environment drift, manual deployment errors, and fragmented assets that make it difficult to reliably scale, govern, and productionize complex data and ML workflows.

With Declarative Automation Bundles (DABs), engineers can easily build, deploy, and operate data and AI projects on Databricks. By defining your entire project as a declarative bundle of assets, DABs help you apply proven software engineering practices like source control, testing, and CI/CD consistently and at scale.

In this session, you’ll walk through structuring real DABs projects, handling dev vs. prod differences, integrating with GitHub Actions or Azure DevOps, and using new workspace authoring and debugging capabilities to keep deployments safe and repeatable.

We’ll close with a first look at BrickOps, Databricks’ new operating model for data engineering in the age of AI, showing how DABs‑powered blueprints, governed deployments, monitoring, and agents work together to reduce toil, detect and troubleshoot pipeline and job issues, and give teams an auditable, AI‑ready foundation for production data products.

speakers.name: Saad Ansari
speakers.bio: Saad is a product manager at Databricks working on Data Orchestration (Workflows) and Developer tooling teams. Prior to this he was CTO and co-founder at Connecterra using Artificial Intelligence to improve sustainability, productivity and animal welfare in the Dairy Industry (think Fitbit for cows!). Saad has also worked as a PM and Developer at Microsoft. He holds a masters in computer science from Stanford.

speakers.name: Kristóf Molnár
speakers.bio: Kristof leads Databricks's Developer Ecosystem PM team, including Declarative Automation Bundles (DABs). An expert in AI-driven engineering productivity, he previously served as a Group PM at Google, where he spearheaded the AI transformation of internal developer tools, resulting in over 50% of new code being AI-written as of late 2025.

title: Build Better & Faster on Databricks with the AI Dev Kit
abstract: AI-assisted development tools have transformed how software is built, yet lack native integration with enterprise data platforms. The AI Dev Kit is an open-source framework enabling AI code-generation services—Claude, Cursor, Gemini, and others—to build and deploy production workloads directly on Databricks. It provides platform-aware tooling allowing AI assistants to generate Databricks-native code adhering to best practices across pipelines, jobs, and Unity Catalog. Critically, it enables non-technical analysts to build robust Databricks workflows through natural language, dramatically lowering the barrier to entry. Since launch in January 2025, the project surpassed 100 GitHub stars in one week with accelerating organic adoption. This session covers architecture, real-world use cases, and implications of AI-assisted development for the Databricks ecosystem.

title: Building “Vehicle 360” at Daimler Truck & Bus — How We Find Needles in a 30,000 Parameter Haystack
abstract: Daimler Truck & Bus built “Vehicle 360” to break down organizational silos on a global scale and create one trusted, up‑to‑date view of each vehicle. We combine configuration and production records, sales and aftersales processes, over‑the‑air updates and more than 300 GB of daily vehicle log files to maintain this view across more than 1,000,000 vehicles — each with 30,000+ parameters. On an open lakehouse architecture, Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines keep data fresh and organized, while Unity Catalog provides unified governance and easy sharing.It gives teams one place to see a vehicle’s current state, drill into recent parameter changes, find vehicles with specific characteristics, orchestrate dynamic over‑the‑air updates, and diagnose issues faster. Beyond day‑one wins, this foundation also enables proactive anomaly detection, AI‑driven support and our journey to a software‑defined vehicle.

Beyond day‑one wins, this foundation also enables proactive anomaly detection, AI‑driven support and our journey to a software‑defined vehicle.

title: Building a Data Telescope to Study the Vast Expanse of Social Media
abstract: Researchers studying extremism and misinformation need massive social media datasets but face infrastructure and budget constraints. Traditional solutions offer dashboard access to pre-aggregated data, limiting flexibility. Princeton’s Accelerator gives researchers direct Databricks workspace access with pre-built Typical Activity Datasets, enabling custom queries and analysis. We built a multi-tenant data and AI-powered platform serving R1 institutions.Our medallion architecture ingests data via Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines: Bronze captures raw data, Silver applies filtering/text enrichments, and researchers can query via semantic search powered by MLOps. Leveraging Databricks Asset Bundles, REST APIs, and Unity Catalog, we built solutions for cross-workspace monitoring. Users who spent months building scrapers start work in days, querying 100+ languages and discovering content patterns while learning valuable skills. This talk shares our architecture for democratizing data infrastructure with engineering rigor.

speakers.name: Kai Pak
speakers.bio: Kai Pak is Director of Engineering at the Accelerator, an international research consortium housed at Princeton University's School of Public and International Affairs. The Accelerator builds shared tools to improve the speed and quality of research on the information environment. Kai leads the platform's technical development, spanning data infrastructure, applied ML, and large-scale pipeline engineering. Prior to Princeton, he spent several years at NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory working on machine learning and remote sensing applications. He holds master's degrees in Applied Physics from Columbia University and Computer Science from UIUC.

speakers.name: Lilly Amirjavadi
speakers.bio: Data scientist with a background in computational social science and a focus on applied machine learning, scalable data pipelines, and actionable insights. I design and deploy end-to-end workflows that support everything from exploratory analysis to production-grade modeling, with experience across industries including research, media, and public sector data. At Princeton’s Research Accelerator, I develop systems that process and analyze large volumes of real-world data, supporting research into digital ecosystems and key performance indicators. My work emphasizes clarity, reproducibility, and measurable impact.

title: Building a Lakehouse Data Strategy for Healthcare
abstract: Michael Uohara—CDAO at HSS has gone all in with Databricks taking HSS's 30 data sources and ingesting them into Databricks. He and his team are under a 18-month transformation effort to modernize their data architecture, focusing on finance, clinical data, operations and HR while creating a foundation where HSS can start to build applications like patient scheduling, surgical parts inventory and MD360—all built on Databricks. Michael is a previous surgeon and now leading the data strategy for the number one orthopedic hospital in the world. He is a visionary in the space and an incredible leader. Besides being an incredible champion and massive Databricks advocate, he approaches his Databricks implementation with calmness, tactical outcomes and has built an incredible culture at HSS. He will discuss his vision to implement a lakehouse architecture at the Hospital for Special Surgery while building a team and culture that can thrive with Databricks' platform.

title: Building a Production: Scale Dimensional Data Mart With Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines and AUTO CDC
abstract: At 84.51˚, we replaced legacy ETL processing in our retail sales data mart with Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines to simplify development while improving reliability at scale. In this session, we’ll walk through how a low-code, declarative approach enables dimensional modeling for both slowly changing dimensions and high-volume fact data.You’ll see how to use AUTO CDC to handle data updates across batch and streaming pipelines, supporting continuously arriving sales transactions alongside rapidly changing product, store and customer attributes. Our ETL crunches approximately 8 million sales transactions and over 80 million purchased items per day in support of our 84.51˚ Stratum platform.Attendees will leave with practical guidance for designing and operating dimensional data marts using Spark Declarative Pipelines, including patterns for CDC, schema evolution, and hybrid batch/streaming workloads that can be applied immediately in production environments.
description_raw: At 84.51˚, we replaced legacy ETL processing in our retail sales data mart with Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines to simplify development while improving reliability at scale. In this session, we’ll walk through how a low-code, declarative approach enables dimensional modeling for both slowly changing dimensions and high-volume fact data.You’ll see how to use AUTO CDC to handle data updates across batch and streaming pipelines, supporting continuously arriving sales transactions alongside rapidly changing product, store and customer attributes. Our ETL crunches approximately 8 million sales transactions and over 80 million purchased items per day in support of our 84.51˚ Stratum platform.Attendees will leave with practical guidance for designing and operating dimensional data marts using Spark Declarative Pipelines, including patterns for CDC, schema evolution, and hybrid batch/streaming workloads that can be applied immediately in production environments.

speakers.name: Scott Gordon
speakers.bio: Scott is a lead data engineer at 84.51˚. He has worked in Data Warehousing and Business Intelligence for several decades. Scott has achieved Databricks Certified Data Engineer Professional as well as previous certifications from TDWI, Oracle, Microsoft and IBM.

speakers.name: Shu Li
speakers.bio: Senior Specialist Solutions Architect at Databricks

title: Building a Unified Data Platform: easyJet's Strategic Evolution With Databricks
abstract: Three years ago, easyJet's data estate was primarily focused on centralizing data from multiple operational systems, with most workloads running in batch and limited real-time capability.This session describes the strategic evolution of easyJet's Data Strategy by adopting Databricks as a single enterprise foundation for data and AI across the organization. It will cover the transition from basic data consolidation to supporting streaming and near-real-time processing, enabling teams to work from a shared, governed Enterprise data platform.The session will also explain how Lakebase was introduced to support low-latency access patterns alongside analytical workloads.Finally, the talk outlines how Databricks was integrated with surrounding cloud and enterprise tooling to form a reference architecture that supports scalability, governance and reuse across the organization, and how this has strengthened easyJet's data estate as a single strategic technology platform.

speakers.name: Shiv Nayak
speakers.bio: Shiv is a passionate Data & AI Architect operating at senior enterprise architecture level within the Data & Analytics domain. As Head of Data & AI Architecture at easyJet, he helps drive the company’s analytics, data science, and AI vision as part of the IT & Strategy leadership team. With over 20 years of international experience spanning travel, finance, telecom, retail, and insurance, Shiv has successfully led large-scale data, analytics, and AI transformation programs worth millions. Beginning his career in ETL and reporting, he has evolved into a strategic thinker, innovative solution provider, and inspiring mentor who fosters high-performing teams. Shiv is deeply committed to driving excellence in data-led digital transformation, promoting best practices and thought leadership, and advocating for diversity, innovation, and talent development. He also advises multiple startups, helping shape their data and AI strategies for growth and scale.

title: Building Agentic Applications on Databricks
abstract: This hands-on course introduces how to build single-agent, multi-agent, and multi-model systems on Agent Bricks, including key components, architectures, and use cases. You'll learn how to create agent tools using Unity Catalog functions to extend agent capabilities. You'll begin by using Genie to build agents that can interact with structured data, before extending these concepts to create a second agent designed for working with unstructured data and external resources. Finally, you'll explore how Agent Bricks provides a declarative approach to building multi-agent applications, offering a streamlined alternative to manual development. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, including workspaces and notebooks Intermediate Python and SQL proficiency Familiarity with Generative AI fundamentals, including LLMs and prompting strategies Familiarity with Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) architecture Familiarity with API usage and function calling for LLM interactions with external tools and services

title: Building Agentic Apps on the Databricks Platform: Architecture, Considerations, and Key Patterns
abstract: Agentic applications are fundamentally different from traditional software: they don't just retrieve data, they reason over it, take actions, and update state in response to the world around them. That makes the database layer a critical design decision, not an afterthought. When agents need to read fresh operational data, write back results, and coordinate across concurrent tasks, the infrastructure underneath determines what's actually possible. This session covers what makes agentic apps so powerful, what can go wrong without the right foundation, and how the Databricks Platform addresses the core requirements. You'll learn how Lakebase provides the low-latency, transactionally consistent operational store that agents need to act on live data, how Unity Catalog enforces governance across agent actions and the data they touch, and how Databricks Apps simplifies deployment and scaling. We'll also walk through a reference architecture that shows how these pieces fit together, from agent orchestration and tool use to state management, observability, and safe rollback using database branching.

speakers.name: Yassine Essawabi
speakers.bio: Yassine is a Specialist Solutions Architect at Databricks, where he helps customers to be successful in designing, building and deploying their Data and AI use cases in production at scale for on-premise and cloud deployments using different technologies. His engagements with customers covers various domains including Machine learning, Automation, Data engineering and Architecture review. He is a certified Solutions Architect on Azure and AWS.

title: Building Agentic Business Applications on Databricks with Lakebase, MCP and Marvis AI
abstract: Many organizations use Databricks for analytics and AI, but still build business applications on separate stacks, disconnected from the lakehouse. We share how we use Databricks as a full application platform, combining analytics, operational data and AI into production-grade systems. We will show how we built DNA Port and Marvis AI using Databricks Apps, Lakebase, Lakeflow Sync Tables and MLflow for governed model lifecycle management. We will also demonstrate how MCP securely exposes trusted lakehouse data to AI copilots. Through real-world demos, we show how user actions write to Lakebase, trigger AI workflows and surface insights in real time. Today, this platform supports 200+ vessels and 300+ on-shore employees, reducing manual reporting by 40% and improving application time to market by 50%. Attendees will learn practical architecture patterns, governance with Unity Catalog, and lessons learned in building scalable, AI-powered enterprise applications on Databricks.

speakers.name: Simon Fassot
speakers.bio: Leading the Software, Data Analytics and AI team at Hafnia

title: Building AI That Matters: Powering Global Healthcare at The Virtue Foundation
abstract: Ever wonder how The Virtue Foundation connects medical resources with underserved regions? As the first collaboration under Databricks for Good, the foundation partnered with us to build an AI-powered platform improving medical outcomes across 73 countries. This talk breaks down the production Lakehouse architecture behind the system that continuously curates healthcare data to match volunteers with communities in need.What we’ll cover:LLM extraction at scale: Processing 5M+ web pages with OpenAI models via bespoke multimodal pipelines running natively on Spark, reducing cost while improving precision.Data engineering on Spark: Running skewed, multi-terabyte workloads using Spark for parallelism, Photon for performance at scale, and production-grade orchestration.Making data trustworthy: Unifying millions of healthcare facilities through entity resolution.A candid, systems-level look at what it takes to move LLM-powered data extraction from proof of concept to global impact.

speakers.name: Nicolas Douard
speakers.bio: Nicolas Douard uses AI and geospatial data to solve real-world problems in global health. As Tech Lead at Virtue Foundation, he builds solutions to identify medical deserts and match underserved communities with healthcare resources. He is also a researcher affiliated with the University of Strasbourg, publishing on large-scale NLP and cross-disciplinary innovation linking engineering and biology.

title: Building an AI Database for Agentic GTM Operations
abstract: Rippling leverages Databricks to power agentic GTM operations across RevOps, PLG, marketing, sales and account management. Built on Apache Iceberg™-compatible tables and Lakeflow, we ingest and unify large-scale market intelligence data, perform ML-based entity resolution across heterogeneous sources, and apply LLM-driven summarization and classification to generate structured outputs.These enriched profiles power a 360° GTM portal that enables personalized outreach and engagement at scale. Using Databricks AI/BI, we conduct deep performance analysis across campaigns, segments, messaging, and personalization dimensions—closing the loop between intelligence generation and revenue outcomes.We'll dive deep into how Rippling combines scalable data pipelines, interoperable Iceberg storage, AI/BI analytics, evaluation frameworks and generative AI on Databricks to move from fragmented GTM workflows to measurable, agentic operations that drive revenue impact.

Using Databricks AI/BI, we conduct deep performance analysis across campaigns, segments, messaging, and personalization dimensions—closing the loop between intelligence generation and revenue outcomes.We'll dive deep into how Rippling combines scalable data pipelines, interoperable Iceberg storage, AI/BI analytics, evaluation frameworks and generative AI on Databricks to move from fragmented GTM workflows to measurable, agentic operations that drive revenue impact.

title: Building an Explainable AI Research Agent With Databricks Genie
abstract: Forecasting sales at more than 7,000 retail stores generates frequent inquiries into forecast drivers, and traditional methods like driver dashboards or SHAP fail to give field leaders clear narratives. Data scientists were spending days writing ad‑hoc SQL to answer basic “why” questions. In partnership with Databricks, we built an AI/BI Genie-powered Research Agent that delivers fast, plain‑language forecast explanations using governed data. The agent joins core datasets, computes variances and accuracy metrics, correlates key drivers and links every answer to underlying SQL, cutting analysis time from days to minutes and revealing model biases. This session covers how we used Unity Catalog as the semantic backbone and encoded business rules in the Genie Knowledge Store so the agent reasons like a retail analyst. We’ll demo the agent with Deep Research and share key lessons and a repeatable blueprint for operationalizing Genie.

speakers.name: Chad Novek
speakers.bio: Chad Novek is a Director of Data Science at CVS Health, leading labor forecasting and staffing analytics for retail and pharmacy operations. He oversees the development and monitoring of large‑scale demand models for store labor drivers and owns multiple decision support and optimization products, including a composite staffing risk metric and a requisition optimization engine for pharmacists and technicians. His teams focus on bringing forecasting, optimization, and applied ML into production to power workforce decisions.

speakers.name: Ruo-Ting Sun
speakers.bio: Ruo‑Ting (Adrienne) Sun, PhD is a Senior Data Scientist at CVS Health, where Adrienne builds large‑scale forecasting and decision systems on distributed data platforms and develops GenAI solutions, including Genie, to enable faster insights generation. Adrienne’s work has driven over $35M in cost savings through PySpark‑based automation of planning and scheduling. Previously, Adrienne was an Applied Machine Learning Scientist at Teladoc Health, developing ML‑driven risk stratification and intervention policies that improved diabetes outcomes. Adrienne holds a PhD from the University of Illinois at Urbana‑Champaign and is based in Chicago.

speakers.name: Rohan Parikh
speakers.bio: Rohan Parikh is a Solutions Architect at Databricks specializing in Data & AI for healthcare and life sciences (HLS). With 10+ years of experience, he helps HLS organizations harness data & AI to improve patient outcomes, drive interoperability, support value-based care, and more.

title: Building Better Apps: Architecture Best Practices and Common Patterns on Databricks Apps
abstract: Building a data-driven application is easy; building one that is secure, performant, and scalable is where most teams struggle. This session provides a definitive field guide to the architectural patterns used by the most successful builders on the Databricks App platform. We will break down the ""how-to"" for the most in-demand app categories: Internal Tools, AI Copilots, Agentic Workflows, and Customer-Facing Products. For each pattern, we will dive into the technical specifics that matter most: - Connectivity & State: Best practices for integrating with Databricks SQL and Lakebase.- Identity & Auth: Implementing On-Behalf-Of (OBO) tokens and service principal delegation to ensure data privacy.- Agentic Design: Orchestrating LLMs and tool-calling within a full-stack environment.- Performance: Strategies for caching and low-latency UI responses. Join us to learn how to avoid common pitfalls and leverage standardized blueprints to ship better apps.

Join us to learn how to avoid common pitfalls and leverage standardized blueprints to ship better apps.

title: Building Data Pipelines with Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines
abstract: This hands-on course introduces users to the essential concepts and skills needed to build data pipelines using Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines (SDP) in Databricks for incremental batch or streaming ingestion and processing through multiple streaming tables and materialized views. Designed for data engineers new to Spark Declarative Pipelines, the course provides a comprehensive overview of core components such as incremental data processing, streaming tables, materialized views, and temporary views, highlighting their specific purposes and differences. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, including workspaces, Apache Spark, Delta Lake, Medallion Architecture, Lakeflow Jobs, and Unity Catalog Experience ingesting raw data into Delta tables using the read_files SQL function (CSV, JSON, TXT, Parquet) Intermediate SQL proficiency, including intermediate-level queries and joins Understanding of ETL concepts and batch/streaming workflows
description_raw:
This hands-on course introduces users to the essential concepts and skills needed to build data pipelines using Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines (SDP) in Databricks for incremental batch or streaming ingestion and processing through multiple streaming tables and materialized views. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026.

  • Experience ingesting raw data into Delta tables using the read_files SQL function (CSV, JSON, TXT, Parquet)
  • Intermediate SQL proficiency, including intermediate-level queries and joins
  • Understanding of ETL concepts and batch/streaming workflows

title: Building Enterprise Applications with Databricks Apps
abstract: This hands-on course introduces Databricks Apps, a new feature that empowers developers to build and deploy secure data and AI applications directly on the Databricks platform. It is primarily designed for data scientists, data engineers, and developers who need to create custom applications that make data insights accessible to non-technical users within their organizations. The course begins with an overview of the main components of Databricks Apps, followed by the deployment of a simple app using available templates. Next, you will learn how to build and deploy a data-driven application that integrates with other platform features. The course then covers how to set up an IDE for Databricks Apps, and how to edit and deploy an app using an IDE. It concludes with how to implement security and best practices for an enterprise app. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Familiarity with core Databricks components, including workspaces, clusters, SQL warehouses, Unity Catalog, Jobs, and Model Serving

title: Building Enterprise-Scale Agentic Claims Automation and AI Observability With Databricks
abstract: As enterprises move from GenAI experimentation to production, the hardest problems are no longer models or agent frameworks—they are enterprise‑scale orchestration and observability.At Suncorp, we have deployed 16 production‑grade agentic AI solutions that are now converging into an enterprise claims automation capability. This session shares the reference architectures and engineering patterns behind this journey, focusing on two pillars: agentic orchestration, where Databricks supports low‑latency, governed workflows and shared agent memory using Delta Lake, Vector Search, Agent Bricks, and Lakebase, and AI observability at scale, where we use MLflow evaluation, OpenTelemetry, Databricks Apps, and AI/BI Dashboards to trace agent behaviour, decisions, quality and risk.Attendees will leave with practical patterns for building orchestrated, observable agentic AI in a regulated enterprise.

speakers.name: Shivam Panicker
speakers.bio: Shivam Panicker is a Solutions Architect at Databricks, specializing in designing and delivering data and AI-driven solutions for enterprise customers, particularly within the financial services sector. With deep expertise in modern data platforms, Shivam works closely with organizations to unlock value from data through advanced analytics, machine learning, and real-time decisioning systems. With a strong focus on emerging AI capabilities, including agent-based systems, Shivam is passionate about how enterprises can operationalise AI at scale to drive efficiency, customer experience, and innovation.

speakers.name: Kranthi Nekkalapu
speakers.bio: As an AI practice executive and GenAI subject matter expert, Kranthi brings deep experience in the global financial services and insurance sectors. His background includes leading Suncorp's ML Engineering and research unit within the COE, where he established a strong record of successfully deploying large-scale AI solutions into production. Kranthi is passionate about bridging the gap between innovative AI capabilities and tangible business outcomes, consistently driving data-driven strategies that deliver measurable results.

title: Building higher quality Genie Spaces: How to ensure accuracy and trust
abstract: High-quality Genie spaces are built on a foundation of clear metadata, curated semantics, and continuous iteration. New capabilities make it faster than ever to bootstrap and develop Genie spaces.In this session, we'll show how to design, refine, and scale Genie spaces that consistently deliver accurate, trusted results. Topics include:- Using Unity Catalog Business Semantics (Metric Views) improve accuracy and consistency from the start- Leveraging Genie Code and knowledge mining to automate space setup- How to define metadata and context in the Genie knowledge store- How to use evaluation and Benchmarks to measure accuracy and guide improvements- Best practices for monitoring and optimizing Genie spaces at scale

speakers.name: Shah Amini
speakers.bio: As an Engineering Leader at Databricks since 2021, Shah has driven innovation across some of the company’s most strategic product areas. Currently leading engineering for Genie Spaces, a flagship AI-powered product that enables users to "talk to their data," Shah is at the forefront of integrating LLMs into enterprise workflows to unlock intuitive and powerful analytics experiences. Before Databricks, Shah held impactful engineering roles at Meta and Google, contributing to massive-scale consumer products and platform security. His work has consistently blended user experience, system reliability, and strategic foresight to drive customer and business value.

title: Building Permission-Aware AI for Enterprise Content with LakeFlow Connect
abstract: In this session, we’ll explore how to build scalable, permission-aware AI applications by ingesting files and metadata from Google Drive and SharePoint into Databricks.We’ll examine how LakeFlow Connect ingests structured files (Excel, JSON), semi-structured data, and unstructured documents (PDF, DOCX, and more) from enterprise content systems, while capturing rich metadata and preserving file-level permissions for secure downstream use. You’ll learn how to process change feeds, handle deletes, manage schema evolution, and synchronize access controls, ensuring that AI systems respect enterprise security boundaries without sacrificing performance or scalability. We’ll then showcase how this ingestion foundation powers high-impact GenAI applications, including knowledge assistants and enterprise search with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), transforming distributed enterprise content into governed, AI-ready intelligence that delivers trusted answers and actionable insights.
description_raw:
In this session, we’ll explore how to build scalable, permission-aware AI applications by ingesting files and metadata from Google Drive and SharePoint into Databricks.We’ll examine how LakeFlow Connect ingests structured files (Excel, JSON), semi-structured data, and unstructured documents (PDF, DOCX, and more) from enterprise content systems, while capturing rich metadata and preserving file-level permissions for secure downstream use. You’ll learn how to process change feeds, handle deletes, manage schema evolution, and synchronize access controls, ensuring that AI systems respect enterprise security boundaries without sacrificing performance or scalability.

We’ll then showcase how this ingestion foundation powers high-impact GenAI applications, including knowledge assistants and enterprise search with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), transforming distributed enterprise content into governed, AI-ready intelligence that delivers trusted answers and actionable insights.

speakers.name: Sandip Agarwala
speakers.bio: Sandip is a Staff Software Engineer at Databricks, where he specializes in designing and building scalable, efficient data ingestion technologies. Prior to joining Databricks, he was the founding engineer and technical lead at Springpath—a scale-out storage startup acquired by Cisco—where he architected and developed a petabyte-scale distributed file system from the ground up for private cloud environments.

title: Building Production Data Products: HDC Journey from Asset Bundle Automation to Genie Orchestration
abstract: Building production data products demands operational excellence beyond architecture. This session reveals MiQ's Historical Data Cube (HDC) transformation: from Unity Catalog design managing 700T+ advertising signals to fully automated Databricks Asset Bundle deployments, powering AI agents and Genie analytics.We demonstrate the complete lifecycle: 1. Unity Catalogue schema supporting AI agent APIs 2. Genie conversational queries across 10-year trends 3. Data quality frameworks ensuring LLM agent accuracy, 4. Asset Bundles achieving infrastructure-as-code with multi-environment promotion— reducing releases from 3 weeks to 4 hours, 5. Genie optimization delivering sub-second performance on petabyte-scale aggregates. Technical deep-dives: medallion architecture in Unity Catalog, incremental processing patterns, DAB orchestration with Jenkins CI/CD, schema evolution maintaining compatibility across 50+ systems, and monitoring frameworks tracking freshness for agent reliability.

title: Building Query Expert MCP: Using RAG to Build an Analytics Agent That Goes Beyond Text-to-SQL
abstract: LLMs are fast and confident SQL writers that can unlock self-serve analytics across your business. But actionable insights require more than SQL syntax—they demand business context, domain expertise and nuances that live outside your schema docs.In this talk, we'll deep-dive into how Block built Query Expert MCP, a scalable AI agent that captures and distributes institutional data knowledge using Databricks. You'll learn how we: Automated documentation at scale—using query_ai sub-agents to generate standardized table documentation across 11,000+ tables Captured SQL nuances—Databricks sub-agents categorize and label domain-specific query patterns across 1M sample queries Human in the loop—Databricks Apps allow users to directly edit, remove or upload context to the agent’s memory Enabled intelligent retrieval—Databricks Vector Search indexes for RAG-powered query generation that scales to a 12,000 person org, while supporting domain-specific scoping for teams' use cases
description_raw:
LLMs are fast and confident SQL writers that can unlock self-serve analytics across your business. But actionable insights require more than SQL syntax—they demand business context, domain expertise and nuances that live outside your schema docs.In this talk, we'll deep-dive into how Block built Query Expert MCP, a scalable AI agent that captures and distributes institutional data knowledge using Databricks. You'll learn how we:

  • Automated documentation at scale—using query_ai sub-agents to generate standardized table documentation across 11,000+ tables
  • Captured SQL nuances—Databricks sub-agents categorize and label domain-specific query patterns across 1M sample queries
  • Human in the loop—Databricks Apps allow users to directly edit, remove or upload context to the agent’s memory
  • Enabled intelligent retrieval—Databricks Vector Search indexes for RAG-powered query generation that scales to a 12,000 person org, while supporting domain-specific scoping for teams' use cases

speakers.name: Philip Azar

title: Building RAG Agents with Agent Bricks
abstract: This hands-on course teaches you how to build retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) agents on Databricks using Agent Bricks’ tooling—including Vector Search, AI Playground, and the Foundation Model APIs. First, you'll begin with prompt engineering and the fundamentals of retrieval and augmented generation. Next, you'll learn how to ingest, process, and prepare documents for use in a retrieval pipeline. Then you'll explore embedding vectors, vector database design, and how to use Vector Search in a Databricks environment. Finally, you will build a retrieval agent end-to-end and trace its behavior using MLflow Tracing for observability and debugging. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, including Unity Catalog Intermediate Python programming experience Basic SQL proficiency for querying and creating functions Familiarity with Generative AI fundamentals, including large language models (LLMs) and prompt engineering Basic knowledge of MLflow

  • Familiarity with Generative AI fundamentals, including large language models (LLMs) and prompt engineering
  • Basic knowledge of MLflow

title: Building Secure AI-Powered Analytics Experiences Across Organizational Boundaries
abstract: Data sharing between organizations has come a long way — but the next wave is about much more than moving files or granting table access. It's about delivering rich, intelligent experiences to partners, customers, and collaborators while keeping sensitive information protected on both sides. In this talk, we explore how the collaboration landscape is evolving: smarter defaults, stronger privacy guarantees, and AI-powered capabilities that make shared data more useful without increasing risk. You'll hear about the engineering principles behind building privacy-safe collaboration features at scale — balancing openness with control, and convenience with governance. Whether you work with external partners, internal business units, or cross-industry data networks, this session offers a look at where modern data collaboration is headed. Plus, see an early look at what's coming in a live demonstration.

title: Building Secure Retail Data Sharing at Walmart With Databricks Delta Sharing
abstract: Secure retail data sharing is becoming a foundation for collaboration between retailers, brands, and partners. In this session we will present how Databricks Delta Sharing and clean rooms can be used to build governed, privacy-aware data-sharing solutions that unlock transaction, customer, inventory, and performance insights for external stakeholders. Attendees will learn practical implementation patterns, clean room design principles, governance best practices, and strategies for delivering secure data products that are accessible to technical and non-technical business users. We’ll also present a Walmart Scintilla Cloud Feeds use case built on Delta Sharing, demonstrating how Walmart built their direct cloud-to-cloud sharing can simplify partner access to first-party retail data, reduce operational complexity, and support faster access to insights.

title: Building the Commercial Lakehouse: AI-Driven, Open and Interoperable
abstract: AstraZeneca modernized its Commercial data estate, transitioning from legacy EMR to a high-performance global commercial lakehouse. At the core lies Unity Catalog (UC), established as the unified governance layer. We demonstrate how UC enables a true open architecture, orchestrating seamless federation for legacy Glue workloads and utilizing Iceberg REST integration to provide zero-copy access for iceberg clients e.g., Snowflake.The transformation was powered by a multi-agent AI migration system, utilizing LLMs and testing agents to automate the conversion of 250+ pipelines into a rigorous Medallion architecture. Key components include Databricks Asset Bundles for versioned CI/CD, Liquid Clustering for storage optimization.Processing complex external data sources (Claims, CRM, Digital), this platform delivered 50% cost savings. This session covers the multi-agent migration architecture, UC-centric interoperability design patterns and lessons from modernizing commercial data at scale.

speakers.name: Alessandro Moro
speakers.bio: Alessandro Moro is a Delivery Solutions Architect at Databricks with nearly a decade of experience in Data and AI across highly regulated industries, including Banking and Pharma. title: Building With Community Connectors: Custom Data Sources, Managed Reliability
abstract: Are you tired of building fragile, costly API scripts for niche data sources that lack enterprise-grade features like lineage, retries and unified governance?Community Connectors are built on the foundational power of Lakeflow Connect and bridge the gap between "DIY" and “fully managed." These new AI-powered, open-source templates help you build, manage and host your own ingestion paths while inheriting many of the robust, built-in features of the Databricks Platform.In this lightning talk, we’ll explore: Built-in tooling: Use out-of-the-box observability, SCD Type 2 handling, automatic retries and more Flexible customization: Use open-source templates to meet requirements that go beyond standard managed connectors Unified governance: Centrally manage credentials, pipelines, data and lineage via Unity Catalog Join us to get started and learn how they offer the flexibility of a DIY approach with the scale and reliability of a managed Databricks service.
description_raw:
Are you tired of building fragile, costly API scripts for niche data sources that lack enterprise-grade features like lineage, retries and unified governance?Community Connectors are built on the foundational power of Lakeflow Connect and bridge the gap between "DIY" and “fully managed." These new AI-powered, open-source templates help you build, manage and host your own ingestion paths while inheriting many of the robust, built-in features of the Databricks Platform.In this lightning talk, we’ll explore:

  • Built-in tooling: Use out-of-the-box observability, SCD Type 2 handling, automatic retries and more
  • Flexible customization: Use open-source templates to meet requirements that go beyond standard managed connectors
  • Unified governance: Centrally manage credentials, pipelines, data and lineage via Unity Catalog

Join us to get started and learn how they offer the flexibility of a DIY approach with the scale and reliability of a managed Databricks service.

speakers.name: Peter Pogorski
speakers.bio: Peter is a Senior Product Manager, focused on data ingestion.

title: Building Workforce Intelligence With Unity Catalog and Databricks Genie
abstract: Mars Inc., with more than 140,000 associates across its confectionery, pet care, and food businesses, set out to reimagine how workforce decisions are made. Leaders needed a single, trusted view of the associate journey to move beyond fragmented dashboards and enable faster and fairer decisions. Partnering with Tiger Analytics, Mars established Associate 360, a unified workforce intelligence platform. A common data model brings together hire-to-retire data across segments, standardizes definitions, and ensures trust through Unity Catalog. Natural language querying enabled through Databricks Genie lets business users get insights and visualizations instantly. Impact delivered: One connected view of the end-to-end associate lifecycle Consistent, trusted metrics across businesses Time-based insights for better workforce planning Faster onboarding of acquisitions without compromising data quality A shift from static reports to decision-ready workforce intelligence

title: Building Your Open Security Lakehouse: Ingest, Normalize, and Model Security Data at Scale
abstract: Many organizations are experimenting with “security data lakes” but struggle to move from DIY projects to an operational architecture the SOC can live in every day. This session walks through Lakewatch’s reference architecture for an open security lakehouse: how to onboard and unify diverse security, IT, and business telemetry on open storage, normalize it to OCSF, and organize it into bronze, silver, and gold tables optimized for SecOps. We’ll cover ingestion patterns for common data sources, Medallion modeling for security analytics, governance with Unity Catalog, index and search strategies, and how to safely bring in multimodal data like emails and documents. You’ll leave with an opinionated architecture to evaluate or design your own security lakehouse, plus guidance on when to augment versus replace existing SIEM tooling.

speakers.name: Kristin Dahl
speakers.bio: Kristin Dahl is a cybersecurity trailblazer with over 15 years of experience in security research and threat intelligence. As a Specialist Architect at Databricks, she empowers organizations to harness the full potential of data and ML in their cybersecurity efforts. Her mission: to revolutionize the field through data-driven insights and foster a safer digital world. With a background spanning air traffic systems, critical infrastructure, and threat analysis, Kristin brings a unique perspective to solving complex challenges. Her expertise in formal methods, threat modeling, and distributed systems allows her to build solutions that push the boundaries of what's possible. A passionate advocate for innovation, Kristin has contributed to cutting-edge research like DARPA's High Assurance Cyber Military Systems. She holds a patent in memory protection that was a recipient of the 2020 Stratus Award for Cloud Computing. She is dedicated to advancing the field through education and outreach.

title: Built on Databricks: How CMSPI Scaled Payments Routing and Optimization Using Lakebase and DBSQL
abstract: CMSPI built a Databricks-native analytics app that automates payments optimization at enterprise scale — replacing spreadsheets with a governed platform that processes billions of transactions and delivers measurable time and cost savings for global merchants. Lakebase powers real-time scenario configuration, while Delta tables and SQL warehouses enable interactive analytics. Databricks Workflows orchestrate a mathematical solver (PROM) that evaluates billions of routing combinations and returns results in 30–40 minutes — eliminating 30+ hours of manual work per initial analysis and uncovering ~10% incremental savings opportunities. This session shares CMSPI’s path to productization: access control with Entra ID groups, MLOps for approval-rate prediction models and external monetization via Delta Sharing to non-Databricks consumers. The architecture demonstrates the “built on Databricks” pattern for secure, governed apps — with plans to expand collaboration using Databricks Clean Rooms.
description_raw:
CMSPI built a Databricks-native analytics app that automates payments optimization at enterprise scale — replacing spreadsheets with a governed platform that processes billions of transactions and delivers measurable time and cost savings for global merchants.

Lakebase powers real-time scenario configuration, while Delta tables and SQL warehouses enable interactive analytics. Databricks Workflows orchestrate a mathematical solver (PROM) that evaluates billions of routing combinations and returns results in 30–40 minutes — eliminating 30+ hours of manual work per initial analysis and uncovering ~10% incremental savings opportunities.

This session shares CMSPI’s path to productization: access control with Entra ID groups, MLOps for approval-rate prediction models and external monetization via Delta Sharing to non-Databricks consumers. His work covers GenAI applications, ML pipelines, real-time analytics, and self-serve BI dashboards. He holds an MS from the University at Buffalo.

title: Bulk Metadata Generation To Bring Genie to Life
abstract: Genie benefits significantly from the presence of column metadata. In this talk we will describe the various components that feed into a high-quality Genie space and review some code you can use to populate the base Unity Catalog column metadata in bulk. The code makes use of customizable prompts and optional data sampling to produce high-quality metadata across entire schemas or even catalogs.

The code makes use of customizable prompts and optional data sampling to produce high-quality metadata across entire schemas or even catalogs.

title: Cascading Failures in Multi-Agent Systems
abstract: Evaluating multi-agent systems requires a fundamental shift in perspective: we are no longer just testing model outputs, but the integrity of the coordination layer. Instead of just getting one bad response, we get a complex palette of hidden—and not-so-hidden—issues like poisoned shared memory, sub-optimal decision patterns, and distributed hallucinations.In this session, we’ll cover how to identify these glitches with MLflow observability, how to move from passive monitoring to active intervention, and which Databricks components can actually prevent the chaos from happening in the first place. The goal of this talk isn't to give you a recipe for every possible disaster, but to broaden your thinking in a world where agents are becoming more autonomous—and significantly more creative with their mistakes.

Throughout her career as a Data Engineer, AI Engineer, and Databricks Solutions Architect, she’s experienced firsthand how challenging it can be to embark on a Data and AI journey. Based on her extensive experience with building real-world AI solutions, architecting and implementing data platforms, and running large-scale performance optimization projects with commercial and open source technologies, she is passionate about breaking complex topics and helping the community democratize data and AI for their projects.

title: Česká Spořitelna: Unifying Oracle, Cloudera and Snowflake Workloads to a Single Platform
abstract: Česká Spořitelna recently selected Databricks as their unified cloud data platform. After long-running PoCs and the evaluation of some of Databricks latest features—SQL functions, multi-statement transactions, exclusive groups, and others—Česká decided to pivot from Snowflake to Databricks, unifying their existing platforms and aligning with the strategy of the Erste Group. This includes the migration of an Oracle DWH, Cloudera and existing Snowflake environments to a unified data platform for all their AI, ML & data science, DWH, and data engineering use cases, cutting massive integration costs necessary to integrate all the legacy platforms.The Ceska’s Head of Data Platforms Engineering, together with the Databricks Lead Specialist Solutions Architect who supported the evaluation, will present the customer journey and why this decision is strategic and aligned with their broader enterprise data strategy.

Model training & MLOPS, Warehousing/Reporting, building AI systems and so on. Now he uses this experience to realize Česká spořitelna‘s and by extension, Erste‘s Group vision, of a unified Databricks platform.

title: Cielo modernized its data platform and migrated 6,000+ pipelines from EMR to Databricks.
abstract: Cielo, a leading LATAM payment processor, operated a fragmented analytics landscape across AWS EMR, Oracle Exadata/DW, and decentralized governance. This architecture increased operational overhead, limited lineage and observability, and constrained AI adoption.In this session, we present how Cielo migrated multiple enterprise-grade platforms to a unified Databricks Lakehouse. Over 6,500 pipelines were assessed and over 3,000 legacy jobs eliminated. The migration leveraged Delta Lake, serverless compute, standardized engineering patterns, and Unity Catalog–ready governance.Key takeaways include a ~40% cost reduction in ingestion pipelines, improved reliability and reprocessing, and more than 1,000 users enabled through self-service analytics. We also share practical migration patterns from AWS EMR and Oracle Exadata/DW to Databricks, governance-by-design lessons, and how AI-assisted refactoring accelerated modernization while improving developer productivity.

title: Clean, Correct Change Data Capture with Spark Declarative Pipelines
abstract: Learn how to construct clean, correct, cost-conscious CDC pipelines that conquer chaotic change, column creep, and complicated SCD calculations. Today, implementing CDC typically means writing complex, expensive merge logic. Handling straightforward cases is manageable—but complexity escalates quickly when you introduce out-of-order data, duplicates, schema evolution, and slowly changing dimensions. What works in development often breaks under real production conditions.Spark Declarative Pipelines (SDP) addresses this with Auto CDC, a declarative API that abstracts away merge orchestration and correctness guarantees. Instead of implementing imperative patterns, you declare intent—such as applying SCD Type 2—and SDP executes it with built-in consistency, incremental processing, and resilience.In this session, we’ll walk through the Auto CDC API, examine how it automates complex merge operations, and show how declarative CDC improves reliability across batch and streaming workloads.

speakers.name: Sandy Ryza
speakers.bio: Sandy is a software engineer at Databricks, working on declarative pipelines. Prior, he was the lead engineer on the Dagster project, and led ML teams at KeepTruckin and Clover Health. He's a committer on Spark and Hadoop, and co-authored O'Reilly Advanced Analytics with Spark.

title: Cleared for Takeoff: Modern Air Operations Intelligence on Databricks
abstract: Amadeus Airline & Airport Operations (AOP) is accelerating its industry-leading platform by migrating from Synapse to Databricks to unlock advanced data, AI and automation capabilities. Serving 135+ airlines and 200+ airports, AOP now gains a unified Delta Lake foundation, shared metadata catalog, secure data sharing and automated workflows. This migration enables faster engineering, real-time analytics, ML-driven disruption management and emerging agentic and MCP-enabled workflows forming the AI backbone for seamless passenger experiences, optimized airport operations and automated decisioning across the global AOP ecosystem.You’ll learn how Amadeus’ aviation expertise and Databricks technology together built the backbone for predictive, real-time aviation operations.

speakers.job_title: Head of Data, AI & Platform, Product

With over a decade in the industry and hands-on experience across Fortune 100 travel organizations, he has been part of teams delivering over a billion dollars business impact through modern data platforms, ML ops & agentic AI systems. A frequent thought leader on AI adoption in travel, Gurpreet brings a practitioner's perspective on what works, what doesn't, and what's worth betting on next

title: Closing the Insight-To-Action Gap With Databricks AI/BI Genie, Apps and Databricks One
abstract: Finance teams often identify insights but lack streamlined ways to operationalize them. In this demo-driven session, KPMG demonstrates how Engagement Management Coordinators (EMCs) within the Finance organization close the insight-to-action gap using Databricks AI/BI Genie, Databricks Apps and Databricks One. EMCs use conversational analytics to evaluate engagement performance and financial risk across scenarios, while Databricks Apps embed those insights directly into workflows such as forecasting revisions and exception handling. Databricks One provides a consistent business-facing entry point supported by Unity Catalog governance. The session shares adoption strategies, governance considerations, and lessons learned from enabling finance teams to move from analysis to action through guided demonstrations.

title: Comprehensive Data Warehouse Migrations With Lakebridge
abstract: Databricks Lakebridge is a free, comprehensive solution for migrating legacy data warehouses from a wide range of source systems. See how we accelerate migrations from legacy data warehouses to Databricks SQL, achieving 50% faster migration than traditional methods. We'll cover the tool’s automated migration process: Discovery: Source system profiling Assessment: Legacy code analysis Conversion: Advanced code transpilation Reconciliation: Data validation This comprehensive approach increases the predictability of migration projects, allowing businesses to plan and execute migrations with greater confidence.
description_raw:
Databricks Lakebridge is a free, comprehensive solution for migrating legacy data warehouses from a wide range of source systems. See how we accelerate migrations from legacy data warehouses to Databricks SQL, achieving 50% faster migration than traditional methods.

We'll cover the tool’s automated migration process:

  • Discovery: Source system profiling
  • Assessment: Legacy code analysis
  • Conversion: Advanced code transpilation
  • Reconciliation: Data validation

This comprehensive approach increases the predictability of migration projects, allowing businesses to plan and execute migrations with greater confidence.

speakers.name: Jonathan Brito
speakers.bio: Jonathan Brito is a Staff Product Manager at Databricks, where he currently works on Databricks SQL and Lakebridge. Prior to his current role, he launched Databricks' suite of Apache Iceberg products as part of the Lakehouse Storage team.

title: Consumer Industries Forum | Sponsored by: Deloitte
abstract: Join retail, consumer goods, and travel & hospitality leaders for a deep dive into how Databricks’ open, agnostic platform is powering the next era of intelligent commerce single foundation built on a single intelligent platform with elusive enterprise knowledge. From real-time personalization to collaborative supply chains connecting retailers and suppliers, discover how the industry’s most ambitious companies are bringing together structured and unstructured governed data to deploy AI that actually understands their business. This forum explores the imperatives reshaping the industry: demand intelligence, dynamic pricing, retail media monetization and AI-powered employee productivity. Through candid conversations and real-world results, hear directly from leaders already delivering measurable outcomes and leave with a practical playbook for scaling AI across the organization.

title: Consumer Industries: The Last Mile of Intelligence— Building Apps That Actually Reach the Frontline
abstract: Building an app has never been easier. Deploying an enterprise app— governed, scalable, connected to real-time data— has never been harder. Store associates, restaurant managers, and operations teams make thousands of decisions a day— but the apps they rely on are slow to build, disconnected from analytics, and starved of real-time context. The reason? A wall between analytical and operational systems that forces teams to maintain fragile ETL pipelines, over-provision databases, and wait months for production-ready tools.Lakebase tears that wall down. Databricks' serverless Postgres eliminates the infrastructure overhead, the ETL pipelines, and the DBA bottleneck that have kept intelligent apps out of frontline hands. In this session, we'll show what it means to build AI-first applications for consumer industries, walking through the end-to-end process: from use case design to live deployment, including a live app build on stage. Hear from brands already shipping Lakebase-powered apps, and leave with a repeatable framework for turning lakehouse intelligence into the tools your frontline teams actually use.

speakers.name: Sathish Gangichetty
speakers.bio: Sathish Gangichetty, a Lead Solutions Architect at Databricks, is a distinguished expert in artificial intelligence and machine learning with over 15 years of industry experience. Prior to his time at Databricks, he worked in the AI/ML practice at various companies like Snowflake, SAS, Activision and Zappos where he honed his skills in leveraging cutting-edge data platforms building and architecting for AI and ML applications. Sathish's expertise is backed by numerous certifications from industry leaders such as Microsoft, Google, AWS, and Databricks. As a thought leader in the field, he regularly shares his knowledge through his blog on Medium, where he explores advanced topics in ML and AI.

title: Control and governing your coding agents with LLMOps
abstract: Coding agents like Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI are transforming how software gets built, but without observability they remain a black box. This talk shows how to take control of your coding agents using MLflow. We cover tracing agent sessions to understand token usage and tool calls, evaluating output quality with LLM judges and custom scorers, routing LLM traffic through AI Gateway for cost control, and automating workflows with MCP and skills. Walk away with practical techniques to monitor, measure, and maximize your coding agent investment.

speakers.name: Yuki Watanabe
speakers.bio: A software engineer at Databricks and a full-time core maintainer of MLflow based in Japan. Leveraging applied machine learning background in several domains to shape tools that makes ML development flow more efficient and reliable.

title: Creating Better Travel Experiences for All — Confronting Airline Pricing Pressures From Legacy Tools
abstract: easyJet carried nearly 90 million passengers last year, flying more than 1,200 routes, with a fleet of 300+ planes. Even as one of Europe’s leading low-cost airlines, the company faced mounting pressure to modernize, as traveler expectations necessitate seamless booking, flexible changes and personalized offers. Across Europe, high costs, thin margins and digital-first competitors only raised the stakes for all airlines. With core systems still tied to a decade-old desktop app and one of the region’s largest SQL Server databases, easyJet struggled with frequent engineering bottlenecks, fragmented workflows and more. Moving to the Databricks Platform helped the airline consolidate their commercial tools into a single hub and cut deployment timelines from up to nine months to three — a pace shift that put innovation back on track for easyJet.

title: Cross-Cloud Delta Sharing: Eliminate Egress cost with Delta Sharing Global Distribution
abstract: Cross-cloud and cross-region data sharing introduces egress costs that grow with recipient query volume, creating a scaling challenge for large-scale data providers. This session covers best practices for architecting cost-efficient Delta Sharing deployments across clouds and regions, and introduces a new Databricks capability designed to change how providers manage cross-cloud/region delivery at scale.
description_raw: Cross-cloud and cross-region data sharing introduces egress costs that grow with recipient query volume, creating a scaling challenge for large-scale data providers. This session covers best practices for architecting cost-efficient Delta Sharing deployments across clouds and regions, and introduces a new Databricks capability designed to change how providers manage cross-cloud/region delivery at scale.

title: Customer Zero: How Retool Scaled 1 Million Databases on Databricks Lakebase
abstract: Learn how Retool, an enterprise AppGen platform for building intelligent applications and agents, became an early adopter of Databricks Lakebase, creating over 1 million serverless Postgres databases to power its operational foundation. We’ll share the architecture, governance, and lessons learned as Retool scaled its own platform to support production-ready apps and agents that turn governed business data into action. You’ll see how Retool’s Lakebase-powered operational layer now gives Databricks customers a proven path to run more workloads and remove data silos, whether they use Retool’s layer or connect directly to their own databases. The session includes a live demo to help you move from prototype to production with unified governance, automation, and lower operating costs through autoscaling.
description_raw:

We’ll share the architecture, governance, and lessons learned as Retool scaled its own platform to support production-ready apps and agents that turn governed business data into action.

You’ll see how Retool’s Lakebase-powered operational layer now gives Databricks customers a proven path to run more workloads and remove data silos, whether they use Retool’s layer or connect directly to their own databases. The session includes a live demo to help you move from prototype to production with unified governance, automation, and lower operating costs through autoscaling.

speakers.name: Abhishek Gupta
speakers.bio: Abhishek Gupta leads Product at Retool, the leading enterprise AppGen platform trusted by over 10,000 companies including Amazon, LVMH, Colgate-Palmolive, and Stripe. He recently spearheaded the launch of the world's first Enterprise AI AppGen platform, enabling anyone to build production-ready internal software using natural language. Prior to Retool, Abhishek served as SVP of Product at Motive, where he led a 70+ person team automating the physical economy for 120,000+ customers, and held product roles at Uber and Box. Abhishek holds a B.A. in Computer Science from the University of California, Berkeley and resides in San Francisco.

title: DABS: do like a pro: all the best tips & tricks
abstract: This session is a live, practical deep dive into Databricks Asset Bundles (DABS) — focused on how to use them like a pro in real projects, not just in docs.Instead of theory, I’ll run a live demo showing the most valuable patterns, shortcuts, and real-world tricks that dramatically improve how you build, deploy, and manage Databricks workflows.The session combines: proven techniques from my articles on Databrickster (Medium) battle-tested practices from real projects hidden features and non-obvious behaviors you won’t find in basic tutorials

speakers.name: Hubert Dudek
speakers.bio: Hubert Dudek is a Databricks MVP and data engineer who enjoys solving messy data problems and making things actually work in production. He builds scalable data platforms using tools like Unity Catalog and Delta Lake. His biggest passions are FinOps—finding smart ways to save money—and CI/CD, making sure data solutions get to production smoothly and reliably. He’s also active in the community as a blogger, YouTuber, and frequent social media contributor, sharing practical tips and real-world insights.

title: Data Analytics with Multi-Modal Data
abstract: AI is becoming a very powerful commodity tool to turn unstructured data into structured data. This has greatly improved the importance of unstructured data and is posing new challenges in the world of data analytics: how to govern the data, how to store the data, how to access the data efficiently, and how to make the data available to ML models. In this talk, we will discuss how we're approaching these problems given the state of the world with open table formats such as Delta Lake and Apache Iceberg, as well as new file formats such as LanceDB.

speakers.name: Dejan Krakovic
speakers.bio: Software Engineer with 16 years of experience of building SQL features in cloud at scale, in Databricks and Microsoft. Recently shipped SQL Stored Procedures, SQL Scripting and Collations support. Currently working on providing the best possible engine for unstructured data for AI.

speakers.name: John Spencer
speakers.bio: John is a Product Manager at Databricks, dedicated to making Unity Catalog work seamlessly with customers’ ecosystems of tools and platforms so they can easily access, govern, and use their data. Previously, he worked at Microsoft and graduated from Stanford with a BS in Computer Science.

title: Data Governance at Scale
abstract: This hands-on course equips learners with hands-on expertise to implement robust, enterprise-scale data governance in Databricks using Unity Catalog. Implement access control at scale with ABAC, enable comprehensive observability and auditing, monitor and assure data quality, visualize and manage complete data and model lineage, and effectively track usage, costs, and data quality across your platform. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, including workspaces, notebooks, and basic cloud concepts Intermediate SQL experience, including creating and managing tables, views, and functions, and granting/revoking privileges Working knowledge of data governance concepts, including access control, security policies, and fine-grained techniques such as row-level filters and column masking Understanding of Unity Catalog's object model (metastore, catalogs, schemas, tables, volumes, functions, models) and basic data lineage principles Basic awareness of metadata management and data discovery practices
description_raw:
This hands-on course equips learners with hands-on expertise to implement robust, enterprise-scale data governance in Databricks using Unity Catalog. Implement access control at scale with ABAC, enable comprehensive observability and auditing, monitor and assure data quality, visualize and manage complete data and model lineage, and effectively track usage, costs, and data quality across your platform.

Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. 

Prerequisites

  • Familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, including workspaces, notebooks, and basic cloud concepts
  • Intermediate SQL experience, including creating and managing tables, views, and functions, and granting/revoking privileges
  • Working knowledge of data governance concepts, including access control, security policies, and fine-grained techniques such as row-level filters and column masking
  • Understanding of Unity Catalog's object model (metastore, catalogs, schemas, tables, volumes, functions, models) and basic data lineage principles
  • Basic awareness of metadata management and data discovery practices

title: Data Governance at Scale with ABAC at Atlassian
abstract: Atlassian’s internal data platform is the backbone for analytics, product insights and business decisions across the company. Managing data access policies at this scale is complex. In this talk, we’ll share how Atlassian embeds robust data governance policies, balancing agility with compliance, and innovation with security. Learn how we handle access management, data classification, and policy enforcement across diverse teams and tools. A key part of our approach is leveraging Databricks Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) to manage and abstract complex business policies. ABAC enables us to flexibly enforce fine-grained access controls (FGAC) using data tagging along with tag-driven policies, adapting quickly to changing requirements while keeping implementation logic abstracted from users. Join us for insights into the challenges, lessons learned, and solutions that keep our data platform secure and well-governed.

speakers.name: Gerald Nakhle
speakers.bio: Gerald is a Senior Software Engineer at Atlassian where he leads the data governance & access controls stream on Atlassian's internal data lake.

speakers.name: Erik IJzermans
speakers.bio: Erik is a product manager on the Unity Catalog team at Databricks, focused on fine-grained access controls for tables and volumes. He helps customers scale their data platforms while staying firmly in control of access and governance.

title: Data Intelligence for SaaS Threat Hunting: How Obsidian uses Databricks at scale
abstract: Security teams are overwhelmed by fragmented SaaS logs, identity signals, and investigation workflows spread across tools. Obsidian Security unifies SaaS security telemetry across cloud apps and identity providers to detect real-world threats like account takeover, suspicious OAuth activity, and insider risk.In this session, Damien Miller-McAndrews (Threat Researcher, Obsidian Security) shares how Obsidian leverages the Databricks Lakehouse to accelerate threat investigations using anonymized, high-volume security telemetry stored in a unified Databricks data lake. See how Databricks enables fast, repeatable investigations by combining scalable query performance, structured and semi-structured data processing, and governance-friendly workflows.Attendees will learn practical patterns for building modern cybersecurity investigation pipelines on Databricks—turning security data into 'investigation ready' intelligence and enabling faster detection, triage, and response.
