speakers.name: Damien Miller-McAndrews
speakers.bio: Damien Miller-McAndrews is a Threat Researcher at Obsidian Security, focused on real-world attacks against SaaS applications and cloud identity systems. He investigates how attackers combine SaaS abuse, identity compromise, and social engineering into fast-moving breaches, and develops detection strategies to help teams respond faster to account takeovers, OAuth abuse, and anomalous access across enterprise SaaS environments. At Obsidian, Damien uses the Databricks Lakehouse to run high-scale investigations across anonymized security telemetry—accelerating triage, enriching threat hypotheses, and turning raw activity signals into investigation-ready intelligence. He brings a practitioner’s perspective to threat hunting and regularly shares research and practical insights to help security and IT teams better protect their organizations.

title: Data Interoperability with Unity Catalog
abstract: This hands-on course explores how to achieve seamless data interoperability between systems using Databricks Unity Catalog as a unified governance layer, using both Delta and Iceberg open table formats. Participants will learn to implement, manage and optimize a data environment where tables can be accessed by both Delta and Iceberg clients, enabling cross platform analytics and eliminating data silos. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Basic understanding of Lakehouse and Medallion architecture Familiarity with Unity Catalog fundamentals for governance Familiarity with designing or developing ETL pipelines using Databricks Basic SQL proficiency for querying and transforming data Basic understanding of Delta and open table format concepts
description_raw:
This hands-on course explores how to achieve seamless data interoperability between systems using Databricks Unity Catalog as a unified governance layer, using both Delta and Iceberg open table formats.  Participants will learn to implement, manage and optimize a data environment where tables can be accessed by both Delta and Iceberg clients, enabling cross platform analytics and eliminating data silos.

Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. 

Prerequisites

  • Basic understanding of Lakehouse and Medallion architecture
  • Familiarity with Unity Catalog fundamentals for governance
  • Familiarity with designing or developing ETL pipelines using Databricks
  • Basic SQL proficiency for querying and transforming data
  • Basic understanding of Delta and open table format concepts

title: Databricks Apps for Everyone: Build Custom Apps Without Writing a Line of Code
abstract: You shouldn't need to be a software engineer or experienced data practitioner to turn your data and AI into something your team can actually use. This session previews a new native AI-powered development experience on Databricks - one where anyone can go from idea to working application through a simple, conversational workflow. We'll show how a purpose-built agent, deeply integrated with the Databricks platform, makes building apps faster and more accessible than anything a general-purpose tool can offer. Whether you're a data analyst, a business user, or a developer who just wants to move faster — the future of app building on the Lakehouse is for you.

speakers.name: Fabian Jakobs
speakers.bio: Fabian is a Senior Staff Engineer at Databricks and the tech lead of the Databricks extension for VS Code. He has been working in the developer tooling space for 10+ years. As a co-founder of Cloud9, which later got acquired by AWS, he built one of the first web-based IDEs. Before joining Databricks, Fabian was a Principal Engineer at AWS responsible for AWS Cloud9 and AWS CloudShell.

title: Databricks Apps: A Magic Wand for Driving Data Quality Adoption and Observability
abstract: Most data quality tools don’t fail because the checks are wrong. They fail because users don’t understand how to use them.In this talk, we’ll share how we rebuilt our internal data quality platform using Databricks Apps and Databricks Workflows table triggers to prioritize usability and holistic visibility. We moved away from a config-file-driven model owned by a small group of platform engineers toward a user-centric approach that empowers teams to create, test and manage their own data quality rules. With a Databricks App as the central interface, users can define and test rules, inspect production run history, and track data quality trends across the tables and teams they own. Combined with table-triggered workflows, checks run automatically as data changes without added operational complexity.This session covers our architecture, key design decisions, and lessons learned, and shows how Databricks Apps can turn data infrastructure into a user-friendly platform.
description_raw:
Most data quality tools don’t fail because the checks are wrong. They fail because users don’t understand how to use them.In this talk, we’ll share how we rebuilt our internal data quality platform using Databricks Apps and Databricks Workflows table triggers to prioritize usability and holistic visibility. We moved away from a config-file-driven model owned by a small group of platform engineers toward a user-centric approach that empowers teams to create, test and manage their own data quality rules.

With a Databricks App as the central interface, users can define and test rules, inspect production run history, and track data quality trends across the tables and teams they own. Combined with table-triggered workflows, checks run automatically as data changes without added operational complexity.This session covers our architecture, key design decisions, and lessons learned, and shows how Databricks Apps can turn data infrastructure into a user-friendly platform.

speakers.name: Trinity Xia
speakers.bio: Trinity Xia is a Senior Software Engineer at Scribd, Inc., with 8 years of experience building robust data platforms and infrastructure. She specializes in data engineering and data infrastructure, and is passionate about making data quality not just reliable but actually enjoyable to work with. In her free time, she has been enjoying video games, pizza making, tea brewing, and truffle hunting.

title: Databricks FinOps Genie — Cost Observability Meets Optimization Insights
abstract: We will showcase Databricks FinOps Genie, a cost observability and optimization capability that combines a Databricks‑native FinOps dashboard, Genie space and FinOps agent, proven at scale across 1,000+ workspaces in a large bank. Learn how to turn Databricks usage and billing data into a lightweight, reusable cost intelligence layer that surfaces key drivers of spend, anomalies and optimization opportunities through a single dashboard. See how a Genie space lets users “talk” to their FinOps data in natural language and receive detailed explanations and recommendations. Take home patterns for using a FinOps agent and recommendation model to right‑size clusters, address anomalous workloads and enable teams on Databricks FinOps best practices, so you can apply the same approach in your own estate.
description_raw: We will showcase Databricks FinOps Genie, a cost observability and optimization capability that combines a Databricks‑native FinOps dashboard, Genie space and FinOps agent, proven at scale across 1,000+ workspaces in a large bank. Learn how to turn Databricks usage and billing data into a lightweight, reusable cost intelligence layer that surfaces key drivers of spend, anomalies and optimization opportunities through a single dashboard. See how a Genie space lets users “talk” to their FinOps data in natural language and receive detailed explanations and recommendations. Take home patterns for using a FinOps agent and recommendation model to right‑size clusters, address anomalous workloads and enable teams on Databricks FinOps best practices, so you can apply the same approach in your own estate.

title: Databricks on Databricks: Automating Service Reliability at Scale
abstract: Rapid product delivery makes service reliability a core engineering challenge. This talk shows how we use Databricks on Databricks to build production systems that automate reliability detection and investigation for user-facing services.I’ll cover 2 systems. KPI Miss Auto-Investigator automates KPI regression analysis by detecting dips, ranking error contributors, computing customer blast radius, and syncing annotations across team and global dashboards. Built with Databricks Jobs and ETL pipelines with inter-job dependencies, it cuts on-call investigation time by 67%.User Error Anomaly Detection is a Databricks-based system for logging, detection, and monitoring. User error logs are ingested via Zerobus into Delta tables, analyzed by Jobs using Asset Bundles, and monitored through SQL Alerts and AI/BI dashboards. The system detects incidents up to 3 hours before customer reports, reducing discovery time by 95% and customer impact by 90%, with 95% recall and 98% precision.

title: Databricks on Databricks: Build Your Own Experimentation Platform
abstract: Commercial experimentation platforms bundle three things: feature enablement, metrics, and analysis. The lakehouse gives you all the pieces to easily build experimentation for yourself.At Databricks, we run A/B testing and sequential analysis for release automation, supporting hundreds of feature releases per month. We have also scaled innovations from our in-house data scientists—methodologies and workflows not available in commercial offerings.We use Unity Catalog to centralize & govern our feature enablement data, Metric Views to define and share metric definitions, and Lakeflow to trigger analyses.We’ll share how we built it on the lakehouse, lessons learned, and how you can use Databricks to build a reliable and scalable experimentation platform.

title: Databricks Performance Optimization
abstract: In this hands-on course, you’ll learn how to optimize workloads and physical layout with Spark and Delta Lake and and analyze the Spark UI to assess performance and debug applications. We’ll cover topics like streaming, liquid clustering, data skipping, caching, Photon, and more. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Basic Databricks development skills, including creating clusters, running notebooks, and importing repos from Git Intermediate PySpark experience, including data extraction, transformations, and use of advanced built-in functions Intermediate Delta Lake experience, including creating tables, incremental updates, file compaction, and version restoration
description_raw:
In this hands-on course, you’ll learn how to optimize workloads and physical layout with Spark and Delta Lake and and analyze the Spark UI to assess performance and debug applications. We’ll cover topics like streaming, liquid clustering, data skipping, caching, Photon, and more. 

Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. 

Prerequisites

  • Basic Databricks development skills, including creating clusters, running notebooks, and importing repos from Git
  • Intermediate PySpark experience, including data extraction, transformations, and use of advanced built-in functions
  • Intermediate Delta Lake experience, including creating tables, incremental updates, file compaction, and version restoration

title: Decathlon’s Race to Unified Governance: Securing Interoperability at Scale
abstract: Decathlon, operating in 70+ countries, aims to make wellbeing accessible through sport. Scaling to thousands of tables strained our legacy metadata management (AWS Glue). Moving to Unity Catalog (UC) unified governance for 2,000+ users but created a key challenge: How to centralize governance within the Databricks Lakehouse while maintaining interoperability with external engines?This session details Decathlon’s migration from AWS Glue to Unity Catalog, focusing on how we ensured "openness" so that external engines—EMR, Athena, Polars, DuckDB—remain compatible and performant under UC. We will share the framework used to automate the transition of thousands of tables and present metrics on improved metadata rendering speeds and streamlined platform administration.
description_raw: Decathlon, operating in 70+ countries, aims to make wellbeing accessible through sport. Scaling to thousands of tables strained our legacy metadata management (AWS Glue). Moving to Unity Catalog (UC) unified governance for 2,000+ users but created a key challenge: How to centralize governance within the Databricks Lakehouse while maintaining interoperability with external engines?This session details Decathlon’s migration from AWS Glue to Unity Catalog, focusing on how we ensured "openness" so that external engines—EMR, Athena, Polars, DuckDB—remain compatible and performant under UC. We will share the framework used to automate the transition of thousands of tables and present metrics on improved metadata rendering speeds and streamlined platform administration.

speakers.name: Benjamin Laot
speakers.bio: Benjamin is Senior Data Engineer at Decathlon Technology. He is experienced in developing and deploying Data Processing pipelines using AWS, Python, Spark, Airflow and Databricks. He is responsible for the Databricks solution at Decathlon Technology He and his team are in charge of writing best practices for processing data on the new data stack and training hundreds of colleagues. Beyond data, Benjamin is passionate about cycling.

title: Deep Dive Into Streaming and Batch ETLs With Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines
abstract: Let's deep dive into the new declarative ETL pipeline framework in Apache Spark™, Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines (SDP). Let's peel it off to learn how SDP's high-level Python and SQL abstractions translate into lower-level Spark SQL and Spark Structured Streaming queries. During the talk, you will learn how SDP automatically resolves complex dependencies and builds optimized Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs) for both batch and streaming workloads. We will walk through the internal state management and orchestration logic that allows SDP to handle retries and incremental processing out of the box, replacing thousands of lines of imperative "glue code." You will leave with a clear mental model of the engine's architecture, the pros and cons of SDP, and be ready to debug and optimize your pipelines. You'll understand where and how to use it (alongside your existing ETL pipelines).

title: Delta Kernel <> Catalog-Managed Tables
abstract: With the introduction of Catalog-Managed Tables, Delta Lake has unlocked unified governance, enforceable constraints, and a path towards multi-table updates.As a result, Delta connectors must adapt to properly load, read, and write Delta Tables, as the Catalog is now the authoritative broker of commits, not the filesystem.In this talk, learn how we designed Catalog Managed Tables support in Delta Kernel—the one right Delta abstraction to rule them all. We’ve built a unified catalog-agnostic API against which connectors can develop, enabled deep and powerful engine integrations for DuckDB, Delta-Spark, and Delta-Flink, all while keeping the complexity of catalog support out of Kernel.

We’ve built a unified catalog-agnostic API against which connectors can develop, enabled deep and powerful engine integrations for DuckDB, Delta-Spark, and Delta-Flink, all while keeping the complexity of catalog support out of Kernel.

Based in San Francisco, Scott is passionate about open-source technologies and building performance-intensive systems.

title: Delta Sharing SecureConnect: Simplify Network Configuration for External Data Collaboration
abstract: As Delta Sharing deployments scale to more recipients, managing per-recipient IP allowlists and allowing direct storage access becomes a significant operational burden. It can also be a blocker for data providers that want to keep their storage hidden behind firewalls. This session covers how Delta Sharing SecureConnect addresses this problem by introducing a Databricks-managed network intermediary that proxies storage access on behalf of recipients. Providers perform a one-time network configuration regardless of the number of recipients. SecureConnect then handles routing across AWS, Azure, and GCP without requiring storage to be exposed to the public internet. We'll cover the architecture, cloud-specific configuration patterns, and how SecureConnect offers private and simple data sharing at scale.

title: Delta Sharing: The Most Widely Adopted Open Protocol for Data and AI Sharing
abstract: AI is only as good as the data that fuels it. To build and scale models, organizations need a way to move past fragmented data silos and complex ETL. Delta Sharing provides the industry’s most widely adopted open protocol for the secure, real-time exchange of the datasets and AI assets required for modern innovation. We’ll dive deep into: Fueling AI With Openness: How to share live data, volumes and AI models with any recipient—regardless of their platform—to accelerate model training and deployment. Latest Innovations: A first look at enhanced governance, automated provider workflows and expanded support for complex assets like AI functions and unstructured data. Real-World Scale: Real-world use cases showing how organizations use the protocol to unify their data ecosystems and manage AI assets at scale. From live demos to architectural best practices, you’ll walk away with a roadmap to unify your data ecosystem and lead the next wave of open collaboration.

title: Democratizing Data and AI at Freshworks With Governed Lakehouse and Freddy AI
abstract: Freshworks has standardized its data and AI strategy on a Databricks‑powered lakehouse called Baikal, built to serve 70,000+ customers and 1,500 internal users across 14 business functions. Databricks ingests data from dozens of operational and third‑party sources into Delta Lake bronze, silver, and gold layers governed by Unity Catalog, enabling high‑quality, discoverable datasets and fine‑grained access control with full auditability. Dedicated BI, AIML, product and functional workspaces run on governed & segregated compute with centralized monitoring, improving reliability, observability and cost attribution. On this foundation, Freshworks trains and operates Freddy AI agents, copilots and insights using Databricks ML, MLflow and an open AI ecosystem to move seamlessly from experimentation to production. This architecture modernizes legacy Hadoop systems, delivers 2–5x faster processing and democratizes AI across the company.

title: Deploying and Monitoring Agents on Databricks
abstract: This hands-on course guides you through deploying and monitoring agentic AI systems in the Databricks environment. You will start by learning how to deploy agents in batch mode using AI Functions and integrate them into data pipelines. Next, you'll explore real-time deployment using Model Serving, including deploying agents as REST endpoints and managing autoscaling, versioning, and governance via Mosaic AI Model Serving. You'll then turn to observability and monitoring, using MLflow tracing and scoring functions to capture metrics, analyze requests and responses, and detect anomalies in production. Finally, you'll learn best practices for deploying agents with built-in trace collection, inference logging, and feedback loops to support ongoing evaluation and robustness. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, including Unity Catalog and Delta Lake Intermediate Python programming experience Familiarity with Generative AI fundamentals, including LLMs, RAG architectures, and prompt engineering Familiarity with Databricks Asset Bundles (DABs) Basic SQL proficiency, including use of functions like ai_query Basic understanding of MLOps/LLMOps principles and software deployment concepts, including CI/CD pipelines, REST APIs, and YAML configuration

title: Designing beautiful, effective dashboards: Best practices for visual story telling in AI/BI
abstract: A great dashboard isn’t just fast and accurate. It’s intuitive, engaging, and easy to act on. Yet many fall short, cluttered with too many visuals, unclear layouts, and designs that don’t translate across devices.AI/BI Dashboards make it easier to build polished, interactive experiences, but strong design still depends on thoughtful choices around layout, hierarchy, and usability. With rising expectations for mobile access, dashboards need to deliver the same clarity whether viewed on a large screen or in the palm of your hand.We’ll cover practical design best practices to help you create dashboards that don’t just display information, but make it easy to understand and act on.- Design dashboards with clear visual hierarchy and focus- Choose the right charts and layouts to communicate insights effectively- Design responsive dashboards that work seamlessly across desktop and mobile- Avoid common design mistakes that cause clutter, confusion, and misinterpretation

title: Destination Databricks: Hertz’s Data Platform Modernization Journey
abstract: Hertz is executing a multi-phase transformation to decommission its 15-year-old Teradata/Informatica/Cognos ecosystem and establish a modern data platform that powers BI and analytics across global reservations, rentals, billing, fleet management and enterprise operations.Hertz’s Databricks-powered data platform enables real-time analytics and enterprise-scale AI while improving data governance and significantly lowering operational expenses.In this talk, we walk through Hertz’s data landscape and our approach migrating from legacy systems.

title: Detecting and Reducing Manipulative AI Outputs With Interpretable Governance Signals
abstract: Large language models are increasingly embedded in data platforms and decision workflows, yet they can generate manipulative and propagandistic content at scale, often without obvious policy violations. In this talk, I present a production-oriented approach to detecting and mitigating manipulation in generative AI systems using interpretable, data-driven signals. Drawing on real-world pipelines I’ve built, I show how manipulative techniques can be operationalized as measurable indicators within AI evaluation workflows. I demonstrate how these signals can be used to monitor model behavior, compare outputs across model versions, and guide alignment strategies that significantly reduce harmful generation while preserving utility. This session focuses on how to integrate responsible AI checks into data pipelines, how to reason about governance beyond binary safety flags, and how interpretable metrics enable more trustworthy AI systems at scale.
description_raw:
Large language models are increasingly embedded in data platforms and decision workflows, yet they can generate manipulative and propagandistic content at scale, often without obvious policy violations. In this talk, I present a production-oriented approach to detecting and mitigating manipulation in generative AI systems using interpretable, data-driven signals.

Drawing on real-world pipelines I’ve built, I show how manipulative techniques can be operationalized as measurable indicators within AI evaluation workflows. I demonstrate how these signals can be used to monitor model behavior, compare outputs across model versions, and guide alignment strategies that significantly reduce harmful generation while preserving utility.

This session focuses on how to integrate responsible AI checks into data pipelines, how to reason about governance beyond binary safety flags, and how interpretable metrics enable more trustworthy AI systems at scale.

speakers.name: Julia Jose
speakers.bio: Julia Jose is a Computer Science Ph.D. candidate at New York University, advised by Rachel Greenstadt, focusing on responsible and trustworthy AI. She is also an Applied AI Engineer at King Street, where she builds and deploys large language model–driven systems in production. Her work uses natural language processing and deep learning to study misinformation, propaganda, and manipulative behavior in LLMs, with an emphasis on detection and mitigation at scale. Previously, Julia worked as a Data Scientist at Atos building production ML systems, and as a Venture Capital and Machine Learning Fellow at Atento Capital. She has also conducted R&D in multilingual NLP and social computing at the Qatar Computing Research Institute.

title: Developing BI Dashboards with AI/BI
abstract: In this hands-on course, you’ll learn how to use the features Databricks provides for business intelligence needs: AI/BI Dashboards and AI/BI Genie. As a Databricks Data Analyst, you will be tasked with creating AI/BI Dashboards and AI/BI Genie Spaces within the platform, managing the access to these assets by stakeholders and necessary parties, and maintaining these assets as they are edited, refreshed, or decommissioned over the course of their lifespan. This course intends to instruct participants on how to design dashboards for business insights, share those with collaborators and stakeholders, and maintain those assets within the platform. Participants will also learn how to utilize AI/BI Genie Spaces to support self-service analytics through the creation and maintenance of these environments powered by the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Basic SQL proficiency for querying data in Databricks Basic familiarity with the Databricks Workspace UI Basic understanding of statistical analysis and data interpretation Familiarity with dashboards and business intelligence concepts

title: Dialing Up with Genie: How Telecom Providers are Winning with Natural Language AI/BI
abstract: In an industry where network reliability and customer experience are sacred, telecommunications leaders need accurate, real-time insights fast. Yet business and data teams have long existed in silos, keeping these crucial insights out of reach for those who need it in-the-moment the most. But now, operators are able to break down these silos with AI-powered conversational business intelligence, made possible by solutions like AI/BI Genie.In this session, discover how Genie is empowering business users across telecommunications to get the answers and insights they need from their data in natural language, allowing them to ask complex questions and receive instant, governed insights. Learn how experts at leading operators including Frontier, GCI, and DIRECTV conversational intelligence are using Genie to eliminate traditional data bottlenecks and identify new strategies to move their business forward. Attendees will walk away with a blueprint for scaling AI-driven conversational BI and get a firsthand look at how Genie is enabling real telecom organizations to do more with their data.

title: Dialogues with Data: Accelerating Pre-Sales Discovery through Agentic AI
abstract: Pre-sales Solution Architects (SAs) lose momentum navigating enterprise information silos. At Databricks, hyper-growth GTM and a fast-changing product stack amplify the problem. We built an Agentic Research Assistant that turns internal GTM knowledge into a conversational solution-design engine aligned to real pre-sales workflows.This session is a technical deep dive into the pipeline: a streaming event-driven architecture integrated with Databricks Model Serving, MLflow, and Lakehouse-native state management for real-time adaptability and observability.Highlights Workflow-to-tool modeling for field-aligned agent actions Lakehouse state store for conversation memory + tool-call trajectories Intent-aware retrieval with Instructed Retrievers Conversational reasoning using a DSPy ReAct extension Continuous optimization with DSPy + GEPA from historical trajectoriesA blueprint to move beyond basic RAG to self-improving agents that accelerate technical discovery and solutioning.

title: Ditch the Message Bus with Zerobus Ingest
abstract: Streaming is evolving—quickly. Modern architectures are eliminating infrastructure sprawl in favor of a direct-to-lakehouse model, eliminating the need for intermediate message buses and the expense of maintaining them. Zerobus Ingest allows you to push data directly to the lakehouse with near real-time writes, high throughput, and support for thousands of concurrent clients. In this demo-packed session, we’ll cover: How Zerobus works and how to build production-ready pipelines—without the heavy ownership costs of DIY infrastructure. The latest supported interfaces (e.g., REST, OTel, and Kafka-compatible APIs), as well as new features designed for massive scale (e.g., liquid clustering, JSON variant type support, etc.). See how global innovators like Joby Aviation and Toyota Motor Corporation have embraced this shift to streamline their real-time architectures. Join us and discover a direct ingestion framework to simplify your stack and accelerate your impact.
description_raw:
Streaming is evolving—quickly. Modern architectures are eliminating infrastructure sprawl in favor of a direct-to-lakehouse model, eliminating the need for intermediate message buses and the expense of maintaining them. Zerobus Ingest allows you to push data directly to the lakehouse with near real-time writes, high throughput, and support for thousands of concurrent clients.

In this demo-packed session, we’ll cover:

  • How Zerobus works and how to build production-ready pipelines—without the heavy ownership costs of DIY infrastructure.
  • The latest supported interfaces (e.g., REST, OTel, and Kafka-compatible APIs), as well as new features designed for massive scale (e.g., liquid clustering, JSON variant type support, etc.).
  • See how global innovators like Joby Aviation and Toyota Motor Corporation have embraced this shift to streamline their real-time architectures.

Join us and discover a direct ingestion framework to simplify your stack and accelerate your impact.

speakers.name: Victoria Bukta
speakers.bio: Victoria is a product manager at Databricks on the LakeFlow team. She previously was a PM at Tabular, and an engineering manager at Shopify where she led teams working on large-scale, low-latency ingestion and pioneered lakehouse adoption. She is based out of Toronto and sails in her spare time.

title: Doubling Medical Safety: Fine-Tuning Open LLMs for Women's Health Without Human Labels
abstract: Enterprises building LLM features in healthcare hit the same wall: satisfying dozens of safety rules simultaneously—crisis escalation, treatment boundaries, referral language—while real user data is off-limits and expert labeling is prohibitively expensive.We'll show how Flo Health broke through using RFT-inspired synthetic fine-tuning, transforming Llama 3.3 70B into a healthcare-compliant assistant for women's health that doubled safety compliance versus our previous iteration. The key insight: instead of investing expert time in labeling, we redirected it into designing LLM judges that scale.Our system uses 60 LLM judges—52 for medical safety, 8 for usefulness—with priority-weighted reward aggregation where P1 safety rules dominate over P2 quality rules. You'll learn patterns for multi-judge evaluation systems, reward aggregation strategies for binary constraints, and why simpler approaches beat complex alternatives. For anyone building AI where "mostly safe" isn't good enough.

speakers.name: Vladislav Nedosekin
speakers.bio: Vladislav Nedosekin is Director of Engineering AI Platform at Flo Health. He leads the AI platform behind Flo Health, focussing on modernising data architectures, accelerating product delivery with AI, and enabling engineering teams to ship faster without compromising medical safety. Before Flo Health, he spent years at AWS helping high-growth companies design cloud platforms built for scale, reliability, and radical cost efficiency.

speakers.name: Michael Shtelma
speakers.bio: Michael Shtelma is a Lead Specialist Solutions Architect at Databricks. Michael focuses on developing and operationalizing large-scale Machine Learning use cases on the Databricks platform. Before Databricks, Michael worked for Teradata, helping Teradata customers build data and machine learning solutions.

speakers.name: Andras Meczner
speakers.bio: András Meczner is a Director of Medical Accuracy and Safety at Flo Health. His team at Flo Health ensures that the features, including the AI initiatives, are medically accurate and safe in the Flo App. He is also a practicing pediatrician at Guy’s and St Thomas’ NHS Foundation Trust and has years of experience building a symptom checker.

title: Dr. Jekyll and Mr. H-AI-de: Using MLflow and AI Judges to measure model alignment and safety
abstract: Emergent misalignment sent shockwaves through the AI community in 2025. We discovered that a model fine-tuned on narrow tasks could suddenly pivot from a "helpful tool" into a "rogue actor." In early 2026, Nature warned that mistrained models "quickly go off the rails," making AI safety a non-negotiable for enterprise innovation. To build with confidence, we must move beyond blind trust toward empirical control.In this 20-minute lightning talk, we will demystify the process of assessing model integrity. We'll walk through the practical steps to:- Build custom judges designed to detect latent misalignment and goal-drift.- Deploy judges in MLflow to automate the evaluation of frontier open-source models.- Benchmark against emerging standards like HarmBench to quantify operational risk.Learn how to stay in control and inherently understand the internal "compass" of your models. The last thing your enterprise needs is an assistant that turns into a monster the moment you look away.

title: Driving Enterprise Transformation With an AI‑Enabled Field Assistant
abstract: Lilly’s presentation will highlight how a dual business and technology strategy is guiding the rollout of an AI field assistant and enabling scalable governance and organizational change management across diverse groups of field users. We will highlight why we chose the platform and how the Databricks ecosystem is accelerating innovation by unifying data, analytics and AI capabilities to support smarter, faster decision making. We expect to share expanded updates on emerging features such as Genie, which will ease prompt constraints and support more abstract, data aware questions, along with new agentic actions that streamline workflows and broaden what users can accomplish through natural language interaction. Throughout the presentation we will share lessons learned from deploying the platform at scale. Together, these advancements illustrate how coordinated business and technical innovation are shaping more adaptive, AI enabled solutions.

speakers.name: thomas raborn

title: Duct Tape to Databricks: 3 years of Brickbot at Data & AI Summit
abstract: Brickbot started as a simple chatbot hacked together for DAIS 2024. DuckDB in memory, parquet files in git, and a prayer that Heroku wouldn't go down. At DAIS 2025 we improved it with tool calling and direct integration with RainFocus, but it was still mostly cobbled together. Now, for DAIS 2026, we've rebuilt it entirely on Databricks with no custom infrastructure, using Agent Bricks, Vector Search, Databricks Apps, Lakebase, UC Functions as MCP tools, and Lakeflow. This talk traces that journey, the lessons learned shipping an AI app to 30,000 conference attendees, and how the Databricks' platform evolved alongside it.

speakers.name: Oleksandra Bovkun
speakers.bio: Oleksandra is a Developer Advocate at Databricks. She enjoys sharing her knowledge, experience, and insights about AI systems, AI governance, and Databricks. Throughout her career as a Data Engineer, AI Engineer, and Databricks Solutions Architect, she’s experienced firsthand how challenging it can be to embark on a Data and AI journey. Based on her extensive experience with building real-world AI solutions, architecting and implementing data platforms, and running large-scale performance optimization projects with commercial and open source technologies, she is passionate about breaking complex topics and helping the community democratize data and AI for their projects.

title: Effective document management and retrieval for generative AI
abstract: Building production RAG systems requires far more than embedding documents. This 90-minute deep dive covers the complete technical pipeline: document structuring principles, semantic vs. fixed-size chunking tradeoffs, contextual and multi-modal embeddings, and advanced retrieval optimization, accompanied by practical implementation on Databricks. We'll demonstrate a real-world Databricks RAG pipeline covering pre-processing at scale, embedding optimization and advanced retrieval techniques—including re-ranking, contextual filtering and real-time quality evaluation. Beyond retrieval, we showcase how Agent Bricks transforms RAG systems into autonomous, agentic applications capable of multi-step reasoning and complex tasks. The session also explores emerging trends—vision-space retrieval, contextual clustering and real-time quality assurance—critical for production systems where accuracy directly impacts outcomes.

speakers.bio: Yevgeniy Ilyin, CFA, is a Senior Solutions Architect at Databricks based in Zurich, helping customers across Central and Eastern Europe develop real-world, innovative data and AI solutions on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform. He brings 25+ years of experience in software architecture and technical leadership across financial services and enterprise technology, with a focus on data platform architecture, MLOps, governance, and agentic AI. Yevgeniy regularly speaks at industry data and AI conferences and develops and delivers hands-on workshops for practitioners. He holds a CFA charter, a Certified IT Specialist credential from ETH Zurich, and an MSc in Physics and Computer Science.

title: Embedded Analytics with Databricks AI/BI
abstract: We often focus on using Databricks AI/BI to power internal business intelligence, but what about delivering those same insights directly to your customers?In this hands-on session, you’ll learn how to embed Databricks AI/BI Dashboards and Genie into external, customer-facing applications. Discover how to seamlessly integrate analytics and conversational AI to turn your apps into data-driven experiences that empower users to make informed decisions right within their workflow.Workshop topics:-Overview of Embedded Analytics with Databricks AI/BI-Embedding Dashboards with iframe-based and token-based methods-Integrating AI/BI Genie using Conversational APIs

speakers.name: Doyoung Jung
speakers.bio: Doyoung Jung is a Solutions Architect at Databricks, where he partners with VC-backed startups to design scalable data systems. With a strong interest in the convergence of AI, BI, and customer-facing applications, Doyoung helps emerging businesses build smarter data products.

title: Embracing Serverless SQL at Riot Games
abstract: At Riot Games, we found that embracing serverless compute—rather than limiting it—allowed us to scale more effectively while improving performance, governance and user experience.In this session, we’ll share Riot’s shift from a cost-first mindset to an enablement-driven approach using Databricks SQL Serverless, with Unity Catalog as a key enabler. Consistent governance, lineage and usage visibility gave us the confidence to adopt serverless while maintaining transparency and accountability.We’ll explore how serverless SQL warehouses support diverse analytics and reporting needs through strong performance, simplified user experiences and elastic, on-demand cost efficiency. We’ll also discuss how serverless compute streamlined the data engineering lifecycle by reducing infrastructure management.Finally, we’ll cover Riot’s broader adoption of serverless for jobs and general workloads, the resulting operational and cost benefits, and how we manage costs in a serverless-first model.

title: Emotional Congruence at Scale: Using Databricks AI to Align Ads and Content
abstract: TV advertisers know context matters, but most contextual signals stop at coarse attributes like genre or keywords. What about emotion? What we watch shapes how we feel—and how we feel influences how we engage with brands.At NBCUniversal, we built an Emotional Congruence engine to quantify whether alignment between these emotional signals drives intent and conversion—and how to scale it using Databricks.Using foundation models served on Databricks, we extract and summarize unstructured signals from content and ads, tagging each with a standardized emotional taxonomy across themes, tones and personal values. Governed end-to-end with MLflow 3.0, the system is orchestrated for rigorous testing and deployment.Testing shows emotional alignment drives measurable lift—often in the double digits. As a result, it is now embedded within NBCUniversal’s broader contextual advertising offering.

title: Empowering Non-Technical Users in Pharma With Dashboards and Chatbots Working Together in Databricks
abstract: The presentation shows how Databricks enables non‑technical users in pharma to gain scientific insights by uniting dashboards, chatbots, documents and data. The foundation is data and documents from source systems organized in Unity Catalog and a semantic layer of schemas, tables with definitions, metrics views with business logics, keys linking datasets, SQL functions, Genie and Agent Bricks. Clinical study data is complex, with interpretation depending on context often described in documents. We use a dashboard for context, with visuals and filters conveying the overarching story, while Genie and Agent Bricks offer insights via natural-language questions. Traditional dashboards excel at data storytelling but lack flexibility and tends to grow into overwhelming complexity, by adding context-aware chatbots we address these challenges. Insights can also be embedded into platforms like Teams and SharePoint, ensuring easy access, while having data and calculations centrally governed.

speakers.name: Half Scheidl
speakers.bio: Half Scheidl is a Senior Delivery Solutions Architect at Databricks with 15+ years of experience spanning cloud architecture, data platforms, analytics, and AI. He focuses on turning ambitious ideas into production-ready solutions, helping customers move from first concept to real-world impact. Known for bridging business goals and technical detail, he translates advanced capabilities into practical, high-leverage outcomes. Half holds a BSc in Computer Engineering and an MSc in IT Management. Before Databricks, he led AI, analytics, and data platform initiatives at H&M. At DAIS, he will focus on how dashboards, semantic layers, and natural-language interfaces can make data exploration more intuitive, reliable, and impactful.

speakers.name: Sinna Lisa Vange
speakers.bio: Principal information & data architect in the pharmaceutical industry with 30 years' experience, mainly in clinical development but also research, pre-clinical, and medical affairs. Core skills include scientific communication, data visualisation, information and data architecture, IT systems design, and leadership. I have in different roles been involved in numerous clinical projects across multiple therapy areas, as well in IT projects spanning resource planning, document management, data standardisation and visualisation, study design, and product master data. I enjoy combining detail with overview, complexity with simplicity, and synthesize it all into meaning that can help others obtain new insights.

title: Enabling a Low-Latency Data Mesh at Warner Music Group Using Lakebase, Iceberg and Unity Catalog
abstract: Today’s enterprises face daunting challenges when creating impact with data, from fundamental trust issues to dynamically evolving AI use cases. Legacy systems with technical debt impede business value even as users expect trustworthy, low-latency insights for both natural language queries and classic insights. Furthermore, pipelines are often distributed across different engines. This environment demands a flexible approach to drive impact that brings cohesion to an often-messy data environment. This session describes how WMG Tech leveraged Databricks to develop a declarative data platform that realizes a compositional data mesh designed to tackle this challenge. This architecture includes: Iceberg as an engine agnostic data layer for access to diverse sources Unity Catalog for compositional access to data products Lakebase for native low latency analytical APIs Together, this platform enables an array of data products that serve AI queries and WMG's applications.
description_raw:
Today’s enterprises face daunting challenges when creating impact with data, from fundamental trust issues to dynamically evolving AI use cases. Legacy systems with technical debt impede business value even as users expect trustworthy, low-latency insights for both natural language queries and classic insights. Furthermore, pipelines are often distributed across different engines. This environment demands a flexible approach to drive impact that brings cohesion to an often-messy data environment.

This session describes how WMG Tech leveraged Databricks to develop a declarative data platform that realizes a compositional data mesh designed to tackle this challenge. This architecture includes:

  • Iceberg as an engine agnostic data layer for access to diverse sources
  • Unity Catalog for compositional access to data products
  • Lakebase for native low latency analytical APIs

Together, this platform enables an array of data products that serve AI queries and WMG's applications.

speakers.name: Michael Jones
speakers.bio: Michael Jones is a Senior Director of Software Engineering at Warner Music Group leading the Data and Insights organization responsible for ML/AI, data engineering and infrastructure, and data serving. He has a background in software engineering and has extensive experience leading engineering teams across a variety of domains including digital ads, cloud, and unmanned systems.

title: Energy & Utilities Industry Forum | Sponsored by: Infosys
abstract: The energy transition is the defining industrial challenge of our time and data is its most critical infrastructure. Join energy and utilities leaders for an essential forum on how Databricks’ Data Intelligence Platform is enabling companies to navigate decarbonization, grid modernization and the rise of distributed energy resources with confidence and speed. Whether you're optimizing upstream exploration, managing a complex grid at scale or building the analytics backbone for a net-zero commitment, Databricks provides the unified, open platform that turns petabytes of operational data into real-time intelligence. This forum explores the imperatives shaping the sector: predictive asset management, emissions monitoring and reporting, energy trading and risk analytics, renewable integration, and AI-powered demand forecasting. Learn how leading energy companies are eliminating data fragmentation and accelerating decision-making from wellhead to wall socket. Gain insights from peers who are already harnessing the power of data to drive the energy transition forward and build lasting competitive advantage. Features leaders from Chevron, Shell, Constellation and Alinta Energy.

speakers.name: Anne Marie Hensley
speakers.bio: Anne Marie Hensley is Chevron’s General Manager of Manufacturing Ops Digital Engineering. She is an enthusiastic and proven IT leader with over 20 years of experience delivering transformational change using technology as a catalyst. One of Anne Marie’s key strengths is her natural ability to take complex ideas and challenges and break them down into manageable and actionable solutions. This, along with her no-nonsense communication style and her contagious optimism, make her an extremely effective leader. Anne Marie earned her bachelor’s degree in civil engineering and a master’s degree in environmental engineering from Manhattan College. She lives in The Woodlands, TX with her husband Chad and two teenagers, Taggart and Mary-Cate.

speakers.name: Jay Cavalcanto
speakers.bio: Jay Cavalcanto is the SVP and CIO at Constellation, where he sets the strategic direction for technology, operations, cybersecurity, and the ongoing growth of the company’s digital platforms. In addition to leading the major initiative to separate Constellation from Exelon, a complex project involving the seamless migration of thousands of users and applications to a secure, modern environment with no technical debt, Jay has played a key role in overseeing multiple large-scale mergers and integrations. His leadership has been instrumental in guiding multi-year transformation projects, managing enterprise-wide technology upgrades, and orchestrating successful implementations of new platforms to support business growth.

speakers.name: John OBrien
speakers.bio: John is the Portfolio Manager for Data, Analytics, and AI at Shell Energy Americas. With 25+ years of experience, he specializes in transforming innovative data solutions into production realities. In 2022, he implemented the first Lakehouse at Shell Energy Trading, now ingesting 2500+ datasets from 60+ sources using both structured streaming and batch techniques. Business users create data products managed directly in Unity Catalog. John leverages Databricks and AI techniques to revolutionize data governance in production. He’s a passionate speaker, sharing his experiences and tips with the community.

speakers.name: Doug Way
speakers.job_title: GM West Gas and Power Trading
speakers.bio: Doug Way brings over two decades of experience to his role as General Manager Trading at Shell Energy North America. Based in San Diego, CA, Doug oversees trading desks in Canada and the United States, focusing on natural gas and power trading, including energy, capacity, and environmental attribute products. Doug's journey with Shell began in 2002 in Calgary, Canada, where he worked on various trade desks primarily focused on natural gas trading. In 2021, he relocated to the San Diego office, taking on responsibility for the gas and power trading desks. His role expanded in 2023 to include managing the North American real-time power trading team. Throughout his career, Doug has prioritized exposure management of various products to meet Shell's customer needs and has been dedicated to developing innovative solutions to help customers achieve their renewable energy goals.

title: Engineering the AI-Powered Practice: Scaling Vertical AI on a Foundation of Three Trillion Data Points
abstract: The promise of digitization in healthcare has long been overshadowed by administrative toil, a landscape of "clicks and fragmentation" where 73% of physician office hours are spent away from patients. To address this, ModMed is building the "AI-Powered Practice," fueled by a clinical engine processing 3PB of data monthly, including three trillion data points. The AI-Powered Practice will bring powerful AI assistants to automate the entire medical practice including clinical, billing, front-office & patient engagement.Learn how the Databricks Platform is supporting this end-to-end AI solution at massive scale: proprietary model training across 750M+ patient encounters and 2B+ diagnoses, plus low-latency production inference via Model Serving. Discover how ModMed, through purposeful collaboration with Databricks, is leading the charge in vertical AI in transforming healthcare operations, reducing provider burnout and elevating the human connection in medicine with visionary, cutting-edge AI.

speakers.name: Venkatesh Jayaraman
speakers.bio: Venk Jayaraman (VJ) serves as chief technology officer at ModMed, where he is responsible for providing strategic direction and technical leadership for the product engineering organization. VJ is a seasoned technology architect with over 30 years of experience driving high-stakes innovation. Today, he leads the charge in AI-powered transformation, moving beyond traditional software architecture to build intelligent, self-evolving systems. A pioneer in operationalizing Lean Agile practices at scale, VJ specializes in blending human ingenuity with machine intelligence to unlock business agility and growth. VJ holds a certificate from the Wharton Chief Technology Officer Program, a master’s degree in engineering management from the University of Florida, and a bachelor’s degree in electrical engineering from India’s National Institute of Technology.

title: Enterprise Observability with ZeroBus and OTEL at Scale with Databricks
abstract: OpenTelemetry has become the industry standard for collecting traces, metrics, and logs from modern distributed systems. However, operating observability platforms at enterprise scale often requires proprietary tools and fragmented data pipelines.In this session, we present an end-to-end architecture for enterprise observability using OpenTelemetry and ZeroBus with Databricks as the analytics and visualization backbone. We will show how OpenTelemetry telemetry streams can be ingested through ZeroBus and stored in Delta Lake, enabling scalable processing and long-term retention of traces, metrics, and logs.We will demonstrate how to build a custom Databricks App to visualize flame graphs, explore distributed traces, and correlate metrics and logs in a unified interface - delivering capabilities similar to proprietary platforms but powered by the Lakehouse.

speakers.name: Aleksandar Dragojevic
speakers.bio: Aleksandar is a Staff FDE at Databricks with more than a decade of software-engineering and technical-leadership experience. They specialize in architecting and delivering high-performance, scalable systems that remain elegant and easy to reason about. At Databricks, Aleksandar partners with enterprise customers worldwide to design, deploy, and operate cloud-native data platforms at massive scale, helping organizations unlock actionable insights and accelerate innovation.

title: Enterprise Reporting and Embedded Analytics with AI/BI Dashboards
abstract: Modern BI isn't just about ad hoc exploration—it's about delivering trusted data to internal teams and external users, wherever they work. Yet many organizations still rely on fragmented tools, leading to inconsistent metrics, duplicated data, and added cost.AI/BI Dashboards bring enterprise reporting and embedded analytics directly into the Databricks Platform—with no separate BI infrastructure and no licenses. From scheduled delivery to Slack and Teams to embedded dashboards and Genie experiences, teams can deliver governed insights at scale.We'll dive into how to design, deploy, and scale reporting and embedded analytics using AI/BI Dashboards and Genie. Topics include:- Production-ready reports with scheduling, parameterization, and consistent metrics- Embedding dashboards and Genie into internal tools and customer-facing apps- Governance, security, and performance at scale- Architectural patterns to scale reporting and embedding without duplicating data, logic, or cost

speakers.name: Maggie Li
speakers.bio: Maggie is an Associate Product Manager at Databricks.

title: Essential Ingredients to Successful Digital Data Transformation Program
abstract: GM Financial led a multi‑year data and AI transformation on Databricks, moving from traditional reporting to a modern analytics and GenAI‑enabled ecosystem guided by a clear enterprise vision. This session shares the people and process “recipe” behind that change: formal data governance and operating bodies, a data strategy board, stewardship practices, and communities of practice that increased literacy, trust, and SOX‑ready data. We will highlight the Databricks Center of Excellence—onboarding, study groups, certifications, office hours and best‑practice sharing—that accelerated adoption and improved CSAT/NPS. The talk also introduces AI/BI Genie—a Databricks chatbot that turns natural language into SQL over curated documentation, enabling governed self‑service analytics. Finally, we show how a Databricks‑centric FinOps framework gives pipeline‑to‑query cost visibility, enables show‑back of spend and drives continuous cost optimization without sacrificing agility.

speakers.name: Latha Subramanian
speakers.bio: Latha Subramanian is an experienced Leader specialized with 28+ years of specialization in AI/ML, Data, Analytics and Reporting domains in several industry verticals ranging from Healthcare, P&C and specialty Insurance, Financial Services, banking, multi-media publishing, technology, high tech industry, auto financing. Latha has led and successfully executed several multi-million-dollar data programs and is viewed as a trusted CXO advisor. She is a distinguished speaker at various CDO/CDAO summits. Latha is an Executive Board member of CDO Magazine. In her spare time, Latha loves traveling the world and spending time with her family.

title: Evolution of the Databricks Commercial Motion: Building Scalable Intelligence on Databricks
abstract: A truly "data-driven" enterprise requires close collaboration between Business and Data Science. In this session, we share our journey using Databricks to bridge the gap between technical complexity and operational reality.We detail our journey: We start by centralizing fragmented sources via Unity Catalog, surfacing descriptive trends via AiBi dashboards. We then move from descriptive to prescriptive insights, using Lakeflow orchestration to automate generation and understand causal effects. Then, we evolve from static insights to dynamic optimization, exemplified by a contract scoring system that helps sellers structure deals, built on MLflow and integrated with Salesforce. Finally, we explores how Databricks' AI products amplify the values of the previous stages.Attendees will learn to unify Databricks’ core capabilities, transitioning from high-level vision to a functional reality where AI and human strategy are woven together.
description_raw: A truly "data-driven" enterprise requires close collaboration between Business and Data Science. In this session, we share our journey using Databricks to bridge the gap between technical complexity and operational reality.We detail our journey: We start by centralizing fragmented sources via Unity Catalog, surfacing descriptive trends via AiBi dashboards. We then move from descriptive to prescriptive insights, using Lakeflow orchestration to automate generation and understand causal effects. Then, we evolve from static insights to dynamic optimization, exemplified by a contract scoring system that helps sellers structure deals, built on MLflow and integrated with Salesforce. Finally, we explores how Databricks' AI products amplify the values of the previous stages.Attendees will learn to unify Databricks’ core capabilities, transitioning from high-level vision to a functional reality where AI and human strategy are woven together.

speakers.name: Jason Huang
speakers.bio: Jason Huang is a Staff Data Scientist at Databricks. His recent work spans statistical methods and packages to quantify the impact of go-to-market motions, deal scoring models guiding strategic deal-making, and pricing-model simulations for serverless and AI products. He partners closely with commercialization, engineering, and product leadership to turn data into strategic decisions. Prior to Databricks, Jason worked on incentive design, pricing, and experimentation at Uber and Robinhood after completing his Econ PhD.

title: Evolving Apache Spark Structured Streaming in Open Source: A Year in Review and the Road Ahead!
abstract: Apache Spark Structured Streaming has quickly become the foundational open-source technology for powering mission-critical streaming ingestion and ETL pipelines worldwide. The Spark 4.1 release introduces powerful updates, most notably a new Real-Time Mode that enables operational pipelines to process data with latencies in milliseconds, all within the Spark ecosystem. Stateful transformations have been significantly bolstered through TransformWithState and stream-stream join enhancements, offering broader coverage and higher performance. Furthermore, robust improvements—such as revamped locking and integrity verification—provide improved stability for stateful queries. Looking ahead to Spark 4.2, the roadmap includes state repartitioning, query evolution, hybrid sources, and other enhancements, further establishing Spark as the gold standard for all streaming workloads. Join us to explore these recent advancements and the exciting future of open-source Structured Streaming!

speakers.name: Jerry Peng
speakers.bio: Jerry Peng is currently an Staff Software Engineer at Databricks extensively working Apache Spark Structured Streaming. Before joining Databricks, he was a Principal Software Engineer at Splunk working on streaming and messaging projects especially with Apache Pulsar and Apache Flink. Jerry is a committer and PMC member of Apache Pulsar, Apache Storm, and Apache Heron projects. Before Splunk, he worked at Streamlio (acquired by Splunk), Citadel, and Yahoo on distributed systems and stream processing. Jerry has been working in the area of distributed systems and stream processing since his days in grad school at the University of Illinois, Urbana-Champaign.

speakers.name: Anish Shrigondekar
speakers.bio: Anish is a Software Engineer at Databricks working on the core streaming team. Prior to joining Databricks, Anish has worked in other systems groups at Splunk Inc, and VMware, Inc. His areas of interest include operating systems, databases, storage, filesystems etc.

title: Faster queries in local laptop mode for Apache Spark
abstract: Apache Spark is optimized for distributed large-scale processing, but this architecture introduces significant overhead for small, local datasets -- often making sub-100 MB queries take several seconds. In this talk, we present a suite of optimizations that dramatically improve Spark's local-mode performance across three fronts: smarter query compilation and task scheduling that eliminates unnecessary shuffles, an Arrow-based df.cache implementation that cuts cache load and query times in half, and a novel shuffle-free execution mode using Java virtual threads for lightweight in-process data transfer. We will walk through the design, benchmarks, and engineering trade-offs of each approach, and show how together they position Apache Spark as a practical gateway engine from laptop-scale exploration to petabyte-scale production.

speakers.bio: Daniel Tenedorio is a senior staff software engineer at Databricks, working on Spark Core and Spark SQL. He interacts with the Apache Spark™ open-source community to develop and review query processing features. Daniel has a breadth of experience in database systems development from building and launching features underpinning multiple major production services over time.

title: Faster, Leaner, and Easier to Debug: PySpark UDFs in 2026
abstract: PySpark has long provided one of the most powerful and flexible ways to bring Python logic to large-scale distributed data processing. In this talk, we focus on two major advances in the PySpark UDF ecosystem: performance optimizations and improved debuggability. First, we introduce Arrow-based execution for Python, including Native Arrow UDFs and Arrow UDTFs, which operate directly on columnar Arrow data without Pandas conversion overhead. This design reduces memory usage, improves support for complex types, and delivers faster execution compared to earlier Python UDF approaches. Second, we present new debuggability enhancements for Python running inside Spark tasks, including built-in faulthandler integration for clearer crash diagnostics and improved profiling support to better understand CPU and memory behavior of UDFs. Together, these improvements make PySpark UDFs not only faster and more scalable, but also significantly easier to diagnose and optimize in production environments.

speakers.name: Tian Gao
speakers.bio: Senior Software Engineer at Databricks Python Core Developer

title: Feature and ML Platform Powered by Databricks
abstract: This presentation offers a comprehensive exploration of the Feature & Machine Learning Platform, focusing on its architectural design, essential components and seamless integration with Databricks. The platform harnesses Databricks for advanced feature engineering, structured streaming, declarative pipeline orchestration and end-to-end model training and serving, supporting both scheduled and event-driven workflows for maximum flexibility. Key technologies highlighted include the DBX Feature Engineering SDK, Apache Spark Structured Streaming and Databricks Lakeflow jobs, all of which contribute to scalable, efficient data processing. Online feature computation is facilitated through the DBX Unity Catalog with Python UDFs, while Databricks Online Feature Stores powered by Lakebase enable real-time use cases. For batch feature consumption outside Databricks, managed Delta UniForm and Iceberg read-only feature tables provide robust solutions.

title: Financial Services Industry Forum
abstract: Financial intelligence beats general intelligence. Trading books reprice in milliseconds, fraud patterns shift with every payment rail, and supervisors in your backyard are already asking how your models work, not if you use them. Generic AI can summarize the noise; it cannot safely move capital or satisfy regulators. Financial intelligence — built on unified, governed, and secure data — can. At the Financial Services Forum, leaders from retail and community banks, asset managers, insurers and advisory firms will share how they are unifying data, deploying domain‑trained models and running agentic AI with firm governance controls. The result: AI decisions that are fast, explainable, audit‑ready and built on the unified intelligence only your data can provide.

title: FinOps for Cybersecurity at Scale: Balancing Cost, Speed, and Safety at HSBC
abstract: When HSBC's cybersecurity team adopted Databricks to power threat detection and security analytics, we knew scale would come fast. What we didn't anticipate was just how fast, or the cost challenges that would follow. We grew from a small pilot to hundreds of security analysts and data engineers, processing massive volumes of security telemetry data daily. The challenge: Most of our users could detect cyber threats but didn't know which queries were scanning terabytes unnecessarily or whether cluster configurations were burning through budget. Traditional approaches said costs were rising but couldn't teach us as the users how to use Databricks efficiently. We share an approach that turns every inefficiency into a teaching moment and empowers security teams to self-optimize without becoming data engineering experts. I'll share the framework and automations we used to grow Databricks usage while flattening the cost curve, all while maintaining rapid threat detection capabilities.

title: Fireside Chat: A Candid Conversation on AI, Data Stacks and What Matters for the Next 24 Months
abstract: Join us for a candid fireside chat with David Meyer, SVP of Product at Databricks and Josue Bogran, VP of Data + AI at zeb.co and Databricks MVP as we cut through the noise around AI, data stacks and enterprise transformation. We’ll unpack what actually matters over the next 24 months: from real-world adoption trends to widespread misconceptions about Databricks and the modern data stack. No slides and no hype, just an honest, interview-style conversation shaped by community questions, grounded in what leading teams are doing today as well as insights from Databricks’ roadmap.

speakers.name: Josue Bogran
speakers.bio: Josue Bogran is a Databricks MVP, product advisor, and VP of Data + AI Architecture at zeb.co, a Databricks and AWS partner with 1,500+ team members. He’s had the privilege of learning from and interviewing Databricks leaders including Ali Ghodsi, Matei Zaharia, and Reynold Xin, and has helped shape several Databricks products behind the scenes. Josue also advises product companies such as EnrollHere.com, a platform for carriers and agencies. He lives in North Carolina with his wife and five children, soon to be six, and when he’s not working on data and AI, you’ll likely find him on a pickleball court. To check out my videos interviews and product demos with Databricks leaders: 1) in/JosueBogran 2) YouTube.com/@JosueBogranChannel

title: Fireside Chat: No Hype, No Slides: The 24-Month Data and AI Decisions That Actually Matter
abstract: There is no shortage of AI predictions. There is a critical shortage of honest conversations about what enterprise leaders should actually do next. In this no-slides, no-hype fireside chat, Databricks SVP of Product David Meyer and zeb.co VP of Data + AI Architecture Josue Bogran — one of the most followed voices in the Databricks community — dissect what is genuinely changing in data stacks and enterprise AI adoption over the next 24 months. They'll surface the most persistent misconceptions leaders have about the modern data stack, reveal what separates organizations generating real returns from those still in experimentation mode, and share candid insight from Databricks' product roadmap. Questions are shaped by the community in advance. This is the session for leaders who want an unfiltered signal, not vendor positioning.

speakers.name: Dmitry Luchnik
speakers.bio: I am a passionate Data & Analytics architect with extensive hands‑on experience of building and scaling enterprise data platforms. I have led the design and evolution of multiple analytics platforms, including serving as lead architect for a Lakehouse platform that enabled scalable, self‑service Data&AI applications. My work has been focused on making data work for everyone by removing bottlenecks and enabling analysts, data scientists, and engineers to focus on insights. Today, as Director of Enterprise Architecture for Data & AI at adidas, I drive enterprise‑wide data and AI governance and the evolution of the technology landscape to ensure data becomes a true competitive advantage in a fast‑moving global company.

speakers.name: Niclas Schlautkoetter
speakers.bio: With over 20 Years with SAP, Niclas Schlautkötter is an experienced business technology professional, specializing in hybrid data, analytics and AI architectures in large enterprises. In his long term consulting career he advised global SAP customers on their data strategy, designing complex, end-to-end IT system landscapes integrating SAP and non-sap technology and leading multi-year SAP implementation projects. Now, Niclas serves as Global Head of Strategic Architecture Advisory for SAP Business Data Cloud leading a global team of Blackbelt IT architects. His team accelerates cloud transformation for SAP’s most strategic customers, helping them maximize the value of their data estates leveraging Business AI and acting as a critical bridge between SAP field organizations and product engineering, ensuring customer centricity remains at the core of SAP’s product development efforts.

title: First‑Class Containers Across Databricks: A Unified, Portable Dependency Management Strategy
abstract: Databricks is bringing first‑class Docker support everywhere. This session shows how we’re making containers a core part of the Databricks developer experience, delivering faster startup, tighter governance, and clearer observability. We’ll walk through the product architecture, the UX investments that reduce time‑to‑first‑result, and how we approach compatibility and safe rollout for critical workloads at scale.

speakers.name: Justin Breese
speakers.bio: Justin is a Staff Product Manager at Databricks. He primarily focuses on their serverless products - versionless runtime, dependency management (environments), and containers. When not thinking about execution engines, you can find him staying busy with soccer, hiking, brewing beer, roasting coffee, and older cars. Justin lives in Topanga, CA with his wife, son, and daughter.

title: Flipping the Switch to Serverless: How McAfee Modernized ~40% of Its Infra and Halved Compute Costs
abstract: At McAfee, we transformed our data platform by migrating over a third of production workloads to Databricks Serverless and empowering 1,000+ end users through SQL Serverless while unifying governance with Unity Catalog. This shift cut idle cluster overhead, delivered $350K in projected annual savings and drove a 34% increase in active monthly users—all with a lean platform team. Our phased, data-driven migration playbook made adoption safe, reversible and repeatable, using decision dashboards and clear revert-to-classic paths to de-risk modernization. So far, we’ve achieved 39% infrastructure modernization—moving 90% of warehouses and 40% of jobs to SQL Serverless. In this session, we’ll share how we governed workloads with Unity Catalog, selected high-impact migration candidates, overcame serverless limitations, and built dashboards and tagging strategies that earned executive confidence. Attendees will leave with a proven blueprint to scale serverless adoption confidently across their own organizations.

title: Forecasting Friction: Unpacking the Trust, Accuracy, and Data Gaps in SaaS Consumption Models
abstract: Forecasting consumption for usage-based SaaS, like Databricks $DBU, faces challenges in three areas:Trust & Adoption: The core issue is the "black box" problem, where forecasts lack explanation. Forecasts with high volatility week over week undermine stakeholder trust. Model Accuracy: Structural limitations lead to inconsistency between account-level and higher-level forecasts. The model also struggles to correctly interpret one-time consumption spikes (e.g., migrations), often misrepresenting them as perpetual growth.Data Signal Quality: Reliable leading indicators are difficult to find. Past customer spend dominates prediction, while other technical signals like product usage and external factors are weak, resulting in ineffective early warning systems (high false negatives).

title: Fox One: Video Content Personalization Powered by GenAI
abstract: *Copresenting with Fox. When Fox introduced Fox One, its short-form video platform, we needed to deliver engaging recommendations at launch despite sparse, noisy engagement signals. Rather than waiting months for reliable data, we used GenAI to bootstrap personalization and guide early decision making under real-world constraints.In this talk, we describe how we designed a modular, production-ready personalization system on Databricks that blends GenAI with classical machine learning and evolves as data matures. We will cover how we used Gemini to extract structure from video content, leveraged Databricks Functions to infer early preference signals, built millisecond-level video retrieval on Databricks Lakebase, and developed offline evaluation using GenAI to provide directional insight when traditional A/B testing was unreliable. Attendees will learn how we weaved GenAI into classical recommendation systems on the Databricks lakehouse to ship personalization under data constraints.
