speakers.name: Nicole Catalino
speakers.bio: Nicole Catalino is a Staff Product Manager on the Emerging Technology team at FOX, leading AI- and data-driven products across news, sports, and entertainment. She builds multimodal platforms that transform video and text into intelligent, searchable systems powering both newsroom operations and consumer experiences. Her recent work includes launching FOX One's "Shorts," an autonomous pipeline that uses AI to detect topics, clip video, and deliver personalized short-form content in real time.

speakers.name: Rini Gupta
speakers.bio: Rini Gupta is an AI Forward Deployed Engineer at Databricks, where she builds and deploys production-grade AI systems across a range of domains. Her work focuses on generative AI, and she specializes in integrating large language models with traditional machine learning and optimization techniques to solve complex, real-world problems. Previously, she worked as a data scientist at C3.ai and Appian. She holds a master’s degree from UC Berkeley.

title: From "Hidden Costs" to "High Efficiency": Scaling DV's Lakehouse Observability
abstract: A practical guide to the internal monitoring tools we built on Databricks System Tables to make our data platform more efficient and performant. Hear directly from our Data Platform Engineering team about what worked for us and what we learned along the way We’ll dive into the backend strategies that transformed our platform, including: Dashboards We Actually Look At: Tracking real costs and table/column sizes. Practical Alerts for Real Problems: Identifying costly unused tables, tables with missing or excessive retention policies, long-running queries, and constantly failing jobs. The "Orphan" Hunt: Programmatically identifying unreferenced files and un-vacuumed data by auditing Delta logs against cloud storage. The Cost/Read Metric: Using recursive lineage queries to expose expensive, high-maintenance tables with little or no downstream value. Footer Analysis: Scanning Parquet footers at scale to isolate metadata bloat from actual data —even in complex nested types.
description_raw:
A practical guide to the internal monitoring tools we built on Databricks System Tables to make our data platform more efficient and performant. Hear directly from our Data Platform Engineering team about what worked for us and what we learned along the way

We’ll dive into the backend strategies that transformed our platform, including:

  • Dashboards We Actually Look At: Tracking real costs and table/column sizes.
  • Practical Alerts for Real Problems: Identifying costly unused tables, tables with missing or excessive retention policies, long-running queries, and constantly failing jobs.
  • The "Orphan" Hunt: Programmatically identifying unreferenced files and un-vacuumed data by auditing Delta logs against cloud storage.
  • The Cost/Read Metric: Using recursive lineage queries to expose expensive, high-maintenance tables with little or no downstream value.
  • Footer Analysis: Scanning Parquet footers at scale to isolate metadata bloat from actual data —even in complex nested types.

title: From 0 to 100: How One NASCAR Team Is Moving up the Grid With Databricks and Zerobus Ingest
abstract: Trackhouse Racing set out to close a narrow competitive gap with a small team of 5 developers. In ~12 months, they went from no platform to a race-ready data and AI stack on Databricks — turning video, telemetry and race reports into decisions teams can act on in hours, not weeks. In this session, Trackhouse will share the playbook and architecture behind three high-impact deliverables: Pit crew performance: Dashboards paired with stop video to quantify where milliseconds are won/lost. Unified race data foundation: Bringing in 25+ bespoke racing systems (timing/scoring, setup sheets, ECU logs, sim outputs — and “weird” sources) with repeatable patterns. Race report intelligence: Enabling engineers and non-technical leaders to retrieve answers instantly. Attendees will leave with a concrete “small team, fast wins” blueprint: how to prioritize the first data products, design reliable ingestion for messy sources and ship analytics and GenAI experiences that actually get used.

In this session, Trackhouse will share the playbook and architecture behind three high-impact deliverables:

  • Pit crew performance: Dashboards paired with stop video to quantify where milliseconds are won/lost.
  • Unified race data foundation: Bringing in 25+ bespoke racing systems (timing/scoring, setup sheets, ECU logs, sim outputs — and “weird” sources) with repeatable patterns.
  • Race report intelligence: Enabling engineers and non-technical leaders to retrieve answers instantly.

Attendees will leave with a concrete “small team, fast wins” blueprint: how to prioritize the first data products, design reliable ingestion for messy sources and ship analytics and GenAI experiences that actually get used.

speakers.name: Nick Ragonese
speakers.bio: With over a decade of experience in data, Nick Ragonese has specialized in driving AI innovation for sports leagues and teams since joining Databricks in 2021.

title: From App to App Ecosystem: An Operating Model for Thousands of Enterprise-Scale Databricks Apps
abstract: When you go from one AI app to a hundred, the bottleneck isn't building — it's governing. Who owns each app? Who manages costs, permissions, and networking? What breaks when a team goes rogue? This session lays out a practical operating model for enterprise-scale app deployment on Databricks: how to divide platform and team responsibilities, how to enforce governance without creating a bureaucratic chokepoint, and how to build an "app factory" foundation where identity, resources, and networking are standardized from day one. Whether you're managing five apps or five hundred, you'll leave with a clear framework for scaling without losing control.

title: From Bottlenecks to Breakthroughs: Empowering Comcast SLT with Self-Service Analytics
abstract: This initiative enables self-service analytics for Comcast’s Senior Leadership Team (SLT) by reducing IT dependency and establishing trusted “golden” KPIs. Using Databricks tools—AI/BI Dashboards, AgentBricks, Genie, and Apps—it provides intuitive interfaces for SLT users to explore data, generate insights, and build reports independently.The platform consolidates BI tools into a unified experience, accelerating insight generation and decision-making. Turnaround times improve from two weeks to about three days, delivering significant cost savings. Standardized KPIs reduce reliance on inconsistent ad hoc reporting, ensuring decisions are based on accurate, trusted data while improving efficiency and agility.

title: From Classic ML to Agentic Predictions
abstract: How DealSumm evolved from one-shot model outputs to an orchestrated system that retrieves evidence, reasons with structure, and verifies every claim - without burning compute.If you have ever built ML for contract analytics, you know the pattern: you ship a model that looks great on a benchmark, then reality shows up. One client has a different template. Another uses a jurisdiction-specific term. A third buries the key clause in an amendment scanned in 2008.At DealSumm, our goal is not just prediction - it is turning messy commercial lease documents into trustworthy, referenced data that teams can review, analyze, and act on inside a single AI-ready platform.The problem is that lease abstraction is not a single prediction problem. It is a chain of dependent mini-decisions: find the right clause, interpret it in context, map it into a client schema, and then prove you did not hallucinate.
description_raw: How DealSumm evolved from one-shot model outputs to an orchestrated system that retrieves evidence, reasons with structure, and verifies every claim - without burning compute.If you have ever built ML for contract analytics, you know the pattern: you ship a model that looks great on a benchmark, then reality shows up. One client has a different template. Another uses a jurisdiction-specific term. A third buries the key clause in an amendment scanned in 2008.At DealSumm, our goal is not just prediction - it is turning messy commercial lease documents into trustworthy, referenced data that teams can review, analyze, and act on inside a single AI-ready platform.The problem is that lease abstraction is not a single prediction problem. It is a chain of dependent mini-decisions: find the right clause, interpret it in context, map it into a client schema, and then prove you did not hallucinate.

title: From Cost Mystery to Cost Mastery COPA Airlines Journey to Lakehouse Efficiency
abstract: In an industry where profit margins are razor-thin and operational variables are global, COPA Airlines has emerged as a "shining star" of financial prosperity. By maintaining the world’s third strongest operating margin and leading the industry in on-time performance, COPA faced a high-stakes challenge: How do you scale world-class operations without exponential data costs?This presentation reveals the "Global Masterclass" in transitioning from legacy data silos to high-velocity lakehouse architecture.

speakers.name: David Garcia
speakers.bio: David Garcia is a versatile leader with over 20 years of experience, currently serving as the Director of Software and Data Engineering at Copa Airlines. He has deep expertise in modernizing data platforms and working with public cloud providers like Microsoft Azure, AWS, and Databricks. David has led and mentored teams of 60+ members across multiple countries, driving large-scale cloud migration, implementing DevOps CI/CD practices, and transforming legacy applications into microservices-based architectures. His achievements include creating a mobile center of competence, deploying omnichannel service layers, and modernizing big data architectures for real-time analytics.

title: From Dashboards to Data Products: MBC Shahid's App-Driven Revolution
abstract: MBC Shahid, the world’s leading Arabic streaming service serving tens of millions of users, has evolved beyond static dashboards to a curated Data Product culture. In this session, discover how we leveraged Databricks Apps, Model Serving and Unity Catalog to operationalize AI for non-technical teams. We will showcase two game-changing internal apps: “Enigma,” which empowers media buyers and leadership teams with predictive show performance, an actionable, interactive real-time voice of the customer and sentiment analysis experience, and a GenAI social media tool that automates viral reel creation by identifying high-impact frames and flagging sensitive content. Join us to learn how adopting a serverless, app-driven architecture is saving thousands of hours, merging the worlds of traditional tabular data, clickstream events, qualitative customer feedback and video data, all on Databricks Lakehouse, while ensuring governance, and delivering massive business value in record time.

title: From Dashboards to Decisioning: How We Rebuilt Marketing
abstract: Every enterprise marketing organization is trying to escape the same trap: fragmented data, backward-looking reporting, and AI pilots that never make it into daily operations. At Databricks, we decided not to layer AI on top of a broken foundation — we rebuilt the foundation first.In this session, we’ll walk through our end-to-end transformation from descriptive analytics to AI-driven insight and activation, powered entirely by the Lakehouse. We’ll show how consolidating marketing signals into a governed, scalable data architecture unlocked a new operating model: real-time intelligence accessible to non-technical marketers, AI-assisted decisioning embedded in workflows, and closed-loop activation across our CDP.We'll talk about the vision, what worked, what broke, and what changed in how marketing teams plan, execute, and measure impact.

speakers.name: Anoop Muraleedharan
speakers.bio: With over a decade of experience in data and analytics leadership, I have successfully built and scaled data infrastructure and analytics functions from the ground up. My expertise spans designing and implementing analytics data platforms, developing advanced analytics strategies, and applying machine learning models to improve effectiveness.

title: From Dashboards to Dialogue: Powering Feature Launches with a Databricks Genie for Gaming Studios
abstract: Launching new product features is exciting, but anticipating every data need in advance is difficult. Traditional dashboards often fall short, leaving stakeholders waiting for answers and slowing product discovery. In this session, we explore how Databricks Genie enables a conversational, self-service analytics experience that empowers users to ask questions, test hypotheses, and iterate faster. We demonstrate how we built a Genie-powered chatbot supporting multiple personas, giving product, operations, and business teams real-time access to trusted data without relying on pre-built dashboards. Attendees will learn how to structure data and semantic layers for flexible analytics, govern access across audiences, and design a system that evolves with product needs. This approach transforms analytics from a static reporting tool into a dynamic partner for product discovery, driving faster iteration, better decisions, and broader adoption of data-driven practices.

title: From Data Platform to App Platform: Building Production Apps on Lakebase
abstract: Your Lakehouse handles analytics beautifully. But the moment someone needs low-latency reads, transactional writes, or an app serving layer, you're stitching together external databases and sync pipelines. Lakebase offers a different path — this session shows you how to build on it.A Databricks MVP will walk through practical patterns for building production apps where Lakebase serves as the operational layer, fully integrated with Unity Catalog governance and Lakehouse analytics. We'll cover architecture patterns, the interplay between Lakebase and Databricks Apps, latency and scaling considerations, and the honest gaps practitioners need to plan around.You'll leave with architecture patterns, design decisions, and a realistic assessment of production readiness.

speakers.name: Doug MacWilliams
speakers.bio: Doug MacWilliams is a director in the Data Engineering and Analytics practice at West Monroe, a management technology consulting company. Doug has a passion for designing and building data solutions at the intersection of business and technology. He architects solutions that unlock data and deliver the needed insights in a pragmatic approach, optimized for long term maintenance and client skill sets. With his business background he understands what insights business leaders want and how to model data to represent the business process. Doug has a proven track record delivering many successful projects and leads teams focused on enterprise data strategies, building data platforms and analytics. He is a thought leader in data fabric methodology. Doug received his undergrad at University of Colorado in Business Operations and started his career in custom manufacturing and furniture making. He pivoted and moved into technology and data with a master’s in computer science focused on Computer Vision from DePaul University 15 years ago. He then worked in telecom and boutique BI consulting company before joining West Monroe 8+ years ago. Doug has had the opportunity to travel extensively around the world, especially during his Semester at Sea in college, and lived abroad after college. He now resides with his family in the Chicago Suburbs. He is a mentor through his alumni program and volunteers in his local community. https://www.westmonroe.com/our-team/doug-macwilliams

title: From Data Silos to Patient 360: Building Real-Time Cohort Analytics With Zero-ETL Graph Technology
abstract: Healthcare organizations generate 50+ petabytes annually, yet 80% remains siloed. This session demonstrates how PuppyGraph's zero-ETL graph layer transforms fragmented Databricks lakehouse data into unified patient 360 views without moving a single byte.The challenge: Identifying high-risk patients requires complex 50+ line SQL queries—slow and inaccessible.The solution: Graph technology excels at entity resolution. By linking attributes (addresses, providers) into networks, it matches records like "John A. Smith" and "J. Smith" through relationship patterns. We’ll compare traditional SQL joins against five-line graph traversals following the patient journey: Patient → Condition → Visit → Prescription.Graph queries mirror clinical thinking—connected journeys, not isolated tables.Key takeaways: Zero-ETL graph analytics on lakehouse data Integrating AI agent-ready graph infrastructure Unified governance through Unity Catalog

  • Unified governance through Unity Catalog

title: From Day-Old Data to Real-Time Retail: Modernizing CDC With Spark Declarative Pipelines and Lakeflow
abstract: Retail operations depend on accurate, timely data, but many organizations still rely on legacy change data capture approaches that behave like batch pipelines, creating delays, data inconsistencies, and operational risk.In this session, we share our real-world journey modernizing ingestion from a retail depot management platform.We will discuss the successes and pitfalls, and how we crafted and curated our new solution iteratively, with the use of new Databricks functionality such as AutoCDC and Lakeflow Connect. Transforming our complicated, cumbersome legacy solution into a streamlined and scalable workflow.

title: From Eight Hours to Eight Minutes: Automating A/B Test Analysis on Databricks
abstract: Mobile game studios run multiple experiments simultaneously, but daily insights are often delayed by manual data preparation, inconsistent methods and ad hoc notebooks. This presentation shows how SEGA HARDlight migrated from Tableau to Databricks and built an automated framework for experiment analysis. It ingests definitions and telemetry, applies uniform statistical models and publishes a daily dashboard with an LLM-generated summary and KPI diagnostics. Unity Catalog manages assets and lineage, while Lakeflow streamlines pipelines. MLflow ensures consistent experiment tracking, and AI/BI dashboards with Genie enable natural language exploration. The dashboard locks in results and decisions, creating an auditable record of truth. Early estimates show over eight hours saved weekly for the data team and roughly half a day saved per new query through automation, aiming to double testing capacity — while also boosting trust and consistency across experiments.

speakers.name: Sanjay Ashok
speakers.bio: Sanjay Ashok is a Solutions Architect at Databricks, acting as a technical advisor to digital-native and emerging enterprises across EMEA. He assists customers in evaluating, adopting, and scaling the Lakehouse platform for production AI and analytics workloads. With over 10 years’ experience in AI/ML, big data, and cloud architecture, he has worked across the US, India, and the UK, including at AWS, developing cloud-native data and ML solutions. At Databricks, he guides organisations from technical strategy to data-driven business transformation in gaming and fintech. His work covers GenAI applications, ML pipelines, real-time analytics, and self-serve BI dashboards. He holds an MS from the University at Buffalo.

speakers.name: Joel Dias
speakers.bio: Joel Dias is a Senior Data Scientist at SEGA HARDlight, where he builds scalable analytics systems for live mobile games. He specialises in automating complex data workflows, combining statistical methods with modern AI to accelerate decision-making across product, live operations, monetization and user acquisition functions. Joel has led the development of Databricks-based analytics and experimentation frameworks that reduce analysis time from hours to minutes, alongside LLM-powered insights and self-service tools that make data accessible beyond technical teams. At Data + AI Summit 2026, he shares how automation and AI can transform A/B testing into a fast, reliable, and scalable decision engine.

title: From Fragmented Data To AI-Driven Portfolio Impact With Digital Operations
abstract: Years of inorganic growth left TK Elevator (TKE) with data silos, inconsistent KPIs and limited ability to steer its services. By consolidating IoT, operational and financial data on a governed Azure Databricks Intelligence Platform — aligning enterprise semantics through the CDP Nexus program — TKE created a single, trusted data foundation from board to field ops. Medallion architecture ensures reliability and reuse, while Unity Catalog provides governance. On this foundation, TKE extended the same semantics and controls to unstructured data such as contracts and service logs, using LLMs to extract risks, obligations and renewal signals. Intelligent agents combine contract insights with operational performance data to support portfolio decisions with human‑in‑the‑loop validation. This session shows how Digital Ops, standardized KPIs and AI‑ready data products leveraging Genie enabled productivity gains and faster, more consistent decisions, setting context for the Session B deep dive.

speakers.bio: 09/2023- today: CDO TK Elevator 2017-2022: Global Head of ZEISS Digital Innovation Partners 2014-2016: Global Head of Strategy and M+A ZEISS Group 2007-2014: McKinsey & Company 2005-2007: German Ministry for the Environment (BMU)

title: From Fresh Data to Big Impact: Afresh and Lakebase Fuel Retail Innovation
abstract: In grocery retail, every hour on the shelf raises spoilage and margin risk. Afresh combats this with data-driven inventory insights delivered directly to fresh department order writers. But running these workloads on standard PostgreSQL caused contention, table locks, ETL failures and heavy maintenance, limiting innovation.This session details how Afresh migrated its mission-critical Postgres instance to Databricks Lakebase—a fully managed, serverless PostgreSQL engine natively integrated with the Databricks Lakehouse. Using Lakebase’s separation of storage and compute, Afresh eliminated locking and scaling bottlenecks. By orchestrating DBSQL http_request pipelines in dbt, Afresh replaced custom sync frameworks with automated, bidirectional data flows, cutting overhead and boosting reliability.Attendees will learn: How to migrate from self‑managed OLTP to serverless Lakebase Performance and reliability strategies Ways streamlined architecture drives efficiency and innovation

Using Lakebase’s separation of storage and compute, Afresh eliminated locking and scaling bottlenecks. By orchestrating DBSQL http_request pipelines in dbt, Afresh replaced custom sync frameworks with automated, bidirectional data flows, cutting overhead and boosting reliability.Attendees will learn:

  • How to migrate from self‑managed OLTP to serverless Lakebase
  • Performance and reliability strategies
  • Ways streamlined architecture drives efficiency and innovation

title: From Job Clusters to DBSQL Serverless: How GetYourGuide Improved DBT Pipelines
abstract: At GetYourGuide, we used to run ETL transformations with an internal tool called Rivulus on top of Databricks job clusters. When we standardized on dbt-databricks, we migrated these workloads to DBSQL Serverless and had to rethink how we design and operate our pipelines. We'll walk you through what changed and what we wished to know before we started.What changed for us: Pipelines run ~60% faster and costs dropped ~20% Time to production for new models went from days to minutes, backfills from weeks to hours ~4 months of annual overhead from DBR version upgrades disappeared 100% of our core models are now automatically documented with AI Finance and marketplace teams now rely on fresher data with less friction Attendees will leave with: A clear view of how the migration to DBSQL Serverless was performed How we turned curve balls into wins along the way Concrete steps to apply similar ideas in their own dbt-databricks setups, including using ai_query for automated model documentation
description_raw:
At GetYourGuide, we used to run ETL transformations with an internal tool called Rivulus on top of Databricks job clusters. When we standardized on dbt-databricks, we migrated these workloads to DBSQL Serverless and had to rethink how we design and operate our pipelines. We'll walk you through what changed and what we wished to know before we started.What changed for us:

  • Pipelines run ~60% faster and costs dropped ~20%
  • Time to production for new models went from days to minutes, backfills from weeks to hours
  • ~4 months of annual overhead from DBR version upgrades disappeared
  • 100% of our core models are now automatically documented with AI
  • Finance and marketplace teams now rely on fresher data with less friction

Attendees will leave with:

  • A clear view of how the migration to DBSQL Serverless was performed
  • How we turned curve balls into wins along the way
  • Concrete steps to apply similar ideas in their own dbt-databricks setups, including using ai_query for automated model documentation

speakers.name: Giovanni Corsetti Silva
speakers.bio: Giovanni Corsetti is a Data Engineer at GetYourGuide, where he focuses on building and evolving large-scale data platform foundations, with a strong emphasis on reliability, scalability, and data governance, including GDPR-related challenges in Europe. As a GetYourGuide user himself, he has explored dozens of countries, giving him a personal connection to the product he helps build. He is also an active contributor to open-source projects, with a strong interest in building and sharing practical, scalable data tooling with the broader engineering community.

title: From Legacy to Lakehouse: Proven Best Practices From Databricks and McKinsey on Modernization
abstract: As enterprises race to operationalize AI at scale, legacy data warehouses have become a critical bottleneck. In this session, Databricks and McKinsey share proven, real-world best practices for modernizing data platforms to unlock analytics, GenAI and agentic workloads with speed, governance and cost efficiency. Drawing from large scale transformations including McKinsey’s Nebula platform, we outline a pragmatic modernization blueprint that balances risk, payback period and business impact. Attendees will learn how to migrate from brittle legacy architectures to an open lakehouse and federated data mesh, reduce total cost of ownership by up to 40%, accelerate time to insight and enable secure self-service analytics and AI across the enterprise. This talk delivers concrete lessons for executives and practitioners navigating modernization in the AI era.

title: From Molecule to Market: How Life Sciences Companies are Compressing the Data-to-Decision Curve with Databricks
abstract: The life sciences industry sits at an extraordinary inflection point. The same AI capabilities that are reshaping drug discovery are now being applied to clinical trial design, real-world evidence generation, digital twin simulation and commercial launch strategy — and the organizations moving fastest are those who've unified these workloads on a single data intelligence platform. In this session, we take a sweeping look at how Databricks is powering the full life sciences value chain: accelerating target identification and molecular screening in R&D, enabling adaptive trial designs with richer patient data, building synthetic cohorts and digital twins that reduce time and cost, and equipping commercial teams with the AI-driven insights needed to reach the right patients faster. We'll explore the common thread running through each of these use cases — a governed, interoperable data foundation — and make the case that the most durable competitive advantage in life sciences today is not any single model or dataset, but the platform on which all of them live.

title: From Oracle Cloud to a Governed Lakehouse: Real-Time Streaming With Spark Declarative Pipelines
abstract: Flutter SEA invested heavily in OCI, but platform limits—high costs, low observability and operational complexity—became blockers for real‑time engagement, event processing and fast delivery. The previous Spark Structured Streaming architecture used custom code with minimal monitoring, no standardized data quality and limited governance, causing slow iteration and high maintenance.This session shares the migration to a governed, observable Databricks Lakehouse using Spark Declarative Pipelines and Delta Lake, built by a “triforce” team: Flutter SEA, Databricks PS and Reply. We’ll cover architectural choices like single‑plex vs. multi‑plex pipelines and their impact on scalability, cost and simplicity, plus data quality, end‑to‑end monitoring, KPIs - 40% savings and 96% bronze-to-silver performance improvements, lessons learned and best practices for modernizing streaming systems to reduce cost, improve governance and enable AI‑driven real‑time engagement.

He is currently the Data Lakehouse & Transformation Senior Manager at Flutter Southern Europe & Africa and has a proven track record in designing and delivering enterprise-scale data platforms, covering everything from architecture to governance, data quality, cataloguing, lineage and privacy compliance.

title: From Petabytes to Proactive Defense: Powering an AI Swarm with Databricks
abstract: As AI accelerates cyber offense in a post-Mythos world, the constraint in security operations is no longer data—it’s the speed of the OODA loop. At Arctic Wolf, we process trillions of events daily across 10,000+ organizations, where delayed access to data means delayed defense. We re-architected our platform on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform—combining Delta Lake, Unity Catalog, Liquid Clustering, and AI/Genie—to enable real-time, agentic security operations at petabyte scale. Liquid Clustering and Predictive Optimization eliminated data skew and costly acceleration layers, reducing file counts by 50%, improving freshness from hours to minutes, and accelerating 90-day queries by up to 8× (51s → 6.6s). Architecture: Multi-PB Delta Lake → Liquid Clustering + Predictive Optimization → GOLD telemetry → Genie-orchestrated Swarm of Experts. Outcomes: sub-second to seconds-level queries, 10–100x alert reduction, >70% precision gains, and earlier detection of cross-tenant attack campaigns. Attendees will learn how to unify data + AI to compress the OODA loop, power agentic workflows, and transform petabyte-scale telemetry into proactive defense.

We re-architected our platform on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform—combining Delta Lake, Unity Catalog, Liquid Clustering, and AI/Genie—to enable real-time, agentic security operations at petabyte scale. Liquid Clustering and Predictive Optimization eliminated data skew and costly acceleration layers, reducing file counts by 50%, improving freshness from hours to minutes, and accelerating 90-day queries by up to 8× (51s → 6.6s).

Architecture: Multi-PB Delta Lake → Liquid Clustering + Predictive Optimization → GOLD telemetry → Genie-orchestrated Swarm of Experts.

speakers.name: Justin Lai
speakers.bio: Justin Lai is a Data Architect at Arctic Wolf, where he designs scalable data and AI platforms to support cybersecurity operations and analytics. He works with engineering, product, and data teams to build systems that power the Arctic Wolf Data Lake, enabling efficient access for both analytics and AI use cases. With a background in cloud infrastructure and deep expertise in Databricks, Justin focuses on building production-grade solutions that support real-time processing, machine learning workflows, and AI model deployment. He has spent the past five years helping teams implement end-to-end data and AI architectures with an emphasis on scalability, performance, and reliability.

title: From Power BI DAX to Metric View KPIs: How Mercedes-Benz Korea Built “Talk to Your Data” at Scale
abstract: Mercedes‑Benz Korea runs its business on a rich set of sales and steering KPIs maintained in Power BI semantic models and a central Master KPI catalog. Over time, this BI‑centric implementation made it hard to reuse KPI logic for new AI experiences and across platforms. To enable “Talk to Your Data” on top of governed KPIs, Mercedes‑Benz Korea partnered with Databricks to move KPI logic into the Databricks Lakehouse and Unity Catalog. In this session, we show how we developed an automated DAX‑to‑SQL transpiler that converts existing DAX measures into Unity Catalog Metric Views, while preserving semantic parity with legacy reports. We’ll walk through the architecture, how we use the Master KPI List as the semantic contract, and how we tuned Databricks Genie with KPI‑aware metadata and instructions. Finally, we’ll share lessons from making AI agents production‑ready—from handling complex DAX patterns to rolling out self‑service “talk to your KPIs” capabilities across markets.
description_raw: Mercedes‑Benz Korea runs its business on a rich set of sales and steering KPIs maintained in Power BI semantic models and a central Master KPI catalog. Over time, this BI‑centric implementation made it hard to reuse KPI logic for new AI experiences and across platforms. To enable “Talk to Your Data” on top of governed KPIs, Mercedes‑Benz Korea partnered with Databricks to move KPI logic into the Databricks Lakehouse and Unity Catalog. In this session, we show how we developed an automated DAX‑to‑SQL transpiler that converts existing DAX measures into Unity Catalog Metric Views, while preserving semantic parity with legacy reports. We’ll walk through the architecture, how we use the Master KPI List as the semantic contract, and how we tuned Databricks Genie with KPI‑aware metadata and instructions. Finally, we’ll share lessons from making AI agents production‑ready—from handling complex DAX patterns to rolling out self‑service “talk to your KPIs” capabilities across markets.

speakers.name: Sai Yang
speakers.bio: Sai Yang is a Solutions Architect for the Automotive Manufacturing sector at Databricks. With 6 years of experience in the data & AI domain, Sai has strong expertise in Data Science, Machine Learning, AI, and Data Governance. At Databricks, he has led multiple large-scale platform migration projects and AI use cases for global leading automotive companies. These initiatives enabled the organizations to build and implement scalable analytics and AI solutions with unified governance on Databricks, helping them become the industry leaders in data and AI. Before joining Databricks, Sai worked in technology consulting, where he designed, developed, and deployed reliable machine learning systems in production for Fortune Global 500 companies.

speakers.name: Fares Kamal
speakers.job_title: Chief Data & AI Officer, MB Korea
speakers.bio: Fares Kamal is the Chief Information Officer at Mercedes-Benz Korea and Mercedes-Benz Group Services Philippines, beginning in November 2020, where the focus is on transforming legacy IT into agile, product-oriented teams to enhance business value. Prior to this role, Fares Kamal held various leadership positions at Mercedes-Benz AG from April 2014 to October 2020, including team lead for IT in retail, where significant contributions included standardizing global IT governance and introducing monetization strategies. Previous experience includes managing major transformation programs in the Russian market, overseeing the international rollout of wholesale systems in Europe, and driving strategic IT projects in Eastern Europe, demonstrating a strong commitment to innovation and operational excellence throughout a career with Mercedes-Benz that began in 2003.

title: From Raw Signals to Real-Time Insights: Building an End-to-End IoT Pipeline With Zerobus
abstract: In the world of IoT, every additional hop in your data pipeline is a tax on speed, a risk to governance and a driver of unnecessary cost. To solve this problem, we developed DataLink, an internal use wireless data collection platform that streams high fidelity data directly into Unity Catalog at near real-time speeds. With Zerobus, we built an IoT ingestion pipeline that delivers fast access and robust governance at a fraction of the traditional cost and complexity. This architecture transforms raw lab data from across the globe into structured insights in near real time. In this presentation, we will take a deep dive into our architectural strategy for connecting IoT devices to Zerobus endpoints and setting up ingestion. We will also discuss how we leveraged alerts, dashboards, and automated failure analysis to streamline workflows for engineering teams. Finally, we will walk through a live demo showcasing the end-to-end pipeline from data generation to consumption.

speakers.name: Kirat Mokha
speakers.bio: Kirat Mokha is a Machine Learning Engineer on Milwaukee Tool’s Analytics, Algorithms, and AI Applications (AAA) team. He earned his bachelor’s in Electrical Engineering and master’s in Machine Learning from Milwaukee School of Engineering in Wisconsin. Kirat began his career at Milwaukee Tool as an Electrical Engineering intern, where he helped build standardized datalogging platforms for new product development. That early exposure sparked a lasting interest in data and algorithms, eventually leading him into ML engineering. Today, his work spans MLOps, data pipelines, Databricks, and IoT systems that bring real-time engineering data into analytics and ML workflows.

speakers.name: Maxwell Merget
speakers.bio: Max is a Sr. Manager of Machine Learning Engineering at Milwaukee Tool, where he leads a cross-functional team spanning firmware, electronics, data engineering, and applied AI. His work focuses on transforming data into user-focused solutions through scalable systems that connect product development, testing, and manufacturing on modern platforms like Databricks. His team has delivered customer-facing innovations such as AUTOSTOP and TORQUE-SENSE, along with internal solutions that enhance how Milwaukee Tool designs and manufactures industry-leading products.

title: From Reactive to Proactive Mobility Insights: Caltrans’ Agentic Solution on a Unified Lakehouse
abstract: Caltrans is transforming Traffic Mobility Insights (TMI) into an agent-forward transportation AI platform built on the Databricks lakehouse and lakebase. At its core is an agentic architecture that unifies structured telemetry, geospatial data, and unstructured documents within a governed Delta foundation—eliminating fragmented pipelines and legacy database silos. Lakebase enables high-performance operational lookups and spatial workloads, while the lakehouse provides scalable analytics, model training, and data sharing. A centralized AI switchboard—powered by Databricks Model Serving and MLOps—routes intent across domain agents, enforces policy guardrails, orchestrates tools, and manages state and evidence. This is decision intelligence, not a chatbot. The future state evolves to a federated, Agent-as-a-Product model, where interoperable agents collaborate under a centralized AI control plane—delivering secure, explainable, and proactive transportation management at statewide scale.

speakers.name: Ajali Sen
speakers.bio: Ajali is a Managing Director at Accenture and leads Public Sector Data and GenAI Solutions for the West US Region. She has 20 years of experience in complex data and analytics transformation coupled with business process change for large clients within state, private sector. She is passionate about using technology to make government services more efficient, accessible, and equitable. Her work focuses on leveraging AI and analytics to enhance public health, education, and social programs. Through strategic insight and human-centered design, she helps states unlock the full potential of their data to serve communities better and create lasting impact. Ajali also leads the Accenture data quality and analytics implementation team for CDPH.

speakers.name: Duper Tong
speakers.bio: Duper Tong has over 30 years of experience in transportation planning, design, traffic safety, electrical systems, and traffic operations across the public and private sectors, including 26 years with Caltrans. He currently serves as Chief of the Division of Traffic Operations, leading statewide initiatives in Generative AI applications for Traffic Mobility and Road Safety Insights, the Middle Mile Broadband Network, the Managed Lanes Program, and Intelligent Transportation Systems. Previously, he was District 10 Deputy District Director of Traffic Operations, overseeing multidisciplinary teams and safety and mobility programs. He began his Caltrans career in the Traffic Investigation and TASAS branches and later served as District 10 Traffic Safety Branch Chief, HQ Office Chief in Traffic Operations, and in the Division of Design. Duper is active with TRB and AASHTO committees. He is a licensed PE (Civil, Traffic Engineering) and holds BS and MS degrees from CSU Sacramento.

title: From Repetition to Reuse: The Evolution of Apache Spark™ Declarative Pipelines
abstract: Learn how to build batch and streaming pipelines faster while improving correctness and reducing operational complexity with Apache Spark™ Declarative Pipelines.Production Spark pipelines often require extensive orchestration code for dependency management, checkpointing, retries, and execution ordering—surrounding a relatively small amount of transformation logic. As pipelines scale, this scaffolding becomes increasingly difficult to maintain and evolve.Introduced in Spark 4.1, Spark Declarative Pipelines (SDP) shifts this model by allowing developers to declare datasets and transformations while Spark constructs and manages the execution plan. By separating what a pipeline does from how it runs, SDP reduces boilerplate and accelerates time to production.We’ll examine the architectural foundations of declarative development in Spark and how SDP handles dependency resolution, parallelization, checkpoint coordination, and failure recovery. We’ll also cover incremental processing and emerging testing patterns for declarative pipelines.

We’ll also cover incremental processing and emerging testing patterns for declarative pipelines.

speakers.name: Andreas Neumann
speakers.bio: With a career defined by "massive scale," Andreas Neumann currently serves as the Technical Lead for Declarative Pipelines at Databricks. From his early work at Yahoo! and IBM to his time at Google and his journey as the founder of Cask Data, Andreas has spent decades solving the data industry's toughest architectural puzzles. Beyond his corporate leadership, he is a dedicated advocate for open-source software, having mentored several Apache projects from incubation to maturity. Andreas holds a PhD in Computer Science for his pioneering research in document querying.

title: From Search to SQL: How Glean Agents Tap Databricks Genie for Everyday Analytics
abstract: Modern enterprises need both the “what” from governed metrics and the “why” from surrounding context. We’ll show how customers use Glean Agents with Databricks AI/BI Genie and SQL actions to turn natural‑language questions into live warehouse queries that any employee can run. A single agent can route a prompt to Genie, execute the generated SQL on Databricks, then combine resulting tables with unstructured context from email, docs, tickets, and Wikis in one answer, all while respecting app‑level permissions. Attendees will leave with concrete architecture and prompt patterns they can use to scale self‑serve analytics without adding more load on data teams. Audience takeaways: Pattern for building Databricks-powered agents that run live, governed queries without extra indexing Examples of combining Databricks results with unstructured work knowledge to deliver a complete story Practical guidance on permissions, debugging Genie output, and tuning Genie spaces for enterprise copilots

Modern enterprises need both the “what” from governed metrics and the “why” from surrounding context. A single agent can route a prompt to Genie, execute the generated SQL on Databricks, then combine resulting tables with unstructured context from email, docs, tickets, and Wikis in one answer, all while respecting app‑level permissions. Audience takeaways:

  • Pattern for building Databricks-powered agents that run live, governed queries without extra indexing
  • Examples of combining Databricks results with unstructured work knowledge to deliver a complete story
  • Practical guidance on permissions, debugging Genie output, and tuning Genie spaces for enterprise copilots

speakers.name: Joel McKelvey
speakers.bio: Joel McKelvey is VP of Product and Solutions Marketing at Glean, where he helps organizations turn AI initiatives into practical, metrics-driven business transformation. He brings more than 30 years of experience across engineering, marketing, and strategy, with deep expertise in AI, machine learning, analytics, and data architectures. His work focuses on how enterprises move from experimentation to execution using AI agents, enterprise search, and trusted data foundations to improve productivity, decision-making, and customer and employee experiences.

title: From SIEM to Lakehouse: A Practical Path to an AI-powered SOC
abstract: Security teams are hitting the limits of traditional SIEM architecture. Rising ingestion costs, fragmented telemetry, and brittle detections force tradeoffs between visibility and budget.This session outlines a practical, phased path from SIEM-centric operations to lakehouse-driven security analytics. We will explain how SOC teams have cut costs and boosted performance by offloading telemetry from legacy SIEMs to Databricks, without breaking detections. We’ll also show how security teams run and correlate detections natively across their data sources, and use AI agents to automate detection engineering, tuning, and triage while reducing noise and vendor lock-in.Attendees will leave with a clear roadmap to building a modern, AI-driven SOC on Databricks, and how they can unlock broader detection coverage, lower costs at scale, improved triage efficiency, and a realistic path to full SIEM replacement.

speakers.name: Alan Mazankiewicz
speakers.bio: Alan is Senior Solutions Architect at Databricks in Germany. At Databricks he works with various cybersecurity customers and partners. He regularly speaks publicly about the intersection of cybersecurity, data and AI.

title: From Space to Scale: What to do (and not do) for Secure, Fast, Production AI/BI
abstract: Learn the essential, Databricks AI FDE -tested playbook for deploying production Genie. Start by scoping topic-focused Spaces that leverage Unity Catalog and knowledge-store metadata along with parameterized example SQL rather than free-text queries. This approach drives both clarity and speed. Before scaling out, establish pragmatic, business-centric benchmarks using trusted assets and value dictionaries to measure what truly matters. Integrate Genie into multi-agent workflows through the Conversation API, Teams, and the integration into Databricks’ Agent Framework. Operationalize at scale with pre-joined and trimmed tables, audit-driven monitoring, and a lightweight review cadence.We'll share proven do's and don'ts, common pitfalls to avoid, and a reusable checklist to accelerate your time-to-value, all grounded in Unity Catalog metadata and real-world enterprise deployments.

title: From Two Hours to Two Minutes: Panasonic’s Global Scale Engineering With the Data Intelligence Platform
abstract: Panasonic’s global footprint—from EV batteries to smart mobility—demands real-time precision. To fulfill the vision of "making tomorrow better," Panasonic transformed its data backbone into a unified Lakehouse. This session explores the engineering feat of unlocking SAP, Salesforce and SharePoint data, slashing SAP data latency from two hours to just two minutes. Learn how Panasonic is scaling from a single use case to an enterprise-wide strategy, deploying Lakeflow Connect to unify ingestion across multiple data sources and AI Query to structure unstructured data. We’ll also review strategic deployment of Auto Loader and Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines for high-scale CDC and Unity Catalog for cross-domain lineage and governance. And hear how automation and Lakeflow Jobs have eliminated overhead and empowered 10+ global teams. Finally, we’ll preview our roadmap for AI Agents, showing how a robust engineering foundation enables the next generation of autonomous intelligence.

Panasonic’s global footprint—from EV batteries to smart mobility—demands real-time precision. To fulfill the vision of "making tomorrow better," Panasonic transformed its data backbone into a unified Lakehouse. Learn how Panasonic is scaling from a single use case to an enterprise-wide strategy, deploying Lakeflow Connect to unify ingestion across multiple data sources and AI Query to structure unstructured data.

We’ll also review strategic deployment of Auto Loader and Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines for high-scale CDC and Unity Catalog for cross-domain lineage and governance. And hear how automation and Lakeflow Jobs have eliminated overhead and empowered 10+ global teams.

Finally, we’ll preview our roadmap for AI Agents, showing how a robust engineering foundation enables the next generation of autonomous intelligence.

speakers.name: Brendan Byrne
speakers.bio: Brendan Byrne is a Data Engineer at Panasonic of North America, specializing in modern data architectures built on the Databricks Lakehouse platform. He played a key role in transforming legacy full‑load pipelines into efficient Change Data Capture solutions, reducing runtimes from hours to minutes while significantly improving stability and manageability. His work leverages Databricks Lakeflow Declarative Pipelines, Lakeflow orchestration, and Auto Loader to deliver scalable, reliable data solutions. He continues to focus on expanding Panasonic’s data catalog and platform capabilities to support enterprise‑scale analytics and innovation.

speakers.name: Priyanka Kasliwal
speakers.bio: Priyanka is a Field Engineering professional at Databricks, specializing in architecting high-scale solutions within the Lakehouse ecosystem. With her system design and architecture background, she focuses on technical enablement—helping organizations move beyond infrastructure to build functional, intelligence-driven solutions. She is passionate about solving engineering hurdles in real-time ingestion and data lifecycle management. With architectural expertise across Azure, AWS, and GCP, Priyanka excels at navigating the shift from legacy silos to unified platforms plus bridges the gap between complex engineering and business ROI, accelerating time-to-value for production-grade innovation.

title: From Unstructured Data to Structured Insights: How YipitData Scales Data Enrichment with AI Agents
abstract: YipitData analyzes billions of unstructured data points at petabyte scale to deliver high fidelity insights to institutional investors and Fortune 500 companies. With 100s of heterogeneous data sources, regex, classic ML and NLP techniques never met our accuracy hurdle, limiting our product breadth for years.In this session, we reframe entity resolution as an agentic problem and share our production-grade enrichment platform built on Apache Spark™, Agent Bricks, Vector Search and Lakebase. This AI-native architecture continuously discovers and tags data at 90%–95% accuracy, reliably covering 60,000+ companies—a 20x improvement.Data/ML leaders, engineers and practitioners will learn: Modular, pipeline design applicable to any classification scenario Batch inference patterns in Spark that streamlines infrastructure Techniques for continuous, low maintenance entity discovery Join us and leave with a blueprint to turn enrichment bottlenecks into self-improving, AI pipelines.

  • Techniques for continuous, low maintenance entity discovery

speakers.name: Anup Segu
speakers.bio: Anup Segu is Chief Architect at YipitData, a leading alternative data provider to institutional investors and corporations. He has built and led YipitData's Data Engineering and Platform Engineering organizations, architected its petabyte-scale data and AI platform, and driven company-wide adoption of lakehouse and agentic AI. Earlier, Anup worked in investment banking at Citigroup and studied at Indiana University.

speakers.name: Edward Goo
speakers.bio: Edward Goo is the Head of Data Engineering at YipitData, a leader in the alternative data industry providing insights to investment firms and Fortune 500 companies. He leads the data engineering organization in building large-scale data pipelines that transform messy, unstructured datasets into high-fidelity insights. His team is focused on centralizing internal data and developing a company master dataset powering entity resolution at scale. Over his eight-year tenure, Edward has architected systems processing billions of data points at a petabyte scale. He holds a degree from Syracuse University and previously worked in investment funds.

title: From Wellhead to Insight: Halliburton’s Real‑Time Data Sharing with Chevron
abstract: How can raw well data be made available more quickly and more consistently across organizations, without relying on manual data movement? In this joint session, Halliburton and Chevron showcase a breakthrough in energy data collaboration. Halliburton shares how they can publish curated well completion and treatment datasets directly into Chevron’s environment using Delta Sharing, enabling more timely visibility into well performance, deferment drivers and optimization opportunities. We’ll walk through the technical architecture that allows Halliburton and Chevron to collaborate securely across Azure and AWS clouds without the latency associated with traditional ETL. We will cover the practical lessons learned during implementation — including establishing robust data contracts and security considerations, and discuss the ROI of Delta Sharing. Attendees will leave with a practical, repeatable understanding on how to deliver trusted, live oilfield data to support more efficient analytics and production decisions, with zero‑copy data sharing.

speakers.name: Shahab Jamali
speakers.bio: As Chief Data Scientist at Halliburton, Shahab Jamali Ghare Tape bridges the gap between complex engineering data and executive-level insights. Leading a team at the intersection of ML, AI, and GenAI, he specializes in creating "decision-ready" data environments. At the 2026 Data + AI Summit, he is showcasing a breakthrough in energy industry collaboration, demonstrating how Halliburton and Chevron use Delta Sharing to eliminate ETL latency and share live well-performance data across cloud boundaries. When he isn't optimizing oilfield datasets, Shahab can be found on the soccer field or playing the Setar.

speakers.name: Kshitij Dawar
speakers.bio: Kshitij Dawar is a Lead Data Architect and Engineer at Chevron, where he designs enterprise‑scale, AI‑ready data platforms across subsurface and wells domains. His work focuses on cloud‑native lakehouse architectures, real‑time data products, master data standardization, and applied Generative AI for extracting insights from structured and unstructured data. With a Ph.D. in Petroleum and Natural Gas Engineering and a strong foundation in machine learning, reinforcement learning, and large‑scale data engineering, Kshitij bridges advanced research with production‑grade AI systems. He is also Vice Chair of the SPE Gulf Coast Section Data Analytics Study Group and an active mentor and speaker on AI, data architecture, and analytics in energy.

title: Game Day at Global Scale: How PepsiCo Foods Uses AI to Feed the World Cup
abstract: FIFA World Cup comes to the Bay Area this June. 70,000+ fans at Levi’s Stadium will consume millions of servings of Frito-Lay products, and millions more will be watching across North America. PepsiCo Foods doesn’t get a second chance to stock the right SKUs, in the right quantities, at the right locations. Get it wrong and you have bare shelves. This session shows how PepsiCo Foods runs 125 million demand forecast combinations weekly on Databricks, achieving 80%+ DSD accuracy while reducing compute cost 60% after migrating from on-prem solution. We’ll demo how supply planners model what-if scenarios in real time using Databricks Apps and Lakebase, query demand drivers through Genie, and how agentic AI is beginning to automate anomalous demand detection across Sales, Marketing, Finance and Supply Chain. You’ll learn: How PepsiCo runs driver-based forecasting at 125M combinations/week on Databricks How Lakebase and Apps enable real-time what-if scenario planning
description_raw:
FIFA World Cup comes to the Bay Area this June. 70,000+ fans at Levi’s Stadium will consume millions of servings of Frito-Lay products, and millions more will be watching across North America. PepsiCo Foods doesn’t get a second chance to stock the right SKUs, in the right quantities, at the right locations. Get it wrong and you have bare shelves.

This session shows how PepsiCo Foods runs 125 million demand forecast combinations weekly on Databricks, achieving 80%+ DSD accuracy while reducing compute cost 60% after migrating from on-prem solution. We’ll demo how supply planners model what-if scenarios in real time using Databricks Apps and Lakebase, query demand drivers through Genie, and how agentic AI is beginning to automate anomalous demand detection across Sales, Marketing, Finance and Supply Chain.

You’ll learn:

  • How PepsiCo runs driver-based forecasting at 125M combinations/week on Databricks
  • How Lakebase and Apps enable real-time what-if scenario planning

speakers.name: Michael Cleavinger
speakers.job_title: VP Global Data Science and Analytics
speakers.bio: Michael Cleavinger is Vice President of Data Science at PepsiCo, leading a global organization that builds AI, machine learning, and decision science capabilities across marketing, planning, supply chain, consumer, commercial, and finance. With experience across retail and CPG, he focuses on turning complex, high-volume data into scalable strategies that drive growth, improve efficiency, and enable better decisions. He has led enterprise forecasting and optimization platforms that reduce cost, increase reuse, and deliver business impact at global scale.

title: Genie at scale: Best practices for enterprise rollout
abstract: As organizations adopt agentic AI at scale, getting access, governance, and deployment right is more critical than ever. Rolling out Genie and AI/BI across thousands of users demands clear policies for user access, data governance, and content distribution to ensure secure, consistent, and scalable adoption—including on mobile devices under enterprise policies.We’ll walk through best practices for deploying Genie and AI/BI at scale, helping you move from isolated use cases to organization-wide impact.Topics include:- Onboarding and provisioning users efficiently across business teams- Managing data access and permissions to ensure secure, governed usage- Publishing dashboards and Genie experiences for maximum reach- Enabling secure mobile access aligned with enterprise device and security policies- Monitoring usage, manage sharing, and identify opportunities to drive adoption- Deployment patterns and best practices for scaling adoption across the enterprise

speakers.name: Dillon Bostwick
speakers.bio: Dillon Bostwick is a Solutions Architect at Databricks, where he’s spent the last five years advising customers ranging from startups to Fortune 500 enterprises. He currently helps lead a team of field ambassadors for streaming products and is interested in improving industry awareness of effective streaming patterns for data integration and production machine learning. He previously worked as a product engineer in infrastructure automation.

speakers.name: Austin Green
speakers.bio: Austin Green is a Staff Software Engineer at Databricks, focused on discovery and sharing surfaces.

title: Genie Code for data science: Work at the speed of your ideas
abstract: What if you could go from a raw dataset to a trained model in a single conversation?Data scientists spend a lot of time on the repetitive parts of the lifecycle: finding data, wrangling it, writing features, tuning hyperparameters, and not enough time on the creative work that actually moves the needle. Genie Code is an autonomous AI partner built into Databricks that changes the equation. Tell it what you want to explore, and it writes the code, runs the analysis, and iterates with you.In this session, we’ll walk through how to apply Genie Code across the data science lifecycle. We’ll cover:- How to use Genie Code for exploratory data analysis and feature discovery- How to build and iterate on machine learning models with less manual effort- When to steer and when to let Genie Code drive- Best practices for working effectively with Genie Code as a data science partner

speakers.name: Gal Oshri
speakers.bio: Gal has been building AI products for the past decade. At Databricks, he works on the Databricks Assistant, helping make it a useful tool for all users across the platform. Previously, he worked on machine learning products at AWS (SageMaker Training), Google (TensorFlow), and Microsoft (ML.NET), contributing to tools used by developers and data scientists around the world.

title: Genie is a game changer
abstract: We would like to share our vision, strategy and learnings with Databricks Genie. You want to know something, you ask, you get an answer. By enabling our users to interact with our data, AkzoNobel can anticipate developments in Supply Chain Management, Finance and Service Delivery more quickly driving impact. This requires a new form of cooperation between business and IT. The responsibilities will be shared and clearly defined as IT will be in control to safeguard standards and the business will provide semantics. For the users there is also a change to be managed, most will think in reports when asking questions to Genie while DeepSearch allows much more. Further innovation with Genie DeepSearch allows us to bring insights to our management in the form of podcasts or videos. Genie is a gamechanger when done right. It allows for completely different ways of interacting with insights but also offers opportunities for IT to simplify the Analytics landscape.

title: Genie, Who Broke My Cluster? Using AI Functions, APIs and Genie Code to understand your workload
abstract: Have you ever wondered what happened in your Databricks environment? Have you spent time looking through logs and audit tables? Join us for this live demo of using Genie code to gain insights into your system. If Genie is new to you, you'll learn how easy it is to get started. If you are already a Genie expert, you'll discover ways to improve and enhance your experience using APIs, joins, AI Functions, and more. Take the guesswork out of auditing by setting up your own Genie with the real-world, practical examples shown in this session.

title: Get Started Ingesting Data with Lakeflow Connect
abstract: Thousands of organizations use Lakeflow Connect to turn raw data into actionable intelligence. In this session, we’ll show you how to streamline your ingestion strategy and accelerate your path to value.We’ll cover: Expanding the 80+ connector ecosystem: Explore what’s new across databases (e.g., Oracle, Postgres, SQL Server), applications (e.g., Salesforce, Workday, ServiceNow, Google Analytics), file sources (e.g., SharePoint, Google Drive), and message buses (e.g., Kafka, RabbitMQ). Introducing open-source tools: Learn how to build, scale, and even open source your own connectors using the same robust framework. Optimizing cost and performance: Discover how to slash costs and complexity by eliminating architecture hops with Zerobus Ingest. Take advantage of new cost and performance levers. And learn what we’re building behind the scenes to help. Join us and learn how to stop battling pipelines and start building value.

title: Getting started with AI Governance on Databricks
abstract: As AI agents move from prototypes to production, a new architectural layer is emerging: the tool gateway. Models alone aren't enough — agents need secure, real-time access to data, APIs, and enterprise systems. Integrating these tools consistently and safely has become one of the biggest bottlenecks to adoption.In this session, we explore how Model Context Protocol (MCP) and tool gateways redefine how agents interact with the world. MCP standardizes how agents discover and use tools dynamically, while AI Gateway acts as the enterprise control plane to govern access, manage credentials, and monitor usage across every tool and model. Together, they replace fragmented APIs with a unified, governed interface — letting agents securely connect to managed, external, and custom MCP servers and select tools at runtime.Through real architectures, you'll see how to design agent systems that are flexible, interoperable, and production-ready across internal data and third-party systems.

title: Getting the Most out of AI in BI: From Dashboard Creation to Intelligent Insights
abstract: AI is rapidly reshaping business intelligence but for many teams, it’s still unclear how to move beyond isolated features to real, end-to-end impact.In Databricks, AI is embedded across the entire BI workflow. From using Genie Code to build and maintain dashboards, to applying AI-driven capabilities like forecasting and key driver analysis, to enabling conversational exploration directly within dashboards, AI becomes part of how analytics is created, delivered, and used.This shift allows teams to move faster, reduce manual work, and unlock deeper insights without adding complexity.In this session, we’ll walk through how to apply AI across the full BI lifecycle:- How to use Genie Code to accelerate dashboard creation and maintenance- How to leverage built-in AI features such as forecasting and intelligent insights- How to embed Genie directly into dashboards for conversational exploration- Best practices for combining AI and BI to drive adoption and real business impact

title: Goldman Sach's Fully-Interoperable Lakehouse With Iceberg
abstract: Goldman Sach's Lakehouse platform with Iceberg, allows zero-copy data sharing and iceberg rest catalog integration across vendor engines, and seamlessly manages trust, entitlements and governance across these engines. This session will detail Goldman Sachs' journey in developing an "Iceberg-first" data platform, leveraging Lakehouse architecture and will cover the critical architectural and design choices that underpinned this initiative.

speakers.name: Abhishek Narang
speakers.bio: Abhishek leads Data Engineering and Data Modeling efforts across the firm, responsible for helping revenue divisions identify, prototype, and use data to meet their client and business objectives. title: Governed Data Sharing & Collaboration at Scale: Deep Dive On Security Best Practices for Zero-Trust Architectures
abstract: Sharing data openly shouldn't mean sharing it insecurely. As enterprises scale Delta Sharing to hundreds of recipients across clouds and platforms, security and governance architectures quickly become the bottleneck. This session dives into how Databricks enables governed, zero‑copy collaboration using open Delta Sharing, Clean Rooms for multi‑party analytics without exposing raw data, and Private Exchanges in Databricks Marketplace for curated partner ecosystems. We'll tackle the three pillars of secure sharing at scale: OIDC token federation for secretless auth with external identity providers, attribute‑based access control (ABAC) for fine‑grained row/column policies, and the Storage Network Gateway for one‑time network setup that eliminates manual firewall/IP allowlisting. You'll see a live zero‑trust reference architecture, backed by Unity Catalog lineage and audit logging, powering cross‑cloud Databricks‑to‑Databricks and open recipients, plus monitoring. Expect code, configs, and field‑tested patterns, including a security checklist, ABAC policy templates, and an OIDC integration guide you can apply Monday morning to scale governed collaboration without replication.

He leads Databricks' internal Digital Natives Delta Sharing SME Group, a community of practice that develops best practices, reference architectures, and field enablement for Delta Sharing at scale. title: Governed Data Sharing at Scale: UBS RiskLab + LSEG via Unity Catalog and Delta Sharing
abstract: UBS and LSEG will jointly present how Databricks Unity Catalog and its Delta Sharing capabilities enable LSEG to provide UBS RiskLab: the bank’s model development lab platform, with direct, compliant access to LSEG’s extensive financial data products. This gives UBS data scientists governed, auditable self-service access, accelerating raw data exploration, business intelligence and rapid prototyping of Data and AI solutions under a robust control framework. The UBS RiskLab team will demonstrate how analytics are accelerated by securely combining UBS internal data with LSEG Delta Share data through Unity Catalog, with policy-based entitlements and centralized governance. This approach reduces the lengthy, siloed analysis typically associated with onboarding external data feeds. With the Unity Catalog AI Assistant and Genie, data scientists can quickly and confidently derive insights from a unified view of UBS internal and LSEG external data.

title: Governing at Hyper-Growth: How HelloFresh Cut Data Access from Days to Minutes with Unity Catalog
abstract: At HelloFresh, speed is our currency. But with 70,000+ tables generated across decentralized domains, our rapid growth threatened to outpace our ability to govern it. We reached a critical inflection point where the "data mesh" approach began to look like a "data mess"—fragmented across AWS Glue, obscured by complex IAM policies, and creating bottlenecks where data access took days to approve.This session unpacks the strategic pivot from reactive catalog management to a unified, scalable governance model on Databricks Unity Catalog. We will move beyond the technical migration to discuss the architectural shifts required to balance autonomy with control. You will learn how we dismantled the trade-off between security and agility, replacing brittle object-based permissions with a streamlined privilege model that empowers data owners.We will share our blueprint for a zero-downtime migration (using Hive Metastore Federation) and the cultural changes required to make governance stick.

title: GPU-Accelerated Operations Research: Production-Grade Optimization with NVIDIA cuOpt on Databricks
abstract: Enterprises face a surge of optimization challenges—workforce scheduling, supply chain planning, resource allocation, etc. that are computationally explosive as scale and constraints grow. Learn to solve these problems with Databricks using NVIDIA cuOpt on serverless GPUs, with large-scale linear, mixed-integer, and routing models in minutes instead of hours. We will walk through end-to-end architectures on the Databricks Lakehouse—parameterizing and submitting optimization jobs to serverless GPU clusters, managing data with Unity Catalog and Delta tables, and orchestrating workloads alongside existing ML pipelines. Learn from real-world examples I.e. shift scheduling, production allocation, and route optimization with performance benchmarks and cost benefits compared to traditional CPU solvers. Leave with practical patterns for modeling constraints, validating feasibility, and knowing when GPU-accelerated optimization can drive measurable gains in efficiency and delivery performance.

title: Grounding Legacy BI: Etihad Airways’ Transition to AI-Powered Self-Service with Genie
abstract: Modern airlines need more than dashboards. They need intelligence at speed. Etihad Airways is transforming its analytics landscape by moving from traditional BI platforms to Databricks AI/BI, redefining how data is accessed, understood and acted upon across the enterprise. This session tells the story behind that shift, beginning with a bold pilot in Catering that demonstrated the power of generative, natural-language insights and sparked immediate executive engagement from day one. We’ll explore Etihad’s journey from complex, siloed reporting to an AI-first, self-service experience powered by Databricks Genie, turning data into an always-on business co-pilot. Attendees will learn how this transformation accelerated speed-to-insight, reduced technical debt, and scaled rapidly across diverse business units. We’ll conclude with a demo of our current Genie implementation and a forward look at Etihad’s evolving roadmap in the Databricks ecosystem, offering practical lessons for anyone.

title: High-Throughput, Low-Latency: The Databricks Playbook for Production Model Serving
abstract: Modern AI applications don’t just need accurate models, they need to serve them at massive scale, with low latency and strict reliability guarantees. In this session, we’ll go under the hood of Databricks Model Serving to show how we support high QPS (300K+ QPS), production-grade workloads on a unified Lakehouse platform. We’ll walk through the serving architecture, autoscaling strategies, and optimizations across GPU/CPU utilization, request routing, and caching that enable sustained high throughput without sacrificing latency or cost. You’ll see real patterns from customer deployments, learn how to design your endpoints for bursty and always-on traffic, and leave with practical guidance for running mission-critical ML and LLM workloads on Databricks Model Serving.

In this session, we’ll go under the hood of Databricks Model Serving to show how we support high QPS (300K+ QPS), production-grade workloads on a unified Lakehouse platform. We’ll walk through the serving architecture, autoscaling strategies, and optimizations across GPU/CPU utilization, request routing, and caching that enable sustained high throughput without sacrificing latency or cost. You’ll see real patterns from customer deployments, learn how to design your endpoints for bursty and always-on traffic, and leave with practical guidance for running mission-critical ML and LLM workloads on Databricks Model Serving.

title: How Data Warehousing Changes in the World of Agents
abstract: The rise of agentic applications is reshaping what we demand from data warehouses. Agents don't operate on yesterday's batch — they require fresh, consistent data with sub-second latency to reason, decide, and act in real time. In this session, we examine why traditional data warehousing patterns, built around scheduled ETL and human-speed concurrency and latency, fall short when the consumer of your data is an army of agents making thousands of decisions per minute. We address the challenges this shift introduces: tighter freshness SLAs, evolving governance when agents —not people — query your data, and the complexity of always-on pipelines. We then explore how real-time lakehouse capabilities — streaming ingestion, incremental materialization, high concurrency and low-latency query serving, and unified governance — support agentic applications at scale. This session offers a framework to make your lakehouse real-time and agent-ready.

title: How Databricks Apps Facilitated Model Retraining
abstract: At Bridgestone, our mission is “serving society with superior quality.” As the world’s largest manufacturer of tire products, Bridgestone has a unique opportunity to leverage data and insights to improve manufacturing efficiency. Bridgestone’s Bandag Retread Solutions has over 65 years leading retread innovations. In this talk, we will focus on how our data scientists used Databricks Apps to improve a computer vision model that facilitates decision-making during the tire retread process. We will show how in 3 weeks, a fully-fledged computer vision labeling application could be built using apps, saving our retread SMEs months of manual effort and thousands of dollars in a 3p software. Our talk will also highlight the benefits of choosing Databricks apps to host our solution: The ease of integration with existing Databricks resources Out of box compliance with internal security standards The speed in developing a front-end application, without the need of managing cloud infrastructure
description_raw:
At Bridgestone, our mission is “serving society with superior quality.” As the world’s largest manufacturer of tire products, Bridgestone has a unique opportunity to leverage data and insights to improve manufacturing efficiency. Bridgestone’s Bandag Retread Solutions has over 65 years leading retread innovations.

In this talk, we will focus on how our data scientists used Databricks Apps to improve a computer vision model that facilitates decision-making during the tire retread process. We will show how in 3 weeks, a fully-fledged computer vision labeling application could be built using apps, saving our retread SMEs months of manual effort and thousands of dollars in a 3p software.

Our talk will also highlight the benefits of choosing Databricks apps to host our solution:

  • The ease of integration with existing Databricks resources
  • Out of box compliance with internal security standards
  • The speed in developing a front-end application, without the need of managing cloud infrastructure

speakers.name: Tim Hanchin
speakers.bio: I specialize in harnessing emerging technology to drive growth. With a strong focus on data, I work at the intersection of people, relationships, and innovation to create value for internal and external stakeholders.

title: How Databricks Ships AI Safely: Inside the Team Enabling Secure Innovation
abstract: The pace of AI development has reset what security teams need to deliver. At Databricks, we've built an AI Security team that operates at the frontier of the technology it defends. This session is an inside look at how. We'll share the human-in-the-loop agents we built to assess security issues across our AI products and services, and why an early, disciplined bet on agent benchmarking got us to 95.8% accuracy on agent-generated findings, and how we use expert reviewers validating every result so nothing slips through. Then we'll open the hood on how we secure the coding agents our own engineers use every day: managed client-side settings, agent sandboxes, an AI Gateway and MCP Gateway enforcing policy server-side where admin access can't reach, and continuous scanning of every Databricks App, plugin, and skill.Real architecture. Real numbers. Real failure modes. A practical look at what a modern AI security function looks like in production.
description_raw:
The pace of AI development has reset what security teams need to deliver. At Databricks, we've built an AI Security team that operates at the frontier of the technology it defends. This session is an inside look at how.
