Then we'll open the hood on how we secure the coding agents our own engineers use every day: managed client-side settings, agent sandboxes, an AI Gateway and MCP Gateway enforcing policy server-side where admin access can't reach, and continuous scanning of every Databricks App, plugin, and skill.Real architecture. Real numbers. Real failure modes. A practical look at what a modern AI security function looks like in production.

speakers.name: Nishith Sinha
speakers.bio: Nishith leads the AI Security Team at Databricks. His team is responsible for securing Databricks AI Products (Genie Code, Model Serving, Databricks One) as well as the enterprise usage of AI within Databricks. Nishith is a subject matter expert in AI Security, specializing in agents identity and security. In the last year, he has filed 10 GenAI Security patents.

speakers.name: Alex Warnecke
speakers.bio: Alex is an AI Security Engineer at Databricks. Prior to that he obtained his PhD from Technical University of Berlin where his research was dealing with the intersection of machine learning and computer security.

title: How Hilton Grand Vacations Optimizes Inventory with AI on Databricks
abstract: Timeshare and hospitality inventory decisions are complex, spanning multiple revenue streams and liabilities, constrained by thousands of contractual and operational rules. This talk explores how HGV’s Inventory AI transformed yield and revenue optimization into an AI-powered, simulation-driven workflow that enforces business guardrails, scores alternative moves, and surfaces real-time revenue impact. Powered by Databricks, the solution builds on our modernization program that migrated thousands of data elements and analytics workloads to Databricks, enabling scalable data engineering and ML operations. We’ll showcase how these components integrate into an AI-first decisioning platform - a conversational interface bridging business users and data science, and the evolution from decision support to semi-autonomous agentic AI for “What If...” simulations that streamline inventory processes. Finally, we’ll share organizational success predictors for enterprise AI adoption.

speakers.name: Evgeny Bob
speakers.bio: Engineering nomad with 20+ years of experience solving genuinely hard problems. Spanning a wide spectrum of tech stack from RF Layer1 FPGA levels towards 100K CPUs HPC clusters. Previously working in Telco, Public Safety and Security spaces. Currently solving for Artificial Travel Intelligence helping customers to get their best memories.

title: How Lakebase and Unity Catalog Are Replacing SQL Server Sprawl in Healthcare
abstract: Transactional systems supporting real-time workflows must simultaneously feed analytical platforms under strict regulatory governance. The traditional answer—SQL Server replication layered with ADF orchestration and parallel OLTP/OLAP governance stacks—means every new application adds another replication chain, pipeline, and governance gap.This session presents a fundamentally different approach: standardizing transactional storage on Databricks Lakebase, a Postgres-compatible database natively integrated with the lakehouse and governed by Unity Catalog. By moving application transactions directly into the lakehouse ecosystem, we eliminated multi-hop replication chains, removed ADF orchestration, and reduced total architecture components by 75%.We cover the governance model enabling Unity Catalog to provide lineage, access control, and audit across transactional and analytical workloads.

By moving application transactions directly into the lakehouse ecosystem, we eliminated multi-hop replication chains, removed ADF orchestration, and reduced total architecture components by 75%.We cover the governance model enabling Unity Catalog to provide lineage, access control, and audit across transactional and analytical workloads.

title: How Mastercard Turns Transaction Data into Trusted Merchant Insights with AI
abstract: We are the first team at Mastercard to build an AI-powered, API-based product on Databricks—GAIME. GAIME is a fuzzy-matching algorithm, which matches transaction/merchant descriptors with Mastercard's real-time merchant database. GAIME consists of entity recognition, candidates retrieval via embeddings + vector search and uses AI-as-a-judge to produce a final match decision. The talk is organized around three foundational pillars of an AI-powered product: governance, responsible AI and quality control. We will discuss how these pillars become accelerators rather than constraints at the regulated financial sector. Finally, we will share the journey of moving GAIME from POC to production and demonstrate what role data science plays in scaling high-impact AI products within a regulated environment.

speakers.name: Tomáš Drietomský

He holds an MSc. in Data Science from the Technical University of Vienna and a BSc. in Computer Science from the Czech Technical University in Prague. title: How Musinsa Built a Self-Optimizing Semantic Layer on Databricks
abstract: Musinsa, South Korea's #1 fashion platform ($3.4B GMV, 8,000+ brands, 10M+ users, 13 countries), faced an architecture trilemma where every pipeline forced trade-offs between productivity, latency and cost. The root cause was not technology but a mental model: "build a table before you analyze." This caused "Organizational Amnesia": marts preserved results but lost the logic. We built a TVF-based semantic layer on Databricks SQL, shifting from "which table to query" to "what logic to compose." Four time-standardized TVFs, demand-driven caching (CTVF) on Delta Lake, and three AI agents (AI/BI Genie, MCP) enable self-optimizing analytics managed via Unity Catalog.Results: 75% fewer managed assets (1,000 to 250), 98% faster analysis (7 days to 2 hours), 3.6x self-service increase (15% to 55%).Attendees leave with a trilemma framework and concrete TVF patterns. Includes a live SQL demo.

speakers.name: Junyoung Chang
speakers.bio: Junyoung Chang is a Data Engineer at Musinsa, Korea's leading fashion platform, where he drives the architecture behind one of Asia's most complex e-commerce data ecosystems. Over the past years across quick-commerce, e-commerce, crypto platforms, EV mobility and fashion tech, he has built critical data infrastructure from zero, spanning real-time pipelines, lineage systems, schema management, and semantic layers, with a consistent focus on solving the productivity-latency-cost trilemma that most data teams quietly accept as inevitable. His broader mission is empowering every colleague to access trusted data easily and accurately, turning data-driven decisions into an organizational default rather than a privilege.

title: How Nubank Migrated to Databricks SQL Reducing Costs by 40% and Doubling Queries
abstract: At Nubank, we successfully migrated to Databricks SQL over 12 months, addressing the needs of our large-scale data environment with 17k users, 40MM monthly queries, and operations globally serving 100MM+ customers. Main goals: improve cost efficiency, enhance scalability, and maintain business continuity during explosive growth. Key aspects: Zero-downtime throughout the 12-month migration period Successfully scaling platform to handle 100% query growth (20MM to 40MM monthly queries) Comprehensive workload migration supporting 17k users globally +40% annual cost savings Leverage of UC for centralized data governance in highly regulated FSI industry This migration significantly improved our cost structure, enhanced our ability to support business growth and provided a scalable foundation for serving LATAM’s largest fintech, ultimately demonstrating that platform transformation and business velocity can succeed simultaneously in highly regulated environments.

  • Zero-downtime throughout the 12-month migration period

This migration significantly improved our cost structure, enhanced our ability to support business growth and provided a scalable foundation for serving LATAM’s largest fintech, ultimately demonstrating that platform transformation and business velocity can succeed simultaneously in highly regulated environments.

speakers.name: Vinicius Pessoa Duarte

title: How Open Lakehouse Architectures Drive Data and AI Platform Modernization
abstract: At GEODIS, we have launched a complete data and AI cloud platform modernization with the three main objectives. First, reduce Platform engineer workload with a range of best of breed managed services—including Databricks. Second, keep all the development and investment done on our previous architecture "compatible" with the new one—no bing bang. And last and most importantly, keep the platform fully open, interroperable and modular.In that context, we have been able to integrate with our legacy Cloudera technology in MS Azure new components—Astronomer, Privacera, Databricks and Starburst, relying on open standards and technologies—full Apache Iceberg™ data lake, catalog interroparability and Apache Spark™ data quality validation.I propose to discuss the challenges we have faced like Iceberg interroperability—Iceberg v2 vs v3, multi-engine integration—Databricks, Starbrust/Trino and Cloudera, and migration strategy—performance and interroperability enforcement.

speakers.bio: Delio began his career at GEODIS a little over 20 years ago. He started on the Infrastructure side, where he was responsible for leading the systems production and datacenter teams. Since 2019, he has held the position of Chief Architect Officer, overseeing Enterprise Architecture and Urbanization for the group. His first mission in this area was to build a common data stack for all the logistics company’s business lines. Since early 2024, the role of Chief Data Officer has been added to his responsibilities within the Architecture domain. His mission is now to bring the two areas together with a pragmatic and efficient approach to Data Management

title: How Pfizer and REDCap Cloud Modernized Clinical Data Sharing
abstract: Pfizer has transformed clinical trial data exchange by moving from manual, batch-oriented ingestion to a real-time, cross-organizational architecture. Previously, fragmented systems delayed data access and complicated collaboration with research partners. This session explains how Pfizer and RCC used Delta Sharing and UC to enable secure, live data access across different cloud providers and platforms. What you will learn Real-time architecture: move from 24-hr batch cycles to 5-min readiness Cross-cloud sharing: share live data between Azure, AWS and external CDMS platforms without egress complexity Simplified governance: managing strict Life Sciences compliance and data privacy at scale using Unity Catalog Operational efficiency: reducing costs by consolidating legacy orchestration into serverless compute and unified workflows. Interoperability: facilitated direct sharing between REDCap Cloud’s CDMS and Pfizer’s internal analytics environment for faster clinical decision-making

title: How Samsara Built a Multi-Agent System to Drive a 36x Lift in Meeting Bookings on Databricks
abstract: Samsara faced a critical bottleneck: sales reps spent 60 minutes researching each prospect, yet meeting booking rates stagnated at 0.18%. To solve this, we built 'Smart Flows' on Databricks—a multi-agent system orchestrating Agent Bricks AI agents, AI/BI Genie and a custom MCP server for deep web research. This architecture moves beyond simple automation to agentic reasoning. A supervisor agent routes tasks: the MCP server gathers account intelligence, while Genie agents query CRM data to hyper-personalize outreach. The state is managed via Lakebase (Serverless Postgres) and reusable Unity Catalog functions. The results were transformative. Prospecting time dropped to under five minutes—12x productivity—and agentic personalization drove meeting booking rates to 6.49%—a massive 36x uplift. We will demonstrate this live, generating a fully researched outreach sequence in real-time to show how agents can drive hard revenue metrics.

speakers.name: Vipul Panwar
speakers.bio: Senior AI Engineer with over seven years of experience building production-grade AI systems across enterprise environments. He holds a Masters in Geophysical Technology from IIT Roorkee, India and began his career at Schlumberger before moving into the AI space at Databricks, where he spent three years building multi-agent frameworks, LLM-powered platforms, and internal AI tooling adopted across 50+ engineering teams. He currently works at Samsara, focused on building platforms for agentic AI systems, LLM evaluation frameworks, RAG pipelines, and enterprise integrations using LangGraph, MCP, and the AWS/Databricks stack. His work sits at the intersection of applied AI research and backend engineering. Based in Bangalore, India.

title: How Supercell Uses Databricks to Serve Nearly 300M Monthly Players and Prevent Player Harm
abstract: Supercell, the creator of hits like Clash of Clans and Brawl Stars, aims to build games that are played for years and remembered forever. To achieve this, they need to blend the art of game design with the science of hard facts. Supercell uses Databricks to democratize data access, simplify data processing and enable everyone from game designers to product managers to make hypothesis-driven decisions that keep nearly 300 million monthly players engaged. In this presentation, Supercell will describe their journey with Databricks, highlighting data ingestion with Auto Loader, part of Lakeflow Connect, Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines and Spark Structured Streaming, orchestration with Lakeflow Jobs, governance with Unity Catalog, and the experiences from going all-in on serverless compute, all at massive scale. Presentation culminates in discussing how Trust & Safety team detects and prevents player harm with advanced machine learning algorithms built on these same data foundations.

title: How TBC Bank Uses AI and Lakebase to Stop Money Laundering
abstract: Anti-money laundering (AML) teams are buried under fragmented tools, scattered data, and manual workflows. Adding more rules has not solved the problem. TBC Bank took a different path, consolidating intelligence on Databricks to create a unified, AI-powered investigation environment.This session shows the production use cases in action: a counterparty AML search engine across sanctions, internal, and external watchlists; an AI engine comparing invoices, contracts, and customs documents; a transaction purpose scanner detecting dual-use and restricted goods; and Genie for natural-language analytics. All built on a shared Lakehouse and delivered through one investigative workspace using Databricks Apps and Lakebase.The result is a fundamental shift in how AML works at TBC Bank: from reactive monitoring to contextual, AI-assisted investigation.

Beforehand I worked at Salesforce and Mulesoft for altogether almost a decade. When I’m not at Databricks, I’m a proud dad of two amazing girls, keeping them entertained with my DJ skills.

title: How to Be An Agentic Analyst
abstract: AI is changing the data analyst role. Staying competitive requires a shift from manual tasks to architecting intelligent systems. This session provides a roadmap for analysts to move beyond technical toil toward building governed AI intelligence layers. We'll explore new ways of working and delivering value throughout the analytics lifecycle, and how Databricks can help.We will cover the following typical analyst journeys:Accelerating data discovery and wrangling with agent mode in Databricks Assistant.Ensuring data consistency by defining business semantics in the Metric View UI.Enabling stakeholder self-service through conversational AI analytics with Genie.Democratizing data products by connecting them to popular tools like Excel and Slack.Building custom, high-impact interfaces for advanced scenarios using Databricks Apps.This session offers a blueprint for becoming an augmented analyst, delivering scalable AI services that grow with your organization.

He’s a 20 year veteran of the data warehousing and BI space, having worn every hat from report development, modeling, and performance tuning to architecture, strategy, and governance. Having been a trusted advisor to well over 1,000 organizations big and small: Kyle has seen things.His proudest moment is being recognized by his peers at Databricks as a Data and Analytics SME - game knows game.When he’s not secretly plotting like all good BI professionals to kill Excel once and for all, he enjoys crafting synth pop soundtracks to imaginary movies, solving chess puzzles, and roaming the countryside with his wife and 4 kids in search of cool museums and awesome fried chicken. He is also an avid collector of dad jokes.

title: How to Bridge the Gap Between No-Code and Pro-Code with Lakeflow Designer
abstract: Data engineering has a bottleneck: there are too many pipelines to build, not enough engineers to build them. Lakeflow Designer changes the equation by giving SQL analysts and citizen developers a visual, no-code interface to create production-grade ETL pipelines, backed by the same engine that powers Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines (SDP). This session explores the full Lakeflow spectrum. We'll start with Lakeflow Designer's drag-and-drop canvas and GenAI assistant, showing how a business analyst can build a medallion pipeline in minutes without writing code. Then we'll show how a data engineer extends that same pipeline with custom Python logic, data quality expectations stored in Unity Catalog, and Data Quality Monitoring with anomaly detection. We'll demonstrate how Lakeflow Connect ingests from sources like Salesforce and PostgreSQL, how SDPs handle the transformation layer, and how Jobs orchestrate. You'll see how visual and code-based interfaces coexist, enabling collaboration.

Data engineering has a bottleneck: there are too many pipelines to build, not enough engineers to build them. This session explores the full Lakeflow spectrum. We'll start with Lakeflow Designer's drag-and-drop canvas and GenAI assistant, showing how a business analyst can build a medallion pipeline in minutes without writing code. Then we'll show how a data engineer extends that same pipeline with custom Python logic, data quality expectations stored in Unity Catalog, and Data Quality Monitoring with anomaly detection.

We'll demonstrate how Lakeflow Connect ingests from sources like Salesforce and PostgreSQL, how SDPs handle the transformation layer, and how Jobs orchestrate.

You'll see how visual and code-based interfaces coexist, enabling collaboration.

speakers.name: Germaine Ong
speakers.bio: Germaine Ong is a Senior Solutions Engineer at Databricks, working with Digital Native Business and Emerging Enterprise customers across ASEAN. Before Databricks, she spent 3.5 years at AWS helping startups and public sector enterprises in the region build and scale their cloud infrastructure. Her data journey started as a Process Engineer in the semiconductor industry, where she felt the pain of going from raw data to usable insights. Now at Databricks, she helps organizations make data accessible to everyone without losing the flexibility engineers need. It's a challenge she knows well, and one she's excited to explore in her session on Lakeflow Designer.

title: How to Govern and Audit Your Coding Agent Usage with Databricks
abstract: As coding agents like GitHub Copilot, Cursor, and Claude Code become embedded in daily development workflows, enterprises face a new challenge: how to govern and audit AI-assisted code generation at scale. Without centralized visibility, organizations risk exposing sensitive data, losing track of usage, and failing to meet security and compliance requirements.In this session, we'll show how Databricks enables end-to-end governance for coding agents across the enterprise. You'll learn how to enforce fine-grained permissions, track usage and costs across teams, and capture complete audit trails of prompts, responses, and tool interactions. We'll also cover how to apply consistent policies across providers — so developers can move fast with coding agents while maintaining the control, visibility, and compliance production environments demand.You'll walk away with the patterns to unlock coding agents safely across your organization — without creating a new compliance liability.

title: How to Modernize Your ETL with Declarative Pipelines
abstract: Did you ever wonder why Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines (SDP) do not allow dynamic parameters or partial recalculations for specific dates? Or why for-loops are restricted in table definitions? Furthermore, how do you choose between a Streaming Table and a Materialized View? These are common struggles users face when starting. While often perceived as tool limitations, these hurdles usually stem from attempting to apply imperative habits to a declarative framework. In this session we will discuss real-world customer examples to demonstrate how to transition from imperative to declarative approach.Stop fighting the framework and start leveraging the declarative paradigm for more resilient data engineering.
description_raw:
Did you ever wonder why Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines (SDP) do not allow dynamic parameters or partial recalculations for specific dates? Or why for-loops are restricted in table definitions? Furthermore, how do you choose between a Streaming Table and a Materialized View?

These are common struggles users face when starting. While often perceived as tool limitations, these hurdles usually stem from attempting to apply imperative habits to a declarative framework.

In this session we will discuss real-world customer examples to demonstrate how to transition from imperative to declarative approach.Stop fighting the framework and start leveraging the declarative paradigm for more resilient data engineering.

title: How to scale governance to External Engines with Unity Catalog Open APIs
abstract: Enterprises are increasingly challenged to scale data and AI initiatives across diverse tools, clouds, and teams without fragmenting governance or slowing innovation. This session explores how organizations are using Unity Catalog as a unified governance layer for enabling secure, interoperable data access across engines and open table formats such as Delta Lake and Apache Iceberg with fine-grained controls. By simplifying access management through automated, identity-based credentials and applying a single, consistent policy framework, enterprises can reduce operational complexity while maintaining strong security and compliance. In this session, we will cover Lakehouse architecture patterns with UC Open APIs, and we will also show a live demo. Attendees will learn how this approach drives governed collaboration, speeds up analytics and AI workloads, and fuels business growth in multi-engine environments.

Enterprises are increasingly challenged to scale data and AI initiatives across diverse tools, clouds, and teams without fragmenting governance or slowing innovation. 

This session explores how organizations are using Unity Catalog as a unified governance layer for enabling secure, interoperable data access across engines and open table formats such as Delta Lake and Apache Iceberg with fine-grained controls. 

By simplifying access management through automated, identity-based credentials and applying a single, consistent policy framework, enterprises can reduce operational complexity while maintaining strong security and compliance. In this session, we will cover Lakehouse architecture patterns with UC Open APIs, and we will also show a live demo. 

Attendees will learn how this approach drives governed collaboration, speeds up analytics and AI workloads, and fuels business growth in multi-engine environments.

speakers.name: Dipankar Kushari

Before Databricks, Dipankar served as a Senior Solutions Architect at AWS, where he built a strong thought-leadership presence — authoring technical blogs, videos, and whitepapers — while guiding customers in architecting robust cloud analytics solutions.

title: How to Solve the Context Gap: Engineering Reliable AI Agents
abstract: The biggest hurdle to production AI isn't the model — it's the context window. What an agent sees, when, and in what format determines whether it reasons correctly or hallucinates. Most teams stitch together RAG, tools, and state management ad hoc, then struggle to evaluate whether their context decisions actually work.This session is a deep dive into context engineering on Databricks: the systematic practice of designing what agents see at every step. You'll learn how to configure RAG systems that go beyond basic retrieval, use Model Context Protocol (MCP) for secure tool and API integration, and manage long-term memory across complex agent workflows.You'll leave with a playbook for building measurement pipelines that empirically evaluate your context decisions — and the patterns to ship agents that stay reliable when users, data, and tasks evolve.

title: How to Turn Every Business User Into a Decision Maker With AI/BI and Databricks One
abstract: "How to Turn Every Business User Into a Decision Maker With AI/BI and Databricks One" is a practical session for leaders who want to move beyond static dashboards and operationalize data-driven decisions at scale. Youssef Mrini and Josue Bogran show how Unity Catalog Metric Views, AI/BI dashboards and AI/BI Genie, delivered through the new Databricks One experience, replace fragmented reports and spreadsheet chaos with trusted, conversational analytics. See how governed KPIs create a shared semantic layer so sales, finance and operations speak the same language while still moving fast. Through live demos, follow a business user from the Databricks One homepage to a dashboard, then asking “why is this changing?” in natural language and exploring scenarios on top of trusted metrics, no SQL required. Leave with a blueprint to turn report consumers into proactive decision makers and close the gap between insight and action.

title: How to Vibe Code Successfully: Make Your Agents Love Databricks
abstract: "To get the most out of your coding agents, you need to set them up for success, especially when letting them loose against a large platform like Databricks. In this talk, we will dive deep into Agent Experience (AX) for building Databricks Apps and Agent.We will discuss AX as a new discipline similar to Developer Experience (DX) and review the most effective tools and best practices for successfully steering agents. We'll discuss how to set up your local development environment for building on Databricks. From agent skills and MCP to reference codebases and AppKit, we will see how these components compose together. By the end, your agents will know Databricks inside and out, allowing you to vibe code more successfully on top of the Databricks platform."

title: How Unified Data Can Help Secure the Future of Global Food Production
abstract: Agriculture generates vast streams of information—from lab research to satellite imagery, factory sensors and farmers’ mobile apps. Each raw field, whether in a data table or on the farm, carries its own fragment of truth. The real opportunity lies in unifying these fields into connected, trustworthy insight.This keynote shows how data mesh principles and Genie help teams break silos, democratize access and enable proactive decisions that lift yields and strengthen resilience. By bringing together the wisdom hidden across our digital and physical fields, we build a future where every field can help fill every plate.

title: How We Built Agent Mode in Genie Spaces
abstract: Agentic analytics promises to go beyond answering questions enabling AI to investigate, reason, and synthesize insights across complex data. But building systems that do this reliably is far from simple.Genie Agent Mode introduces a new approach to conversational analytics, where AI can explore multiple hypotheses, interact with data iteratively, and generate structured explanations. Under the hood, this requires careful orchestration of planning, validation, and context management to ensure results are both accurate and trustworthy.In this session, we’ll go behind the scenes to explore how Genie Agent Mode works and what it takes to make agentic analytics production-ready:- How Genie Agent Mode plans and executes multi-step analytical workflows- How the system maintains context and evaluates intermediate results- How accuracy and reliability are enforced through validation and safeguards- Key lessons learned building agentic systems for real-world data use cases

speakers.name: Ryan Ziegler
speakers.bio: Ryan Ziegler is a software engineer on the Genie team at Databricks focused on building high-quality, helpful agentic experiences. He has most recently worked on agent monitoring and steering, token use optimization, and building new interfaces to better leverage agents.

title: Human-in-the-Loop Compliance at Global Scale: Databricks Apps + Lakebase Casework + SME Labeling
abstract: In this session, we share a practical pattern we call “PolicyOps” on Databricks: converting long policy documents into cited, versioned, queryable policy units, then operationalizing a human‑in‑the‑loop workflow that captures domain SME judgment as structured labels. We’ll show how to parse and structure large policy PDFs into clause-level records (including page-aware citations), how to extract decision criteria into queryable tables, and how to connect policy interpretation to real operational review.We’ll then demonstrate an SME review queue built with Databricks Apps — designed for high-signal feedback (not just thumbs up/down) — with Lakebase used to manage case workflow state (assignment, status, reviewer actions, approvals). The result is a scalable workflow that keeps humans in control of edge cases while systematically converting implicit SME knowledge into reusable labels and corrections that improve quality over time.

title: Implementing Data Governance with Unity Catalog
abstract: This hands-on course equips learners with the skills to design, implement, and manage catalog structures and large-scale data organization using Unity Catalog in Databricks. Participants will learn to configure secure and scalable storage integrations, as well as implement access controls and isolation strategies for effective data governance. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, including workspaces and notebooks Intermediate SQL experience, including creating tables, views, and functions, and managing database object permissions Understanding of Unity Catalog's hierarchical object model (metastore, catalogs, schemas, tables, volumes, models) Basic understanding of data governance concepts, including access control, row-level security, column masking, and fine-grained access controls Basic knowledge of data lineage, metadata management, and cloud computing concepts (compute, storage, identity management)
description_raw:
This hands-on course equips learners with the skills to design, implement, and manage catalog structures and large-scale data organization using Unity Catalog in Databricks. Participants will learn to configure secure and scalable storage integrations, as well as implement access controls and isolation strategies for effective data governance.

Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. 

Prerequisites

  • Familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, including workspaces and notebooks
  • Intermediate SQL experience, including creating tables, views, and functions, and managing database object permissions
  • Understanding of Unity Catalog's hierarchical object model (metastore, catalogs, schemas, tables, volumes, models)
  • Basic understanding of data governance concepts, including access control, row-level security, column masking, and fine-grained access controls
  • Basic knowledge of data lineage, metadata management, and cloud computing concepts (compute, storage, identity management)

title: Inside Adobe’s Near Real-Time Cloud Spend Monitoring System
abstract: Cloud costs are rising faster than budgets, yet teams often discover expensive operations only after the spend has occurred. We moved from reactive cost reporting to proactive, near real-time monitoring—reducing SQL warehouse costs by 51% and Serverless compute by 14% in less than 30 days.This talk covers how we built a production-grade monitoring system managing millions in annual Databricks spend across petabyte-scale data, 94,000+ jobs, and millions of compute hours, with 15-minute monitoring cycles and zero infrastructure cost.The key challenge was attribution: Databricks assigns SQL warehouse costs to warehouse creators, not query executors, obscuring who drives spend. We solved this by combining Databricks system tables—`billing.usage`, `query.history`, and `compute.warehouse_events`—to enable query-level cost attribution without added compute.Attendees will learn the architecture, implementation patterns and reusable strategies to build proactive cost monitoring at any scale.

speakers.name: Rajeshwari Raghuraman
speakers.bio: Raji Raghuraman is a Senior Data Science Engineering Manager at Adobe, where she leads the Data Engineering team in the Digital Imaging Data Science (DIDS) team responsible for analytics infrastructure supporting Photoshop, Lightroom, and Fresco. With over 20 years of experience in AI-driven analytics, data architecture, and engineering leadership, she specializes in building petabyte-scale data platforms that balance performance, cost, and compliance. At Adobe, Raji is driving the Data Roadmap Strategy for the Digital Imaging Data Science team through thought leadership on Privacy Automation Framework, scalable architectures for their Petabyte scale data, while achieving significant cost reductions through innovative monitoring and automation. Prior to Adobe, Raji held senior Data engineering Manager role at eBay in Risk Decision Science. She holds an M.S. in Software Systems, M.C.A., and completed MIT Professional Education in Applied Data Science.

title: Inside our Lakeflow Journey at SEGA: Scaling Streaming Analytics Across Titles
abstract: At SEGA Europe, we process billions of game telemetry events daily to power real-time analytics for live ops, player experience optimisation, and business intelligence. We have rebuilt our streaming platform on Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines, moving from fragile, manually managed jobs to a resilient, self-healing lakehouse architecture. This approach was battle-tested during the launch of Football Manager 2026, where scale and reliability were critical.This session shares our production journey with Lakeflow—the wins, lessons, and unexpected challenges of operating at gaming scale. We’ll cover our migration from legacy streaming infrastructure, achieving sub-minute latency, and handling evolving event schemas. Topics include stream fan-out from Kinesis to Bronze, schema evolution, late and out-of-order events, data quality checks, performance tuning, and cost control. We’ll also share quantified results from FM26 and practical patterns for teams evaluating Lakeflow at scale.

With over 10 years’ experience in AI/ML, big data, and cloud architecture, he has worked across the US, India, and the UK, including at AWS, developing cloud-native data and ML solutions. His work covers GenAI applications, ML pipelines, real-time analytics, and self-serve BI dashboards. He holds an MS from the University at Buffalo.

title: Insights at Scale With Agentic AI: How AI Ready Data is Transforming Commercial Life Sciences
abstract: Pharma leaders face pressure to accelerate commercialization and improve launch performance, yet many commercial analytics teams are held back by fragmented data, brittle dashboards and heavy manual insight generation. At the same time, generative AI is rapidly advancing from copilots to fully-agentic systems, creating new opportunities and risks in regulated environments.In this session, Merck and ZS show how Merck is deploying Agentic AI using Databricks AI/BI Genie and Agent Bricks to build domain aware data agents on trusted, governed data products. These agents automate high-friction workflows across marketing, field effectiveness and brand performance, cutting time to insight by over 50% and enabling faster, more consistent decisions.We will share Merck’s blueprint for building context-rich data products, embedding business semantics and orchestrating agent workflows that integrate with existing BI—offering a practical, scalable architecture for Agentic AI in pharma.

title: Integrating Lakebase Databases into Intelligent Applications
abstract: This hands-on course teaches Databricks application developers how to use Lakebase as the operational database for intelligent, data-driven applications on Databricks. Learners explore how Lakebase enables applications powered by analytics, BI, and AI. Through guided exercises, the course covers Lakebase core concepts and architecture, provisioning and managing database instances, connecting from Databricks SQL, notebooks, and external clients, and applying best practices for connection management, permissions, and environment configuration. By the end of the course, developers will be able to confidently integrate Lakebase into production-ready data and AI applications, safely manage database resources, and unlock unified analytics and AI on live operational data. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, including workspaces and notebooks Intermediate SQL proficiency, including SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE statements Intermediate Python programming experience, including functions, exceptions, and working with dictionaries and lists Familiarity with OLTP fundamentals, including client-server relationships, ACID properties, database authentication, and concurrent access

title: Intelligent Document Processing With Lakeflow Connect and AI Parse
abstract: Enterprise knowledge is often trapped in PDFs, images and office docs—out of reach for AI. This lightning talk will walk you through the end-to-end process for intelligent document processing.We’ll showcase a demo of how to ingest this unstructured data securely and with governance using Lakeflow Connect—preserving permissions, metadata and lineage.Then, see how to activate the data by deploying production-grade agentic workflows with Agent Bricks for functions like search, summarization and business intelligence.We'll highlight customer success stories demonstrating how this process transforms fragmented documents into a governed foundation for data intelligence, enabling rapid, secure AI at scale.

title: Introducing the Vibe to Value Workshop: Your Fast Track to Building Databricks Apps
abstract: This is not the workshop—it’s your invitation to one. In this 20-minute lightning talk, we’ll introduce the Vibe to Value workshop: a 4-hour, hands-on experience where you install a real Databricks app and use it to learn how to build your own Databricks Apps. We’ll walk through what the workshop covers, who it’s for, and why it’s different: instead of learning via slides, you learn by using an app that’s already running on Databricks, “vibe coding” your way from simple edits to a working app on top of your own data. You’ll see how the flow works end-to-end, what you’ll build, and how much you can accomplish in just one afternoon, even if you’re new to Databricks Apps. You’ll leave this session with a clear understanding of the opportunity, how to sign up for the full Vibe to Value workshop, and how to coordinate with your Databricks team to bring it to your organization.

We’ll walk through what the workshop covers, who it’s for, and why it’s different: instead of learning via slides, you learn by using an app that’s already running on Databricks, “vibe coding” your way from simple edits to a working app on top of your own data. You’ll see how the flow works end-to-end, what you’ll build, and how much you can accomplish in just one afternoon, even if you’re new to Databricks Apps.

You’ll leave this session with a clear understanding of the opportunity, how to sign up for the full Vibe to Value workshop, and how to coordinate with your Databricks team to bring it to your organization.

title: Introduction to Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines: Reliable Data Pipelines Made Easy
abstract: Building data pipelines often means hand-writing complex data engineering patterns like incremental processing, CDC/SCD handling, and backfills in every job.Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines (SDP) are the next evolution in how transformations are written in Spark by default. You declare the data you want to produce, and the platform applies proven execution semantics for you. Built on an open, declarative standard for Spark pipelines, SDP helps teams move faster on hard problems while scaling production best practices consistently.In this session, you’ll see how SDP: Provides a necessary foundation for agentic data pipeline development Reduces the amount of custom logic required for incremental and CDC workloads Makes batch and streaming pipelines easier to build, debug, and evolve over time Standardizes reliability and correctness across pipelines as they scale We’ll walk through concrete examples that show how declarative pipelines simplify otherwise complex data engineering patterns.
description_raw:
Building data pipelines often means hand-writing complex data engineering patterns like incremental processing, CDC/SCD handling, and backfills in every job.Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines (SDP) are the next evolution in how transformations are written in Spark by default. You declare the data you want to produce, and the platform applies proven execution semantics for you. Built on an open, declarative standard for Spark pipelines, SDP helps teams move faster on hard problems while scaling production best practices consistently.In this session, you’ll see how SDP:

  • Provides a necessary foundation for agentic data pipeline development
  • Reduces the amount of custom logic required for incremental and CDC workloads
  • Makes batch and streaming pipelines easier to build, debug, and evolve over time
  • Standardizes reliability and correctness across pipelines as they scale

We’ll walk through concrete examples that show how declarative pipelines simplify otherwise complex data engineering patterns.

speakers.name: Ray Zhu
speakers.bio: Ray Zhu is a Director of Product Management at Databricks, where he focuses on data engineering and streaming products. He's passionate about building products that empower data practitioners of different skillsets to simplify and scale their data pipelines, enabling greater customer and business value.

speakers.bio: Zoé is a Staff Product Manager on Spark Declarative Pipelines at Databricks. She holds an MBA from Stanford University, and an MS and BS in Applied mathematics from Ecole Polytechnique, France.

title: Introduction to Unified Data Engineering with Databricks Lakeflow
abstract: Teams today struggle with pipeline fragility, brittle orchestration, and tool sprawl that slows delivery and increases maintenance costs. Lakeflow eliminates that friction by unifying ingestion, transformation, and orchestration on the Data Intelligence Platform, with reliability and governance built into the foundation.This session shows how Databricks Lakeflow turns Spark into the most advanced execution engine for data engineering on the planet— with agentic data pipelines underpinned by managed ingestion connectors, declarative transformations, native orchestration, and intelligent incremental processing built into execution, not layered on top.

Lakeflow eliminates that friction by unifying ingestion, transformation, and orchestration on the Data Intelligence Platform, with reliability and governance built into the foundation.This session shows how Databricks Lakeflow turns Spark into the most advanced execution engine for data engineering on the planet— with agentic data pipelines underpinned by managed ingestion connectors, declarative transformations, native orchestration, and intelligent incremental processing built into execution, not layered on top.

speakers.name: Manish Dalwadi
speakers.bio: Manish works on Lakeflow, Databricks' unified, streamlined and perfomant data engineering platform. Prior to Databricks he led product teams across BigQuery and Compute Engine at Google and helped build Amazon Aurora at AWS.

title: KISS-ing Your Data+AI Platform: Rapid Multi-Cloud Migration to Databricks
abstract: Faced with a strategic shift toward marketplace autonomy, Adevinta transformed its global data+AI strategy by migrating from complex DIY stacks on GCP and AWS to a unified Databricks platform in under 4 ¿? months. This session, led by our Principal Architect and Product Director, provides a deep dive into how we overcame technical and organisational "old ways of working" to achieve this high-velocity transition. Beyond data engineering and data warehousing capabilities, we will focus on how we migrated our AI/ML platform to Databricks, demonstrating how a unified foundation in Unity Catalog and the Databricks AI stack has accelerated our migration and time to production, and how this foundation is now helping in our ambition to unlock even more value from data+AI across the company.
description_raw: Faced with a strategic shift toward marketplace autonomy, Adevinta transformed its global data+AI strategy by migrating from complex DIY stacks on GCP and AWS to a unified Databricks platform in under 4 ¿? months. This session, led by our Principal Architect and Product Director, provides a deep dive into how we overcame technical and organisational "old ways of working" to achieve this high-velocity transition. Beyond data engineering and data warehousing capabilities, we will focus on how we migrated our AI/ML platform to Databricks, demonstrating how a unified foundation in Unity Catalog and the Databricks AI stack has accelerated our migration and time to production, and how this foundation is now helping in our ambition to unlock even more value from data+AI across the company.

title: KRAFTON at Scale: Architecting Real-Time Game AI with MLflow & Serving
abstract: This session explores KRAFTON's journey of deploying real-time AI for PUBG: BATTLEGROUNDS, a title with 200M+ users. We share how a lean engineering team overcame global-scale challenges using Databricks. 1. Real-time anti-cheat MLOps: We detail the transition from an hourly batch system to a sub-minute real-time pipeline using Structured Streaming and an online feature store. By optimizing Model Serving endpoints, we drastically reduced latency and slashed costs to 1/10th, enabling just three engineers to support a massive global player base. 2. Esports win prediction: We discuss the serving strategy for Cox PH models to handle 64-player dynamics. Solutions include sequence buffering to ensure data ordering and incremental feature engineering for O(1) efficiency, delivering stable predictions with sub-200ms latency.We conclude with Databricks MLOps patterns bridging the gap between research and production, alongside our roadmap for next-gen VLM-based anti-cheat.

title: Lakebase 101: core workflows and the Lakebase experience
abstract: New to Lakebase? This session walks you through the core workflows that define the Lakebase experience: provisioning a database, connecting your first application, using branching for safe development, applying point-in-time recovery, and controlling costs with autoscaling. Through live demos, you'll see how each feature maps to real-world scenarios and why running your operational database directly on the lakehouse changes what's possible for applications, analytics, and AI. No prior Lakebase experience required.

speakers.name: Youssef Mrini
speakers.bio: Youssef Mrini is part of the pre-sales team in the EMEA Region at Databricks and helps customers to adopt the databricks technologies. Youssef is an ex-data engineer. He holds a degree in big Data and have been working on various areas for the last couple of years

title: Lakebase and Serving Endpoints Implementation in Real-Time Pricing
abstract: This breakout session presents a real-world, one of the first in Europe's FSI Industry, production implementation of Databricks Lakebase and Serving Endpoints for real-time pricing in a large-scale insurance environment. We will walk through how we implemented Databricks-native architecture for low-latency data and model access via REST APIs calls. New architecture is a replacement of external Azure PostgreSQL database and intermediate applications layers.Based on experience at one of the largest insurance companies in Poland we will share how Lakebase was stress-tested on high data volumes and is being introduced to production using baby-steps approach. The session includes architecture decisions, performance observations and code-level examples.Attendees will gain the practical guidance on building end-to-end, real-time and data model serving within Databricks along with a clear understanding of how Lakebase fits into architecture.

title: Lakeflow Connect: Cutting Enterprise Ingestion Costs by up to 90% on Databricks
abstract: Enterprise CRM-to-lakehouse pipelines built on traditional third-party ingestion tools can be expensive, time-consuming to implement, and often deliver stale data batches. We replaced this with Databricks Lakeflow Connect for a Fortune 500 client — significantly reducing monthly costs, cutting setup time from weeks to hours, and achieving near-real-time CDC ingestion. This talk delivers a live 7-step walkthrough: connector selection, OAuth setup, DLT pipeline config, object selection, Unity Catalog destination mapping, CDC monitoring, and production scheduling. Takeaways include a cost justification framework demonstrating significant multi-year TCO savings, a migration blueprint from legacy and cloud-native ingestion tools, and governance best practices with Unity Catalog lineage.
description_raw:
Enterprise CRM-to-lakehouse pipelines built on traditional third-party ingestion tools can be expensive, time-consuming to implement, and often deliver stale data batches. We replaced this with Databricks Lakeflow Connect for a Fortune 500 client — significantly reducing monthly costs, cutting setup time from weeks to hours, and achieving near-real-time CDC ingestion.

This talk delivers a live 7-step walkthrough: connector selection, OAuth setup, DLT pipeline config, object selection, Unity Catalog destination mapping, CDC monitoring, and production scheduling.

Takeaways include a cost justification framework demonstrating significant multi-year TCO savings, a migration blueprint from legacy and cloud-native ingestion tools, and governance best practices with Unity Catalog lineage.

speakers.name: Rishabh Pandey
speakers.bio: Rishabh Pandey is a Databricks MVP (2025, 2026) and data engineering professional at Celebal Technologies, leading end-to-end delivery from solution design to performance optimization and debugging complex ETL pipelines. He specializes in Delta Lake, Structured Streaming, Delta Live Tables (DLT), and Unity Catalog, with strong hands-on expertise in PySpark, Spark SQL, and cloud platforms across Azure and AWS. A Databricks Solutions Architect Champion and member of the Product and Technical Council, he holds 26 Databricks certifications and focuses on enabling scalable Lakehouse solutions, data quality, and reconciliation frameworks for global enterprises.

title: Lakeflow Designer: No-code ETL— Authored With AI, Built for Production
abstract: As demand for data grows, central data teams can’t build every transformation themselves. But traditional “self-service” ETL tools often introduce new problems: logic leaves governed platforms, pipelines live in parallel runtimes, and productionization requires costly rewrites by data engineering teams. Lakeflow Designer addresses this gap by giving analysts a no-code, AI-first way to do data prep on the same Lakeflow execution model used by data engineering teams. Instead of introducing a parallel toolchain, Designer produces governed, production-grade transformations that fit directly into existing data engineering workflows. In this session, you’ll see how Lakeflow Designer: Enables analysts to do data prep without moving data outside Databricks Preserves centralized governance, quality, and lineage through Unity Catalog Lets data engineers scale pipeline development by directly operationalizing designer-authored logic— without rebuilding, translating, or refactoring
description_raw:
As demand for data grows, central data teams can’t build every transformation themselves. But traditional “self-service” ETL tools often introduce new problems: logic leaves governed platforms, pipelines live in parallel runtimes, and productionization requires costly rewrites by data engineering teams.

Lakeflow Designer addresses this gap by giving analysts a no-code, AI-first way to do data prep on the same Lakeflow execution model used by data engineering teams. Instead of introducing a parallel toolchain, Designer produces governed, production-grade transformations that fit directly into existing data engineering workflows.

In this session, you’ll see how Lakeflow Designer:

  • Enables analysts to do data prep without moving data outside Databricks
  • Preserves centralized governance, quality, and lineage through Unity Catalog
  • Lets data engineers scale pipeline development by directly operationalizing designer-authored logic— without rebuilding, translating, or refactoring

speakers.name: Jason Messer
speakers.bio: Jason Messer is a Product Manager on the Developer Experience team at Databricks, focusing on the Notebook and SQL editor. Before joining Databricks, Jason was one of the first software engineers at an applied AI startup, where he developed a passion for product management and creating simple, empowering products. This led him to Microsoft Azure, where gained valuable experience as he helped drive the Linux and cloud-native computing strategy as a PM. Jason holds a Computer Science degree from Brigham Young University.

title: Lakeflow Designer: Removing the Technical Barrier to Self-Service Data Prep
abstract: For most teams, the biggest bottleneck isn't the generation of insights it is the work thats required to get the data wrangled the right way. For too long, the ability to transform data and automate workflows has been locked behind complex code, leaving business analysts dependent on overstretched data engineering teams.Lakeflow Designer changes the equation with a no-code, visual canvas powered by Genie that lets anyone go from a natural language prompt to a production-ready workflow in minutes.This breakout will cover:- How Lakeflow Designer fits into the overall Databricks ecosystem and what differentiates it from other low-code tools- How to get started and leverage natural language through Genie to build a workflow with zero previous experience- How Designer’s visual workflows translate into production-grade code that is governed, scalable, and ready for enterprise use

speakers.name: Emanuel Zgraggen
speakers.bio: Emanuel Zgraggen is an engineer at Databricks building AI-native tools for data and analytics. He previously co-founded Einblick, a collaborative visual data platform acquired by Databricks, and holds a Ph.D. in Computer Science focused on human-computer interaction, data visualization, and machine learning.

title: Lakeflow Jobs: Modern Orchestration for the Data Platform
abstract: Modern data platforms need more than a scheduler. They need orchestration that understands data, integrates with the platform, and scales with both engineering and AI workloads. Join this session to see how Lakeflow Jobs helps teams orchestrate pipelines, notebooks, dbt projects, SQL, and external components, natively on the Databricks Platform.We will show how Lakeflow Jobs goes beyond basic scheduling with data-aware orchestration that understands the assets and systems it is coordinating. You will see how teams can trigger workflows based on data changes, build modular workflows in Python, orchestrate external systems alongside Databricks workloads, and use Declarative Automation Bundles (DABs) for a more reliable CI/CD path.We will also cover how Serverless Jobs and observability features such as lineage and system tables help teams reduce operational overhead and better understand what is happening across their workflows. Finally, we will preview how agentic authoring and diagnostics can speed up building, troubleshooting, and improving production jobs, and how you can embed agents directly into your workflows.Whether you are modernizing from legacy schedulers or building new workloads on the lakehouse, this session will share practical patterns, product direction, and live demos for modern orchestration on Databricks.

speakers.name: Saad Ansari
speakers.bio: Saad is a product manager at Databricks working on Data Orchestration (Workflows) and Developer tooling teams. Prior to this he was CTO and co-founder at Connecterra using Artificial Intelligence to improve sustainability, productivity and animal welfare in the Dairy Industry (think Fitbit for cows!). Saad has also worked as a PM and Developer at Microsoft. He holds a masters in computer science from Stanford.

title: Large scale Member and Claims Data Platform modernization Using Databricks on AWS GovCloud
abstract: Johns Hopkins is undergoing a major cloud transformation, moving from a legacy SQL Server EDW to a modern, scalable data and analytics platform. In this session, the JHHP and CitiusTech team will share best practices for designing and implementing an enterprise‑grade Databricks platform on AWS GovCloud—critical for supporting Johns Hopkins’ partnerships with the armed forces and Tricare. The session highlights architectural principles for building a modular, ingestion‑ready platform that unifies diverse data sources, along with results such as reducing workload runtimes from 22+ hours to 2–3 hours for full loads and under an hour for incremental runs. Presenters will also cover lessons learned in optimizing pipelines, using a Brickbuilder‑certified metadata ingestion framework, and enabling a strong data‑quality engine. Attendees will leave with practical guidance for large‑scale cloud modernization in regulated environments while creating reusable, enterprise‑wide data assets.

speakers.name: Tyler Sipe
speakers.bio: Experienced healthcare technology and analytics leader with a proven track record of driving innovation and efficiency in healthcare systems. Expertise in leveraging data analytics and emerging technologies to optimize member care, streamline operations, and improve outcomes. Skilled in leading cross-functional teams, implementing strategic initiatives, and navigating complex regulatory environments. Passionate about using technology to address healthcare challenges and enhance member experiences. Looking to leverage my skills and experience to drive positive change in the healthcare industry.

speakers.name: Riz Qazi
speakers.bio: Strategic Thought Leader with over 23 years of extensive experience in Healthcare and Information Technology with 15 years of those leading and managing complex multi-million dollars programs and business transformation initiatives. Demonstrated expertise in cultivating and leading high-performing geographically dispersed teams in support of ROI-backed advances in Product delivery and Digital transformation initiatives. Managed multiple platform migrations to support Admin cost reductions. Adept at cultivating strong relationships with stakeholders, fostering collaboration, and implementing innovative solutions to enhance operational efficiency.

title: Latest innovations in BI and AI for business users
abstract: Databricks is setting a new standard for BI—combining unified semantics with AI-powered agents to truly deliver on the promise of Agentic Business Intelligence.Whether through natural language with Genie, AI/BI Dashboards, or a combination of both, users can move seamlessly between conversational and visual analysis in a single, integrated experience.In this session, we’ll cover:- An end-to-end walkthrough of agentic BI on Databricks- The latest innovations and customizations capabilities to simplify access for business users- How unified semantics power consistent, trusted answers across Genie and AI/BI Dashboards- How agents, Genie, and dashboards work together to accelerate analysis- How conversational and visual workflows come together to accelerate insights and actions

title: Latest innovations in Genie Code: The Future of Agentic Data Work
abstract: AI coding assistants have changed how developers write code, but most stop at suggestions. Genie Code is an autonomous AI agent designed specifically for data teams. It doesn't just help you write code. It plans and executes full workflows, and it monitors your production systems in the background, triaging and fixing pipeline failures before your team even notices.In this session, we'll walk through the newest capabilities in Genie Code and how they change the way data work gets done. We'll cover:- What’s new in Genie Code- How it builds and maintains production data and AI workflows end to end, from pipeline creation to model serving observability- How Unity Catalog integration gives Genie Code the enterprise context, governance, and accuracy that general-purpose agents lack- Best practices for working with Genie Code as an autonomous partner, including Agent Skills and persistent memory

speakers.name: Weston Hutchins
speakers.bio: Weston Hutchins is a PM Lead on the Developer Experience and Applied AI teams at Databricks. Before Databricks, he led PM teams at Google and Microsoft. He has over 18 years experience as a PM, launching a number of infrastructure and developer-focused products across Google Cloud, Azure and Visual Studio. Weston holds a degree in Computer and Electrical Engineering from the University of Florida.

title: Learn How We Secure and Govern Lakewatch on the Databricks Trusted Lakehouse
abstract: Security data is among the most sensitive in the enterprise, yet many SIEM deployments lack consistent, fine-grained governance. Learn how Lakewatch leverages Unity Catalog to provide centralized governance across your entire security ecosystem.We will dive deep into the Lakewatch RBAC model, demonstrating how to manage Principals (Users, Groups, and Service Principals) across protected resources like Datasources, Detection Rules, and Notables. You’ll learn how to implement Lakewatch’s unique inherited permission model—where cases automatically inherit context from parent resources—to streamline investigations while maintaining strict "need-to-know" access.Takeaways include:-Implementing table, row, and column-level security for telemetry-Defining Viewer, Creator, and Manager scopes at Workspace and Resource levels-Practical patterns for SOC, Cloud, and Data teams to collaborate safely-Sharing data with partners via Delta Sharing without compromising auditability or lineage.

speakers.name: Arun Pamulapati
speakers.bio: Arun is a core member of Databricks' Field security practice and has 25 years of experience in building data products, including over a decade of experience in building AI products. Arun led and co-created the Security Analysis Tool (SAT) and SSPM for Databricks, he is coauthor of Databricks, and AI Security Framework (DASF). Arun has a bachelor’s in computer science, holds Cloud security and AI/ML industry certifications, and contributes to HITRUST and FAIR Institute AI workgroups.

title: Less Infrastructure, Higher Impact: Nationwide’s Serverless Transformation with Databricks
abstract: Simplifying infrastructure accelerates data work and drives outcomes. The future of analytics isn’t about managing clusters; it’s about empowering users with seamless, scalable compute. In this session, Nationwide and Databricks share a real-world journey from server-managed infrastructure to Databricks Serverless, covering all-purpose compute, jobs compute, and SQL warehouses. We show how serverless simplified operations, sped up development cycles, reduced cost variability, and improved the user experience. We highlight the biggest wins, explore key design decisions that shaped success, and close with practical best practices to help your organization confidently accelerate its move to serverless.

speakers.name: Praveena Edward Panneer Selvam
speakers.bio: Praveena Edward is the Lead Data Engineer for the Data Management and Governance team at Nationwide Insurance. She is currently responsible for leading the UC migration effort, which involves the complex transition of tables from HMS to UC. As one of the first teams to implement HMS Federation, she has been at the forefront of solving migration challenges. Her work includes enabling sensitive data frameworks and supporting partner onboarding through scalable and secure data solutions. Prior to this role, Praveena brought several years of experience in data engineering, including architecting and developing enterprise-scale data warehousing applications and delivering quality solutions through collaboration and thoughtful planning.

title: Let AI Work on Your Database: Safe AI-Driven Development with Database Branches
abstract: AI coding agents have drastically improved the way we work in so many areas of software development, but because LLMs are inherently non-deterministic, many developers are still hesitant to let AI agents touch their databases because of the risk of destructive actions. In this talk, we’ll show how to safely leverage AI in real workflows using Lakebase’s branching capabilities. With the tools and strategies we’ll discuss, you will be able to create isolated code and data environments that enable AI coding agents to build in parallel, experiment safely, and iterate faster, without risking production data. By the end of this talk, attendees will walk away with practical patterns to integrate AI into their development workflows with confidence.

title: Let Your Agent Pilot Your Fleet: Business Insights From 35,000 Feet
abstract: We run a data platform at Thales IFE that tracks how millions of passengers interact with in-flight entertainment at 35,000 feet. While the data was widely available, business teams still depended on analysts to answer questions or build yet another dashboard. This session shows how we made an AI assistant the primary interface for business and operational insight using Databricks Apps and how it was integrated with existing business systems and portals. We walk through a real scenario: A business user opens a Databricks App, interacts with the AI agent and explores passenger usage & system performance data. The same agent can also be embedded into existing business applications, bringing consistent data access directly into daily workflows. The talk focuses on outcomes and lessons learned: What Databricks simplified, where challenges appeared and what it takes to move AI agents from experiment to production for our business users.
description_raw:
We run a data platform at Thales IFE that tracks how millions of passengers interact with in-flight entertainment at 35,000 feet. While the data was widely available, business teams still depended on analysts to answer questions or build yet another dashboard.

The talk focuses on outcomes and lessons learned: What Databricks simplified, where challenges appeared and what it takes to move AI agents from experiment to production for our business users.

speakers.name: Simon PREVOTEAUX
speakers.bio: Simon Prevoteaux is a Principal Data Scientist at Thales, leading the global data and AI platform for the company's In-Flight Entertainment and Connectivity business. He specializes in building end-to-end data products on Databricks — from lakehouse architecture to deployed AI models serving airline customers. A Databricks Certified Data Engineer and GenAI Engineer, Simon brings eight years of experience delivering data platforms across aviation, space, and defense. When he's not wrangling data, you'll find him catching waves on Florida's Space Coast.

speakers.name: Yann Nicolas
speakers.bio: International technology leader with 16+ years in IT, having lived and worked across multiple countries. Currently Chief Data & AI Officer at Thales, I hold a Master's in Computer Science and have led the design and delivery of complex Cloud, Data, and AI solutions across In-Flight Entertainment and Connectivity, HR, Retail, Insurance, Air Traffic Management, and Urban Security. At Thales InFlyt Experience, I lead an engineering team building transformative AI and Data use cases for aircraft passengers and airlines, while empowering employees with AI-driven capabilities. Grounded in cloud platforms and end-to-end architecture, I'm passionate about turning data into business leverage. My multicultural experience and track record span technical leadership and people management — building high-performing teams, driving innovation, and mentoring staff for success. Outside work, I still love coding for fun and training for the next marathon.

title: lululemon: How We Modernized Our Audience Segmentation and Where It's Taking Us With Agentic AI
abstract: At lululemon, our CRM Audience Data Science team operates PAVE—an ML platform scoring millions of guest profiles for personalized marketing across email, push, web and paid media.With growth came pain: data scientists managing deployments instead of building models, no path to production, and marketing waiting weeks for new segments. The bottleneck was never the model. We came to DAIS 2025 with these problems and left with a plan: DABS, Unity Catalog, and MLflow 3.0 as the foundation for a live system.Key areas we will cover: DABS: parameterized pipelines that any team member can deploy in one command Multi-stream architecture where each new model type inherits the same pipeline pattern Unity Catalog migration seeding an enterprise feature store now scaling beyond PAVE MLflow 3.0 for unified tracking, registry and model promotion across all streams Agentic AI roadmap: next best action for marketing partners and generative model monitoring
description_raw:
At lululemon, our CRM Audience Data Science team operates PAVE—an ML platform scoring millions of guest profiles for personalized marketing across email, push, web and paid media.With growth came pain: data scientists managing deployments instead of building models, no path to production, and marketing waiting weeks for new segments. The bottleneck was never the model. We came to DAIS 2025 with these problems and left with a plan: DABS, Unity Catalog, and MLflow 3.0 as the foundation for a live system.Key areas we will cover:

  • DABS: parameterized pipelines that any team member can deploy in one command
  • Multi-stream architecture where each new model type inherits the same pipeline pattern
  • Unity Catalog migration seeding an enterprise feature store now scaling beyond PAVE
  • MLflow 3.0 for unified tracking, registry and model promotion across all streams
  • Agentic AI roadmap: next best action for marketing partners and generative model monitoring

speakers.name: domenic fayad
speakers.bio: Domenic is a full-stack Data Scientist on the Audience Science team at Lululemon - building the production ML systems that decide what email you receive, what push notification you open, what social media ad finds you, and more. With 7+ years shipping end-to-end data products with Databricks, his work centers on applied ML and causal inference: uplift modeling, propensity modeling, and incrementality measurement that drive decisions at scale. Endlessly curious, inspired by technology and sports, and a firm believer in continuous growth. Occasionally vibe codes.

speakers.name: jeroen ruissen
speakers.bio: Jeroen started his career as a Data Scientist following a Masters in Mathematics from the University of Amsterdam and currently leads the Audience Data Science team of lululemon. He has worked across industries in end-to-end model development, and gets a lot of energy from creating ML solutions that deliver real business impact. When not working, he's running around the Vancouver seawall in lululemon's running kit.

title: Manufacturing & Transportation Industry Forum | Sponsored by: SAP
abstract: The factory floor, the assembly line and the supply chain have always been defined by precision, and now artificial intelligence is raising the bar. Join fellow manufacturing and automotive leaders for a deep dive into how Databricks' Data Intelligence Platform is powering the next era of Industrial AI. From real-time sensor data to unified lakehouses that eliminate data silos, discover how the world’s most complex industrial operations are turning raw data into competitive advantage. This forum will explore the critical imperatives reshaping the industry: predictive and prescriptive maintenance that reduces unplanned downtime, AI-driven quality inspection, connected vehicle analytics, supply chain resilience, and the convergence of OT and IT data. Databricks brings together the governance, scalability, and open ecosystem needed to move from pilot to production across every plant, line, and model. Hear directly from industry innovators who are already leading the transformation and leave with actionable frameworks for deploying Industrial AI at scale.

speakers.name: Shiv Trisal

He regularly connects with senior executives to unfold data & AI strategies and unlock competitive advantage in their businesses. Shiv majored in Electronics Engineering from the Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology in Punjab, India and holds a MBA from the Wharton School at the University of Pennsylvania.

speakers.name: Matthias Gohl
speakers.bio: 09/2023- today: CDO TK Elevator 2017-2022: Global Head of ZEISS Digital Innovation Partners 2014-2016: Global Head of Strategy and M+A ZEISS Group 2007-2014: McKinsey & Company 2005-2007: German Ministry for the Environment (BMU)

speakers.name: Christian Jung
speakers.job_title: VP of Digital, Data & AI Foundation
speakers.bio: Christian Jung leads Digital, Data & AI Foundation at TK Elevator and has been driving TKE’s digital journey since 2016. His work spans IoT-based digital products for internal operations and customer-facing services, including MAX, TKE’s global IoT and predictive maintenance solution. He has built and scaled data capabilities and teams, leading semantic, AI-ready data foundations that create trusted insights and business value. More recently, his focus has expanded to agentic AI in service operations, combining connected asset data, enterprise context, and AI to support context-aware field service.

speakers.name: Dora Tien
speakers.bio: Dora leads the Supply Chain Systems & Analytics team at Xylem, where she focuses on modernizing enterprise planning technologies and advancing data maturity to enable AI readiness and autonomous decision-making. She brings cross-industry experience across automotive, retail, and aerospace, with prior roles at Tesla, Rivian, Amazon, and General Electric. Dora specializes in product management for custom supply chain software solutions, combining deep operational expertise with a passion for building intuitive, data-driven systems.

title: Marketing Forum
abstract: Marketing is the most AI-ready function in the enterprise: always-on, data-rich and powered by continuous experimentation. But realizing that potential requires humans, agents and applications to work together on a shared foundation of trusted, real-time context. The CMOs who define the next decade will be the ones who treat that foundation as a core marketing imperative. Join marketing leaders for a deep dive into how Databricks’ unified data and AI platform is powering agentic marketing transformation. From unified Customer 360 to autonomous campaign orchestration, discover how the most ambitious marketing organizations are turning their architecture into a competitive advantage. This forum explores the imperatives reshaping the function: proving marketing impact in real time, personalizing at scale without multiplying fragmented data and moving from manual execution to outcome-driven AI workflows that adapt as channels, models and customer behavior evolve. Through candid conversations and real-world results, hear directly from marketing leaders already delivering measurable outcomes and leave with a practical playbook for building the governed data and AI foundation that makes agentic marketing trustworthy at scale.

title: Media & Entertainment Industry Forum
abstract: AI in media: what’s hype and what’s really possible? Join us at the Media & Advertising Industry Forum to learn how industry leaders are unlocking the full potential of AI, launching production grade solutions that achieve real business outcomes. Hear from leaders across Media, Advertising, Gaming and Sports to learn how Databricks solutions are helping them drive value from maximizing advertising ROI to unlocking real time hyper personalization and superior audience experience. Discover how data and AI will continue to shape the future of audience and fan engagement, ad monetization and content production.
