speakers.name: Chandos Quill
speakers.bio: Chandos has 25 years of experience building global solutions for clients across Data, Digital Advertising and Marketing Technologies. She is a proven expert in data-driven marketing by delivering revenue through leadership roles across partnerships, product management and corporate development functions. In her role as SVP, Channel and Alliances at Epsilon, Chandos is responsible for driving incremental revenue and profitability for the business by extending Epsilon products into new channels and markets as well as creating new solutions that add value to Epsilon clients.

speakers.name: Alan Ochoa
speakers.bio: An accomplished Digital Transformation and Data Activation expert with over 15 years experience managing global strategies, marketing campaigns and digital experiences. Alan has a passion to develop digital strategies that truly enhance the consumer experience.

title: Meet Bricksy - The Hyper-Personalized, Privacy-First AI Copilot for the Software-Defined Vehicle
abstract: As automotive industry pivots to the Software-Defined Vehicle (SDV), the challenge has shifted from collecting data to interacting with it in real-time. Drivers demand a seamless, personalized experience, while OEMs demand data sovereignty and low-latency interventions.Bricksy, a next-gen AI agent powered entirely by Databricks is an android app. We'll demonstrate a unified architecture for building voice-enabled driver companion that thinks, remembers, and protects having:1. Real-Time Nervous System: Vehicle telemetry (Engine Temp, TPMS) streams directly into Unity Catalog via Zerobus, enabling near real-time anomaly detection.2. Infinite Memory & Context: How Bricksy utilizes Vector Search and Lakebase to instantly recall car manuals, service history, and long-term driver preferences.3. AI That Comes to Your Data: How manufacturers can deploy proprietary tools and functions directly on the Lakehouse, ensuring sensitive telemetry never leaves their governance boundary.

speakers.name: Hussain Jagirdar
speakers.bio: Hussain Jagirdar is a GenAI Architect at Databricks based in Bengaluru, India, with over 7 years of experience in AI and Engineering. He builds compound AI systems that connect LLM agents to real-world hardware - most recently Bricksy, an MCP-powered AI copilot for smart vehicles. He holds a Master's degree in Computer Science from IIT Kharagpur.

title: Metric Views — What They Are, How They Work, and How to Model Them
abstract: Databricks has always had a strong foundation for data engineering, but one thing has been missing: a semantic layer that lives inside the platform. Metric Views finally change that.With Metric Views, measures and models are defined directly in Unity Catalog and reachable from notebooks, the SQL editor, dashboards, and Genie. Data engineers and analysts are finally working from the same definitions, no more translating DAX back into SQL to understand what a number means.In this session we will walk through what Metric Views are and what the setup looks like in practice. Then we will look at the modeling decisions, whether to build small specialized views or broader shared ones, how those choices affect reuse and consistency, and how they affect what Genie returns and how dashboards behave.New to metric views or already using them, you will leave with a clearer picture of how to approach them.

title: MGM Enterprise Data Hub: Accelerating Data Delivery With DABS CI/CD Approach
abstract: MGM Resorts is unifying our data estate, leveraging Unity Catalog for centralized governance, access control and cross‑environment consistency across gaming, hospitality and partner ecosystems. We updated our framework with DABS reducing delivery time for gold tables by 67%, enabling automated deployment and validation in under 5 min, cutting deployment code by 75%.We: Enabled isolation and targeted lifecycle management via a multi-bundle architecture with shared utilities in a common wheel Improved reliability with a GitHub Actions pipeline for sequential DEV → UAT → PROD deployments, with consistent governance across environments using Unity Catalog Freed engineers to focus on business logic through centralized configuration, SCD handling, and Apache Spark™ lifecycle management Key takeaways: CI/CD best practices using standardized databricks.yml Higher reliability and faster delivery through a bundle-based pattern Metrics and lessons to drive low-risk, predictable releases across the org
description_raw:
MGM Resorts is unifying our data estate, leveraging Unity Catalog for centralized governance, access control and cross‑environment consistency across gaming, hospitality and partner ecosystems. We updated our framework with DABS reducing delivery time for gold tables by 67%, enabling automated deployment and validation in under 5 min, cutting deployment code by 75%.We:

  • Enabled isolation and targeted lifecycle management via a multi-bundle architecture with shared utilities in a common wheel
  • Improved reliability with a GitHub Actions pipeline for sequential DEV → UAT → PROD deployments, with consistent governance across environments using Unity Catalog
  • Freed engineers to focus on business logic through centralized configuration, SCD handling, and Apache Spark™ lifecycle management

Key takeaways:

  • CI/CD best practices using standardized databricks.yml
  • Higher reliability and faster delivery through a bundle-based pattern
  • Metrics and lessons to drive low-risk, predictable releases across the org

speakers.name: Jennifer Young
speakers.bio: Jennifer has built her career at the intersection of data management, data engineering, governance, and business intelligence, working across industries including government, healthcare, retail, technology, and casino hospitality. She has led high‑performing data teams within large, complex organizations and currently serves as Vice President of Data at MGM Resorts International. Previously, Jennifer was a Data & AI Specialist at Microsoft, where she partnered with enterprise clients on data strategy and Azure implementation. A Las Vegas resident of over 20 years, she enjoys crafting, reading, dining out, traveling, and spending time with her four‑year‑old Boston Terrier.

title: Migrate to Lakeflow Jobs: Modernizing Data Orchestration on Databricks
abstract: Still running orchestration outside Databricks? That often means more tools to manage, less context about your data, and more overhead connecting pipelines, schedules, and dependencies across systems.Join this session to learn how to migrate to Lakeflow Jobs, Databricks’ native orchestrator for data and AI workloads. We will cover practical migration patterns, the tooling available to accelerate the move, and the tips, techniques, and best practices that help teams reduce risk and avoid common pitfalls.You will learn: Why teams are consolidating orchestration onto Databricks How common migration patterns from legacy orchestrators map to Lakeflow Jobs What tooling, tips, and techniques can speed up migration and simplify rollout The best practices that help teams move faster with less operational overhead What real customer migrations look like in practice, including key challenges, tradeoffs, and outcomes
description_raw:
Still running orchestration outside Databricks? We will cover practical migration patterns, the tooling available to accelerate the move, and the tips, techniques, and best practices that help teams reduce risk and avoid common pitfalls.You will learn:

  • Why teams are consolidating orchestration onto Databricks
  • How common migration patterns from legacy orchestrators map to Lakeflow Jobs
  • What tooling, tips, and techniques can speed up migration and simplify rollout
  • The best practices that help teams move faster with less operational overhead
  • What real customer migrations look like in practice, including key challenges, tradeoffs, and outcomes

speakers.name: Saad Ansari
speakers.bio: Saad is a product manager at Databricks working on Data Orchestration (Workflows) and Developer tooling teams. Prior to this he was CTO and co-founder at Connecterra using Artificial Intelligence to improve sustainability, productivity and animal welfare in the Dairy Industry (think Fitbit for cows!). Saad has also worked as a PM and Developer at Microsoft. He holds a masters in computer science from Stanford.

title: Migrating to Serverless: The Databricks Journey
abstract: The future of the Data Intelligence Platform is Serverless, and Databricks is leading by example. We aren't just telling customers to migrate; the Data Platform team is actively moving our own mission-critical ETL and BI workloads to Databricks Serverless SQL and Compute first. This session chronicles our internal migration journey. We will share the raw "before and after" metrics on cost, latency, and engineering overhead. We will be transparent about the challenges we faced as early adopters and the specific best practices we developed to run a massive-scale, serverless-first platform that powers the business.

speakers.name: Bruce Wong
speakers.bio: Bruce leads our internal Data Platform function at Databricks. We build, own and operate our enterprise lakehouse for Databricks where all employees and all teams inside use Data and AI to solve problems and guide our decisions. Previously Bruce held technical leadership roles at Netflix, Twilio and StitchFix where he gained deep experience building planet scale systems in a cloud-native environment. Bruce also formed the Chaos Engineering team and led the initiative at Netflix to achieve ultimate resilience. Bruce has also shares his experience and insights serving on product advisory boards previously for DataDog, PagerDuty, currently for DataBricks.

title: Millisecond Pricing: High-Velocity Feature Serving on the Lakebase
abstract: Fragmented architectures kill pricing precision. Real-time systems require millisecond latency across millions of features without training-serving skew. We replace slow, siloed pipelines with a high-velocity pricing engine built on the Databricks Lakebase, Tecton, and Fennel with Genie and Apps.Technical Highlights:- millisec Serving: OnlineFS provides rapid online serving for millions of features to power complex pricing models.- Rapid Updates: Fennel enables instantaneous feature updates, allowing the online system to react to data shifts in real-time.- Unified Foundation: Databricks Lakebase provides the high-performance backbone for governed, enterprise-scale feature retrieval at scale.- Intelligent Analytics: Databricks Genie and integrated dashboards deliver real-time visibility into pricing performance and financial risk.See how to build a scalable, autonomous blueprint for real-time decisioning that bridges massive historical datasets with sub-millisecond execution.

speakers.name: Wawrzyniec Ryszka
speakers.bio: Laurence is a Senior Solutions Architect and Insurance SME at Databricks, based in London and covering the EMEA insurance and reinsurance market. He works with carriers, reinsurers and Lloyd's syndicates on modernising pricing, actuarial workflows, claims and underwriting on the Lakehouse — helping insurers scale the tools they already rely on with the power of Databricks and agentic AI, without losing their actuarial rigour. Before Databricks, Laurence spent six years at WTW, working inside the pricing and capital modelling software that much of the industry runs on. He now partners with insurers to extend that foundation into governed, real-time, AI-native operations across the policy lifecycle.

speakers.name: Rahul Pathak
speakers.bio: Rahul Pathak is a Senior Specialist Solutions Engineer and AI Subject Matter Expert at Databricks, helping organizations unlock the full value of data, machine learning, and generative AI at scale. He specializes in designing and deploying modern AI and data platform solutions that enable enterprises to accelerate innovation, improve decision-making, and deliver measurable business outcomes. With deep expertise across data engineering, machine learning, MLOps, and AI applications, Rahul partners closely with customers to build scalable, production-ready solutions on the Databricks Intelligence Platform. He is passionate about bridging business and technology, helping teams turn complex data challenges into impactful, real-world solutions.

title: Mission-Critical Inference: Powering High-Scale AI in Production
abstract: You've shipped your model. Now it needs to serve millions of requests a day without blinking — at the right latency, the right cost, and with zero downtime. The architecture that handled your prototype won't cut it in production, and most teams learn that the hard way.This session walks through the architecture, tuning knobs, and operational patterns that let Databricks Custom Model Serving handle the world's most demanding inference workloads. You'll see how GPU autoscaling matches capacity to traffic, how request batching drives down cost per call, how traffic splitting enables safe rollouts, and how built-in observability lets you catch regressions before users do.Bring your hardest production challenges — you'll leave with concrete patterns for running high-scale AI inference reliably, and the playbook to keep it fast, governed, and cost-efficient as demand grows.

title: Mo’ Agents Mo’ Problems: How We Simplified Lakehouse Threat Hunting as a Service at Scale
abstract: Security teams face an impossible ratio of millions of events, thousands of alerts, and a handful of analysts. Most hunt reactively or not at all. We built a threat hunting platform on Databricks that flips this equation. Our architecture spans the full stack: Lakeflow DLT normalizes 20+ sources into OCSF, billions of events are governed in Unity Catalog, and detection pipelines form the foundation for specialized AI agents that hunt autonomously. These agents generate hypotheses, investigate the lakehouse, enrich IOCs via MCP tools, and produce findings without prompting. The critical constraint: agents never see raw telemetry. They query pre-aggregated views, keeping billion-row datasets out of the LLM context while preserving investigative depth. You'll learn how we architected safe agent-to-lakehouse communication, orchestration patterns for autonomous hunt cycles, and detection at scale, while hearing about our service delivery at petabyte scale.

speakers.name: Mike Saxton
speakers.bio: Mike Saxton is a Senior Cybersecurity Leader at Booz Allen Hamilton where he currently supports Security Operations efforts at the intersection of defense and Artificial Intelligence (AI) efforts. As a prior Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) and Deputy Program Manager to a large DoD Global Security Operations Center, Mike has established and rebuilt multiple cybersecurity programs; developed first of kind, innovative approaches to rapid detection and response of Cyber threats, and helped clients establish their cybersecurity strategy and vision. Additionally, Mike serves as a Regional Director for the Information Technology Disaster Resource Center (ITDRC), a non-profit organization that re-establishes IT communications after natural disasters.

title: Modern Data Modeling at Scale: Advanced Patterns
abstract: Unlock the truth behind data modeling in Databricks. This session will tackle the top 10 myths surrounding relational and dimensional data modeling. Attendees will gain a clear understanding of what Databricks Lakehouse truly supports today, including how to leverage primary and foreign keys, identity columns for surrogate keys, column-level data quality constraints and much more. This session will talk through the lens of medallion architecture, explaining how to implement data models across Bronze, Silver and Gold tables. Whether you’re migrating from a legacy warehouse or building new analytics solutions, you’ll leave equipped to fully leverage Databricks’ capabilities, and design scalable, high-performance data models for enterprise analytics.

speakers.name: Shannon Barrow
speakers.bio: Shannon has spent the last 6 years at Databricks helping enterprise customers modernize their analytics platforms to maximize value from their data and drive down the total cost of ownership. Specializing in performance for data engineering and SQL consumption, he is a leading SME in benchmarking and POC efforts across Enterprise North America. Before joining Databricks in 2019, he was a Principal in Accenture's Innovation and Thought Leadership team. Shannon has a Masters in Computer Science from Georgia Tech and 2 Bachelor Degrees from Florida State University.

speakers.name: Kyle Hale
speakers.bio: Kyle Hale’s journey into data and analytics began at age 8 when he created his first database to analyze baseball statistics, and hasn’t stopped yet. He’s a 20 year veteran of the data warehousing and BI space, having worn every hat from report development, modeling, and performance tuning to architecture, strategy, and governance. Having been a trusted advisor to well over 1,000 organizations big and small: Kyle has seen things.His proudest moment is being recognized by his peers at Databricks as a Data and Analytics SME - game knows game.When he’s not secretly plotting like all good BI professionals to kill Excel once and for all, he enjoys crafting synth pop soundtracks to imaginary movies, solving chess puzzles, and roaming the countryside with his wife and 4 kids in search of cool museums and awesome fried chicken. He is also an avid collector of dad jokes.

title: Modern Data Warehousing
abstract: This hands-on course is designed for data professionals who want to explore the data warehousing capabilities of Databricks. Assuming no prior knowledge of Databricks, it provides an introduction to leveraging Databricks as a modern cloud-based data warehousing solution. Learners will explore how use the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform to ingest, transform, govern, and analyze data efficiently. Learners will also explore Genie, an innovative Databricks feature that simplifies data exploration through natural language queries. By the end of this course, participants will be equipped with the foundational skills to implement and optimize a data warehouse using Databricks. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Basic SQL proficiency and understanding of data querying concepts General knowledge of data warehousing concepts, including tables, schemas, and ETL/ELT processes Some experience with BI or data visualization tools (recommended but not required)

  • Basic SQL proficiency and understanding of data querying concepts
  • General knowledge of data warehousing concepts, including tables, schemas, and ETL/ELT processes
  • Some experience with BI or data visualization tools (recommended but not required)

title: Modernization Without Illusions: Migrating a Business-Critical Equities Data Warehouse to Databricks
abstract: Transitioning a mission-critical data platform is rarely straightforward—especially when the legacy system already works and the environment is tightly regulated.This session shares real-world lessons from moving a high-stakes post-trade data platform to a cloud-native Databricks architecture, focusing on the trade-offs that determine whether teams merely migrate or truly land well. We explore how decisions around design, execution, and platform-native patterns shape performance, cost and business SLAs from day one—and why treating migration and modernization as a binary choice often leads to poor outcomes.Through concrete examples, we highlight how principle-driven interventions turned initially underperforming designs into stable, scalable solutions, offering a candid look at the realities of transitioning critical data platforms without sacrificing predictability or value.

We explore how decisions around design, execution, and platform-native patterns shape performance, cost and business SLAs from day one—and why treating migration and modernization as a binary choice often leads to poor outcomes.Through concrete examples, we highlight how principle-driven interventions turned initially underperforming designs into stable, scalable solutions, offering a candid look at the realities of transitioning critical data platforms without sacrificing predictability or value.

title: Modernizing Clinical Data Integration at Novo Nordisk With Lakebase and the Databricks Lakehouse
abstract: Novo Nordisk transformed how vendor-sourced and vendor-facing clinical trial data is ingested, operationalized and exposed by building an API layer on the Databricks lakehouse powered by Lakebase. By replacing fragmented, self-hosted Postgres silos with Lakebase, a Postgres engine integrated with the lakehouse, we now make data available 30x faster than with legacy self-hosted Postgres.This session walks through the end-to-end architecture and migration. We’ll talk about why Lakebase was chosen—horizontal scale and simplified operations—and how these capabilities reduce friction and speed onboarding. We’ll further expand on the blueprints we employed for Lakebase development, including governance and deployment patterns that can be adopted across an organization, and elaborate on the lessons learned along the way. Attendees will learn how we modernized ingestion of structured and unstructured data to achieve consistent, auditable data in a regulated environment.
description_raw:
Novo Nordisk transformed how vendor-sourced and vendor-facing clinical trial data is ingested, operationalized and exposed by building an API layer on the Databricks lakehouse powered by Lakebase. By replacing fragmented, self-hosted Postgres silos with Lakebase, a Postgres engine integrated with the lakehouse, we now make data available 30x faster than with legacy self-hosted Postgres.This session walks through the end-to-end architecture and migration. We’ll talk about why Lakebase was chosen—horizontal scale and simplified operations—and how these capabilities reduce friction and speed onboarding. We’ll further expand on the blueprints we employed for Lakebase development, including governance and deployment patterns that can be adopted across an organization, and elaborate on the lessons learned along the way.

Attendees will learn how we modernized ingestion of structured and unstructured data to achieve consistent, auditable data in a regulated environment.

speakers.name: Henrik Lynge
speakers.bio: Henrik Lynge is a seasoned IT leader and Sr. Director of Architecture & Strategy at Novo Nordisk (Jan 2026), with 20+ years of experience in R&D. He leads architecture across R&D, with a strong focus on GxP-compliant IT systems and data platforms. Previously, he worked with remote sens-ing, GIS and solution development across Danish Environmental Protection Agency, Hedeselskabet, and contributed through various roles at Novo Nordisk. He holds an MSc in Biol-ogy from the University of Copenhagen and executive education from CBS and Harward Business school. He has published books, Game assets and builds solutions with Python, Vibe coding, and CI/CD, happy to read and learn on topics that he does not master yet. Labour of love - boat restore.

speakers.name: Arvid Vinther Normann
speakers.bio: Arvid Normann is Domain Architect in Architecture & Strategy at Novo Nordisk. With 20+ years in IT and 12 years at Novo Nordisk, he specializes in integration and modern platform capabilities—AI, Streaming, APIs and data platforms. Previously, he served as an SME for integration projects with some of the biggest clients in Northern Europe. Passionate about technology, Arvid is a coder at heart.

speakers.name: JZTS (Jonatan Selsing)
speakers.bio: Jonatan Selsing is a Principal Architect, employed at Novo Nordisk. He has a research background in astrophysics and am now a part of Global Data and AI where he operationalises and enterprise scales the most sophisticated, contemporary computational frameworks and platforms in a highly regulated industry. He works across the industry and focus on driving large projects of strategic importance that are aligned to business goals. Through the focus on the future of computers, he ensures that Novo Nordisk is ahead in the digital age.

title: Modernizing Data Governance at Thailand’s Oldest Bank To Prepare for an AI-First World
abstract: Founded in 1907, Siam Commercial Bank (SCB) is Thailand’s oldest bank and Asia’s third oldest operational bank. In 2022, SCB executed a landmark transformation, restructuring into a holding-company structure under SCBX. This shift separated the organization into 12 core subsidiaries —dramatically increasing the complexity of data governance, data security and data quality across the group. Today, SCBX operates its enterprise data platform on Databricks, supporting more than 1,000 daily active users, 30,000+ production jobs, 6,000+ governed tables and an aspiration to scale data quality to 20,000+ rules. This session outlines how SCBX’s Data Governance CoE is leveraging Databricks Unity Catalog to design and scale enterprise-wide data governance within the organization. Attendees will learn practical lessons on operationalizing data quality at scale with DQX, and ABAC to standardize data policies. We will share how this strong governance accelerates trust, analytics and AI adoption.

Attendees will learn practical lessons on operationalizing data quality at scale with DQX, and ABAC to standardize data policies. We will share how this strong governance accelerates trust, analytics and AI adoption.

title: Molecules, Merchants & Models: How Structure-First AI Makes Promotion Intelligence Trustworthy
abstract: The presentation, "Molecules, Merchants & Models: How Structure-First AI Makes Promotion Intelligence Trustworthy," describes a promotion intelligence system built on the Databricks Lakehouse. Retail promotions interact through substitution, complementarity and spillover effects that merchants intuitively understand, but most tools fail to explain.Our system models item and promotion relationships as reusable graph structures—Promo Molecules—learned from over 70 billion rows of transaction data. These structures explicitly surface demand shifts, including substitutes, halo effects and cannibalization. We apply Explainable AI (XAI) at the relationship level—exposing deterministic, merchant-validated signals.Foundation models then reason probabilistically to answer natural-language “what-if” questions. This combined approach enables explainable, merchant-trusted promotion decisions at scale, without manual overrides or black-box predictions.

speakers.name: Karthik Iyer
speakers.bio: Karthik Iyer is Group Vice President of Intelligent Merchandising at Albertsons, with 25+ years of experience driving global transformation across retail and banking. He leads enterprise pricing and promotion intelligence, bringing together model-ready data, Explainable AI, statistical machine learning, deep learning, and agentic AI into a unified decisioning system. His work focuses on orchestrating the full AI lifecycle-from data to prediction to reasoning to action-enabling real-time decisions at scale. Previously, he pioneered CNN-based digital onboarding at NAB, widely covered in Australian media. He is pursuing doctoral research in AI focused on world models, latent representation learning, and explainability using JEPA.

title: Move Fast, Stay Secure: Enterprise AI Agent Security in Practice
abstract: Agentic AI is moving from POCs to mission-critical workloads- and the attack surface is expanding fast. As LLM-driven agents reason, plan, and act across enterprise data and tools, a new class of risk emerges: the "Lethal Trifecta," where sensitive data access, untrusted inputs, and external actions converge to make prompt injection attacks exploitable end-to-end. Karol Piekarski (Experian) and Nishith Sinha (Databricks) present the Databricks Agentic AI Security Framework (DASF v3), drawing on both Databricks' own AI infrastructure which handles millions of daily queries, and patterns from Fortune 100 deployments. The session walks through how the Databricks platform breaks the Lethal Trifecta at each layer: constraining what agents can access, hardening how they process inputs, and limiting what a compromised agent can actually do. Finally, see how the Databricks Security team built, open-sourced, and actively uses BlackIce, a containerized AI red teaming toolkit, to shift security from a point-in-time exercise to continuous, automated validation. Leave with a practical framework for moving fast on AI without compromising governance or security.

speakers.name: Nishith Sinha
speakers.bio: Nishith leads the AI Security Team at Databricks. His team is responsible for securing Databricks AI Products (Genie Code, Model Serving, Databricks One) as well as the enterprise usage of AI within Databricks. Nishith is a subject matter expert in AI Security, specializing in agents identity and security. In the last year, he has filed 10 GenAI Security patents.

speakers.name: Karol Piekarski
speakers.bio: Karol Piekarski is a Lead DevOps Engineer working at Experian, where he oversees the security posture of complex enterprise systems. Joining via the acquisition of insurtech startup Gabi in 2021, Karol played a key technical role in the transition, combining startup agility with enterprise rigor. Recognized as a Wiz MVP for his contributions to the cloud security community and an active contributor to the security frameworks, Karol holds comprehensive credentials including CCSP, CKA, and AWS Security & DevOps Professional. Recently, Karol has focused on adapting these mature DevSecOps methodologies to the emerging "wild west" of Generative AI, researching how to secure LLMs against injection and extraction attacks.

title: Multitenant Architecture at Scale: Fewer Workspaces, Less Admin, No silos
abstract: As analytics and AI workloads grow, most enterprises scale by adding more Databricks workspaces and infrastructure. Over time, this leads to admin overhead, duplicated standards, slow onboarding, and analytics, ML, and GenAI teams operating in silos.In this session, we share our lessons learned at Procter & Gamble, one of the largest Databricks workspace deployments. We show how we reversed that pattern by designing a multi-tenant Databricks architecture built to adapt to constant change and scale with minimal admin effort. Databricks is a multi-tenant platform—this session shows how to maximize it at enterprise scale.Using a “hub-and-spoke” approach, we consolidated workspaces while preserving team autonomy and execution speed. Attendees will learn how to:• Design a multi-tenant architecture that scales without increasing admin overhead• Consolidate workspaces while maintaining team ownership• Run analytics and AI workloads on a shared platform without creating silos

speakers.name: Zhang, Valeria
speakers.bio: Valeria Zhang is a Senior Solution Architect at Procter & Gamble, where she leads enterprise data platform architecture for analytics and AI across global business domains. She has built Databricks-based platforms serving thousands of users, consolidating distributed data environments into governed, multi-tenant architectures and standardizing batch, streaming, SQL, and machine learning workloads. Her work focuses on reducing platform sprawl, enabling scalable adoption, and improving cross-domain collaboration.

title: NBCUniversal Media Group's Journey to Databricks
abstract: NBCUniversal launched Peacock in 2020 with a BigQuery based reporting and analytics data stack. This provided a solid platform for the business to grow on. As data volumes and computational needs increased, in 2025 it became necessary to evaluate other technology. With an eye on data sharing, scalability and of course, cost, Databricks rose to the top of the bunch. In this talk, we will discuss the factors that led to this decision, the process of migration and the road ahead.

title: New Foundations of Delta Lake with Kernel and Spark's Data Source V2
abstract: When the Delta Lake project started back in 2018, Spark had the very first version of its Data Source APIs that allowed Spark to access process data in external sources (like Cassandra, Kafka etc). These Data Source V1 (DSv1) APIs, was limited to only basic features to allow communicate the data from source to Spark leaving a LOT of the heavy lifting (stats based data filtering, advanced upsert support, etc.) for the data source implementation to figure out. Delta Lake had build all the necessary intelligence to provide users a fantastic, high-performance, intuitive experience and command support that had set the standard for last 8 years. Now Spark as a engine has caught up by building a lot more intelligence within the engine itself and is now capable of doing a lot more of heavy lifting on behalf of data sources. These are the Data Source V2 (DSv2) APIs, and in this talk we are going to explore how we updating Delta to use DSv2 to build new foundations for the next decade of Delta.

speakers.name: Rahul Potharaju
speakers.bio: Rahul is one of the Engineering Leaders at Databricks, where he leads Delta and several other storage-related efforts.

speakers.name: Tathagata Das
speakers.bio: Tathagata Das is a Staff Software Engineer at Databricks and has been one of the core developers of Apache Spark (especially Structured Streaming) and Delta Lake. He is a member of Apache Spark PMC, and a Delta Lake committer. He is also one of the authors of Learning Spark: Lighting-fast Data Analytics (2nd edition). Previously, he was a grad student in the UC Berkeley at AMPLab where he conducted research about data-center processing frameworks and networks with Scott Shenker and Ion Stoica.

title: Next-Generation Data Platform Transformation
abstract: The Move to Cloud initiative represents far more than a technological migration; it marks a milestone in the bank’s digital transformation journey. Delivered through collaboration across data platform, data engineering, infrastructure, architecture and centralized ingestion, it established a new foundation for data. The program migrated 16 petabytes from Cloudera and ScyllaDB environments to a modern, secure and scalable cloud architecture, increasing speed, efficiency and resilience for business growth and data-driven decisions. It also generated USD 40 million in projected cost avoidance over five years and reduced 216,000 vulnerabilities, representing 38% of the technology landscape, engaging more than 100 organizational areas. More than a project, it transformed how the bank manages and values data, positioning technology as a driver of competitiveness and future innovation.

speakers.name: Laila Kurati
speakers.bio: With more than 20 years of experience as a strategic executive in the data domain, I have extensive experience leading transformation projects in large companies such as Serasa Experian, Claro, and Banco Santander.

title: O(n*m) Architecture in Open Source. What Could Possibly Go Wrong?
abstract: There's a quiet crisis going on in open source right now — whenever an open table format wants to build a new feature or integration, there's an exponentially more amount of work to do to incorporate it. A project that's increasingly harder to contribute to is the death of innovation.Kernel sets out to solve this problem by rewriting the source of truth. When other projects adopt Kernel, they no longer need to do any additional development for new features — they just appear. Join us for a deep dive into how we turned this project on its head, to solve not just these problems, but future problems we anticipate in open source.What started out as a contribution to Delta, could change the way that open table formats fundamentally integrate everywhere.Join Robert Pack, open-source contributor for Delta, Iceberg, Arrow and all round Rust enthusiast, and Holly Smith, tech communicator, who bullied Robert into this talk.

speakers.bio: Holly Smith is a developer advocate and multi-award-winning Data and AI expert and tech educator with over a decade of experience working at the cutting edge of Data and AI in various capacities. One of the first employees at Databricks, she is a renowned public speaker and teacher. She is well known for her YouTube series on AI + data engineering.

speakers.name: Robert Pack
speakers.bio: Robert has extensive experience in designing and implementing Data & AI platforms within large multinational organizations. Through this work he has been an avid contributor to the open lakehouse ecosystem - specifically Delta Lake. Now at Databricks, his focus is entirely facilitating and contributing to the open source ecosystem for building lakehouse architectures.

title: Oil and Gas: Powering the Future with Data-Driven Energy Intelligence
abstract: Explore how two of the world's leading oil and gas companies are harnessing the power of data and AI to transform exploration, production, and sustainability operations. TotalEnergies and Repsol share how they are leveraging the Databricks to unlock insights from massive datasets, optimize asset performance, and accelerate their transitions toward cleaner energy futures.

speakers.job_title: Head of HSE data strategy & analytics

speakers.name: daniel escuder
speakers.bio: CDAO with more than 20 years of experience, currently at Repsol, leads the corporate data strategy, the transformation towards intelligent data models, information governance and the incorporation of advanced analytics and AI capabilities within the D&A platform to generate business value and accelerate digital transformation. In his current role, he promotes scalable data models, FinOps, robust governance practices, and a focus on the strategic use of data as a key asset for decision-making. In addition, he combines his current work with teaching in various Big Data Analytics & Data Science programmes at institutions and business schools (such as IE and The Valley Digital Business School).

title: OLTP on the Object Store: Reimagining Postgres with the Lakebase Architecture
abstract: Traditional databases force architects into a familiar set of tradeoffs: scale compute and you pay for storage you don't need, add read replicas for analytical queries and you introduce replication lag, separate your OLTP and OLAP systems and you're managing data movement and consistency across boundaries. These aren't bugs in the design; they're consequences of tightly coupled storage and compute. Lakebase decouples storage from compute and runs Postgres natively on object storage. In this session, you'll learn what that architecture looks like, why it changes the tradeoff calculus, and what it makes possible: analytical queries running directly on operational data without read replicas, compute that scales to zero when idle, and transactional workloads sitting alongside lakehouse analytics on the same underlying data. We'll map the architecture to concrete use cases, including real-time applications and AI workloads that need fresh operational data, and hybrid workloads that previously required two separate systems. You'll leave with a clear mental model of how Lakebase works and where it fits in your stack.

Lakebase decouples storage from compute and runs Postgres natively on object storage. We'll map the architecture to concrete use cases, including real-time applications and AI workloads that need fresh operational data, and hybrid workloads that previously required two separate systems. You'll leave with a clear mental model of how Lakebase works and where it fits in your stack.

title: One Platform, One Policy: Governing Operational and Analytical Data with Lakebase and Unity Catalog
abstract: As operational and analytical workloads converge on the lakehouse, governance can't be an afterthought. This session explores how Lakebase and Unity Catalog work together to provide a unified governance model across transactional and analytical data, eliminating fragmented security policies, redundant access controls, and data duplication that traditional architectures create. You'll learn how Unity Catalog serves as the single layer for discovery, auditing, and access control across Lakebase and the lakehouse, enabling teams to build applications and AI systems on governed, shared data with confidence.

speakers.name: Stefania Leone
speakers.bio: Stefania is a product manager at Databricks working on Unity Catalog and the Databricks Runtime. She holds a PhD in Computer Science from ETH Zurich.

speakers.name: Jonathan Katz
speakers.bio: Jonathan Katz is a Sr. Staff Product Manager at Databricks on the Lakebase Team. He's on the Core Team of the open source PostgreSQL project.

title: One Stack for Retail AI: From Transactional Planning to Agentic Intelligence on Databricks
abstract: Retail AI platforms are no longer just analytics or ML systems — they must power real-time planning, massive-scale AI and intelligent decisioning on a single foundation. In this session, we share how our retail AI SaaS product is built entirely on Databricks’ unified platform.What we do uniquely on Databricks:We run classic AI (forecasting, optimization) and GenAI + agentic workflows together in a single Databricks stack, eliminating fragmentation between ML pipelines, planning logic and intelligent decision automation.Our hyperlocal forecasting USP (unique model per SKU–Store via ensemble selection) is possible only because Databricks enables massive distributed ML at low cost, scaling to millions of combinations without infrastructure sprawl.Hierarchical forecasting across store > city > region > national levels, combined with genetic optimization for inventory and replenishment, runs at production scale using Apache Spark™’s distributed compute and optimized execution engine.

title: Open Streaming Meets Governed Lakehouse: Running Apache Flink With Unity Catalog–Managed Delta Tables
abstract: As organizations adopt open streaming engines like Apache Flink alongside centralized governance platforms, interoperability becomes critical. This talk explores how the Flink Delta Connector bridges Flink’s real-time processing model with Delta Lake tables managed by Unity Catalog. We’ll explain how the connector preserves Delta’s ACID guarantees, integrates with UC-managed metadata, and respects centralized governance when writes originate from external Flink clusters. Through architectural deep dives and practical examples, we show how teams can build end-to-end streaming pipelines that combine Flink’s scalability with Databricks’ unified governance and lakehouse architecture.

title: OpenAI + Hertz Global + Databricks: Turning Customer Feedback into Business Impact
abstract: Generative AI is moving from proof-of-concept to production at scale, but the path from lab to customer-facing workload remains unclear for many enterprises.In this joint session, we'll explore the common use cases that leading enterprises are adopting. OpenAI will share insights from across their customer bases on model selection, serving infrastructure, latency and cost optimization.Then Hertz Global will walk through their production implementation: how they operationalized GPT-powered NPS survey summarization across thousands of daily surveys for storefront managers, and deployed Whisper for real-time call transcription to accelerate high-value lead conversion. You'll see how their Databricks Apps consume and present summarized insights, even delivering automated audio briefings to branch managers.This session will equip you with the use case patterns, architectural blueprints, and real-world lessons that separate successful deployments from stalled pilots.

title: Operating Databricks at Enterprise Scale: 60–70% MTTR Reduction Through SQL & AI Functions
abstract: As Databricks scales enterprise-wide, IT Operations teams face mounting challenges: controlling analytics costs, maintaining reliability and supporting business-critical workloads. A global semiconductor manufacturer leveraged Databricks SQL and AI functions to transform operations at scale—turning cost control and incident response into repeatable, automated workflows.Databricks SQL and AI functions create an autonomous optimization engine, Unity Catalog ensures enterprise governance and production-safe validation guarantees zero SLA risk.Databricks SQL provides the analytical foundation, enabling deep workload analysis through query metadata, execution plans and table statistics. AI functions layer intelligent reasoning on top, automatically generating optimized SQL, reconstructing data lineage and validating code transformations—all within the platform. These are leveraged to address five key problems and the operations team could save a significant time and achieve cost savings.

title: Operationalizing Fine-Grained Data Security With ABAC at Enterprise Scale
abstract: As Amgen’s analytics platform scaled across teams and data domains, enforcing fine-grained data access through direct user and group entitlements became difficult to operate, govern and audit. Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) enabled a shift to policy-driven access based on business attributes, delivering consistent enforcement, improved scalability and reduced operational overhead across the platform.This session details how ABAC emerged as the next step in Amgen’s data security architecture. We will cover early challenges with third-party access control tools, our migration to Databricks Unity Catalog, and the progressive adoption of dynamic views, row-level filtering and column-level masking. These capabilities laid the groundwork for scalable, attribute-driven access control.Attendees will learn the key architectural choices, implementation of tradeoffs and governance results behind deploying ABAC on Databricks at Amgen.

speakers.name: Jinesh Kunjumon
speakers.bio: Technology enthusiast with a passion for problem solving and a proven track record in designing, developing, and deploying scalable data warehouse and analytics solutions across industries. Experienced in modernizing legacy systems, building data lakehouses, and leading cloud migration initiatives. title: Optimizing AI/BI Dashboard performance: Best practices for speed and scale
abstract: Slow queries, long load times, and inconsistent responsiveness don't just frustrate users — they limit adoption and erode trust in your data. In this session, we'll teach you how to apply the right design patterns and leverage the latest AI/BI Dashboard capabilities — including automatic materialization and the Reyden engine — to deliver consistently fast, interactive performance at scale.You'll walk away knowing how to:- Design datasets and queries for fast, interactive dashboard performance- Leverage caching, query optimization, and warehouse configuration effectively- Use automatic materialization for the right queries to maximize performance gains- Identify and fix common performance pitfalls

speakers.name: Daniel Streit
speakers.bio: Daniel is a Staff Software Engineer at Databricks.

title: Performance Tuning the Data Platform at Databricks
abstract: Even on the world's fastest data platform, inefficient code can bring pipelines to a crawl. In this technical deep dive, the Data Platform team shares the specific optimization techniques we use to tune our heaviest, most complex internal workloads. From optimizing file sizing and Z-ordering to leveraging predictive I/O and materialized views, we will share the "power user" tips and tricks that we use to shave hours off our runtimes. If you want to know how the experts tune Databricks for maximum performance, this is the session to attend.

Bruce also formed the Chaos Engineering team and led the initiative at Netflix to achieve ultimate resilience. title: Phar Lakehouse: The Evolution of Our Data Market
abstract: As organizations migrate to the lakehouse architecture, they often struggle with the fragmented management of physical storage, authentication, and logical governance layers. This session introduces "Phar Lakehouse," a robust data product designed to standardize and automate the deployment of Databricks Unity Catalog across complex enterprise environments. We will explore how the Phar Data Platform team leveraged infrastructure as code (Terraform) to transform disjointed infrastructure tasks into a unified “black box” process. By providing a standardized storage foundation based on the Medallion Architecture, Phar Lakehouse enables squads to deploy tailored schemas—including specialized Utilities and PII-secure layers—without manual overhead.

title: Pilot to Platform: How NAB Deployed Databricks Genie at Scale Across the Bank
abstract: While initial generative AI adoption is simple, operating it at scale demands strong governance, clear controls, and coordinated rollout across teams. In this session, NAB shares how it implemented Databricks Genie as a first‑class engineering capability, from early prototypes to bank‑wide adoption.We will walk through the key decisions and patterns that made Genie genuinely useful for technical teams: integrating with existing SDLC and data workflows, how NAB approached onboarding and change management, some example high‑value use cases and how their benefits were measured.Attendees will leave with a practical blueprint for scaling Databricks Genie in their own organisations, rollout phases, adoption playbooks, and lessons learned on what to avoid. Whether you are just experimenting with Genie or planning an enterprise rollout, this session will help you turn a promising pilot into a reliable, governed platform for AI‑assisted development.

title: Pipeline to Production in Minutes: How a Leading P&C Insurer Achieved End-to-End DevOps on Databricks
abstract: P&C insurance data platforms must deliver analytics rapidly while meeting stringent governance and regulatory requirements. Although Databricks is widely adopted as the analytics backbone, many P&C organizations continue to struggle with inconsistent deployments, manual promotions and fragmented DevOps practices. This lightning talk demonstrates how a P&C organization implemented true end-to-end DevOps on Databricks using Asset Bundles, Databricks Apps and GitHub Actions. Learn how a Git-driven, declarative deployment model uses Asset Bundles to package notebooks, workflows and DLT pipelines with environment-aware configuration, enabling consistent promotion across multiple environments, while GitHub Actions orchestrates CI/CD pipelines for validation, automated deployment and auditability. The session highlights how Databricks Apps are integrated into the same pipeline to operationalize analytics use cases as secure, production-ready data product governed by Unity Catalog.

Learn how a Git-driven, declarative deployment model uses Asset Bundles to package notebooks, workflows and DLT pipelines with environment-aware configuration, enabling consistent promotion across multiple environments, while GitHub Actions orchestrates CI/CD pipelines for validation, automated deployment and auditability.

The session highlights how Databricks Apps are integrated into the same pipeline to operationalize analytics use cases as secure, production-ready data product governed by Unity Catalog.

title: Powering bespoke experiences with Genie: APIs, integrations, and embedded AI agents
abstract: Genie delivers trusted conversational analytics—and now it can reach your users wherever your they work.With Genie, users can now talk to their data directly from the tools they already use: Teams, Slack, Confluence, or custom web apps. Whether you’re using our native Copilot Studio/Foundry integrations or building with the Genie Conversation APIs/SDK, Genie can now bring natural-language analytics into everyday workflows.In this session, we’ll show how to build and deploy Genie-powered experiences across your organization:- Build custom conversational apps using Genie Conversation APIs and MCP- Leverage native integrations with Teams and Slack, and extend into Microsoft Power Platform and Copilot Studio- Design AI agents that operate seamlessly within real business workflows- Apply best practices for secure, scalable, and governed integrations

title: Powering KPMG Audit’s AI Future with Databricks
abstract: What does it take to turn a complex audit estate into an AI‑ready lakehouse in months, enabling auditors to move faster without losing trust? KPMG migrated nearly 400 SQL Server scripts into Databricks SQL in three months using lakehouse patterns, Lakebridge, and AI‑assisted refactoring — cutting manual effort while maintaining audit‑grade governance, lineage and performance. This session unpacks the blueprint: unified governance on Delta, SQL Serverless to handle extreme peak‑season demand and secure cross‑entity data access built for regulated environments. A focused enablement program onboarded 200+ users rapidly, embedding modern engineering practices and an AI‑first mindset. Finally, we’ll explore how this platform now powers AI/BI Genie and Lakeflow Designer, putting governed natural‑language and low‑code analytics directly into the hands of citizen analysts and front‑line auditors.

title: PRADA Serves Looks and High Quality Business Metrics with Lakebase
abstract: PRADA’s advanced analytics and KPI calculations had evolved across multiple disconnected systems, each serving different teams and use cases. This fragmentation made it difficult to maintain consistent definitions, reuse existing logic, and deliver a single trusted view of performance across the business. As data volumes and reporting demands increased, the organization needed a way to reliably manage and compute thousands of KPIs across regions, product categories, and time periods while still providing fast, decision-ready insights. To address this, Prada implemented a unified analytics architecture supported by Lakebase as its central data foundation. Lakebase consolidated previously siloed data into a single governed and scalable environment, enabling high-volume storage, efficient querying, and low-latency KPI computation. On top of this foundation, a standardized KPI model and microservices-based APIs provided consistent, easily accessible analytics across departments and systems, transforming fragmented reporting into a trusted, enterprise-wide capability for data-driven decision making.

title: Predictable Migrations: Scaling Lakebase Adoption with Provider-Agnostic Readiness Tools
abstract: Successful migration from managed OLTP services to Lakebase depends on one factor: identifying architectural nuances before the transition begins. Instead of a "move-and-fix" approach, teams need a rigorous, automated way to audit environments and quantify technical debt. This session introduces a Unified Readiness Assessment Framework designed to evaluate any managed OLTP workload for Lakebase compatibility.We will explore how an automated engine interrogates database metadata to audit schemas, extensions, and configuration limits. By analyzing version disparities and provider-specific constraints, the tool generates a high-fidelity compatibility roadmap. This approach allows architects to identify blockers early, estimate refactoring with precision, and build a high-integrity execution plan. Attendees will learn to standardize pre-migration checks, transforming a high-stakes transition into a predictable, well-orchestrated architectural upgrade.

She started out as a Solutions Architect helping customers design schemas and optimize queries, and now spends her time building tools and writing content to make Neon and Lakebase easier to work with. Based in Seattle, WA, Savannah cares about making complex database concepts approachable and meeting developers where they are.

title: Press Start: From Gaming to Real-Time Analytics with Databricks Apps
abstract: What if classic video games could teach you new tricks — and what if community gameplay data could power automation? Join Andrew Sitz (Databricks MVP) and Scott Haines (Staff Developer Advocate) as they build a live gaming environment on Databricks. We'll load a Game from Unity Catalog Volumes, host interactive gameplay through Databricks Apps, and capture every input and state change as streaming data. Using Lakebase and Delta Lake, we persist player sessions, track progress, and serve real-time leaderboards with low latency and full ACID guarantees. From there, we explore how aggregate play data reveals community patterns — surfacing strategies and insights players might never find alone. Finally, we show how recorded gameplay can train simple automation strategies, closing the loop from observation to action. Come for the nostalgia, stay for the data engineering.
description_raw:

Using Lakebase and Delta Lake, we persist player sessions, track progress, and serve real-time leaderboards with low latency and full ACID guarantees. From there, we explore how aggregate play data reveals community patterns — surfacing strategies and insights players might never find alone.

Finally, we show how recorded gameplay can train simple automation strategies, closing the loop from observation to action. Come for the nostalgia, stay for the data engineering.

speakers.name: Scott Haines
speakers.bio: Scott Haines is a seasoned software engineer specializing in massive distributed data systems and streaming technologies. Over the past 18 years, he’s built and scaled data infrastructure at leading companies, including Yahoo!, Twilio, Nike, and, currently stepped into a role as a Staff Developer Advocate at Databricks. Scott was a Databricks Beacon/MVP for five years before joining Databricks, he’s the author of books on Apache Spark and Delta Lake, and makes it his mission to help organizations successfully adopt open-source.

title: Privacy-Centric Identity Resolution for Analytics and Activation
abstract: Identity resolution sits at the core of modern data and AI. As signals disappear and privacy constraints tighten, the ability to unify fragmented customer records across partners directly impacts data quality, analytics accuracy, and model performance. In this session, we show how Databricks Clean Rooms enable organizations to build a trusted view of customer data by resolving identities across datasets without exposing raw PII. Join us for a technical deep dive with LiveRamp as we demonstrate how a durable identity backbone supports accurate, privacy-safe matching across organizations. You will learn how to standardize fragmented data into interoperable models, improve match rates, and unlock higher-fidelity analytics through governed collaboration using Clean Rooms. We will also cover practical implementation patterns, including enforcing privacy controls and scaling compliant collaboration across multiple partners. The result is a foundation for identity-first analytics that improves your marketing outcomes while maintaining strong privacy standards.

We will also cover practical implementation patterns, including enforcing privacy controls and scaling compliant collaboration across multiple partners. The result is a foundation for identity-first analytics that improves your marketing outcomes while maintaining strong privacy standards.

title: Production Lakebase Patterns: OAuth Token Lifecycle, Async Pooling, and Caching for Databricks Apps
abstract: Lakebase gives you PostgreSQL on Databricks, but production applications need patterns the documentation doesn't cover. From deploying a conversation persistence layer for an energy sector knowledge assistant, we share battle-tested solutions: Proactive OAuth token refresh that recreates connection pools before credentials expire, async connection pooling with psycopg3 sized for Databricks Apps concurrency, LRU caching with TTL for high-frequency thread lookups, and upsert patterns with conflict resolution for concurrent agent writes. Every pattern is production code running on Databricks Apps today. This lightning talk is for developers who've tried Lakebase in a notebook and want to know what it takes to run it in a real application with real users.

Lakebase gives you PostgreSQL on Databricks, but production applications need patterns the documentation doesn't cover. Every pattern is production code running on Databricks Apps today.

This lightning talk is for developers who've tried Lakebase in a notebook and want to know what it takes to run it in a real application with real users.

title: Provisioning for the Agentic Era: How Databricks Built a Self-Serve Infrastructure Vending Machine
abstract: As Databricks' go-to-market organization grew past 7,000 people — and AI agents became first-class consumers of platform resources alongside human engineers — our field team ran into a familiar problem: shared demo environments couldn't keep up. Too many users needed admin-level access, platform limits became real constraints, and it was hard to see who was using what, whether that "who" was a person or an agent.In this session, we'll share how we addressed that by building the Field Engineering Vending Machine (FEVM), a Databricks App that gives every engineer — and every agent — just-in-time, isolated, and governed environments on demand. Instead of filing tickets or juggling shared workspaces, engineers describe what they're trying to do — build a demo, run a hackathon, reproduce an issue, spin up an agentic workflow — and FEVM delivers a fit-for-purpose environment in minutes, then cleans it up automatically when it's no longer needed.We'll show how this "vending machine" model has improved speed, control, and cost visibility for thousands of users and countless agent runs, and why building it as a Databricks App has become a blueprint for how we dogfood the platform internally — for the agentic era and beyond.

title: Read-Time CDF in Delta Lake
abstract: Traditionally, enabling Change Data Feed (CDF) in Delta Lake incurs a "write tax"—increasing storage costs and latency to materialize changes during ingestion.In this session, we introduce Read-Time CDF, a new architecture that unlocks zero-overhead writes by deferring change computation to query time. By leveraging the new unified CDC interface in Spark Data Source V2 and Delta’s Row Tracking, users can now query row-level changes without ever explicitly enabling delta.enableChangeFeed.
description_raw: Traditionally, enabling Change Data Feed (CDF) in Delta Lake incurs a "write tax"—increasing storage costs and latency to materialize changes during ingestion.In this session, we introduce Read-Time CDF, a new architecture that unlocks zero-overhead writes by deferring change computation to query time. By leveraging the new unified CDC interface in Spark Data Source V2 and Delta’s Row Tracking, users can now query row-level changes without ever explicitly enabling delta.enableChangeFeed.

speakers.name: Gengliang Wang
speakers.bio: Gengliang Wang, a committed Apache Spark PMC Member and Committer, actively works on important Spark projects including ANSI SQL mode, TIMESTAMP_NTZ data type, and data sources. His contributions extend to enhancing the SQL compiler and UI. In addition, he's engaged in a project using large language models to streamline Spark application development. His work underscores a dedication to improving and making Apache Spark more user-friendly.

title: Real-Time Context Engineering for AI With Databricks
abstract: Just like we don’t put crude oil in a car, we refine it into gasoline. AI can’t run on raw data either. It needs continuous, refined context. Serving fresh context is the missing architectural layer for reliable AI. In this session, learn how Confluent Intelligence and Databricks turn operational data into low-latency context for AI agents and applications. See how Confluent’s fully managed streaming platform feeds trusted data into the Databricks lakehouse for Agent Bricks and AI/BI. We’ll demo real-time architectures for anomaly detection, product personalization, and AI-powered data enrichment, showing how to keep ML models and LLMs grounded in the live state of your business, not stale batch snapshots. You’ll leave with practical, streaming-native patterns to move from brittle pipelines and demos to production-grade AI on Databricks while reducing TCO.

speakers.name: Sean Falconer
speakers.bio: Dr. Sean Falconer is Confluent’s AI Entrepreneur in Residence, driving AI strategy and thought leadership. Sean has built and launched products that have impacted millions. As the founder of Proven.com, he led engineering, product development, and go-to-market strategies, successfully guiding the company to acquisition. At Google, Sean worked on enhancing developer experience and advancing conversational AI technologies. He holds a Postdoc in Bioinformatics at Stanford University, where he collaborated with the World Health Organization to modernize the International Classification of Diseases. He has published extensively on topics ranging from AI to quantum computing, reflecting his diverse expertise and contributions to the field.

title: Real-time Data, Real Business Results: An Introduction to Streaming on Databricks
abstract: Real-time data isn't just a technical aspiration, it's a competitive advantage. Whether you're detecting fraud in milliseconds, personalizing a customer experience mid-session, or attributing an ad impression to a conversion in real time, the gap between "fast enough" and "actually fast" has real business consequences.This session introduces Real-Time Mode and TransformWithState in Apache Spark™ Structured Streaming, Databricks' answer to ultra-low latency stream processing without the overhead of a separate streaming engine. We’ll walkthrough how these capabilities work, what problems they solve, and when to use them.Industry-leading customers, such as Coinbase, needed millisecond-level guarantees for financial transaction processing; DraftKings, whose live betting platform demands real-time odds calculation at scale; and MakeMyTrip, processing travel signals in real time to serve better search results to millions of users. We'll also unpack the ad attribution use case— a domain where latency directly impacts revenue attribution accuracy and where Real-Time Mode is proving transformative.

We'll also unpack the ad attribution use case— a domain where latency directly impacts revenue attribution accuracy and where Real-Time Mode is proving transformative.

title: Real-Time ML Features for Payment Fraud: Architecture Patterns at Scale
abstract: Checkout.com is a leading global digital payments provider, processing over $300 billion in e-commerce volume annually. Our fraud models depend on fresh features, but batch pipelines limited how quickly we could detect new fraud patterns. In this session, we’ll show how we built real-time feature pipelines using Spark Structured Streaming on Databricks, reducing feature computation to a few seconds and unlocking an eight-figure revenue opportunity. We’ll cover our technical approach, including custom stateful aggregations with transformWithStateInPandas, event-time watermarking for late payments, and TTL-based state management for deduplication. We’ll also discuss cost optimization at billion-event scale, and consistency between online and offline features. By incorporating real-time features into our fraud models, we achieved a 2% increase in PR-AUC, translating into millions in prevented fraud losses - offering a practical blueprint for real-time machine learning at scale.
description_raw:
Checkout.com is a leading global digital payments provider, processing over $300 billion in e-commerce volume annually. We’ll cover our technical approach, including custom stateful aggregations with transformWithStateInPandas, event-time watermarking for late payments, and TTL-based state management for deduplication. We’ll also discuss cost optimization at billion-event scale, and consistency between online and offline features.

By incorporating real-time features into our fraud models, we achieved a 2% increase in PR-AUC, translating into millions in prevented fraud losses - offering a practical blueprint for real-time machine learning at scale.

speakers.name: Dinis Peixoto
speakers.bio: Dinis Peixoto is a Staff Machine Learning Engineer at Checkout.com, where he architects high-scale distributed systems that power global payment intelligence. He specializes in the end-to-end lifecycle of machine learning systems, designing resilient architectures for real-time feature engineering at billion-event scale with high-throughput, and low-latency model serving.

speakers.name: Matt Slack
speakers.bio: Matt Slack is a Senior Specialist Solutions Architect at Databricks, specialising in helping customers solve their data engineering and geospatial challenges, and building out their modern data platforms. He has a strong background in low latency streaming pipelines at scale, creating innovative solutions with Databricks and other technologies. Previously he has led on the build and architecture of multiple cloud data platforms across a range of industries, including Finance, Transportation and E-commerce. Matt has a Bachelor's in Computer Science from the University of York.

title: Redact at the Speed of AI: De‑identifying PII in the Lakehouse with ai_query
abstract: Teams need to unlock data responsibly. This lightning talk shows how to use AI Functions—specifically ai_query—to automatically redact PII across both structured tables and unstructured documents in Databricks.We'll walk through how it works for tabular data, then show how the same pattern extends to free-text fields like clinical notes. We'll briefly touch on batching for throughput and checking output quality. A brief live demo runs against realistic unstructured healthcare notes, de-identifying patient information so analysts and ML teams can experiment without risk. You'll leave with reusable queries, sample prompts for both structured and unstructured redaction, and a practical starting point to operationalize PII redaction in your Lakehouse.

You'll leave with reusable queries, sample prompts for both structured and unstructured redaction, and a practical starting point to operationalize PII redaction in your Lakehouse.

title: Redefining Detection Engineering With Interactive AI Agents in the SOC
abstract: AI is transforming how Security Operations Centers (SOCs) operate, with tools that augment detection engineers and analysts to combat threats. We show how one SOC at a major hedge fund adopted AI to enhance detection engineering, accelerating creation, and minimizing response time. By integrating the Mosaic AI Agent Framework with Databricks Notebooks & Apps, this SOC provided detection engineers an AI assistant within their existing workflow. This agent accelerates detection baselining, anomaly detection, and filtering false positives while giving engineers control over final logic, ensuring human expertise remains central. The modular architecture enables adding capabilities like MITRE ATT&CK mapping, threat intelligence enrichment, and custom patterns without rewriting core logic. We demonstrate this assistant and how Databricks AI tools, including Genie and MLflow Evaluation, contribute to faster development, increased efficiency, and shortened response times to emerging threats.
description_raw:
AI is transforming how Security Operations Centers (SOCs) operate, with tools that augment detection engineers and analysts to combat threats. We show how one SOC at a major hedge fund adopted AI to enhance detection engineering, accelerating creation, and minimizing response time.

By integrating the Mosaic AI Agent Framework with Databricks Notebooks & Apps, this SOC provided detection engineers an AI assistant within their existing workflow.

This agent accelerates detection baselining, anomaly detection, and filtering false positives while giving engineers control over final logic, ensuring human expertise remains central. The modular architecture enables adding capabilities like MITRE ATT&CK mapping, threat intelligence enrichment, and custom patterns without rewriting core logic.

We demonstrate this assistant and how Databricks AI tools, including Genie and MLflow Evaluation, contribute to faster development, increased efficiency, and shortened response times to emerging threats.

speakers.name: Riley Nastase
speakers.bio: Riley Nastase is an AI/ML Engineer at Rearc, where he builds production AI, ML, and analytics systems for high-stakes data problems. He is especially interested in AI that supports decision-making in domains that demand human expertise in the loop. With a background spanning data science and engineering delivery, Riley focuses on practical systems that are grounded in reliable data, evaluated rigorously, and designed to help experts move faster. He has strong opinions about hallucinations and false confidence, and believes that the best systems keep human discernment and accountability at the center.

title: Reimagining SIEM: SAP ECS’s Journey to an Open Security Lakehouse
abstract: SAP Enterprise Cloud Services (ECS) is rethinking the traditional SIEM model by building a Security Data Platform with Lakewatch and Anvilogic shifting to an open, AI-ready foundation for cybersecurity. We’ll walk through how ECS designed and operationalized the platform to replace SIEM-centric workflows with a lakehouse approach that lowers total cost and time to respond using agentic AI. We will discuss the full architecture—routing with Cribl and Kafka, ingestion into Lakewatch, detection engineering in Anvilogic, incident response and reporting. We will close out with agentic AI use cases on the roadmap that are now enabled through the open lakehouse approach.
description_raw:
SAP Enterprise Cloud Services (ECS) is rethinking the traditional SIEM model by building a Security Data Platform with Lakewatch and Anvilogic shifting to an open, AI-ready foundation for cybersecurity.

We’ll walk through how ECS designed and operationalized the platform to replace SIEM-centric workflows with a lakehouse approach that lowers total cost and time to respond using agentic AI. We will discuss the full architecture—routing with Cribl and Kafka, ingestion into Lakewatch, detection engineering in Anvilogic, incident response and reporting. We will close out with agentic AI use cases on the roadmap that are now enabled through the open lakehouse approach.

speakers.name: Alan Mazankiewicz
speakers.bio: Alan is Senior Solutions Architect at Databricks in Germany. At Databricks he works with various cybersecurity customers and partners. He regularly speaks publicly about the intersection of cybersecurity, data and AI.

title: Replace your Legacy SIEM with Lakewatch: Build Your Own Custom Apps with Lakehouse & Lakebase
abstract: Many legacy SIEMs charge on ingestion, lock data behind proprietary formats, and limit AI customization. Databricks Lakewatch changes that. As the Open Security Lakehouse, Lakewatch lands 100% of your telemetry in open formats on Delta Lake — and because the data is open, security teams can pull any signal into custom apps built natively on Databricks. This session walks through a production SecOps platform on Lakewatch, ingesting 13TB/day across 22 sources at up to 80% lower cost. OCSF-normalized SDP pipelines deliver sub-15-minute detection with 94% MITRE ATT&CK coverage. A React + FastAPI app on Databricks Apps, backed by Lakebase, shows the art of the possible — AI threat hunting with Claude and custom analyst workflows no prebuilt SIEM could surface.

speakers.name: Surya Sai Turaga
speakers.bio: My name is Surya! In the past I have played the role of a Field Engineering Leader and evangelist in the Advanced Analytics Space, I come with strong conceptual hands-on experience in Agentic AI, Cloud (AWS+Azure+GCP), Big Data and Data Warehousing environments. I worked as a developer and implemented FSLDMs on Teradata. I am passionate about turning ideas as Agentic AI automation systems. I enjoy developing high-performance teams, mentoring data enthusiasts and working in cross-functional environments.

speakers.name: Anand Rao
speakers.bio: As a Solutions Architect at Databricks, I support public sector clients in addressing their Data and AI challenges by leveraging Databricks' robust platform. By emphasizing collaboration and understanding client needs, I help deliver tailored solutions that enable organizations to meet and exceed their strategic goals. My work focuses on integrating Data and AI technologies to drive innovation and transformative outcomes.

title: Rethinking Database Cost with Lakebase Autoscaling
abstract: Traditional databases charge you whether you use them or not. Lakebase's serverless autoscaling changes that, scaling compute up to match traffic spikes and down to zero when idle so you only pay for what you actually use. In this lightning talk, you'll see how autoscaling works in practice and what the cost implications look like across workloads from development environments to production applications.
description_raw: Traditional databases charge you whether you use them or not. Lakebase's serverless autoscaling changes that, scaling compute up to match traffic spikes and down to zero when idle so you only pay for what you actually use. In this lightning talk, you'll see how autoscaling works in practice and what the cost implications look like across workloads from development environments to production applications.

title: Revolutionizing Construction Intelligence: Scaling Procore Analytics With Delta Sharing & AI
abstract: Procore serves as the backbone for global construction data. Historically, providing seamless access to large-scale analytics meant complex ETL pipelines and high egress costs. This session explores how Procore Analytics pivoted to a “zero-copy” architecture using Databricks Delta Sharing. We will detail our cloud connector solution, showcasing how we securely share petabyte-scale data via Unity Catalog. We’ll highlight a global enterprise that leveraged this architecture to break down internal silos. By combining Procore capital project data with diverse enterprise datasets, they unlocked high-value use cases with data primed for AI and agent execution. Attendees will learn how the open Delta Sharing protocol enables frictionless “Bring Your Own Warehouse” capabilities, reducing TCO and latency. We’ll share technical lessons on optimizing cross-cloud performance and building an AI-ready foundation for the world’s most complex industry.
