title: Rewind, Replay, Recover: Building Operational Resilience at NAB With Spark Declarative Pipelines
abstract: Discover how National Australia Bank (NAB) scales production streaming with Spark Declarative Pipelines (SDP), using Streaming Tables and Materialized Views to enable rewind and replay — restoring pipelines to a consistent state, applying fixes safely and reprocessing only affected data. Explore how this approach reduces brittle manual interventions and avoids full refreshes by leaning on declarative semantics, governed data products, and robust checkpoint/versioning patterns and helps proactively prevent bad data from reaching downstream systems and ensures that data demographics can be safely restored to their previous usable state in the Lakehouse. Walk away with actionable designs, checklists and success criteria for resilient, cost‑efficient streaming at enterprise scale in regulated environments. Outcomes include faster recovery, lower operational effort and cost, improved reliability and correctness and sustained performance as concurrency grows.

speakers.name: Partha Sarathi Sahoo
speakers.bio: Partha Sarathi Sahoo is a Senior Delivery Solution Architect at Databricks, where he works with enterprise customers across Australia and New Zealand to design, implement, and operationalize data and AI platforms on the Databricks Lakehouse. With over 20 years' experience spanning data engineering, big data architecture, analytics, and cloud platforms, he has built and scaled data solutions across the aviation, cloud services, and financial sectors industries in roles at Barclays, Emirates Airline, Amazon Web Services (AWS), and now Databricks. Partha is passionate about operational resilience, data reliability, and helping organisations move from legacy platforms to modern Lakehouse architectures that deliver real business value.

speakers.name: Dheeraj Puli
speakers.bio: Dheeraj Puli is Head of Data Reliability Engineering at National Australia Bank (NAB), where he leads teams responsible for building resilient, future‑ready data platforms and improving how more than 500 engineers deliver and operate data at scale. With over 18 years’ experience across data engineering, architecture, delivery and consulting, he has designed and run automation‑focused data environments on platforms including Teradata, big data systems, Databricks and Snowflake in senior roles at NAB and previously Commonwealth Bank, Teradata and IBM Global Services. Dheeraj is known for his passion for automation, data quality and data‑driven solutions, using DevOps practices and reliability engineering to solve complex business problems

title: Running LLaMA at Scale: Production Inference on Databricks Model Serving
abstract: Serving LLMs at consumer scale stresses everything: throughput, latency, correctness and cost. At Superhuman—formerly Grammarly—we built a production system that serves a fine‑tuned LLaMA‑3B model for grammatical error correction to millions of monthly users on Databricks Model Serving. This talk shares what we validated and how: direct‑ingress patterns for high RPS, load testing toward 100K+ QPS targets, multi‑region failover and cold‑start mitigation, and observability. We’ll cover validation checks—A/B tests, golden sets—plus the cost model we used to compare against our internal vLLM-based stack, including levers like dynamic batching, autoscaling and speculative decoding. Expect concrete configs, dashboards and a candid readout of wins, incidents and unit‑economics tradeoffs that informed our ramp from pilot to majority traffic.

speakers.name: Christoph Stuber
speakers.bio: Christoph is a Machine Learning Engineer at Superhuman, where he focuses on production model inference optimization for NLP systems. He studied computer science at TU Berlin and has worked across industries on data and ML engineering projects spanning computer vision, logistics, and natural language processing. He's passionate about making ML models fast, reliable, and cost-effective at scale.

speakers.name: Mykhailo Troianovskyi
speakers.bio: Myke Troianovskyi is a Machine Learning Software Engineer at Superhuman (formerly Grammarly), where he scales production ML services serving tens of millions of users and partners with Databricks on ML inference. He built and drove company-wide adoption of an in-house ML serving framework that enabled rapid ML adoption and scaled to support user growth. Over 14 years in the industry, Myke has worked across back-end engineering, infrastructure, observability, and MLOps for companies ranging from enterprise consultancies to consumer-scale products.

title: Safe Skies: Automating Trust at Scale With AI-Generated Contracts at Boeing
abstract: In aviation, "bad data" is an operational risk. For large enterprises with hundreds of data products, manual stewardship stifles innovation. To ensure "safe skies," we transformed governance and quality into engineering disciplines.This session explores our "Governance as Code" architecture using DQX and Ontos on Unity Catalog. We demonstrate using GenAI to bootstrap trust, scanning raw data to auto-generate Open Data Contract Standards (ODCSq). We then show how DQX acts as the enforcer, compiling contracts into pipelines that quarantine violations and alert on drift.AI-assisted contracts: Using LLMs to profile data and draft rigorous ODCS contracts, cutting onboarding time.Automated quality: Deploying DQX to enforce row-level quality, quarantine bad data and trigger drift alerts.Semantics-first: Using Ontos to map technical assets to business meaning for a discoverable data marketplace.Join us to learn how we replaced bureaucracy with automation and engineering rigor.

speakers.name: Michael Hewitt
speakers.bio: As a Technical Leader and Data & AI Architect at Boeing Vancouver, I bring nearly 20 years of industry experience with six years focused on Databricks, data, and AI. Three years ago I led the roll-out and build-out of Boeing’s first Data Mesh on Databricks, centering governance on Unity Catalog to standardize metadata, lineage, access controls, and policy automation. I helped established reusable data-product patterns that reduced time-to-production, introduced MLOps and model governance and mentored cross-functional teams to scale adoption. I now focus on Governance-as-Code for a discoverable data marketplace and accelerating safe, production-grade data and AI across the enterprise. Honored to speak at the Databricks & AI Summit.

speakers.name: Alan Reese
speakers.bio: Alan Reese is a Resident Solutions Architect at Databricks, where he has spent much of the past year embedded with Boeing's data and architecture teams, collaborating on the quality and governance framework featured in this session — ODCS contracts, DQX enforcement, and Unity Catalog integrated with CI/CD. He has led enablement workshops on the pattern across Boeing's federated teams and within the broader Databricks community. Alan also supports multi-quarter engagements in the energy vertical and has architected end-to-end agentic Gen AI solutions using Agent Bricks, the Agent Framework, and Genie. Before Databricks, he spent over a decade as a data leader, most recently as Principal Data Architect at Cotton Holdings, where he built the team and greenfield lakehouse that established the company's first modern data platform. He holds BS and MS degrees from Texas A&M University.

title: SAP and Databricks: Unlock the Full Potential of Databricks on Your SAP Data
abstract: The SAP BDC Connector on the Databricks Platform enables, for the first time, the unification of core SAP ERP, supply-chain, and financial data with operational and IoT telemetry in a single lakehouse, unlocking predictive maintenance, supply-chain optimization, and AI-driven insights that were previously out of reach. In this session, we also discuss new innovations in SAP BDC Connector and how customers are leveraging it. Then, you'll hear from CAF (Construcciones y Auxiliar de Ferrocarriles), a global leader in rail and bus manufacturing. CAF became one of the first adopters of SAP BDC, bridging rich IoT telemetry streaming from trains across three continents with the SAP modules that run their business. Don't miss this session if you are an SAP and Databricks customer, learn the use cases and lessons learned from bridging these two worlds.

speakers.job_title: Head of Data & AI Centre of Excellence

title: Scale Trust, Not Headcount: AI-Powered Semantic Engineering at Southern Glazer’s Wine & Spirits
abstract: Trust in business decisions erode when reports, AI Agents, and operational systems provide different values for the same KPIs. The solution is a unified semantic fabric in which KPIs are managed as versioned, testable, and reusable assets—like software—implemented entirely within Databricks without added tooling overhead. Organizations no longer need hordes of data and semantic engineers, manually coding pipelines and maintaining definitions. With AI agents automating data transformations, semantic alignment, and business rule updates, organizations can reduce manual engineering effort, accelerate delivery, and lower operating costs while improving data trust. This session showcases how Southern Glazer's Wine & Spirits is implementing an AI-augmented approach to build their Metrics Bank and Semantic Layer in Databricks. You'll discover real-world results: reduced manual engineering effort, accelerated time-to-insight, and scaled decision-making capabilities across the enterprise.

title: Scaling a Multi-Agent App Platform with Databricks @ SKULD
abstract: How we designed, scaled, and shipped a multi‑agent AI app platform for Skuld—built 100% natively on Databricks—and the unified App + AI + Data patterns that made it possible. Maritime insurance claims depend on shifting legal facts, mixed‑format case files, and strict audit requirements, and standard chunking plus top‑k filtering lost provenance and broke under load. At Skuld, we built a multi‑agent app on Databricks: Lakeflow Jobs runs parsing and enrichment pipelines, Model Serving powers synchronous agent calls, Databricks Apps handles secure user workflows, and Unity Catalog governs access end to end. The talk dives into our evidence‑first retrieval pattern, where documents become source‑linked fact cards, conflicting facts stay visible for review, and agents return exact citations instead of opaque answers. Attendees leave with a hosting decision framework and benchmark data on parsing throughput, queues, token cost, rate limits, and latency.

speakers.name: Miran Hadziomerovic
speakers.bio: Miran is the Senior Executive and AI Lead at Skuld, the marine P&I insurer, where he runs AI across three fronts: product delivery, enterprise governance, and building AI competence across the organisation. He focuses on moving AI past pilots - shipping systems that claims handlers and underwriters actually use, with trust, auditability, and access controls designed in from the start. Alongside the build work, he puts the governance frameworks and learning programmes in place so the rest of the business can adopt AI responsibly. Before Skuld, Miran spent years as a Senior IT Consultant across AI, ML, DevOps, infrastructure, and data/software engineering, leading cross-functional teams on complex projects for clients across industries.

speakers.name: Domonkos Pal
speakers.bio: Domonkos Pal is Head of Digital Products at Hiflylabs, where he helps enterprises design and build data and AI applications on Databricks for clients across Europe and the US. His work centers on Databricks Apps, Lakebase, Genie, agents and the broader app ecosystem — particularly how to make AI applications scalable, secure, and maintainable in production, with a strong focus on DevEx and agentic engineering workflows. As the world's first Databricks Apps MVP (2025–2026), Domonkos returns to the Data + AI Summit stage in 2026.

title: Scaling Agentic AI and Enterprise Data Mesh With Databricks Lakebase
abstract: Zillow is evolving from a search platform into an AI-native "Super App." To move agentic AI from prototype to production, we solved a critical challenge: providing agents with a reliable, high-performance sense of state.Persistent memory is central to this shift. By integrating Databricks Lakebase, we’ve bridged the gap between the data lake and the millisecond latency real-time agents require. This allows our AI to maintain long-term, vectorized context—enabling agents to remember, learn and act with precision.As Lakebase adoption grows, it is catalyzing enterprise-wide innovation. By powering real-time segmentation and communications, it reduces architectural friction and accelerates our Data Mesh strategy, paving the way for Zillow’s next generation of real-time data.

title: Scaling AI Agents at Mercedes-Benz: Unified AI Governance in a Multi-Cloud Enterprise Ecosystem
abstract: Managing AI at the scale of Mercedes-Benz requires more than just models—it requires a unified AI ecosystem. With thousands of practitioners across global business units, the challenge was providing a single point of entry for AI capabilities while maintaining strict governance across multi-cloud and multi-model environments. In this session, we go under the hood of the Mercedes-Benz AI Ecosystem. We will detail the technical architecture that integrates Databricks Agent Framework, Model Serving, and AI Gateway to create a governed interoperability for AI agents. We’ll discuss the "how-to" of: Centralizing discovery: Building an internal marketplace for foundation models and reusable agents Unified governance: Integrating Mercedes-Benz AI Marketplace into Databricks AI Gateway Operationalizing agents: Deploying and publishing AI agents for self-service at Mercedes-Benz Attendees will leave with a technical blueprint for building an interoperable enterprise AI agent platform.
description_raw:
Managing AI at the scale of Mercedes-Benz requires more than just models—it requires a unified AI ecosystem. With thousands of practitioners across global business units, the challenge was providing a single point of entry for AI capabilities while maintaining strict governance across multi-cloud and multi-model environments. In this session, we go under the hood of the Mercedes-Benz

AI Ecosystem. We will detail the technical architecture that integrates Databricks Agent Framework, Model Serving, and AI Gateway to create a governed interoperability for AI agents. We’ll discuss the "how-to" of:

  • Centralizing discovery: Building an internal marketplace for foundation models and reusable agents
  • Unified governance: Integrating Mercedes-Benz AI Marketplace into Databricks AI Gateway
  • Operationalizing agents: Deploying and publishing AI agents for self-service at Mercedes-Benz

Attendees will leave with a technical blueprint for building an interoperable enterprise AI agent platform.

speakers.name: Sai Yang
speakers.bio: Sai Yang is a Solutions Architect for the Automotive Manufacturing sector at Databricks. With 6 years of experience in the data & AI domain, Sai has strong expertise in Data Science, Machine Learning, AI, and Data Governance. At Databricks, he has led multiple large-scale platform migration projects and AI use cases for global leading automotive companies. These initiatives enabled the organizations to build and implement scalable analytics and AI solutions with unified governance on Databricks, helping them become the industry leaders in data and AI. Before joining Databricks, Sai worked in technology consulting, where he designed, developed, and deployed reliable machine learning systems in production for Fortune Global 500 companies.

speakers.name: Markus Heubach
speakers.bio: Markus is Product Manager for the AI Ecosystem at Mercedes‑Benz. He leads multiple product teams building a cloud‑agnostic AI ecosystem across the enterprise. In his role, he shapes the strategic direction of AI initiatives and drives their execution in complex, cross‑functional environments. Markus brings extensive experience from large‑scale AI and data programs.

title: Scaling at Scale: The Journey of RAIP and DS Genie at Repsol
abstract: Since launching its digital transformation program in 2018, Repsol has developed RAIP, Repsol Artificial Intelligence Products, a framework that encapsulates years of analytical expertise and supports hundreds of production-grade AI models across the organization. Within RAIP, solutions such as REPSENS for time series forecasting have been validated in demanding industrial environments.With the rise of generative AI, rather than treating it as a separate paradigm, we focus on integrating generative capabilities with proven AI assets to maximize impact. In this session, we present how DS Genie builds on RAIP to automate advanced workflows such as time series forecasting. We also compare this approach with foundation models such as TimeGPT and Chronos, and discuss how this agentic paradigm extends to pricing and optimization use cases. It augments existing AI models while preserving governance, reliability, and operational control and democratizes access to RAIP across business units.

speakers.name: Victor Vaquero Soto
speakers.bio: Víctor Vaquero Soto is a Senior Data Scientist specializing in advanced analytics, time series forecasting, and scalable data solutions. At Repsol, he develops forecasting libraries for low-code platforms, enabling rapid deployment of predictive models across industrial and energy systems. He holds a Ph.D. in Physics, focused on nuclear and particle physics, where he applied Big Data techniques to large experimental datasets. His background combines research and industry experience to turn complex data into actionable insights. Passionate about AI and data engineering, he builds tools that bridge research and real-world applications, and explores how generative AI can make advanced solutions more accessible.

title: Scaling Custom LLMs with vLLM and Databricks Model Serving: Fast, Flexible, and Production-Ready
abstract: Databricks Model Serving supports deployments ranging from classical ML models on custom-CPU workloads to foundation models with dedicated provisioned throughput endpoints. But what about the use-cases that need any of the 1000s of other open-source or fine-tuned LLMs? Deploying them efficiently can be challenging.This breakout session explores deploying LLMs on custom-GPU endpoints with vLLM. In it, we’ll examine:- Serverless GPU Compute: How it simplifies the deployment process, saving hours on configuration work alone.- vLLM + GPU workloads: How they work in tandem on the platform to deliver high-throughput inference with scalable infrastructure.- Implementation: A step-by-step code walkthrough for packaging models, configuring vLLM runtime, and deployment! Serving LLMs on GPUs doesn’t have to be scary - learn more about how Databricks enables you to deploy even your most demanding model serving workloads!

title: Scaling Cybersecurity Analytics Without Scaling Costs
abstract: Cybersecurity teams often struggle with the cost and scale of analyzing massive telemetry. JetBlue solved this by building a security-first analytics platform on the Databricks lakehouse. By leveraging Delta Lake, Auto Loader, and Databricks SQL, JetBlue ingests high-cardinality data into low-cost cloud storage, applying compute only when necessary. This enables long-term retention and flexible analysis that legacy tools find prohibitively expensive. Using Databricks SQL, they developed a fastly-focused security layer to: Detect anomalies and bot abuse Measure CAPTCHA effectiveness Analyze attack trends by IP, geography, and path Support real-time and historical investigations Decoupling storage from compute resulted in faster investigations, better visibility, and significant savings. Attendees will learn how to use Databricks to scale detection and investigation workflows cost-effectively.
description_raw:
Cybersecurity teams often struggle with the cost and scale of analyzing massive telemetry. JetBlue solved this by building a security-first analytics platform on the Databricks lakehouse.

By leveraging Delta Lake, Auto Loader, and Databricks SQL, JetBlue ingests high-cardinality data into low-cost cloud storage, applying compute only when necessary. This enables long-term retention and flexible analysis that legacy tools find prohibitively expensive.

Using Databricks SQL, they developed a fastly-focused security layer to:

  • Detect anomalies and bot abuse
  • Measure CAPTCHA effectiveness
  • Analyze attack trends by IP, geography, and path
  • Support real-time and historical investigations
  • Decoupling storage from compute resulted in faster investigations, better visibility, and significant savings. Attendees will learn how to use Databricks to scale detection and investigation workflows cost-effectively.

title: Scaling Data and AI Trust: How Credicorp Built a Cross-Institution Data Product Marketplace
abstract: As financial groups scale across regions and subsidiaries, the challenge is no longer collecting data; it’s governing, sharing and trusting it at an enterprise level. Eduardo Torres, CDO at Credicorp and Alvaro Villanueva, Credicorp’s Data Marketplace Product Owner, will share their team’s journey to building a data product marketplace that spans financial institutions and countries using Databricks Unity Catalog. Torres & Villanueva will share how they built a reliability layer at the foundation of their data product marketplace with data and AI observability, enabling multiple subsidiaries to publish and discover trusted data products without costly duplication or security risk. They will dive into the team’s approach to key challenges, including operating models, incentives and change management when subsidiaries are also competitors. Attendees will learn how trusted data across a data product marketplace unlocks a unified customer view, and real business impact at enterprise scale.

title: Scaling Databricks Genie 10x at GSK Global Supply Chain: Building a Production AI/BI Factory
abstract: Most organizations don't struggle to prototype conversational AI—they struggle to get it reliably into production at scale. At GSK's Global Supply Chain, operating across 37 GMP-regulated manufacturing sites with thousands of daily decisions, we built an AI/BI factory designed to move Databricks Genie from early experimentation to enterprise-grade production—serving analysts, planners and decision-makers across a complex, regulated environment.This talk explores the journey from pilot to platform: how we scaled Genie 10x by treating it not as a standalone tool, but as a production system embedded in a broader agent operating model—one that fuses technology, process, governance and organizational ways of working into a repeatable delivery engine.What we’ll cover: Genie Spaces lessons learned and best practices Enterprise observability dashboard for Genie CI/CD migration and deployment automation for DevOps Semantic caching for Genie

Most organizations don't struggle to prototype conversational AI—they struggle to get it reliably into production at scale.   • Genie Spaces lessons learned and best practices
  • Enterprise observability dashboard for Genie
  • CI/CD migration and deployment automation for DevOps
  • Semantic caching for Genie

She then mastered Python and Spark (when it was still running on Hadoop). Joining a small data and AI startup as a machine learning engineer, she grew with the company and the wider adoption of AI, becoming tech lead and then head of innovation, delivering AI solutions to real-world problems, in the cloud. title: Scaling Energy Deal Evaluation With Databricks Lakebase
abstract: Quantum Capital Group screens hundreds of energy investment opportunities each year, supported by more than 1.5 billion records across public, premium and proprietary datasets. While its Databricks Lakehouse already provided a scalable and governed foundation, the next challenge was enabling teams and downstream tools to work more efficiently from the same trusted data as deal volume and complexity increased. In this session, Quantum shares how it extended its Lakehouse with Databricks Lakebase to introduce relational structure and familiarity without sacrificing openness or scale. Attendees will learn how Lakebase integrates with Delta Lake, Unity Catalog and Lakeflow to support governed, materialized datasets that teams can access using SQL, geospatial tools and existing analytics platforms. The talk focuses on how relational access and governance helped Quantum scale human-led diligence across engineering and investment teams, evaluate complex deals with greater confidence.

The talk focuses on how relational access and governance helped Quantum scale human-led diligence across engineering and investment teams, evaluate complex deals with greater confidence.

title: Scaling Executive-Ready Insight with Databricks AI Query on AI/BI
abstract: Creating executive-ready metric summaries is harder than it looks. Leaders expect insights that are accurate, consistent, action-oriented, and concise, yet many organizations still rely on manual reporting—driving high effort, slow turnaround, and inconsistent interpretation. In this session, we share a real-world example of sales strategy and analytics teams automating daily pipeline summaries using Databricks AI Query within an AI/BI dashboard. Each summary previously took ~30 minutes per territory—an estimated 36k manual hours annually. By standardizing business logic, strengthening the Databricks data foundation, and applying AI selectively, we scaled executive-ready insights to hundreds of leaders. The key lesson: 80% of impact comes from clear strategy logic and trusted data; AI delivers the final 20% by generating consistent natural-language summaries at scale. Attendees will learn a practical model to encode executive expectations, govern AI outputs, and reduce time to insight.

title: Scaling for MHHS: Octopus Energy’s 50x Cost-Efficient Margin Data Engineering
abstract: As the global energy system moves to intermittent renewables, the challenge for data engineering is providing a clear signal to consumers for when the energy is at cheapest and cleanest. This talk is for data engineers and leaders navigating the UK’s Market-wide Half-Hourly Settlement (MHHS) transition. Facing a 48x surge in data volume for 8M+ customers, Octopus Energy re-engineered its margin systems to bridge the gap between monthly billing and half-hourly settlement.The Results:Data Efficiency: 98.8% reduction in rows processed (from 25B to 300M).Cost Savings: 50x cost reduction per settlement date, avoiding $1M in annual infrastructure increases.Velocity: Improved data freshness from weekly to daily.By decoupling the pipeline into grain-specific modules and applying Spark and Incremental optimisations, we transformed a regulatory hurdle into a scalable engine for margin visibility. Aligning your data architecture with the business logic enables a sustainable energy future.

title: Scaling Geospatial Analytics at S&P Global Energy: From Billions of Points to AI-Powered Map Agents with Databricks
abstract: At S&P Global Energy, we transformed our geospatial analytics architecture to handle billions of records with query results in seconds — removing the need for separate geospatial databases and reducing costs using our Oil & Gas well production data. Architecture evolution: Unified single-source-of-truth on Delta Lake without redundant geospatial systems Databricks Spatial SQL with native spatial types enabling performant queries Databricks SQL Serverless delivering on-demand H3 aggregations Interactive Databricks app demo for large-well datasets. What’s next – AI geospatial agent (work in progress): Natural language map interactions powered by Agent Bricks Genie-driven text-to-SQL for dynamic map layer creation 50+ analytical tools like chart generation from spatial queries Attendees will learn practical patterns for scaling geospatial workloads, leveraging Spatial SQL with native spatial types, H3 indexing, and see how AI agents are reshaping spatial analytics workflows.
description_raw:
At S&P Global Energy, we transformed our geospatial analytics architecture to handle billions of records with query results in seconds — removing the need for separate geospatial databases and reducing costs using our Oil & Gas well production data.

Architecture evolution:

  • Unified single-source-of-truth on Delta Lake without redundant geospatial systems
  • Databricks Spatial SQL with native spatial types enabling performant queries
  • Databricks SQL Serverless delivering on-demand H3 aggregations

Interactive Databricks app demo for large-well datasets.

What’s next – AI geospatial agent (work in progress):

  • Natural language map interactions powered by Agent Bricks
  • Genie-driven text-to-SQL for dynamic map layer creation
  • 50+ analytical tools like chart generation from spatial queries

Attendees will learn practical patterns for scaling geospatial workloads, leveraging Spatial SQL with native spatial types, H3 indexing, and see how AI agents are reshaping spatial analytics workflows.

As Geospatial Specialist Leader, he is responsible for enabling and developing a team of strong subject matter experts (SMEs). He also represents field interests with product management, marketing, and engineering to package high-quality geospatial features and field-delivered assets for our customers and partners.

title: Scaling Governed AI Beyond PoCs: A Public Sector Agent Architecture with Lakebase and AgentBricks
abstract: Many AI pilots succeed, but productionisation remains difficult in regulated environments where governance, auditability, and state management are required. This session is grounded in Skills SA’s Organisational Self-Assessment (OSA) process, a compliance-driven workflow combining structured operational metadata and unstructured free-text submissions. Around 200 assessments are reviewed annually, requiring significant assessor effort (~1,800 hours).We present a multi-agent architecture built on Databricks Lakebase and AgentBricks. A single Databricks App invokes specialised agents via AgentBricks. OSA data is stored in Delta tables, with Lakebase providing operational state management and governance. Agent prompts and scoring rules are versioned as governed data, and AI/BI Genie is used for natural-language queries over operational metadata.Attendees will learn practical patterns for running production AI systems in regulated environments.

title: Scaling Human Connection with Real-Time AI Conversation Monitoring
abstract: With over 300 million messages exchanged since 2013 and more than a decade of expertise, Crisis Text Line is now leading the implementation of LLMs in the mental health nonprofit space. We will discuss a set of conversation monitoring capabilities built on the Databricks ecosystem that provide unprecedented observability into live, high-stakes conversations. These models surface risk signals, quality metrics, and contextual insights. We will demo how these capabilities enable co-pilot-like support that helps crisis responders and clinical supervisors focus on what they do best—supporting people in need.

title: Scaling Micro-Offers With Hybrid AI
abstract: This work proposes a pipeline for responsible investment offer personalization in Brazilian Open Finance, integrating data governance, segmentation (K-Means), propensity modeling (Random Forest) and an ethical filtering layer via LLM-based digital twins. Applied to 427,929 Caixa Econômica Federal customers, the model achieved an AUC-ROC of 0.57. Digital twin validation reduced the high-propensity pool from 213,764 to 6,543 eligibles, applying suitability rules and generating auditable justifications to mitigate overcontact. Distributive analysis indicated a concentration of offers among higher-income profiles. In a pilot within the My Wallet app, the approach yielded a 21.0% positive intention rate, surpassing the 3–5% generic average. Results suggest that combining Open Finance, ML and GenAI enhances assertiveness and algorithmic governance, highlighting the need for formal fairness metrics and large-scale causal experiments.
description_raw:
This work proposes a pipeline for responsible investment offer personalization in Brazilian Open Finance, integrating data governance, segmentation (K-Means), propensity modeling (Random Forest) and an ethical filtering layer via LLM-based digital twins. Digital twin validation reduced the high-propensity pool from 213,764 to 6,543 eligibles, applying suitability rules and generating auditable justifications to mitigate overcontact.

Distributive analysis indicated a concentration of offers among higher-income profiles. In a pilot within the My Wallet app, the approach yielded a 21.0% positive intention rate, surpassing the 3–5% generic average. Results suggest that combining Open Finance, ML and GenAI enhances assertiveness and algorithmic governance, highlighting the need for formal fairness metrics and large-scale causal experiments.

Learn how Databricks customers turned their data into an enterprise backbone that is scaling responsible AI, powering product innovation, and unlocking natural language query, removing the analytics bottleneck to enable faster, more strategic decision making.

He oversees Enterprise Risk and Compliance teams that are putting AI to work—leveraging a unified data infrastructure to automate risk and compliance workflows. Joel enables responsible innovation through forward-looking risk frameworks that keep pace with emerging technology, including the Hazel Network, a bank lead tokenized deposit and payments initiative on blockchain rails—keeping Vantage Prepared for What's Possible™. He started at the OCC in 2007 examining community banks, held risk and operations roles at a Texas bank, and served as a Director within USAA's Enterprise Risk Office.

title: Scaling Trustworthy AI: Takeda’s Self-Service AI Foundation on Databricks
abstract: Takeda and ZS share a bold vision for the future of enterprise AI—one where innovation scales through a secure, self-service AI foundation with governance built in by design. In this session, Takeda and ZS share how they are building a unified AI foundation on Databricks, enabling teams to develop, deploy and operate AI confidently in a highly regulated environment. We will walk through Takeda’s AI foundation architecture—built on the Databricks Mosaic AI framework and leveraging managed model serving endpoints and enterprise wide vectorization patterns—while also demonstrating how automated, compliant project provisioning and Takeda-specific responsible AI guardrails enable secure, scalable delivery of AI-driven applications. Attendees will learn how Takeda’s self-service AI foundation accelerates experimentation, improves reuse and reduces operational friction while strengthening trust, audit readiness and enterprise-wide adoption of AI.

title: Securing the Databricks Lakehouse — a Multi-Layered Defense Against Outside and Inside Threats
abstract: As organizations transition mission-critical workloads to the Databricks Lakehouse Platform on AWS, the traditional “castle-and-moat” security model is no longer sufficient. This presentation outlines a comprehensive, defense-in-depth strategy designed to protect data assets from both external adversaries and internal risks. By synthesizing identity federation, network hardening and dynamic governance, organizations can achieve a “Zero Trust” posture that scales with their data growth.

By synthesizing identity federation, network hardening and dynamic governance, organizations can achieve a “Zero Trust” posture that scales with their data growth.

title: Securing the Grid: Modernizing E&U Security Operations with Lakewatch (AI-Native SIEM)
abstract: Energy & Utilities (E&U) face a perfect storm: surging OT/ICS ransomware, strict NERC CIP/TSA regulations, and an explosion of IT/OT telemetry. Traditional SIEMs, hampered by ingest-based pricing and data silos, are failing to keep pace. Enter Lakewatch: Databricks’ next-generation SIEM built natively on the Lakehouse.This session explores how Energy Queensland is moving beyond traditional SIEM constraints to build a unified security data platform. We will dive into the Lakewatch, demonstrating how to eliminate "ingest taxes" and unify IT, Cloud, and OT logs using OCSF-aligned schemas. Learn how to leverage SQL-based detection engineering and AI-driven workflows to hunt threats across petabytes of historical data in seconds. Whether you're struggling with Splunk costs or the complexity of IT/OT convergence, discover how a decoupled storage and compute architecture provides the scale, flexibility, and AI-readiness required to protect critical infrastructure.

speakers.name: Shane Atherton
speakers.bio: Shane is an Account Executive at Databricks, where he partners with Queensland's largest energy organisations to help solve their biggest challenges. He's most passionate about the positive impact leading with data can have on better and faster decision making.

title: Semantic Modeling Best Practices for BI and AI
abstract: AI is making self-service data real, but it creates a new challenge: ensuring humans and AI interpret data the same way. When logic is fragmented across tools, inconsistencies emerge, trust erodes, and AI insights become unreliable.Databricks addresses this with a unified approach to semantics across BI and AI. UC Business Semantics provide governed definitions, AI/BI Dashboards add local semantics, and the Genie Knowledge Store adds domain context to improve how AI answers. Together, these layers enable consistent BI and accurate AI. We’ll cover best practices for designing and operationalizing semantics across Unity Catalog, dashboards, and Genie:- Define and manage UC Business Semantics for governed metrics and dimensions- Use Dashboard Semantic Models and Metric Views for report logic- Use Genie Knowledge Store to improve AI accuracy with domain context and SQL- Combine central semantics and local curation across BI and AI- Avoid common pitfalls in semantic modeling

speakers.name: Chris Eubank
speakers.bio: At Databricks, Chris focuses on enabling business users to unlock insights through expressive and fast interactive dashboards. Previously, he drove efforts in data modeling at Tableau.

title: Serverless at Bank Scale: NAB's Complete Workload Migration to Serverless at Enterprise Scale
abstract: Explore how National Australia Bank (NAB) adopted Databricks Serverless across notebooks, jobs, declarative pipelines, SQL Warehouses and Model Serving—delivering platform reliability, cost efficiency and performance without compromising security or governance. Hear NAB’s blueprint: phased migration, workload prioritization and cross‑team operationalization. Learn the essentials—Unity Catalog for fine‑grained governance, plus Private Link and Secure Egress Gateway for private connectivity—and hard‑won lessons from uplifting Spark to declarative pipelines (DLT/LDP), moving SQL to serverless warehouses, and operationalizing model serving with observability and guardrails. Walk away with actionable patterns, checklists and success criteria for regulated environments. Outcomes include broad serverless adoption, faster MLOps cycles, better SQL performance, lower ingestion cost and sustained query scale—a pragmatic path to bank‑grade serverless at enterprise scale.

speakers.name: Tom McMeekin
speakers.bio: Tom McMeekin is a Senior Solutions Architect at Databricks, where he partners with some of Australia’s largest enterprises to design and scale modern data and AI platforms. With a background spanning cloud architecture, analytics, and cybersecurity, Tom specialises in helping organisations unlock value from their data through secure, cost-effective, and governed solutions. Prior to Databricks, he held senior roles at AWS and brings over a decade of experience in enterprise technology strategy. Tom is a regular speaker and contributor on topics such as serverless data platforms, AI governance, and the future of analytics in regulated industries.

speakers.name: Allan Cuthbertson
speakers.job_title: Head of Platform Engineering and Design
speakers.bio: Allan is a data professional with over 20 years' experience across a number of sectors(Banking, Telecoms, Trading and Advertising ) in the UK, USA and now Australia. He has worked in and led teams across the data lifecycle - architecture and design, ingestion, transformation, visualisation, science, and platform operations. His broad experience helps him deliver assets that are usable, reliable, and integrated into businesses that solve real customer problems. What’s the point of having a Data Lakehouse if no one is going to use it? At NAB, Allan is a Distinguished Engineer focussed on embedding the banks Databricks Lakehouse environment and decommissioning heritage platforms.

title: Serverless Scala on Databricks: Running Apache Spark™ JARs Without Managing Clusters
abstract: Scala remains a critical language for some of the largest and most performance-sensitive Apache Spark™ workloads. But running Scala pipelines shouldn’t require managing clusters, dependencies and runtime configuration by hand.This lightning talk introduces Serverless JARs on Databricks, now in Public Preview and heading to GA. We’ll show how teams run existing Scala Spark applications on serverless compute, what changes operationally and what stays the same.This lightning talk will cover: When serverless execution makes sense for Scala Spark workloads Best practices for packaging and running Scala JARs Common migration pitfalls and how to avoid them The goal is to help Scala teams move to serverless execution without rewriting code or giving up performance and control.
description_raw:
Scala remains a critical language for some of the largest and most performance-sensitive Apache Spark™ workloads. But running Scala pipelines shouldn’t require managing clusters, dependencies and runtime configuration by hand.This lightning talk introduces Serverless JARs on Databricks, now in Public Preview and heading to GA. We’ll show how teams run existing Scala Spark applications on serverless compute, what changes operationally and what stays the same.This lightning talk will cover:

  • When serverless execution makes sense for Scala Spark workloads
  • Best practices for packaging and running Scala JARs
  • Common migration pitfalls and how to avoid them

The goal is to help Scala teams move to serverless execution without rewriting code or giving up performance and control.

title: SharePoint to Lakehouse with Lakeflow Connect — Top 5 Powerful GenAI Patterns
abstract: Many organizations have rich SharePoint data but struggle to use it at scale for intelligence, reporting, and automation. In this demo-heavy session, we demonstrate how to leverage valuable SharePoint content as a first-class data source for data intelligence and analytics by ingesting it into your lakehouse.Using the new Lakeflow Connect SharePoint connector, we will demonstrate incremental, governed pipelines (via Unity Catalog) that ingest both structured data (Excel, CSV, lists) and unstructured content (PDFs, Office files, images). Data lands as streaming tables enriched with SharePoint metadata to power downstream analytics, search, and AI.We will walk through five low-lift, high-impact patterns: driving an AI-powered BI dashboard from Excel and list data, summarizing and analyzing PDF reports, querying PowerPoint decks via natural language in a secure Genie Room, OCR’ing scanned images for text extraction, and consolidating many CSV reports into a single streaming table.

title: Sneak Peek: How We Use Databricks Apps to Power Field Demos at Scale
abstract: How does Databricks keep thousands of field engineers on-message, with the right demo ready for every customer conversation? The answer: we run our demo program on Databricks Apps. In this session, you’ll get a behind-the-scenes look at how we use Apps to catalog, search, and launch hundreds of demos across products and industries. We’ll walk through the FE Demo Catalog, a Databricks App backed by Lakebase that acts as the single source of truth for demos—complete with AI search, links to live environments, recordings, decks, and scripts—so any seller or architect can quickly find “the one right demo” for their meeting. We’ll also show how our Demo Builder flows let field teams submit and update demos in a structured way, keeping quality high without central bottlenecks. If you’ve ever wondered how to scale demo excellence inside your own company, this “Databricks on Databricks” story will give you a concrete pattern for using Databricks Apps to run demo catalogs and demo tools at scale.

title: Spark Connect: The Path to Modern, AI-Ready Spark
abstract: "Spark Connect is more than a new client interface for Apache Spark. It is the foundation for a modern, AI-ready, and production-friendly Spark experience. As AI assistants generate more Spark code, the defaults they learn will shape how the next generation of data applications is built. That makes Spark Connect a strategic shift, not just a technical one.In this session, we will share our vision for making Spark Connect the default way developers build with Spark. We will discuss how DataFrame-first guidance, better AI-generated code compatibility, and machine-readable API discovery can make Connect the easiest path for developers. We will also cover the work needed to build production trust, including closing migration gaps, improving observability, and increasing ecosystem compatibility. Attendees will leave with a clear view of why Spark Connect matters now and what it will take to make it the standard interface for modern Spark applications."

title: Spark DSV2: Growing Up Fast
abstract: Spark’s DataSource V2 integration has taken a major step forward, beginning with the addition of a procedure catalog and row identifier support to enable richer table management and row-level operations across modern data sources. Building on this foundation, recent updates improve MERGE INTO with safer schema evolution and enhance partition filtering for more efficient query planning and execution. DML summaries now provide clearer visibility into write behavior, while key cache fixes resolve long-standing correctness issues in Spark execution. DataSource V2 has also been extended with first-class SQL features such as table constraints, complex default values, and generated columns, laying the groundwork for more advanced table semantics. We’ll also look ahead to deeper Change Data Feed (CDF) integration in Spark to support robust incremental processing, highlighting what’s available today and what’s coming next as Spark continues to close the gap with traditional warehouse systems.

speakers.bio: Szehon Ho is a software engineer at Databricks working on Apache Spark. Previously, he was working on Apache Iceberg at Apple, and is an Apache Iceberg PMC. Prior to that, he was working on Apache Hive at Cloudera and Criteo, and is an Apache Hive PMC.

title: Sponosred by: Acryl DataHub | How Genworth built a governed, AI-Ready data platform with Databricks & DataHub
abstract: Migrating over a million datasets from an on-prem platform to the cloud is not simply a technology refresh. A transformation of this scale requires rethinking how data is governed, surfaced, and trusted. Genworth learned that building at this scale demands a structured approach to metadata management and governance — both to make the right data available across the enterprise and to build the foundation for trustworthy AI. By integrating Databricks with DataHub, the leading open-source metadata management platform, Genworth's governance team built a unified metadata layer enabling automated lineage, incident notification, and data reconciliation. In this talk, we'll share how this foundation is being extended to power business users and AI agents with richer contextual metadata. Attendees will leave with concrete patterns for connecting modern lakehouse infrastructure to enterprise metadata management, and a candid view of progress so far and next steps on the AI journey.

title: Sponsored by: Acceldata | Trust at the Speed of Agents: Reliable AI and Data on Databricks
abstract: As enterprises deploy AI agents across Databricks at unprecedented pace, trust at production speed requires a complete trust platform. This session covers how Acceldata integrates three layers — data observability, agentic data management, and Agent Observability — into a single operating model for reliable AI on Databricks. Each layer reinforces the next: observability without action is alerting, action without observability is guessing, and agents without governance is risk. From anomaly detection through autonomous remediation to agent trust, this approach points toward the self-governing lakehouse where data, agents, and policies stay in lockstep without humans in every decision.

title: Sponsored by: Actian | Scaling AI: Why Data Products and Contracts are the Essential Foundation for Success
abstract: Actian reasons that scaling AI is inherently risky and often fails without the foundational implementation of data products and data contracts. Currently, 95% of Gen AI initiatives deliver zero ROI, and Gartner predicts that 40% of agentic AI programs are predicted to be canceled by 2027 due to poor data quality, siloes, and a lack of governance. To bridge this gap, Actian defines data products as curated, reusable datasets managed as independent assets, supported by data contracts—formal agreements between data producers and consumers regarding schemas and quality thresholds. According to a 2026 survey of 300+ leaders, organizations with company-wide data products are 3.4x more likely to have multiple AI projects in production and 3.3x more likely to successfully deploy agentic AI. By embracing these frameworks, enterprises can de-risk their AI investments and achieve the high-level data integrity required for autonomous success.

speakers.name: Jean-Georges Perrin
speakers.bio: Jean-Georges Perrin (“JGP”) is a Senior Product Manager at Actian, leading initiatives in Data Products and Data Contracts. With 25 years of experience in data architecture—including a tenure as Principal Architect at Expedia—he now chairs the Linux Foundation’s Bitol project, driving the Open Data Contract Standard (ODCS). The author of Implementing Data Mesh and Spark in Action, JGP is a recognized thought leader, Lifetime IBM Champion, and Data Mesh MVP dedicated to balancing data governance with innovation.

speakers.name: Jean-Geroges Perrin
speakers.bio: Jean-Georges Perrin, famously known as “JGP,” is a Senior Product Manager at Actian. He leads critical initiatives in Data Products and Data Contracts, driving the company’s mission to build the industry’s most intelligent data platform. With 25+ years of expertise in data architecture and engineering, JGP focuses on designing systems that balance strict governance with usability and innovation. He believes data should work for people,not the other way around. Previously, JGP was Principal Architect at Expedia, where he defined the strategic direction for global data architecture. He currently chairs the Linux Foundation’s Bitol project, leading global efforts to standardize data through the Open Data Contract Standard (ODCS). He is an acclaimed author who wrote Implementing Data Mesh and Spark in Action. His industry impact has earned him titles like Lifetime IBM Champion and Data Mesh MVP. JGP remains a premier voice in modern data, bridging the gap between technology and strategy.

title: Sponsored by: Acxiom | From Weeks to Minutes: Democratizing Marketing Analytics with Governed AI Agents on Databricks
abstract: Enterprise marketing analytics is constrained not by data, but by specialization and handoffs. Translating business intent into reusable strategies takes weeks across multiple teams. In partnership with Acxiom, Prophecy built a specialized AI agent that transforms business intent into production-grade, governed data pipelines. The agent learns schema, relationships and data distributions, understands business intent, automatically invokes analytical models (propensity, affinity, segmentation) and compiles deterministic Spark and SQL workflows executed on Databricks under Unity Catalog governance. What once required multiple teams and weeks of iteration is reduced to a swift, flexible, governed conversational workflow executed by a single marketer - no SQL, no handoffs. This architecture embeds institutional knowledge into reusable AI skills, enabling marketers while maintaining full auditability, lineage and reproducibility, fundamentally reshaping how marketing teams operate.

His focus is to help clients modernize their mar-tech stack, support their in-housing initiatives, and enable personalization at scale.

speakers.bio: Bhaskar leads AI product management at Prophecy where he builds AI Applications for Marketing, Structured Finance and Auditing. He has been and engineering and product leader in data and AI startups for over 17 years and was one of the earliest adopters of Spark.

title: Sponsored by: Alteryx | The Foundation Comes First: Building a Tax Data Strategy That Scales with AI
abstract: AI, agents, predictive modeling, and the next wave of tax innovation depend on something deceptively simple: the right data, in the right place, ready to use. In this session, Cheria Coram, Director, Tax Technology & Data Enablement at Starbucks, shares her vision for evolving tax data strategy from reactive data wrangling to intentional foundation building. Drawing on her experience leading data and automation for a global tax function, she'll cover how Alteryx unlocked efficiency in data transformation and why the next critical unlock is centralizing, governing, and designing tax-processed data for reuse across the tax lifecycle. She'll also share where the team is headed next, using Alteryx with Databricks to build a scalable foundation that enables tax-ready modeling today and positions the team to layer on analytics, AI, and whatever comes next.

Cheria Coram is Director of Tax Technology & Data Enablement at Starbucks, where she sets the vision and leads the strategy and delivery of data and automation solutions across the tax function. Her work focuses on elevating the tax data experience, enabling scalable compliance, strong controls, audit readiness, and insight-driven support of business growth. Her background includes expertise in financial accounting and reporting for taxes, SOX controls, redesigning processes, and leading tax-focused transformation efforts.

title: Sponsored by: Amperity | GOAL! How Amperity helped Seattle Sounders & Baltimore Ravens fan engagement in Futbol & Football
abstract: Just in time for the World Cup, join Amperity to learn how they are using unified customer data to help fans of futbol and football at the Seattle Sounders and the Baltimore Ravens have a better experience. At the Sounders, Amperity helped increase YOY season ticket deposits by 80% and create a fan experience outside the stadium as meaningful as watching a great match inside the stadium. At the Ravens, Amperity delivered value within the first week, unblocking two year stalled marketing goals and better fan engagement in 45 minutes.

title: Sponsored by: Amperity | How 5 of the Largest Airlines Are Turning Customer Data Into a Competitive Edge
abstract: Airlines face real pressure right now, from rising fuel costs to shifting travel patterns. But the ones pulling ahead are the ones making smarter use of their customer data. Amperity and Databricks are helping five of the largest airlines unify customer data across every touchpoint to build a complete picture of traveler preferences, reduce media spend, and deliver personalized experiences that drive loyalty. Join this session to hear how they did it and what you can apply to your own roadmap.

title: Sponsored by: Anomalo | How GM Uses an Intelligent Data Analyst to Scale AI with Trusted Data
abstract: As General Motors transforms vehicles into software-defined, intelligent assistants, processing trillions of data points with absolute integrity is a safety and business imperative. To scale this vision, GM is migrating to the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform and operationalizing a large-scale data mesh architecture. With Databricks and Anomalo, GM embeds automated data quality into every domain, reducing centralized engineering burden and ensuring AI-ready data integrity across the organization.

title: Sponsored by: Artefact | The workforce you won't recognise: Building the hybrid organization with AI Agents
abstract: Most AI strategies are stuck at the POC stage, not because the technology isn’t ready, but because the data foundation isn’t. In this session, we explore what high-performing organizations are actually building: hybrid workforces where AI agents and humans coordinate through a shared, governed data layer. We’ll show how leading organizations are structuring hybrid teams, why most agent deployments fail to reach production, and what it looks like when agents truly work alongside humans, not as assistants, but as part of the workforce, driving faster decisions, execution, and outcomes. We'll also hear directly from the field. Joining us is the CTO of Danone, who will share how one of the world's leading consumer goods companies is thinking about this shift. Join us to see a live multi-agent system in action and learn a practical blueprint for moving from isolated AI experiments to a production-grade hybrid workforce.

speakers.bio: Ghadi Hobeika is an experienced sales & marketing, data and e-commerce specialist with a strong background in digital media, growth hacking and analytics. He is the Managing Partner & CEO, North America at Artefact, a global data-driven services company specialising in consulting for data transformation and digital & data marketing. Artefact is an independent global network with 36 offices in 27 countries, working with over 300 of major brands around the globe, such as Samsung, L'Oréal and Sanofi.

title: Sponsored by: Astronomer | Unifying Data and AI Orchestration with Apache Airflow
abstract: In a high-pressure kitchen, great service depends on mise en place: everything prepared, organized, and ready before the rush. Production AI demands the same discipline. This talk shows how Apache Airflow unifies data pipelines and AI workflows in one, observable control plane and system of record, replacing fragmented observability, failure handling, and team handoffs. We'll walk through real projects covering multi-agent orchestration, AI-driven pipeline branching, and vision model integration, each illustrating a distinct production pattern, all running on Airflow. The best kitchens don't just execute recipes, they make the rush look effortless.
description_raw: In a high-pressure kitchen, great service depends on mise en place: everything prepared, organized, and ready before the rush. Production AI demands the same discipline. This talk shows how Apache Airflow unifies data pipelines and AI workflows in one, observable control plane and system of record, replacing fragmented observability, failure handling, and team handoffs. We'll walk through real projects covering multi-agent orchestration, AI-driven pipeline branching, and vision model integration, each illustrating a distinct production pattern, all running on Airflow. The best kitchens don't just execute recipes, they make the rush look effortless.

speakers.name: Kenten Danas
speakers.bio: Kenten leads the Developer Relations team at Astronomer and has a background in data engineering and data science. She has first-hand experience adopting and running Apache Airflow at large companies, and is passionate about helping other data engineers scale and get the most out of their data orchestration. When she isn't working with data she's typically outside trail running or skiing.

title: Sponsored by: Astronomer | Your AI Strategy Has a Context Problem. Orchestration Solves It.
abstract: Every enterprise has an AI strategy. Few have the foundation to deliver on it. Most treat context as something defined upfront and consumed downstream. Semantic layers define metrics. Ontologies model relationships. Neither captures what actually ran. Enterprise context is not static. It is evidence that accumulates continuously: which pipelines ran, what changed upstream, what humans corrected. Orchestration is the only layer where that evidence accumulates naturally, not as declared truth, but as a continuous record of what actually happened. That is what production AI runs on. This talk outlines the architectural pattern that makes it possible.

speakers.name: Carter Page
speakers.bio: Carter Page is the EVP of R&D at Astronomer, the home of the modern data orchestration platform. A 30-year veteran of enterprise data infrastructure, Carter previously led data engineering at Two Sigma, overseeing the firm's mission-critical global data pipelines. Prior to Two Sigma, Carter spent a decade at Google, where he led the development of many of the early products and technologies of GCP, including Cloud Bigtable.

title: Sponsored by: Atlan | Mastercard's "Context by Design": Engineering AI-Ready Data Products at Scale
abstract: At Mastercard, trust has always been the product. For six years, that trust has been engineered in layers: privacy by design, then data by design, and now context by design. Each evolution built on the last. Now, as AI agents begin transacting at the speed of commerce, the latest layer matters most — an Enterprise Context Layer, built on Databricks, that machines can read, reason over, and act on without human intervention at every step. Join Fabien Thiaucourt and Vivek Radhakrishnan of Mastercard, and Austin Kronz of Atlan, to learn how engineering and enablement co-own context as a dual mission (and why the productive tension between them is the reason governance actually sticks), how automated metadata and human-centered product thinking combine to build AI-ready data products on Databricks, and how this foundation sets the stage for text-to-SQL and conversational search — and every AI use case that follows.

speakers.name: Austin Kronz
speakers.job_title: Director of Data and AI Strategy
speakers.bio: Austin focuses on enabling humans of data to do more, together. This includes helping customers understand and implement modern data concepts like Data Mesh or DataOps and evangelizing the active metadata category. Austin spent the last 5 years at Gartner as a Director Analyst in their Data and Analytics group.

title: Sponsored by: Atlan | The Enterprise Context Layer: Why Agents Fail — and the Fix — Demystified and Demoed
abstract: Everyone's talking about context layers. Nobody agrees on what one is — a semantic layer with a better name? A knowledge graph in a trench coat? AI is moving from pilot to production — and it made data teams into janitors: cleaning up hallucinations, debugging agents. But the context agents need most is what data teams already own. The definitions, the lineage, the institutional knowledge. That's not a cost center. It's your most strategic AI asset. You already have Databricks — maybe Unity Catalog too. But your agents still don't know which "revenue" definition to use, which tables to trust, or what the business actually means. The gap between pilot and production isn't a model problem. It's a context problem. Join Prukalpa Sankar and Varun Banka to see it live: dark Databricks tables become AI-ready — automated lineage mapped, AI-bootstrapped definitions added, Context Quality Score green — and a Genie query returns a governed response via MCP. All live. All on stage.

speakers.name: Prukalpa Sankar
speakers.bio: Prukalpa Sankar is the co-founder of Atlan, a modern collaborative data workspace. She has previously co-founded a leading data for good company where she led data teams for organizations like the UN, the World Bank, and several large governments.

title: Sponsored by: Boomi | Action Is the New Insight: Agentic Workflows in Practice
abstract: The CDO is now the CDAIO. But while the hype focuses on agentic analytics, real value lies in action. Using the latest research from industry professionals, we’ll reveal how top leaders are moving beyond insights to agentic workflows. We’ll break down the essentials—golden data, governance, and security—needed to successfully activate AI. Don't just watch the AI revolution; lead it. Join us to learn how to bridge the gap between data and execution and get started today.

We’ll break down the essentials—golden data, governance, and security—needed to successfully activate AI. Don't just watch the AI revolution; lead it. Join us to learn how to bridge the gap between data and execution and get started today.

speakers.name: Travis Price
speakers.bio: Travis Price is the Vice President of Global Field Engineering at Boomi, where his team was built with one purpose: helping customers navigate the complexity of their data and application environments so they can deliver real business value and build the trusted data foundation that powers agentic AI. His perspective on enterprise data carries real weight because he has operated at every layer of the problem. He knows what it looks like from the platform side at IBM and SAP, from the application side at Workday, and from the cloud and AI strategy side after six years leading the Global Data and AI Strategy practice at Amazon Web Services. His career journey has given Travis a view of enterprise data transformation that is simultaneously technical, commercial, and organizationally grounded, earned from working the problem at every level of the stack and every stage of the customer journey.

title: Sponsored by: Capgemini | The Foundations for a Million Agents
abstract: Every organization can build an agent — almost nobody is ready to manage a million of them. This session lays out what it actually takes, starting with AI Persona definition: how the business delegates to an agent as a team member, not a system. It then breaks apart four disciplines that must scale independently. Integration & Ecosystem spans MCP, Unity Catalog, and the governance of AI through MLflow, grounding agents in trusted data and policy. Development and Discovery use Genie and Databricks Marketplace to build and evolve agents. Business Management, through Mosaic AI and MLflow, drives accountability. AI Resource Management makes IT the HR function for the AI workforce. Underpinning it all is why a streaming Delta Lake architecture is central to an AI future. This is the architecture for a million agents.

speakers.name: Steve Jones
speakers.bio: Steve Jones is a published author on topics from Cloud and SOA to AI Ethics and Generative AI, and in journals from IEEE Software to the Financial Times. He has been Capgemini’s global lead for Java, where he was a member of several JCP groups including JavaSE 6. He also led the initial group efforts on Cloud and SaaS, and established Capgemini’s global businesses in MDM and Big Data. Steve has built some of the largest next-generation data platforms, and his architectural approaches, such as the Business Data Lake, have become standards with organizations such as Open Group. Most recently, he has been focusing on the challenge of the 50% AI business and the organization and technical changes required to achieve this.

title: Sponsored by: Capital One | Secure Data Collaboration: A Modern Enterprise Enclave Architecture
abstract: Collaborating on sensitive data is critical for business insights but creates significant security and compliance risks. This session introduces a secure data enclave architecture that enables cross-functional analysis on sensitive enterprise data (like PII or IP). Learn how this model strengthens data privacy and provides a comprehensive, auditable trail without moving raw data.

Learn how this model strengthens data privacy and provides a comprehensive, auditable trail without moving raw data.

title: Sponsored by: Capital One Software | Utilizing Context and AI for Cost Optimization & Security
abstract: Managing data systems at scale often leads to hidden inefficiencies and risks that can impact business performance. Capital One will share research on how to leverage context-aware AI to drive significant cost savings compared to manual optimization and strengthen data security posture of large-scale systems. Attendees will gain actionable insights into the role and impact of context on data cloud optimization and security.

title: Sponsored by: CDW | Ask Your Data Anything — The Day Trust Flatlined
abstract: You deployed Genie. The demo worked. Ninety days later, adoption is flat and nobody can explain why. CDW's Head of Presales and Chief Architect break down the gap between Genie deployment and Genie adoption — and the four MVDG conditions (Ownership, Definitions, Lineage, Guardrails) that close it.

title: Sponsored by: CEI | The Agentic Enterprise: How AI Engineering Is Rewiring Business Models
abstract: Most enterprises are adopting AI, yet few are realizing meaningful transformation because they remain stuck thinking in terms of models and isolated use cases. In this session, Sunil Senan introduces the Agentic Enterprise, a new way to understand how AI is fundamentally reshaping how organizations operate and create value. He offers a reality check on why today’s AI approaches fall short, then outlines the shift from models to systems, from systems to enterprises, and ultimately to new business models. Attendees will explore how AI engineering enables autonomous, adaptive operations and how business transformation emerges when AI execution is tightly linked to real-world outcomes. The session concludes with real patterns, lessons learned, and a clear call to action for leaders ready to move from experimentation to enterprise‑wide impact.

speakers.name: Sunil Senan
speakers.bio: Sunil Senan is Global CEO of CEI and an industry leader in data and AI‑led enterprise transformation. Throughout his leadership career, Sunil has guided organizations through large‑scale transformation initiatives that move data and AI from experimentation into core business operations. He is known for driving enterprise‑wide AI adoption, aligning advanced analytics and AI systems to business models, and linking technology investments to measurable business outcomes. Sunil regularly advises executive teams on how AI engineering enables autonomous, adaptive enterprises and is a sought‑after voice on the evolution from AI systems to new business models.

title: Sponsored by: Coalesce | The Age of Data Exhaustion
abstract: No one talks about it, but data teams are tired. They're tired of managing thousands of tables, brittle transformation logic and AI pilots built on foundations no one fully trusts. The problem isn’t data volume. It’s overload. Code overload demands disciplined transformation. Data overload demands intelligent cataloging. And chaos erodes trust. In this session, we’ll show how modern data teams restore order with a metadata-driven data operating layer that standardizes transformation, connects governance to build-time and makes data quality proactive instead of reactive. Because AI doesn’t fix disorder, it amplifies it.

speakers.name: Douglas Barrett
speakers.bio: Douglas brings over 30 years of experience delivering data-driven solutions for large organizations worldwide. Beginning his career in London, he has worked across industries including banking, airlines, retail, and gaming across the UK, Europe, Australia, New Zealand, and the United States. He focuses on helping organizations modernize their data platforms using technologies like Databricks to unify data, analytics, and AI. Douglas advocates for strong business engagement and rapid, iterative delivery, using modern data and AI software to align business needs with data realities—enabling scalable, maintainable solutions that can endure for years.

title: Sponsored by: Cognizant | An AI Data Foundation for Decision Intelligence for Medtronic, in Partnership with Cognizant
abstract: We established a robust Digital Data Foundation to enable more informed, data-driven decision-making across the enterprise. Our approach emphasized the development of both foundational and analytical data products, supported by advanced AI and business intelligence capabilities to deliver deeper analytics and intuitive insights. Conversational BI enhanced accessibility and adoption across the organization, while AI agents driven automation streamlined data engineering processes and significantly improved data quality and enabled multiple business LOBs processes with the right data and analytics. Databricks serves as the unified platform for data engineering and AI/ML workloads, with Unity Data Catalog delivering a comprehensive, integrated view to enable efficient data governance and discovery.

title: Sponsored by: Cognizant | The Autonomous Data Value Chain: How Agent Bricks is Redefining Data Engineering on Databricks
abstract: Most data teams have modernized to the Lakehouse—but the data value chain is still manual, fragmented, and slow. Engineers jump between discovery, modeling, pipelines, quality checks, governance, and ops, stitching together tools and workflows that don’t scale with AI. This lightning session shows what comes next. You’ll see how Agent Bricks act as the production‑grade foundation for building intelligent, cost‑aware agents on Databricks—and how Data Value Chain Agents turn the end‑to‑end lifecycle into a coordinated, autonomous system. Using Cognizant’s Agentic AI–driven Data Value Chain, we’ll walk through how domain‑specific agents for discovery, modeling, engineering, quality, governance, and operations collaborate through orchestration—not handoffs. This is not a theoretical future. You’ll learn how teams are moving from pipelines to agent‑orchestrated workflows, where engineers shift from writing glue code to directing intelligent agents that continuously optimize.

title: Sponsored by: Confluent | How Grainger Powers Real-time Supplier Compliance with Confluent and Databricks
abstract: Modern procurement demands more than periodic reviews — it requires a continuous, intelligent pulse on every supplier relationship. In this session, Grainger shares how they moved from batch pipelines and manual scorecarding to a real-time supplier compliance platform built on Confluent and Databricks. You'll learn how Confluent's Tableflow seamlessly bridges streaming Kafka data into Delta tables within Databricks Unity Catalog — eliminating brittle ETL, unifying schemas, and giving Procurement teams the live insights they need to make faster, more confident decisions about their supplier portfolio.

speakers.name: Paul Cappelluzzo
speakers.bio: Paul is the Principal Data Architect at Grainger, where he focuses on bringing order and insight to complex data environments through enterprise strategy, architecture, innovation, collaboration, and governance.

speakers.name: PRADEEP NAGISETTY
speakers.bio: Pradeep is a Sr. Engineering Manager at Grainger where he drives scalable event-driven architecture, API strategy, and developer experience across cloud-native platforms.

title: Sponsored by: Confluent | Simplify AI-Ready Tables with Confluent Tableflow, Databricks, & Unity Catalog
abstract: Learn how Confluent and Databricks are simplifying the path from real-time data to governed, analytics- and AI-ready tables. This session will show how Confluent Tableflow automatically materializes Kafka topics into Delta tables and registers them with Unity Catalog — eliminating the need for custom streaming pipelines. We’ll walk through how this integration helps data engineers reduce ingestion complexity, enforce data governance and make real-time data immediately usable for analytics and AI.

This session will show how Confluent Tableflow automatically materializes Kafka topics into Delta tables and registers them with Unity Catalog — eliminating the need for custom streaming pipelines. We’ll walk through how this integration helps data engineers reduce ingestion complexity, enforce data governance and make real-time data immediately usable for analytics and AI.

title: Sponsored by: Datadog | 4,500 Jobs, No Blind Spots: AccuWeather's Serverless Databricks Migration with Datadog
abstract: AccuWeather delivers forecasts to 1.5 billion people daily. When their pipelines fail, severe weather warnings don't reach the people who need them. The team used to wait 90 minutes before responding to any alert. Not because fixes were hard, but because monitoring that fires on every transient failure trains teams to ignore everything. This session shows how AccuWeather migrated to Serverless Databricks Workflows and operated approximately 4,500 weekly Lakeflow Jobs with Datadog Data Observability. We’ll cover how AccuWeather using serverless cut compute costs in half and the pre-flight pattern that catches failures before expensive cluster jobs run. Additionally, Datadog turns Databricks system table data into alerts built around weather-specific logic leading to over 50% fewer unactionable alerts and incident response 80% faster. This is due to firing incidents only after five failures in a 45-minute window, and only when multiple forecast models fail the same critical hour.

title: Sponsored by: Datadog | Experimentation & Product Analytics in the Agentic World: Bringing User Signals to Every Release
abstract: When developing software with autonomous agents, the bottleneck shifts from writing code to evaluating it. Agents can generate changes continuously. What limits progress is how quickly you can tell whether those changes are acceptable. Agentic development looks less like traditional software engineering and more like reinforcement learning: a loop of build, release, evaluate, where the fastest signals dominate. If your unit tests return in seconds and your conversion data takes two weeks, guess which one shapes the product. Teams need fast proxies for signals that arrive too slowly to influence an agent's build loop, and governance is needed to reason about signal quality, noise, and coverage. This session introduces an evaluation infrastructure that orchestrates synthetic tests, production telemetry, analytics, and controlled releases to gate autonomous changes. Datadog is building toward this layer, but the framework applies to any team rethinking infrastructure for an agentic world.
