speakers.name: Chetan Sharma
speakers.bio: Che Sharma is a Director of Product Management at Datadog, which he joined following Datadog's acquisition of Eppo (where he was founder and CEO). After pivotal roles as the 4th data scientist at Airbnb and an early data scientist at Webflow, Che founded Eppo to offer the first commercial experimentation platform to rival tools built in-house at the world's biggest tech companies. Now leading Datadog's newly-built Experiments, Feature Flags, and Product Analytics products, Che is again focused on helping product and data teams drive growth, make smart decisions, and build influence as key leaders of the emerging AI era.

title: Sponsored by: Dataiku | Dataiku + Databricks: Your Stack for Production-Ready AI Agents
abstract: In this session, discover how Dataiku extends the Databricks lakehouse to accelerate AI and agent development, orchestration, and governance. We will explore how teams can rapidly build AI and GenAI applications using visual, collaborative workflows; create and orchestrate intelligent agents leveraging Databricks capabilities like Vector Search and Genie; and ensure enterprise-grade governance with built-in lifecycle management, monitoring, and controls. Learn how organizations are moving from experimentation to production faster by combining Databricks’ powerful data platform with Dataiku’s unified AI layer, enabling seamless collaboration across data, engineering, and business teams to deliver real-world AI at scale.

We will explore how teams can rapidly build AI and GenAI applications using visual, collaborative workflows; create and orchestrate intelligent agents leveraging Databricks capabilities like Vector Search and Genie; and ensure enterprise-grade governance with built-in lifecycle management, monitoring, and controls. Learn how organizations are moving from experimentation to production faster by combining Databricks’ powerful data platform with Dataiku’s unified AI layer, enabling seamless collaboration across data, engineering, and business teams to deliver real-world AI at scale.

title: Sponsored by: dbt Labs | Automating Multi-Tenant Analytics from Zero to Production
abstract: isolved is an HR and payroll platform operating in a multi-tenant software architecture. Each instance of isolved must be securely isolated, onboarded quickly, and made analytics-ready without manual overhead. With rapid growth through new tenant launches, isolved modernized on Databricks and adopted dbt to standardize ingestion, governance, and delivery at scale. In this session, isolved’s Head of Data, Sohan Chatterjee, will share a repeatable and production-tested pattern for metadata-driven acceleration: provisioning per-tenant ingestion infrastructure via IaC , auto-generating dbt source definitions from lakehouse metadata, and implementing staging-layer tests to catch issues early in a PII-sensitive environment. Attendees will leave with practical lessons for scaling onboarding, operationalizing trust, and delivering high-impact use cases such as customer health scoring and external partner reporting.

speakers.name: Sohan Chatterjee
speakers.bio: Sohan Chatterjee is a data engineering and analytics leader building enterprise-scale data platforms across Fortune 100/500 organizations and high-growth SaaS. Currently leading analytics engineering at isolved, he built the company's core data platform on Databricks and Snowflake — spanning semantic layer design, partner data sharing, and ML-powered customer intelligence. Previously a Senior Manager at CapTech Consulting, Sohan has led large-scale data modernization initiatives across healthcare, financial services, supply chain, and retail. He holds a B.S. in Industrial and Systems Engineering from Georgia Tech and is based in Philadelphia, PA.

title: Sponsored by: dbt Labs | dbt + Databricks Partner Well Architected Framework for AI Apps
abstract: Building AI apps on Databricks is straightforward. Building AI apps that are trusted, governed, and production-ready is a different challenge entirely. In this talk, we walk through the Databricks Partner Well-Architected Framework with dbt as the data foundation. We’ll show how structured, tested, and semantically rich data flows from dbt into Unity Catalog, and from Unity Catalog into any AI application running on Databricks. We'll deploy a live managed app on Databricks that pulls governed assets directly from Unity Catalog with lineage, contracts, and semantic definitions built end-to-end in dbt. The result is an architectural blueprint where your AI apps aren't guessing at the data beneath them; with dbt, they have the required context to be governed, reliable, and right. You’ll leave with a Well-Architected reference pattern for AI apps on Databricks, and a clear picture of how dbt and Unity Catalog become the governed data infrastructure every AI use case depends on.

title: Sponsored by: definity | From Observability to Agentic Data Engineering: What It Actually Takes
abstract: Data observability is now standard in lakehouse platforms, but it stops at reactive monitoring. As teams push toward proactive agentic workflows, LLMs alone aren’t enough. Without production-aware context, ability to take action in-flight, and automated guardrails, AI remains limited to suggestive copilots trained on generic data. This session breaks down what it takes to build agentic capabilities to continuously optimize cost, resolve incidents in-motion, and evolve pipeline code. We’ll show how definity enables them through full-stack context across data, pipelines, and infrastructure; runtime execution control; and built-in validation loops – turning signals into autonomous action inside Spark pipelines.

speakers.name: Roy Daniel
speakers.bio: Roy is the CEO and co-founder of definity, the Agentic Data Engineering platform for the Lakehouse and Spark ecosystem, helping enterprise teams optimize cost, improve reliability, and accelerate developer velocity. Prior, Roy held data and product leadership positions across Fortune 500 companies, most recently at FIS/Worldpay.

title: Sponsored by: Deloitte | Driving Enterprise Transformation with Agentic AI
abstract: Enterprises across Technology, Media, and Telecommunications (TMT) are moving beyond isolated AI pilots to true transformation by deploying agentic AI – systems that adapt, learn, and drive automation across business functions. Join China Widener to explore Deloitte’s approach and see how the AI Transformation Advisor accelerates strategic alignment, operating-model readiness, and measurable value realization in TMT environments. We’ll examine where agentic AI is delivering ROI today within the tech and media landscape. You’ll learn how leading industry organizations are redesigning processes around human and agent collaboration, investing in data-driven, real-time architectures, and putting governance and controls in place to scale responsibly across complex ecosystems. Leave with actionable strategies and real-world examples for aligning technology, talent, and trust to drive sustainable AI-powered transformation across the TMT sectors.

speakers.name: China Widener
speakers.job_title: Vice Chair and US TMT Industry Leader
speakers.bio: With over 25 years of professional experience, China leads Deloitte’s Technology, Media and Telecommunications Industry and offers a unique perspective on the future of this industry and its sectors. She is deeply passionate about industry convergence and the need for trustworthy AI, and has co-authored articles on technology frameworks in the enterprise, delivery model analysis, and closing the talent gap and also serves on the Board of Directors, Deloitte US. As a principal of Deloitte Consulting LLP, she has guided clients through technology-enabled business transformations and operating model optimizations to achieve performance outcomes and competitive advantage. Beyond her client work, she is also a dedicated leader, leading multiple National Communities and local Inclusion Councils in support of all of our professionals.

title: Sponsored by: Deloitte | Generating Realistic, High-Fidelity Synthetic Financial Data on Databricks
abstract: Access to customer financial data is often slowed by privacy regulations, hindering innovation in risk management and personalization. Deloitte’s dual-model solution on the Databricks Platform generates high-fidelity synthetic data to overcome this challenge. It combines generative adversarial networks for structured data (demographics, creditworthiness) and LLMs for behavioral insights (price sensitivity), complete with an AI-automated quality review. This provides a safe foundation for developing models, testing risk algorithms, and simulating market strategies. This session demonstrates how this approach accelerates financial innovation while reducing compliance risk by replicating customer data utility without exposing sensitive information.

He has consistently delivered measurable impact across fraud detection, financial risk, market surveillance, healthcare, and e-commerce, building intelligent systems that reduce operational losses, automate critical workflows, and drive data-driven decision making at enterprise scale. He is deeply passionate about engineering end-to-end ML and big data solutions that create real-world business value.

title: Sponsored by: Deloitte | Scale Trust, Not Headcount: AI-Powered Semantic Engineering at Southern Glazer's Wine and Spirits
abstract: Trust in business decisions erode when reports, AI Agents, and operational systems provide different values for the same KPIs. The solution is a unified semantic fabric in which KPIs are managed as versioned, testable, and reusable assets—like software—implemented entirely within Databricks without added tooling overhead. Organizations no longer need hordes of data and semantic engineers, manually coding pipelines and maintaining definitions. With AI agents automating data transformations, semantic alignment, and business rule updates, organizations can reduce manual engineering effort, accelerate delivery, and lower operating costs while improving data trust. This session showcases how Southern Glazer's Wine & Spirits is implementing an AI-augmented approach to build their Metrics Bank and Semantic Layer in Databricks. You'll discover real-world results: reduced manual engineering effort, accelerated time-to-insight, and scaled decision-making capabilities across the enterprise.

title: Sponsored by: Domo | Eliminating the Warehouse “Tail Problem”: AI-Powered Optimization on Databricks + Domo
abstract: Warehouse operations don’t fail at the start of the day—they fail at the end. As real-world conditions diverge from static planning assumptions, bottlenecks emerge across palletizer lanes, creating a costly “tail problem” that requires constant manual intervention. This session explores how one logistics operator reimagined warehouse automation by combining Databricks and Domo to create a self-adaptive system. As palletizers fall behind, go offline, or deviate from expected performance, the platform continuously analyzes live data from Databricks and recommends exactly what actions to take—what to move, where to move it, and why. Through an interactive Domo application, supervisors can execute AI-driven recommendations, simulate changes, and respond instantly to disruptions with automated evacuation workflows. The result: a self-aware, adaptive system that replaces reactive firefighting with proactive optimization—improving efficiency, reducing downtime, and delivering measurable ROI.

title: Sponsored by: Domo | From 20 Hours to 2: Databricks + Domo—Billion-Row Insights, Delivered to Every Customer
abstract: What if you could transform multi-day analytics cycles into near real-time iteration—without sacrificing scale or sophistication? We explore how combining Databricks serverless SQL warehouses with Domo Cloud Amplifier unlocks unprecedented speed on billion-row datasets—cutting cycles from 20+ hours to just 1–2. But speed is only half the story. With Domo Publish, you can deploy curated, governed content to dedicated child Domo instances, giving your customers their own fully interactive analytics environment to explore, update, and build on. Paired with Domo Everywhere, you can scale governed, read/write data products across your entire customer base—all managed from a single parent instance. Finally, we'll showcase how Domo AI and MCP accelerate MVP development, turning complex data into high-impact insights faster than ever. If you're looking to operationalize analytics, deliver data products your customers truly own, and respond to change at speed—this session is your blueprint.

speakers.name: Daniel Gouveia
speakers.bio: Dan Gouveia is a Principal Solution Consultant at Domo with over 20 years of experience helping organizations turn data and technology into a competitive advantage. A customer-facing advisor at the intersection of business and technology, Dan specializes in solving complex business challenges through scalable data and analytics solutions. He partners with some of Domo’s largest enterprise customers, working across the organization—from marketing and sales to finance and HR—to transform fragmented data into actionable insights. Known for his ability to translate business needs into technical execution, Dan helps companies modernize their analytics ecosystems and drive faster, smarter decision-making.

speakers.name: Max Isley
speakers.bio: Max Isley is Director of Business Intelligence & Analytics at Live Nation Entertainment, where he leads analytics strategy and AI-powered data solutions for one of the world's largest live entertainment companies. With over 10 years in the industry, Max has built production AI systems that integrate Databricks to drive sponsorship and business decisions at scale. He specializes in translating complex data infrastructure into actionable intelligence for commercial teams.

title: Sponsored by: Domo | From Prediction to Action: Agentic AI for Ambulatory Care on Databricks
abstract: Healthcare organizations don’t suffer from a lack of data—they suffer from a lack of timely action. No-shows, late arrivals, and provider delays create millions in lost revenue and poor patient experiences, yet most systems stop at reporting the problem. In this session, we demonstrate how Databricks powers real-time predictive intelligence, while Domo operationalizes it through agentic AI workflows that take action. Using live appointment, patient, weather, and traffic data, AI agents continuously evaluate no-show risk and dynamically decide the next best action—triggering SMS outreach, recommending overbooking strategies, and alerting staff before disruptions occur. Attendees will see how this closed-loop system transforms static analytics into autonomous decisioning, with full governance, auditability, and human-in-the-loop validation. The result is a proactive care model that reduces no-shows, stabilizes clinic throughput, and delivers measurable ROI within weeks—not months.

title: Sponsored by: EXL | From Dilemma to Decision-Ready: Turning Data into Actionable Intelligence
abstract: Enterprises face a growing chasm between raw data and actionable insights. Fragmented lineage, inconsistent quality, disconnected governance, and unstructured data complexities undermine trust in analytics and impede informed decision-making. This session explores how EXLdata.ai bridges this gap by embedding an agentic, semantic intelligence layer directly into Databricks technologies like Delta Lake and Unity Catalog. This integrated approach overcomes obstacles to transform raw data into reliable, context-rich intelligence at scale. Attendees will learn how to: • Reconstruct data lineage to ensure traceability from source to insight • Automate data ingestion & transformation using coordinated, multi-agent workflows • Integrate technical & business metadata for transparent, business-aware governance • Shift data quality control to continuous, proactive anomaly detection • Activate unstructured data through embedded ML and semantic enrichment

title: Sponsored by: EY | Executive Decisions at-Speed: Accelerating AI with Trusted Data Products on Databricks
abstract: As AI adoption accelerates, executives face a critical challenge: data readiness. This session reveals a proven strategy for building scalable, trusted data products for Fonterra using the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform. Learn how domain-driven, reusable data assets on Delta Lake with Delta Sharing, lay the foundation for reliable AI outcomes for Fonterra, using Databricks Agent Bricks and Genie. Discover how standardized, curated data products reduce preparation time, boost experimentation, and deliver consistent results. Real-world use cases from Fonterra demonstrate how this approach powers enterprise-wide AI and self-service analytics. Gain actionable insights to prioritize data product development, foster a culture of trust, and drive faster, more confident decision-making at scale.

title: Sponsored by: EY | The Future of Finance Is Now
abstract: Finance is at a turning point: fragmented data and manual processes areout, and forward-thinking leaders are embracing transformation. This session revealshow today’s CFOs are leveraging Databricks Data and AI services to revolutionizefinance functions, FP&A, Controllership, Supply Chain, Risk, and Tax, by buildingtrusted, governed, and scalable data foundations. Attendees will learn how to move from process-centric to data-centric models with afocus on people, process, and technology, including the launch of a scaled FinanceData Office and unified data platform. Explore how EY’s Finance Data Intelligence,powered by Databricks, drives automated data ingestion, improved data quality, and asingle source of truth. Discover how these innovations reduce risk and enable faster,smarter decision-making for modern finance teams.

title: Sponsored by: F5 | Who’s Controlling Your Agents?
abstract: You’ve built and deployed your agents, but once they’re live, how do you ensure they stay within bounds? Agents don’t fail loudly. Poisoned vector data, prompt injection, or dangerous tool calls aren’t flagged, they’re executed. That can lead to data leakage, unauthorized actions, and silent drift outside intended boundaries. In this session, F5 shows how to enforce runtime guardrails that give full visibility into agent behavior, tool usage, and decision paths, so teams can secure agents without breaking workflows. Key Takeaways Enforce boundaries on agent behavior, tool use, and data access at runtime Gain visibility into agent actions across multi-step workflows Use red teaming to uncover vulnerabilities before production and auto remediate
description_raw:
You’ve built and deployed your agents, but once they’re live, how do you ensure they stay within bounds?   Agents don’t fail loudly. Poisoned vector data, prompt injection, or dangerous tool calls aren’t flagged, they’re executed. That can lead to data leakage, unauthorized actions, and silent drift outside intended boundaries.   In this session, F5 shows how to enforce runtime guardrails that give full visibility into agent behavior, tool usage, and decision paths, so teams can secure agents without breaking workflows.  

Key Takeaways

  • Enforce boundaries on agent behavior, tool use, and data access at runtime
  • Gain visibility into agent actions across multi-step workflows
  • Use red teaming to uncover vulnerabilities before production and auto remediate

title: Sponsored by: Fireworks AI | Own Your AI: Beat Frontier Models With Your Own Data - Presented by Fireworks AI
abstract: The best AI companies aren't renting intelligence from closed model APIs—they're building their own. In this session, Roberto from Fireworks AI busts three common myths about training open models: that closed models are always better, that training is hard, and that you need massive labeled datasets. Drawing on real production examples from real-world examples from clients, he'll show how supervised training, direct preference optimization, and reinforcement training turn your production data into a compounding moat—and why the best model for your product is the one you own.

Drawing on real production examples from real-world examples from clients, he'll show how supervised training, direct preference optimization, and reinforcement training turn your production data into a compounding moat—and why the best model for your product is the one you own.

title: Sponsored by: Fivetran | Building an AI-ready data foundation at Superhuman with Databricks and Fivetran
abstract: As Superhuman expanded its AI platform across Grammarly, Coda, Superhuman Mail, and Superhuman Go, more of the business began to rely on timely data from Salesforce, Outreach, Pardot, Stripe, Zendesk, Qualtrics, and other third-party systems. The challenge went far beyond moving data into Databricks. Go-to-market, finance, and customer teams needed faster, reliable access to trusted data without turning every new data request into weeks of custom engineering. In this session, Superhuman shares how it standardized third-party ingestion on Fivetran, built a self-serve operating model in Databricks, and used Connector SDK plus AI-assisted development to cut specialized connector work from months to days. The result: faster reporting and alerting for a $300M ad program, support for internal AI-ready workflows, and a broader data foundation that contributed to a 10% lift in LTV.

title: Sponsored by: Fivetran | From Backlog to Breakthrough: Inova Scales Data & AI with Fivetran and Databricks
abstract: Healthcare organizations operate some of the most complex data environments, spanning thousands of systems across clinical, financial, and operational domains. At Inova Health, this complexity created an opportunity to rethink how data could better support analytics and AI at scale. In this session, Inova shares how they accelerated a 4-year data modernization roadmap into just 6 months by standardizing on a modern data foundation with Fivetran, dbt, and Databricks. By replacing fragmented pipelines with a unified, automated data movement layer, Inova reduced data movement costs by up to 8x and cleared a backlog of 500+ requests. With near real-time, trusted data now powering decision-making across the organization, Inova is enabling faster insights, improving operations, and building scalable analytics and AI applications on Databricks — creating a strong foundation for the next generation of intelligent systems.

title: Sponsored by: Fivetran | Open Data Infrastructure: Built for agentic AI
abstract: As AI accelerates the pace of change, demanding fresher data, diverse formats, and support across multiple engines, many teams discover their infrastructure was built for reporting, not real-time AI at scale. Open Data Infrastructure is redefining how organizations design for analytics, operations, and AI. By leveraging Fivetran as an interoperable data foundation, organizations can embrace open standards, separate storage from compute, and keep data portable across clouds and engines, preserving adaptability while scaling AI and operational workloads with Databricks. Join this session to learn how organizations: -Build an open, future-proof foundation that supports analytics, operations, and AI without repeated replatforming. -Preserve control over costs and data access as AI workloads increase in scale and complexity. -Design architectures that prevent platform lock-in through interoperability across ingestion, transformation, and activation. -See ODI brought to life in a live demo.

By leveraging Fivetran as an interoperable data foundation, organizations can embrace open standards, separate storage from compute, and keep data portable across clouds and engines, preserving adaptability while scaling AI and operational workloads with Databricks. Join this session to learn how organizations:

-Build an open, future-proof foundation that supports analytics, operations, and AI without repeated replatforming.

-Preserve control over costs and data access as AI workloads increase in scale and complexity.

-Design architectures that prevent platform lock-in through interoperability across ingestion, transformation, and activation.

-See ODI brought to life in a live demo.

speakers.name: Kelly Kohlleffel
speakers.job_title: Sr Global Director, Partner Sales Eng
speakers.bio: Kelly Kohlleffel leads the Fivetran Global Partner Sales Engineering organization, working with a broad ecosystem of technology and consulting services partners on modern data product and solution approaches. He also hosts the Fivetran Data Podcast where some of the brightest minds across the data community talk about their data journey, vision and challenges. Before Fivetran, he was with Hashmap and NTT DATA (data solution and service consulting), Hortonworks (in Hadoop-land) and Oracle. You can connect with Kelly on LinkedIn or follow him on Twitter.

title: Sponsored by: Fractal | From Questions to Action: Conversational AI for Occasion‑Based Marketing
abstract: This presentation introduces a Conversational AI–driven BI platform for marketing, designed by Fractal for occasion‑based marketing at a global snacking company. Built on Databricks, the platform uses DB Genie and AI/BI to translate plain‑English questions into governed analytics, Agent Bricks to orchestrate multi‑step reasoning and insight workflows, and Unity Catalog to ensure enterprise‑grade governance, security, and trust across data assets. The experience is further strengthened by Fractal’s behavioral sciences, which enabled personalized, data-led campaigns, supported by neuroscience for emotionally resonant messaging.

title: Sponsored by: Genpact | Enabling AI-Ready Data
abstract: Discover how Genpact and Baker Hughes are revolutionizing data platforms with an AI-driven Lakehouse designed for seamless human and agent interactions. This session will explore a four-layer approach: integrating master data for a single, trusted foundation; building ontologies to connect jobs, cash, and invoices; enabling natural language queries through a metrics layer—no SQL or analysts required; and leveraging the Agent Bricks multi-agent framework to orchestrate workflows end-to-end. Attendees will gain actionable insights into creating a unified, intelligent platform that transforms data into decisions and connects every step from job to cash.

speakers.name: Matthew Arellano
speakers.bio: Matthew Arellano is Senior Vice President at Genpact, where he leads the Databricks Business Groups, shaping the company's global strategy across data modernization, agentic AI, and enterprise platform transformation for Fortune 500 clients. Before Genpact, he co-founded XponentL Data, building next-generation data products and AI-powered accelerators that are now embedded in Genpact's Data & AI practice, and prior to that led the Global Data, Analytics and AI Strategy at Accenture. With nearly 20 years at the intersection of data, cloud, AI and enterprise transformation, Matthew brings a practitioner's perspective on how organizations move from data and AI strategy to measurable business outcomes, at scale.

title: Sponsored by: Glean | From Analytics to Action: Powering Databricks Insights with Full Enterprise Context
abstract: Great AI answers need more than governed data alone. They need enterprise context—the people, documents, conversations, and workflows that turn an insight into action. In this session, we’ll show how Glean + Databricks bring those pieces together in one natural-language experience. With Glean connected to Databricks through Genie, employees can ask business questions in plain English and get answers grounded in Databricks governance and permissions, enriched with the broader company context around it. The result is a faster path from analytics to decisions, with trusted insights that are more accessible, more actionable, and more useful across the business. In this session, we’ll demonstrate how this unified experience works by showing how sample questions are routed from Glean to Databricks Genie, returning answers with a clear path back to the underlying data—so organizations can make data-driven decisions more accessible, trustworthy, and actionable across the business.

title: Sponsored by: Glean | How Enterprises Turn Databricks Data Into Everyday Value with Glean
abstract: Data only drives real business value when it is made actionable and accessible to everyone who needs it. See how customers are turning Databricks investments into day-to-day business value with Glean by bringing governed data together with the enterprise context needed to make it actionable. We’ll share two examples: Real estate platform Zillow’s employee-facing HR agent, which helps answer workforce questions in Slack using trusted company context and people data, and a leading automotive manufacturer’s use of Glean AI to support engineering and product workflows with easier access to structured data and company knowledge. The result: Faster self-service, fewer bottlenecks for technical teams, and more useful AI in the flow of work.

title: Sponsored by: Glean | Powering Enterprise-Grade Agents: Glean + MCP for Databricks Agent Bricks
abstract: Databricks Agent Bricks is a powerful foundation for building agents on structured data – but enterprise questions require more than tables alone. They require deep, permission-aware context across documents, Slack, Jira, CRM systems, wikis, and institutional knowledge. In this session, we’ll show how Glean integrates with Agent Bricks to unlock the missing layer of enterprise context. By combining Databricks capabilities like Genie and Unity Catalog with Glean’s unified access to knowledge across 100+ SaaS applications, agents gain the ability to explain what happened, why it happened, and what actions to take next. We’ll cover the architecture, governance model, and real examples of agents that blend live data with enterprise knowledge to deliver more complete, actionable outcomes.

abstract: As AI reshapes the modern data stack, data leaders face a new challenge: finding tools that serve everyone from seasoned engineers to business users who have never written a query from scratch. Hex tackles both with deep-dive analytics and conversational analytics. Mikey Flynn, Director of Core Data at Rivian, will explore how he replaced a fragmented data ecosystem, drove Hex adoption across the company, and unlocked insights across the business.

title: Sponsored by: Hightouch | How Chick-fil-A Turns Guest Data into Caring Experiences
abstract: At Chick-fil-A, hospitality has always started with a warm welcome at the counter—and with a vision to be the world’s most caring company, that care now guides how the brand stewards guest data. Using Databricks and Hightouch, Chick-fil-A is building a secure guest intelligence and activation layer that can understand every visit, order, and interaction while rigorously protecting customer data. This session follows that journey: how the team brings restaurant, app, and loyalty data together in Databricks and uses Hightouch’s Composable CDP to turn trusted insights into more relevant, timely lifecycle programs and offers across channels. Attendees will leave with a vision and reference architecture for caring, data-driven guest marketing on Databricks.

title: Sponsored by: Hightouch | How XP Drove a 72% Lift in Account Opens and 35% Activation Increase with Agentic CRM
abstract: XP is one of the largest financial institutions in Brazil, and they didn't get there by treating onboarding as a generic funnel. After proving the value of their first-party data in Databricks, XP teamed up with Hightouch to tackle a stubborn problem: millions of sign-ups that never became active accounts in traditional CRM campaigns. In this session, XP will show how they implemented Agentic CRM by turning their Databricks lakehouse into an AI decisioning engine that continuously tests offers, channels, and timing on governed first-party data, then activates those decisions in real time across XP's marketing stack via Hightouch. By putting AI decisioning at the center of onboarding, XP drove a 72% lift in account open rates and a 35% increase in activation conversion in their first few weeks—while maintaining full control of experimentation, governance, and monitoring. You'll leave with a blueprint covering data pipelines, decision policies, measurement, and cross-functional buy-in.

title: Sponsored by: IBM | Empower AI with real-time context across hybrid data estates
abstract: AI agents can dramatically accelerate your ability to react to new information and make trusted decisions — but only when they have the right context from your data. However, preparing data sets for each individual AI use case is time consuming and expensive — hindered by fragmented data landscapes, inconsistent governance, and data complexity. In this session, learn how to power AI agents with real-time context from unstructured and structured data across hybrid data landscapes — without migration. Empower your AI agents, and your business, to act with confidence.

speakers.bio: Ed Anuff is a visionary technology leader and entrepreneur, currently leading IBM's product strategy for data as Chief Product Officer for IBM watsonx.data. He was previously Chief Product Officer at DataStax, which was acquired by IBM in 2025. With over 30 years of experience, he has shaped industry-defining products—from launching one of the first Internet search engines at Wired, founding enterprise portal pioneer Epicentric, and guiding Apigee through its IPO and acquisition by Google. title: Sponsored by: IBM | Scale AI agents from prototype to production into enterprise systems
abstract: AI doesn’t deliver value in the lab—it delivers in production. In this live demo, see how IBM moves from building agents with IBM Bob to running them at scale with watsonx Orchestrate, the enterprise AI control plane. We’ll show how agents go from creation to real-world deployment, with full visibility, governance, and control. This is what enterprise AI looks like when it’s not a pilot—but a system you can trust and scale.

title: Sponsored by: Immuta | How Vizient AI Transformation Starts with Scaling Data Access with Immuta
abstract: As organizations scale AI, the biggest barrier is often not the model — it is governed, scalable access to trusted data. In this session, Vizient shares how it is building a Data Access Framework powered by Immuta to support both human and AI consumers with secure, policy-driven access to enterprise data. The session explores how Vizient is approaching data access as a strategic capability rather than a series of manual approvals and one-off controls. Attendees will learn how Unity Catalog serves as the governance foundation for discoverability and trusted metadata, while Immuta provides dynamic policy enforcement — ensuring the right users, applications, and AI systems access only the data they are authorized to use. Attendees will leave with a practical framework for rethinking data access in their own organizations — one that supports business users, data teams, and AI systems without compromising control.

title: Sponsored by: Impetus Technologies | Powering Context-Aware Agentic AI at Scale: Why Modernization Must Come First
abstract: AI models & agents can't access, understand, or interpret enterprise data trapped in legacy silos. Enterprises must build a powerful, domain-aware context layer that reduces hallucinations and drives accurate, scalable, trustworthy agentic AI outcomes. Impetus LeapLogic Suite helps realize this by enabling automated, risk-free modernization of legacy data estates. Discover how to transform massive legacy workloads and embedded business logic into a truly AI-ready, context-aware data foundation on Databricks. Session highlights: Closing the Context Gap - from raw, fragmented data sources to enterprise AI that acts, reasons & self-corrects Role of ontology-driven modernization & domain mapping in AI context interpretation Automating modernization of legacy workloads with end-to-end lineage visibility & validation Deploying production-ready, context-aware agents on Agent Bricks Best practices and real-world success stories

title: Sponsored by: Infogain | AI-Accelerated Real Time Supply Chain Operations on Databricks
abstract: Enterprises are reimagining supply chains by consolidating fragmented data into a unified Lakehouse, enabling real-time decisions, AI-driven forecasting, and end-to-end visibility from factory to last mile. In this session, we will showcase how we innovated with AI to accelerate migration, modernization, and transformation of supply chain operations for global customers. This solution addressed critical business challenges like demand volatility, misaligned inventory, shipment risks, and decision latency due to fragmented systems and batch-era architectures that significantly impact cost and performance of operations. Our real time and event-based solution is powered by Structured Streaming, Delta Lake, Unity Catalog, Agent Bricks and AI/BI Genie architecture to drive ~20+% faster operations, ~$15M+ cost savings, and ~60% reduction in manual effort leading to resilient supply chain operations at scale.

abstract: In the race to AI adoption, data management emerges as the critical enabler that transforms raw data into an actionable asset. Informatica delivers data management agents and an agentic workflow framework that accelerates the operationalization of agents built on Agent Bricks. By unifying all enterprise data assets for a 360-degree view - validated, governed and enriched with business context, Databricks customers on an agentic transformation journey can experience faster agent deployments and shorter time to value, with no data left behind and easier adherence to compliance mandates. In this session, you will: -Explore trends and data management strategies for turning fragmented data into trusted data intelligence -Learn how you can leverage Informatica-managed MCP servers for governance and master data management (MDM) in your agentic workflows -Find out how you can accelerate data readiness for your Agent Bricks agents

title: Sponsored by: Informatica | Govern Like You Mean It: Centralized Governance, Security and Compliance for Databricks and Beyond
abstract: Holistic data governance is not only for keeping compliance officers happy, it’s also imperative for data democratization, improving analytics and AI, and unlocking new revenue streams. title: Sponsored by: Informatica | Unlocking AI-Ready Data: How To Transform Pharmaceutical Research with Informatica & Databricks
abstract: Fragmented, inconsistent data is one of the biggest barriers to AI in life sciences. In this session, learn how to use Informatica MDM to build a unified, AI-ready data foundation on the Databricks platform. We'll cover: -Golden Records on Databricks — How Informatica MDM unifies disparate data sources into a single, authoritative record -Enabling AI in Life Sciences — How governed master data unlocks AI/ML workloads across clinical research and drug safety -MDM for Pharmaceutical Research — How Informatica MDM's matching, merging, and survivorship capabilities solves real pharmaceutical research challenges at scale Walk away with a concrete blueprint for turning messy enterprise data into a competitive advantage.

title: Sponsored by: Insight | Use the Data Built Tool (dbt) to build a Databricks SQL Warehouse
abstract: If you haven't used dbt, you should. It enables companies to transform data via the medallion architecture, though it usually leaves extraction and loading to tools like Fivetran. While the seed command handles small static data, dbt’s core strength lies in modeling. In this presentation, you will master the project, profile, schema, and properties YAML files. We will cover vital materialization strategies, including seeding, snapshots, tables, views, and materialized views. A key advantage is dbt’s versatility; its adapters allow you to run the same models across Databricks, or PostgreSQL. We will also learn how to use macros to bridge vendor differences in dates and currency. To solve the documentation gap, we’ll use Claude Opus to generate descriptions from schemas. Finally, we’ll prove dbt’s scalability, highlighting a project managing 150 TB of data and 8,000 models. Don't miss out on the industry standard for data engineering—register for the event now!

title: Sponsored by: Insight| Beyond the Sandbox: Using Lakebase Instant Branching for High-Stakes AI Agent Deployment
abstract: As AI agents evolve from chat-based assistants into autonomous systems, the cost of data errors and unintended actions grows. How do you let an agent update pricing or modify records without exposing production systems safely? Enter Lakebase. In this session, we demonstrate a safety‑first architecture using Lakebase’s serverless Postgres and Instant Branching capabilities. You’ll see a live demo of an Autonomous Pricing Agent responding to real-time market signals. Instead of writing directly to production, the agent triggers an Instant Branch—a zero‑copy, isolated clone of the operational database created in seconds. Here, we run automated pre‑flight validations, business rules, and AI/BI impact analysis before selectively merging approved changes back to the primary database. Attendees will learn how to implement Git‑for‑Data style workflows, manage agent state and memory using pgvector, and continuously sync changes using Lakehouse Sync for auditability and downstream analytics.

title: Sponsored by: Keebo | Teach Your Warehouse to Drive Itself: Cutting Costs with Reinforcement Learning on Databricks SQL
abstract: Databricks SQL has transformed how teams interact with their data, delivering fast query performance, seamless scalability, and a lakehouse architecture that brings together the best of data warehousing and data engineering. It’s an incredibly powerful platform, but getting the most out of it requires constant attention to cost and performance. Warehouse workloads are dynamic. Query demands shift around the clock, teams grow, and what was perfectly tuned last week may be wasteful or underperforming today. Maximizing efficiency on Databricks SQL is not a one-time task. It’s an ongoing challenge. That’s where Keebo comes in. In this session, we’ll show how Keebo uses reinforcement learning to learn your unique workload patterns and continuously optimize your Databricks SQL warehouses in real time, maximizing performance while reducing costs. No manual tuning. No trade-offs. Just a warehouse that gets smarter over time.

title: Sponsored by: KPMG | From Blank Page to Production: How KPMG built a Full-Stack Al Factory on Databricks
abstract: Enterprises no longer struggle to experiment with AI—they struggle to operationalize it safely and at scale. In this session, KPMG reveals how you can evolve from traditional software engineering into an AI‑native approach that accelerates delivery while preserving enterprise governance. By combining agentic development (like Claude Code), Databricks’ built‑in agent skills, and KPMG’s proprietary plugins, teams can focus on articulating what to build while intelligent agents assemble the solutions. Leveraging the Databricks platform end‑to‑end—including Apps, Unity Catalog, and model serving—we have simplified the lifecycle from ideation to production. The result is a scalable AI Factory that enables rapid deployment, allowing you to deliver agent‑based use cases faster, with greater agility, and with the strict controls enterprises demand.

title: Sponsored by: LakeFusion | When MDM Meets Ontology: A New Architecture for Enterprise Data Intelligence
abstract: Every enterprise has data. But most struggle to extract true meaning. We've spent decades building separate systems for analytics and operations, separate tools for data quality and governance, separate platforms for master data and transactional data. The result: fragmented records, conflicting hierarchies, and business processes that can't trust the data they depend on. This talk introduces the concept of an operational ontology: a data architecture that doesn't just describe what your data should look like, but enforces it at scale, in real time, inside the lakehouse. Drawing from production deployments that consolidated 100M+ records across dozens of source systems in weeks (not months), we'll show how LakeFusion’s lakehouse-native entity resolution, streaming data quality, and AI-powered stewardship combine to build something traditional MDM and top-down knowledge graphs never could: a living, governed, operational model of your business that AI agents can actually act on.

title: Sponsored by: Lovelytics | Automating High-Stakes Finance Workflows with AI Agents
abstract: Finance back office teams spend significant time on manual, document-heavy workflows like remittance reconciliation and proof of delivery validation. We present how we deployed two production-grade automation use cases for Lippert, saving 250+ hours monthly with over 90% model accuracy. We first cover remittance reconciliation from lockbox PDFs spanning hundreds of pages. The solution uses a deterministic extraction pipeline that segments documents, normalizes headers, cleans OCR noise, validates line items, and applies customer enrichment to produce finance-ready tables and exception-focused audit trails. Next, we detail proof of delivery automation, where standard OCR struggles with crossed-out quantities and signatures. This use case combines computer vision and LLMs using Azure Document Intelligence and OpenCV smart cropping with a safety-first validation layer. We share design tradeoffs, failure modes, and lessons learned from deploying finance agents in production.
description_raw: Finance back office teams spend significant time on manual, document-heavy workflows like remittance reconciliation and proof of delivery validation. We present how we deployed two production-grade automation use cases for Lippert, saving 250+ hours monthly with over 90% model accuracy. We first cover remittance reconciliation from lockbox PDFs spanning hundreds of pages. The solution uses a deterministic extraction pipeline that segments documents, normalizes headers, cleans OCR noise, validates line items, and applies customer enrichment to produce finance-ready tables and exception-focused audit trails. Next, we detail proof of delivery automation, where standard OCR struggles with crossed-out quantities and signatures. This use case combines computer vision and LLMs using Azure Document Intelligence and OpenCV smart cropping with a safety-first validation layer. We share design tradeoffs, failure modes, and lessons learned from deploying finance agents in production.

speakers.name: Sudhir Gajre
speakers.bio: Sudhir Gajre is the Managing Director of the AI and GenAI Practice at Lovelytics, where he leads the Generative AI practice. He brings over 20 years of consulting experience, driving complex data and AI transformations across industries including healthcare, financial services, media, insurance, and retail. Sudhir also serves as an Adjunct Professor at the University of Nebraska at Omaha and is a member of the Databricks Technical Council. A frequent conference speaker and blogger on GenAI and Databricks, he is known for turning innovation into impact through hands-on expertise and practical frameworks that bridge strategy and execution.

title: Sponsored by: Lovelytics | Cutting the AI Noise: A CEO Guide to Real ROI with Robert Herjavec
abstract: Robert Herjavec is a Shark Tank investor and tech entrepreneur who has seen which AI bets pay off and which fail. He joins Lovelytics CEO Scott Love for a direct talk on how leaders move AI from a cost center to a driver of operating leverage. This fireside chat skips the hype to focus on the cold math of enterprise deployment. Scott and Robert discuss how to audit your AI roadmap with the same scrutiny an investor brings to a term sheet. You will learn to use the Databricks Platform for executive oversight to ensure your AI spend is secure, compliant and hitting the P&L. They share a blunt assessment of common "innovation theater" projects that waste time and capital. The discussion provides a protocol for deciding which initiatives to fund and which to kill. You will learn to measure success through margin expansion rather than technical milestones. Leave with a plan to prioritize high-value projects on Databricks that deliver measurable shareholder value.

speakers.name: Robert Herjavec

He founded Herjavec Group (renamed Cyderes), which grew into one of the largest global cybersecurity firms and was acquired by private equity in 2001. He remains as a shareholder and board member. Robert has invested in and advised dozens of technology companies and is known for backing founders with strong vision and operational discipline.

title: Sponsored by: Microsoft | Unlocking the Microsoft Data & AI Ecosystem with Azure Databricks: From Insight to Impact
abstract: In today’s data-driven landscape, organizations need more than analytics—they need a unified platform that turns raw data into actionable intelligence across the Microsoft ecosystem. This session explores how Azure Databricks serves as the backbone of modern data architecture, integrating with Microsoft Entra ID, Foundry, Agent 365, Power BI, and Microsoft Fabric to accelerate innovation. Learn how to use Azure Databricks for scalable data engineering, advanced analytics, and AI-driven solutions while enabling real-time collaboration and governance. Through practical examples and architectural patterns, we’ll show how to eliminate data silos, optimize performance, and empower teams to deliver insights faster. Whether building pipelines, deploying ML models, or enhancing BI, this session provides the strategies and tools needed to unlock the full potential of your Microsoft data ecosystem.

title: Sponsored by: MinIO | How a Fortune 500 Manufacturer Runs Databricks AI on 250 TB of On-Premises Data
abstract: A Fortune 500 semiconductor manufacturer generates hundreds of terabytes of production data across facilities in two international regions. That data must stay on-premises for regulatory compliance, latency sensitivity, and economics of scale — but the data science team depends on Databricks for the AI modeling that drives yield improvement and quality assurance. Rather than replicating data to the cloud or building custom ETL pipelines, they deployed MinIO AIStor with native Delta Sharing embedded at the storage layer. Databricks queries live Delta and Iceberg tables on-premises through Unity Catalog with zero data movement. No copies. No pipelines. No additional infrastructure. In this session, hear how the solution was architected, what decisions were made and why, and what the team learned operating it in production. Includes perspectives from MinIO and Databricks, a technical walkthrough, and a live demo of AIStor Table Sharing connected to a Databricks workspace.

speakers.name: Denis Dubeau

Denis is a seasoned professional with significant industry experience in Data Engineering and Data Warehousing with previous stops at Greenplum, Hortonworks, IBM and AtScale.

title: Sponsored by: MinIO | On-Prem to Databricks: Zero-Copy Analytics with Native Delta Sharing for Iceberg and Delta Lake
abstract: Organizations need to give Databricks direct access to on-premises data without copying it or building pipelines. Traditional approaches introduce duplication, complexity, and risk, especially when sovereignty or latency requirements prevent cloud movement. AIStor Table Sharing is MinIO's native Delta Sharing protocol implementation embedded in the AIStor binary, delivering streamlined administration, consistency, and full governance while enabling secure, read-only access for consumers such as Databricks. Key highlights: -Native Delta Lake and Apache Iceberg table support -Authentication via bearer tokens and OAuth2/OIDC -Zero-downtime share, schema, and table administration -Bare-metal, containerized, and hybrid deployment support -Practical guidance deploying with Databricks Attendees will learn how AIStor Table Sharing eliminates data movement, reduces egress costs, enforces granular security, and enables hybrid analytics while keeping data under organizational control.
description_raw:
Organizations need to give Databricks direct access to on-premises data without copying it or building pipelines. Traditional approaches introduce duplication, complexity, and risk, especially when sovereignty or latency requirements prevent cloud movement. AIStor Table Sharing is MinIO's native Delta Sharing protocol implementation embedded in the AIStor binary, delivering streamlined administration, consistency, and full governance while enabling secure, read-only access for consumers such as Databricks. Key highlights:

-Native Delta Lake and Apache Iceberg table support

-Authentication via bearer tokens and OAuth2/OIDC

-Zero-downtime share, schema, and table administration

-Bare-metal, containerized, and hybrid deployment support

-Practical guidance deploying with Databricks

Attendees will learn how AIStor Table Sharing eliminates data movement, reduces egress costs, enforces granular security, and enables hybrid analytics while keeping data under organizational control.

title: Sponsored by: Monte Carlo | Building the Foundation for Trusted AI in Regulated Industries
abstract: The enterprises with the most to gain from AI are often the slowest to deploy it, and in regulated industries, that caution is rational. The risk is real. Data quality failures don't just break pipelines in financial services. They break compliance, erode model trust, and end careers. The bar for "production-ready" AI is fundamentally different when your data touches trades, audits, or PII. In this session, Barr Moses, CEO of Monte Carlo, joins Lenny Rosenfeld, VP of Data Science & Software Engineering at Nasdaq for a conversation on what it takes to get trusted AI into production in financial services. They'll cover AI observability, data lineage, and governance frameworks built for regulators, sharing the failures they've seen, the patterns that work, and what they'd do differently.

His experience includes data-intensive computing, AI, applied analytics, and distributed systems.

Monte Carlo works with companies such as Cisco, American Airlines, and NASDAQ to help them drive positive business outcomes with reliable data and AI.

title: Sponsored by: Monte Carlo | Building Trusted AI on Certifiable Data Assets at American Airlines
abstract: At American Airlines, trusted data is the runway for business value. In this session, Andrew Machen, Head of Data Governance at American Airlines, will share how his team stood up a full governance program—establishing operating models, policies, committees, and certifiable data assets—in just over a year to ensure data is ready for scale. By certifying underlying data assets, American ensures every dataset meets rigorous quality and governance standards. He’ll share how data + AI observability plays a key role in monitoring and validating data health, enabling data stewards and engineers to focus less on manual oversight and more on driving impact. He’ll also dive into how his team is extending their program to AI governance to deliver measurable AI outcomes, accelerate trusted AI enablement, and generate early returns.

In the last few years he has focused on creating a community of hundreds that is passionate about making data as fast and easy as possible.

title: Sponsored by: Monte Carlo | Why Your Agents Keep Failing (And What to Do About It)
abstract: Every company is shipping AI agents right now. Most of them are failing, and nobody knows until a customer or an exec calls it out. The problem isn't the models. It's that teams are building agents on top of data they don't monitor, running them without observability, and trusting outputs no one has tested. In this session, I'll walk through the three places agents actually break in production: the data going in, the agent's own behavior, and what comes out the other side. I'll share a framework for catching these failures before your users do. The goal is simple: stop shipping pilots that quietly die, and start shipping agents that hold up when it matters.

title: Sponsored by: Moveworks | The end of standalone analytics: How AI is merging insight and action
abstract: Analytics as a standalone category is changing fast. As AI closes the distance between systems of intelligence and systems of action, the old model — generate insight, hand it off, hope someone acts — is breaking down. In this session, Florian Schouten, GVP of Product Management for Workflow Analytics at ServiceNow, explores what comes next: a world where AI continuously surfaces insights from your Databricks data and triggers automated workflows before a human even opens a dashboard. See how ServiceNow and Pyramid Analytics are making this vision real — combining governed, AI-powered analytics with the workflow automation that turns data into measurable enterprise outcomes. If you're building on Databricks and wondering what the future of analytics looks like, this session is for you.

speakers.name: Florian Schouten

His career spans three decades and a consistent mission: turning complex data into clear, actionable decisions. Florian has built and shipped pioneering analytics products at companies ranging from venture-backed startups to large public enterprises — including ThoughtSpot, Mode Analytics, Digital.ai, and Oracle (via PeopleSoft), where he led a $1B+ analytics applications business. He holds advanced degrees from Tilburg University and Delft University of Technology, and lives in the Boston area.

title: Sponsored by: Nagarro | The Agentic Enterprise: What has to be True Before AI can Act Autonomously
abstract: Most companies have shipped AI by now: copilots, dashboards, recommendation engines. The models work, the data platforms exist, yet AI still usually sits behind a human. It advises, but rarely acts. That gap comes down to orchestration, governance and organization design. This session will explore what must be in place before AI can run autonomously, and what changes when it does. We’ll walk through enterprise scenarios where agentic AI runs on a Databricks backbone, and the architecture and governance choices that made it possible. We’ll also cover where human oversight still belongs and it's importance.Highlights: Why AI programs get stuck between "working demo" and "running in production," and what closes that gap Walkthrough of agentic workflows on Databricks including decision movement between AI and people Nagarro's Fluidic Intelligence framework for treating AI as a continuous system What leadership teams need to start doing as well as stop doing

speakers.job_title: Managing Director & Head of Data and AI
speakers.bio: Anurag leads global data and AI services at Nagarro. As a trusted advisor to senior business leaders, he brings extensive strategy, technology, and client experience to his role and spearheads data and AI-powered transformation initiatives across industries and enterprise functions. He is a recognized thought leader on data and AI, and his insights on scaling enterprise AI, AI governance, and digital transformation frequently feature in industry articles, interviews, technology forums and events. An alumnus of IIT Delhi, he has held leadership roles at IBM, Microsoft, and Nokia and is deeply committed to inclusion and mentoring emerging talent.

title: Sponsored by: Neo4j | Financial Crime Hides Between the Rows
abstract: Fraud rings do not announce themselves in a single row. They hide two or three hops away from the obvious suspects, woven into transactions that look ordinary until you trace the full network. The analyst tools stay the same, what changes is what the data reveals. Your Databricks Lakehouse already holds the data. What it lacks is a way to traverse it as a connected graph. We walk through a live fraud investigation and show how Neo4j turns days of manual analysis into a query Databricks Genie answers in seconds. The hidden patterns do not stay hidden once they become columns. Neo4j scores every account; those scores land in your Lakehouse as ordinary dimensions. Databricks Genie needs no changes; it queries graph scores the same way it queries region or balance. Before enrichment: a flat list. After: accounts at the center of distinct fraud communities. Attendees leave with the notebook and graph data model on GitHub to replicate this on their Delta Lake data.

title: Sponsored by: Neurealm | AI driven Approach to Accelerate Data Modernization by up to 25%
abstract: Enterprises often struggle with slow, manual data migrations & fragmented data quality processes, limiting their ability to adopt AI at scale. This session introduces our approach to data modernization driven by our framework of ‘NeuFlow,’ an autonomous, no-code data engineering approach designed to accelerate data journeys and deliver AI-ready data. Our approach towards AI driven Data modernization unifies intelligent migration (DataBridge), automated data quality (TrustFlow) and reconciliation (MatchFlow) into a single AI-powered framework represented by Neuflow. Attendees will learn how organizations can reduce migration timelines by up to 25%, automate nearly 50% of data quality operations, and achieve 20–30% lower operational costs, while improving trust, governance, and readiness for AI-driven use cases.

speakers.name: Pragadeesh J
speakers.bio: Pragadeesh J is a data leader with 22+ years of experience, including 5+ years specializing in Databricks and Lakehouse architectures. As Director of Data Engineering at Neurealm, he leads large-scale migrations and AI-ready data initiatives for regulated sectors like healthcare. A Databricks Certified Data Engineer Professional, Pragadeesh merges technical mastery with strategic leadership to build scalable, governed platforms. He is the architect of NeuFlow, an autonomous, no-code framework that accelerates migrations and enhances engineering efficiency. His expertise focuses on driving measurable business impact through automated workflows and robust data governance, ensuring organizations are primed for the AI era.

title: Sponsored by: Nimble Gravity | When Agents Should Ask for Help: Designing Human Escalation in Agent Bricks
abstract: Autonomous agents can handle a lot, until they can't. The real challenge isn't building an agent that runs, it's building one that knows when to stop and who to ask. In this session, we walk through a practical pattern for connecting AI agents to the humans they work alongside, without pulling those people out of their day. Using Agent Bricks, Databricks-native AI functions, and MLflow evaluation, we show how an agent can assess its outputs, escalate to colleagues when needed (via Teams or Slack), and continue autonomously once it has what it needs. Through a live demo, you'll see the full cycle: an agent processes and reasons over documents, evaluates its own work, routes targeted questions to people, handles partial and non-responses gracefully, and logs timing and feedback for future improvement. You'll leave with a reusable pattern for building agents that augment human decision-making and a clear picture of how Databricks makes the oversight layer part of the platform.

title: Sponsored by: NTT Data | From Months to Days: Using AI DevKit to Democratize Data Development and Accelerate Insights
abstract: Every data team rebuilds the same infrastructure. The same pipelines, the same mapping, the same delivery bottlenecks — sprint after sprint. What should take weeks takes months, and the gap between business intent and production-ready data only widens. At NTT DATA, we changed the equation. In this session, Anvesh Vallabhaneni shares how NTT DATA extended the Databricks AI Dev Kit — transforming natural language inputs like requirements docs and meeting transcripts into fully governed, production-ready Databricks artifacts across pipelines, Unity Catalog, AI/BI dashboards, and Model Serving. Projects that demanded months of specialized engineering now ship in days. Less code. Less dependency on specialized talent. More governed, AI-ready data at enterprise scale. Leaders will leave with a clear understanding of how AI-assisted development compresses time-to-insight, reduces engineering overhead, and enables data platforms to scale without scaling headcount.

title: Sponsored by: NTT Data | Powering the Grid: Southern Company, NTT DATA & Databricks' Modern Data & AI Foundation
abstract: Southern Company's Power Delivery, with NTT DATA and Databricks, is building a modern data and AI foundation for the utility grid of the future. This session features Shane Powell and Greg Stuhlman, sharing the evolution of their multi-year collaboration from early analytics modernization to a future Lakehouse-driven architecture. NTT DATA leveraged its global Databricks partnership to co-engineer utility-specific solutions. Recent successes include a modernized insights application reducing data load times from 15 minutes to under 3 seconds through redesigned data models, optimized compute, and modern React front end. The session explores AI readiness through improved data quality, governance, and lineage enabling intelligent grid applications, fleet safety, and AI forecasting while maintaining regulatory rigor. Attendees learn architectural decisions and lessons from building scalable data infrastructure for regulated enterprises.

Built on a rigorous AI orchestration framework, AI now generates campaign takeaways, surfaces performance drivers, and automatically guides recommendations across teams. This session explores the interplay between analytics and AI and the requirements for moving a solution into production.

All without diverting engineering from their core roadmap. In this session, they share how a semantic layer made it possible to scale AI-powered analytics across their customer base.

title: Sponsored by: Onehouse | From JVM Gymnastics to Vectorized Performance in One K8s Operator Deploy
abstract: You open the Spark UI and watch your executors doing full JVM gymnastics. Heap/off-heap flips, Tungsten memory juggling, GC pauses, and shuffle spills everywhere. Dynamic allocation struggles, podTemplate nightmares, and memoryOverhead prayers are the daily norm. In this 20-minute lightning talk I’ll show you how to drop a new Kubernetes Operator into your Spark-on-K8s cluster and instantly unlock a high-performance vectorized native execution engine. Zero code changes, no new APIs, no rewrites. You’ll see exactly how the operator reconciles every SparkApplication CR and SparkExecutorPod, replacing the JVM gymnastics with true native performance. Plus real production results: 3x+ faster workloads and 40-60% lower cloud spend. Leave with a clear, actionable playbook to upgrade Spark on Kubernetes in one operator deploy.

speakers.name: Kyle Weller
speakers.bio: Kyle Weller has been building data platforms and data products for over 10 years. He currently runs product development at Onehouse.ai building a Universal Data Lakehouse that automates data platforms to run on Apache Hudi, Delta Lake, and Apache Iceberg. Before Onehouse, Kyle was a Product Lead for Azure Databricks, he built AI products with AzureML, and has architected several planet scale data engineering platforms for Cortana, Bing, Office. Kyle has seen how hard it is for data engineers to build DIY data lakes and he looks forward to the rise of the managed Lakehouse. Outside of work Kyle volunteers as an entrepreneurship coach for incarcerated individuals.

title: Sponsored by: OneTrust | From Innovation to Accountability: Governing Data and AI on Databricks with Guardrails
abstract: As organizations scale data and AI initiatives on Databricks, speed and trust must advance together. This session explores how modern enterprises can operationalize trusted data and AI by combining security principles with actionable governance guardrails. Learn how the Databricks Data and AI Security Framework can be extended with policy‑driven controls that ensure consistent access, use, and governance of data across analytics and AI workflows. Join us for this discussion and live demo to see how guardrails can be applied across the AI lifecycle to help organizations manage risk, respect data‑use requirements, and demonstrate compliance without disrupting innovation. Attendees will gain practical insight into turning governance from static policies into enforceable, auditable controls that scale with Databricks - enabling confident, responsible data and AI initiatives at enterprise scale.

Attendees will gain practical insight into turning governance from static policies into enforceable, auditable controls that scale with Databricks - enabling confident, responsible data and AI initiatives at enterprise scale.

speakers.name: David Wells
speakers.bio: David Wells is a Senior Specialist Solutions Architect at Databricks, where he supports enterprise clients in building scalable, analytics-driven data platforms. With a background in data fusion, organizational design, and cross-functional analytics enablement, he brings deep experience from the financial services sector. Recently, David developed the Databricks AI Governance Framework, a pragmatic, adaptable approach to AI governance designed to help organizations align teams, tools, and data around shared principles, enabling innovation and control in complex data environments.

title: Sponsored by: Perficient | From Second Gear to Full Throttle: Unlocking Trapped Value in Your Databricks Investment
abstract: Most Databricks customers are driving a race car in second gear—access to 12+ products while actively using only 2-3. The problem isn't adoption; it's activation. This session reveals how one organization transformed from standard data engineering to full platform activation, lighting up a dozen products without platform migration. Through two parallel workstreams, we'll explore the journey from foundational data engineering to production-ready agentic AI. The first workstream demonstrates real-time Customer 360 integration, moving from batch reports to sub-100ms queryable APIs. The second showcases autonomous AI agents performing production work humans cannot do at scale—all governed by the same Unity Catalog managing traditional data assets. You'll learn the utilization gap framework, pattern-based approaches that replaced 20-30 weeks of bespoke engineering, and proof that the path from data engineering to agentic AI doesn't require buying more—it requires using what you own.

title: Sponsored by: Pogo | You're Overthinking It: What Analyzing $500B in Consumer Spend Taught Us About Building AI Agents
abstract: At Pogo, we built AI research agents to help brands analyze hundreds of billions in consumer spending data. Our biggest lesson: the breakthrough came after we threw most of it away. We started with a complex system of specialized agents. It was fragile, unreliable, and impossible to iterate on. When we simplified to a single agent, everything changed. In this session, we’ll share why simpler agent architectures dramatically outperform complex ones, what the frontier AI labs’ own designs reveal about where the industry is heading, and how this approach powers Pogo’s AI research agent - where brands analyze purchase data from 3M Americans and interview thousands of verified buyers through an AI video interviewer, with results in hours.

speakers.name: Luke Morgan-Scott
speakers.bio: Luke Morgan-Scott is the Head of Data at Pogo. Pogo’s AI Qual platform allows brands to find and conduct video interviews with thousands of purchase verified shoppers simultaneously within an hour. Pogo’s app helps 3M users monetize their SKU-level purchase data, capturing 1 in 150 shopping trips in America. Pogo has raised over $32m from top VCs, and has been ranked as the #1 loyalty app in America by Newsweek in 2024 and 2025.

title: Sponsored by: Polestar Analytics | Real-Time Working Capital on Databricks: From Siloed P2P, O2C & Inventory to Cash Flow Action
abstract: Over $1.8 trillion remains trapped in working capital globally - not due to a lack of data, but because that data is fragmented, delayed, and disconnected from decision-making. Most finance teams operate across siloed P2P, O2C, and inventory systems - each capturing only part of the picture. The gaps between them are where visibility is lost and cash leakage begins. This session is about closing that gap on the Databricks platform. We'll walk through how - To unify P2P, O2C, and inventory data into a single, governed financial model using Delta Lake and Unity Catalog. - Lakebase enables real-time querying across that unified layer. - Mosaic AI continuously monitors working capital metrics like DSO, DPO, and cash conversion cycle, detects anomalies as they emerge, diagnoses root causes, and surfaces corrective actions. So, instead of reacting to static reports, finance teams get a closed-loop system: detect, diagnose, simulate, and execute - enabling faster, data-driven action at scale

speakers.name: Alex Sidarenka
speakers.bio: Alex Sidarenka is a strategic alliances leader at Polestar Analytics with deep expertise in Cloud, Data, and AI ecosystems. Alex has spent his career building and scaling high-impact partnerships across the technology sector - leading Cloud, Data & AI alliances, and across multiple enterprise software ventures. He holds Databricks certifications in Gen AI & LLM, bringing hands-on platform fluency to his partnership work. At Polestar Analytics, Alex focuses on driving go-to-market success through the Databricks alliance, translating joint capabilities into real enterprise outcomes.

title: Sponsored by: Precisely | Architecting Agentic-Ready Data Pipelines with Databricks and Precisely
abstract: Agentic AI is accelerating how enterprises operate and make decisions, but most data isn't ready to support it. High-value customer data is buried in unstructured sources—support tickets, call center transcripts, and scanned documents—creating a gap where agents act fast but not always correctly. In this session, we show how Precisely and Databricks Agent Bricks transform this untapped data into agentic-ready data: accessible, contextualized, current, reliable, governed, and automated—so AI agents can act, decide, and execute with accuracy and confidence. Using a multi-agent architecture on Databricks, we demonstrate how free-form text becomes structured information. We extract key customer attributes and improve record integrity using Precisely's native capabilities for data integration, quality, governance, location intelligence, and enrichment—embedded directly into Databricks pipelines via native UDFs, unifying structured and unstructured data to ensure accuracy and reliability.

title: Sponsored by: Prophecy | Accelerating Investment Decisions with Specialized Claude Code
abstract: Portfolio managers at Waterfall Asset Management evaluate hundreds of loans daily and track performance on Databricks. But loan “tapes” arrive as spreadsheets in high volume and inconsistent formats across originating institutions, making bids dependent on time-consuming data cleaning and harmonization before standard metrics and dashboards can be computed. Waterfall Asset Management and Prophecy built a specialized Claude Code “harmonization” agent that automates onboarding into a common schema, requiring only a final validation pass where users review AI confidence, reasoning, and lineage and make any edits. In production, WFAM achieved 93% first-pass accuracy—now approaching 100%—reducing reliance on data experts and materially accelerating investment decisions.

speakers.name: Nabi, Shehzad
speakers.bio: Shehzad Nabi is Chief Technology Officer at Waterfall Asset Management, overseeing all aspects of the firm's technology function. Waterfall specializes in structured credit and asset-backed finance — markets defined by complexity and data intensity. The firm is building the technology capabilities to match: scaling how it works with data, accelerating investment workflows, and creating the operational foundation to grow with precision. Prior to Waterfall, Shehzad spent over a decade at Goldman Sachs across London and New York in asset management and equities technology.

title: Sponsored by: Qualytics | Your Agents Are Flying Blind: How to Close the Data Control Gap
abstract: Traditional quality frameworks validate data at rest, after ingestion, after transformation, and at the end of the pipeline. But AI agents don't follow that path. They query on demand, pulling from Unity Catalog tables that may have drifted, and they ingest new data from APIs and external sources that have never passed through a quality gate. This is the control gap: the space between where you enforce quality and where AI actually consumes data. In this session, Gorkem Sevinc (CEO, Qualytics) introduces validate-at-use, the principle that data quality should be enforced when an agent reads or receives data. He walks through a live Databricks lakehouse pipeline where an agent encounters stale-validated data and net-new inputs, then closes the gap in real time using Qualytics as the data control layer. You'll leave with a concrete architecture pattern for making your pipelines agent-ready by embedding validate-at-use controls directly into the agent loop.

speakers.name: Gorkem Sevinc
speakers.bio: Gorkem Sevinc is a technology leader and entrepreneur with extensive experience founding and scaling startups. He is the Co-Founder & CEO of Qualytics, the Data Control Layer for Trusted Context, an AI-augmented platform that scales data quality coverage while keeping humans in control of what "trusted" means. Previously, he co-founded Facet (financial services), Scene Health (digital health), and served as Managing Director of the Johns Hopkins Medicine Technology Innovation Center. Gorkem earned his B.S. in Computer Science from The University of Kansas and his MSE in Computer Science & Surgical Robotics from Johns Hopkins University. He lives in Annapolis, MD with his wife Dotti, dog Tilly, and cat Luna.

title: Sponsored by: Qubika | Building a Modern Data Foundation on Databricks: Lessons learned from FanDuel Migration
abstract: Modern data platform migrations are no longer purely technical initiatives. They are business programs that reshape the foundation for operations, compliance, real-time decision making, and future AI adoption. In this session, Qubika shares how we partnered with FanDuel to migrate core workloads from Amazon Redshift to the Databricks Lakehouse in a highly regulated, real-time environment. We cover the key engineering challenges, including rebuilding governance and metadata in Unity Catalog, validating data consistency at scale, and preserving developer velocity across large, multi-team migrations. We then show how these lessons led us to design an AI-powered Migration and Governance Agent, built natively on Databricks and powered by Lakebase as its persistent memory layer, to manage long-running workflows, reason over dependencies, and automate Databricks-ready governance artifacts. Attendees will leave with a practical blueprint to industrialize migrations and reduce risk.
