speakers.name: Sebastian Diaz
speakers.bio: With over 15 years of experience in the technology space — including more than six years leading data and AI teams — I’m recognized as a thought leader in the field. I regularly speak at top industry events such as the DAMA-Wisconsin Expo, MinneAnalytica in Minneapolis, ConnectIA, Disney’s Data & Analytics Conference (DDAC), and Databricks Meetups. My talks on generative AI, large language models, and data-driven innovation are designed to offer clarity and direction to CTOs and senior technology leaders navigating today’s evolving landscape.

title: Sponsored by: Quest | From Back Office to Boardroom: The New Era of Data Modeling for AI
abstract: For decades, data modeling lived quietly in the back office—essential, but overlooked. AI has changed that. Today, data modeling is no longer a technical afterthought. It is the difference between AI that works and AI that hallucinates, breaks, or fails compliance. Yet most organizations are still applying yesterday’s modeling practices to tomorrow’s AI problems. This session challenges that mindset. We’ll explore how data modeling is evolving into a strategic, AI-era discipline—powering trusted data products, enabling semantic clarity for GenAI, and more! This is not your grandfather’s data modeling. Learn how modern approaches—augmented by automation, metadata intelligence, and AI—are transforming modeling from static documentation into a dynamic, living system that drives business outcomes. See how solutions like Quest erwin Data Modeler help organizations move faster, reduce risk, and turn data models into a competitive advantage in the age of AI.

speakers.name: Yetkin Ozkucur
speakers.bio: As the leader of a global solutions team, and over 25 years’ experience in this space, I have the privilege of working at the intersection of technology, business strategy, and customer success. My focus is on delivering innovative, solution-oriented strategies that empower organizations to unlock the full value of their data. With a deep passion for data, AI and data intelligence, I’m committed to helping enterprises transform how they manage, understand, and leverage information. I thrive in roles where technology meets impact—where the right solutions can drive better decisions, accelerate growth, and build trust.

title: Sponsored by: Quest | From Model to Marketplace: A Better Way to Manage Data End-to-End
abstract: Most organizations don't have a data problem — they have a data management gap. The distance between a well-defined data model and a trusted, self-service data product that business teams can actually use is where value goes to die. This session closes that gap. We'll walk through how Quest's portfolio of solutions drives a complete data management journey — from foundational modeling through integration, quality, cataloging, and all the way to publishing data products into an internal marketplace for self-service access inside Databricks. The result: data that arrives in Databricks already understood, already trusted, and ready to drive real business outcomes — with zero-downtime transitions and no disruption along the way. Whether you're an executive accountable for data value, an engineer building and managing pipelines, or a business user tired of waiting on IT to get you what you need — this session will show you a better way to manage data end to end.

speakers.name: Sue  Laine
speakers.bio: Sue is the Director of Sales Strategists/DI Thought Leader for the Erwin Data Intelligence product line. A thought leader in the application of technology and business process to solve real business problems, Sue has over 20 years’ experience working with metadata on the buy side as a customer and the sell side as a vendor, including implementation, showcasing, and program support. She has worked to structure and drive enterprise data management programs. Additionally, Sue has extensive support experience to sustain these initiatives within an organization. She has supported a wide range of clients, including financial, insurance, healthcare, manufacturing and e-Commerce with a general need to provide data-driven business practices. Sue is responsible for launching and guiding Quests Data Intelligence solution's superior Modeling/Data Intelligence technology in fresh, modern offerings that deliver excellent value for today's challenging business demands.

title: Sponsored by: Quest Software | From Logical Model to Databricks: Automating Data Product Publishing at Scale
abstract: What if data products could be built in days, not months — and deployed directly into Databricks? We'll introduce the Automated Data Product Factory (ADPF): a model-first framework that transforms how organizations define, govern, and publish data products at scale. We’ll walk through a live use case showing how to automate data product publishing into Databricks and Unity Catalog, using logical specifications to accelerate time-to-value across your data landscape—without rebuilding everything from scratch on each platform. You'll walk away understanding: How the ADPF generates logical data product specs How those specs power automated publishing into Databricks & Unity Catalog What a model-first strategy means for governance at scale Best for: CDOs, CDAOs, and data leaders accountable for AI-ready data; Data Engineers and Architects scaling delivery across hybrid environments; and Business Users ready to create data products tied to real outcomes, without going to IT.

title: Sponsored by: Red Hat | Why AI Evaluation Breaks at Scale — and How to Fix It End-to-End
abstract: Most teams evaluate AI in pieces — one script for agent behavior, another for safety, another for adversarial testing — with no shared results, no audit trail, and no defensible answer to "is this ready to deploy?" The accumulation of disconnected tools isn't a gap you can close by adding another tool. It's an organizational and architectural failure, and it compounds as models and agents move into regulated or high-stakes environments. This session confronts the gap directly. We leverage Evaluation-Driven Development (EDD): a structured methodology that connects behavioral, safety, and adversarial evaluation layers into a single, reproducible workflow — with an audit trail your compliance team can actually use and demonstrate it with open-source tooling that integrates with MLflow. Whether you own the model, the platform, or the risk, this session gives you a shared framework for answering the question that matters: Is this AI-enabled capability ready to be deployed?

title: Sponsored by: Reducto | Better Agents with Better Data: How leading AI teams ingest unstructured data at scale
abstract: As AI agents become more capable, their performance is increasingly bottlenecked not by model quality but by the quality of data they consume. This talk explores how leading AI teams across startups and Fortune 10 enterprises tackle the challenge of ingesting unstructured data at scale — from complex PDFs and scanned documents to messy real-world files — and shares practical patterns for building more reliable agents through better data pipelines.

speakers.name: Adit Abraham
speakers.bio: Adit Abraham is the co-founder and CEO of Reducto, a platform that helps leading AI teams transform unstructured documents into structured, reliable data for production pipelines. With industry-leading accuracy, Reducto powers organizations ranging from Fortune 10 companies to Harvey and beyond—processing billions of pages each month and redefining AI ingestion for enterprise-scale intelligence.

title: Sponsored by: Reltio | From fragmented records to trusted context: Unified 360 profiles for Databricks
abstract: Enterprise AI struggles when customer, product, supplier, and other core data remains fragmented across systems. In this lightning talk Mike Frasca, VP of Product Management at Reltio, will show how Reltio Data Sharing with Databricks gives teams live, governed access to trusted 360 profiles via Delta Sharing—bringing unified entities, relationships, interactions, and calculated attributes into Databricks seamlessly. The result is trusted context that improves AI accuracy, accelerates BI, Analytics/ML, and GenAI workloads, and preserves governance, lineage, and auditing through Unity Catalog.

speakers.name: Mike Frasca
speakers.bio: Mike is VP of Product Management at Reltio, responsible for overseeing strategic technical guidance to all customer facing Reltio teams and assisting with long term product strategy and innovation at Reltio. Prior to joining Reltio in 2016, Mike led the technical side of MDM implementation teams at multiple consulting firms and was a principal architect at IBM and Initiate Systems. He has worked on some of the most complex MDM implementations and led data architecture designs used by Fortune 100 companies today.

title: Sponsored by: Retool | Responding to Signals That Drive High-Stakes Decisions with Databricks Genie
abstract: Conversational AI is making it much easier to understand what’s changing in your data. The harder problem now is acting on the signals that shape high-stakes business decisions early enough to reduce missed targets, operational disruption, or compliance exposure. In this lightning session, we’ll demo how those signals can move from explanation to action inside Databricks Genie Spaces using Retool agents directly on governed enterprise data. A question in Genie becomes a shared response that brings in the right stakeholders and triggers follow-up steps across systems directly from the same workspace. You’ll see how acting on signals earlier can help your teams adjust plans sooner, resolve issues faster, and influence the decisions that matter most.

Today he helps organizations design and deliver AI powered applications and agents.

title: Sponsored by: Retool | The Last Mile of Enterprise AI Adoption: How Reckitt Operationalizes AI on the Lakehouse
abstract: Reckitt operates a global portfolio of health and hygiene brands across more than 60 countries, including brands like Lysol and Durex, where speed, agility, and efficiency are critical to staying competitive. After building its Gen AI platform on Databricks, the company set out to deliver faster, more actionable consumer insights to marketing teams as part of a broader business transformation, ultimately reducing insight delivery time by up to 60 percent. In this session, Retool shares how Reckitt unified marketing data across sources and embedded Gen AI directly into governed marketing workflows on the Databricks Lakehouse, replacing manual reporting with execution paths that scale. Attendees will gain practical insights into the business and architectural decisions, integration patterns, and governance approaches used to embed Gen AI into governed workflows at enterprise scale.

speakers.name: Tom Konewka
speakers.bio: Tom Konewka is a Strategic Solutions Architect at Retool, a platform to rapidly design and deploy software. His diverse background in infrastructure and applications with roles at Cisco and AWS over 10 years have allowed him to guide Enterprises through digital transformations at scale. Today he helps organizations design and deliver AI powered applications and agents.

speakers.name: Steve Miller
speakers.bio: Steve is a business development executive with a focus in driving strategic alliances and partnerships. Steve's focus transcends on-paper relationships by working closely with partners to develop mutually beneficial relationships and integrations that lead to ecstatic customers and to growth for all platforms. Steve also has experience in territory management and consulting around emerging technology solutions, especially cloud-based infrastructure and application solutions. Through these efforts, Steve developed unique expertise in helping customers strengthen their technology portfolio.

title: Sponsored by: RowZero | AI spreadsheets and the modern data stack: security, governance, and self-serve analytics
abstract: Your enterprise data is in the cloud, but most business teams still work in Excel, disconnected from Databricks and other cloud systems. Teams export CSVs of data out of cloud tools and immediately lose the scale, security, and live connectivity of the cloud. In the transition to the cloud, most organizations have overlooked the most important tool—the spreadsheet. This session explores the role spreadsheets currently play and why making the spreadsheet a first-class citizen in your cloud data stack is a big opportunity. If your organization has modernized its data stack but still relies on legacy spreadsheet workflows, this talk will show how to bridge the gap and give business users secure access to Databricks data in a governed, cloud spreadsheet that is AI-enabled and scales with your data size.

title: Sponsored by: S&P Global | Leveraging Genie to Remove Friction From Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)
abstract: S&P Capital IQ Workbench, powered by Databricks, allows users to easily test and evaluate S&P Global data. During this session we'll discuss how Databricks Genie further removes friction from exploratory data analysis (EDA) for both business and technical evaluators. Business users can ask natural-language questions and receive curated answers, tables, and visualizations, without writing code. Technical users can validate findings, extend analyses, and operationalize workflows in the same secure notebook environment. Attendees will learn how natural-language querying accelerates time to insight, increases engagement (measured by logins/queries run), and improves customer workflow understanding (feedback from what questions clients ask). We’ll close with success metrics and examples of how this approach shortens sales cycles and improves conversion beyond today’s baseline.

speakers.name: Onik Kurktchian
speakers.bio: Onik is the Head of the S&P Global Marketplace Workbench. He leads product development for Analytical Platforms and Services at S&P Global Market Intelligence, a division of S&P Global. In his role, he is responsible for creating products and services that enable data exploration and workflow solutions across client segments. Previously, he has worked as a Product Specialist across multiple S&P Global products within Desktop & Feed Solutions.

title: Sponsored by: Safe Software | All Data, Any AI: Building MCP-Ready Ecosystems with FME
abstract: Most AI initiatives can reason, but few can act. Your Databricks lakehouse has the data, but not the enterprise connections to put AI to work. Without them, AI remains a passive chatbot, not an autonomous agent. Bridging this gap has meant writing brittle, hard-to-scale glue code. MCP and FME solve this with a blueprint for a scalable AI Tool Factory. Safe Software CEO Don Murray shares how FME uses MCP to transform complex workflows into plug-and-play tools for any AI. You’ll learn how to: •Eliminate Technical Debt: Deploy pipelines into governed MCP tools without writing code. •Bridge the Data Gap: Connect AI agents to spatial data, legacy systems, and real-time feeds the Lakehouse can’t easily reach. •Deploy at Scale: Quickly build, test, and ship AI tools. •Actionize the Lakehouse: Turn static insights into autonomous enterprise actions. Learn the All Data, Any-AI, MCP-enabled strategy to turn your Databricks platform into an actionable AI powerhouse.
description_raw:
Most AI initiatives can reason, but few can act. Your Databricks lakehouse has the data, but not the enterprise connections to put AI to work. Without them, AI remains a passive chatbot, not an autonomous agent. Bridging this gap has meant writing brittle, hard-to-scale glue code. MCP and FME solve this with a blueprint for a scalable AI Tool Factory. Safe Software CEO Don Murray shares how FME uses MCP to transform complex workflows into plug-and-play tools for any AI. You’ll learn how to:

•Eliminate Technical Debt: Deploy pipelines into governed MCP tools without writing code.

•Bridge the Data Gap: Connect AI agents to spatial data, legacy systems, and real-time feeds the Lakehouse can’t easily reach.

•Deploy at Scale: Quickly build, test, and ship AI tools.

•Actionize the Lakehouse: Turn static insights into autonomous enterprise actions.

Learn the All Data, Any-AI, MCP-enabled strategy to turn your Databricks platform into an actionable AI powerhouse.

speakers.name: Don Murray
speakers.bio: Don Murray is a Canadian entrepreneur, co-founder and CEO of Safe Software, a company at the forefront of data integration. His entrepreneurial journey began in 1993 when Safe Software was launched, driven by a vision to enhance data integration technology. Don Murray's visionary leadership and dedication to innovation have been instrumental in driving Safe Software's growth and success. His commitment to developing solutions that bring life to data continues to propel the company forward as a leader in data integration solutions.

title: Sponsored by: Salesforce | How Salesforce Gets More Done Daily with Slack and Slackbot
abstract: Discover how Salesforce uses Slack and Slackbot to drive efficiency at scale. This session explores how teams across IT, sales, and beyond leverage automation and workflows to reduce ticket volume, accelerate resolution times, and streamline operations. Learn how Salesforce deploys Slack agents for routine requests, builds custom solutions for everyday work, and develops processes that deliver measurable productivity gains while strengthening internal alignment.

Learn how Salesforce deploys Slack agents for routine requests, builds custom solutions for everyday work, and develops processes that deliver measurable productivity gains while strengthening internal alignment.

title: Sponsored by: Salesforce | Tableau & Databricks: Demystifying the Open Semantic Interchange (OSI)
abstract: Explore how Tableau and the Open Semantic Interchange (OSI) provide the essential metadata layer that ensures your metrics remain consistent and trusted for both humans and agents. You will learn how our current analytics integration and future roadmap for semantic interoperability allow agents to work seamlessly inside the systems that run your business.

title: Sponsored by: Salesforce | Turn Databricks Data into Action with Trusted Context from Salesforce Data 360
abstract: Your data platform is powerful, but data alone doesn’t drive outcomes. To operationalize AI and deliver real-time customer experiences, teams need trusted context: governed data, business logic, and unified customer intelligence. In this session, discover how Salesforce Data 360 connects directly to your Databricks lakehouse to activate data without moving or duplicating it. Using Zero Copy access, you can unify customer profiles, power real-time personalization, and ground Agentforce agents in trusted enterprise data so they act accurately at scale. We’ll explore three practical use cases: building unified profiles from lakehouse data, enabling AI agents with governed context, and activating data across sales, service, and marketing channels. Zero Copy is the foundation. Trusted context is what drives action. Leave with a clear blueprint to maximize the value of your Databricks investment.

title: Sponsored by: SAP | SAP and Databricks turn SAP Data into AI-Driven Business Outcomes
abstract: Enterprise AI often stalls due to fragmented data foundations. This session shows how SAP Business Data Cloud and Databricks delivers a unified, governed data fabric for AI that reduces data movement, data delays, and silos. Learn how to combine SAP’s business context with Databricks lakehouse data to enable analytics and AI at scale. Through real-world use cases like cash flow forecasting, tariff impact analysis, and supply chain risk modeling, see how you can accelerate AI and drive measurable outcomes.

speakers.name: Rams Srinivasan
speakers.bio: Ramaswamy Srinivasan (Rams) is a senior technology and data transformation leader with over 25 years of experience across Information Technology, Telecommunications, Consulting, and Executive Management. He currently serves as Global Head of Strategic Solution Advisory at SAP, where he leads a global team focused on Data and AI, helping organizations unlock value from enterprise data at scale. In his role, Rams works closely with customers and partners on complex Data, AI and analytics initiatives—positioning modern data platforms to enable advanced analytics, real-time insights, and AI-driven use cases. His team plays a key role in translating customer challenges and field learnings into product innovation and roadmap evolution.

title: Sponsored by: Sigma | How Sigma Makes Databricks AI Safe and Scalable for Every Business User
abstract: Databricks is rapidly advancing its AI capabilities—from Genie Spaces to LLM-powered analytics. But for most organizations, making that innovation accessible to everyday business users in a way that’s intuitive, governed, and scalable remains the real challenge. Sigma activates Databricks AI to build intelligent, user-facing workflows that accelerate productivity. Learn how teams use natural language querying, assistants, and agents to analyze data, automate decisions, and take action directly on live Databricks data. Through real-world examples, discover how organizations are operationalizing AI and turning investment into measurable business outcomes.

speakers.name: Zalak Trivedi
speakers.bio: RESPONSIBILITIES: Zalak Trivedi leads product direction for AI and Enterprise Platform at Sigma, driving innovation to ensure customers can deliver high-performance agentic analytics for their organizations and within their own applications. He champions Sigma's product for companies to offer data insights in their native ecosystem and promotes these benefits through conferences, whitepapers, blogs, and media appearances. SIGMA CAREER: Zalak joined Sigma in April 2022 and leads the AI & Enterprise Platform charters ROOTS: Zalak brings experience across the tech industry, having held roles at OvationCXM, Oracle, and Acquire. He holds an MBA from UC Davis and a Bachelor's in Engineering from BITS Pilani.

title: Sponsored by: Sigma | How Unilever Optimizes Supply Chain Inventory with Sigma and Databricks
abstract: Unilever’s supply chain team spends hours each day identifying inventory gaps across retailers and distribution centers. By combining disparate datasets, Unilever built a supply chain analytics application using Sigma on Databricks. The solution enables data-driven supply chain decisions with on demand self-service analytics at speed. This centralized “command center” gives teams a daily view of opportunities, reduces manual analysis by up to half a day, and helps balance inventory across locations.

speakers.name: Phillip Nelson
speakers.bio: Phil Nelson is a Commercial & Customer Analytics Manager at Unilever, where he helps transform retail and supply chain data into actionable business insights. With over 15 years of experience in the CPG industry, Phil uses data and BI tools to create solutions for retailer and business teams, helping them meet their goals. He is passionate about enabling data-led, informed, and impactful decision-making across the business.

title: Sponsored by: Sigma | Reinventing Healthcare Finance: How HSS Built XRAY on Databricks with Sigma
abstract: When a new CFO set out to deliver more reliable, enterprise-wide data, Hospital for Special Surgery (HSS) modernized its approach—consolidating fractured financial and operational systems on Databricks and cutting report turnaround from 30 days to 24 hours. HSS implemented the Databricks lakehouse to bridge several disparate systems to finally connect the dots to tell a full financial story from operational driver to bottom-line result. This was achieved by redesigning the operating model: a cross functional data product team was formed with one singular vision to transform the organization's approach to financial reporting. This structure connects financial expertise, frontline context, data engineering, and analytics. The result is XRAY—a governed, real-time financial visibility solution powered by Sigma that enables cross-functional data conversations and empowers the entire organization to actively find insights at scale.

speakers.name: Richard Lee
speakers.bio: Working in the intersection of Product + Data; passionate about healthcare, learning, golf, and pintxos.

title: Sponsored by: Slalom | From Legacy Drag to AI Value: A Framework for Databricks AI Success
abstract: Enterprise AI success is not just about models or platform selection. It depends on prioritizing the right work for AI, proving measurable business value, enabling the organization to adopt new ways of working, and reducing the drag of legacy systems. In this session, Slalom shares a practical framework for scaling AI on Databricks using accelerators mapped to each part of that equation. We will show how VelocitiIQ helps modernize legacy data platforms, how Lakefoundry turns ideas into executable Databricks solution plans, how the AI Value Platform builds confidence through ROI modeling and prioritization, and why organizational enablement is essential for moving from pilots to production. Attendees will leave with a simple, actionable model for connecting modernization, value realization, and AI adoption at scale.

title: Sponsored by: Snorkel AI | The Art & Science of Benchmarking Agents
abstract: Our ability to measure AI has been outpaced by our ability to develop it, and this eval gap is one of the most important problems in AI. We need more enduring benchmarks to close this gap, and consequently advance entire new vectors of capabilities for the field. In this talk, I'll share our learnings evaluating agents, drawing from experience working with nearly all global frontier labs and leading academics. We'll discuss the science (i.e., mechanics that make benchmarks rigorous and effective) and art (i.e., intangibles driving ambitious and enduring benchmarks) of building great benchmarks. I'll close by sharing some of the learnings from Open Benchmarks Grants— a $3M initiative in partnership with Hugging Face, Together AI, Prime Intellect, Factory, and others.

speakers.name: Saurabh Singh
speakers.bio: Saurabh Singh is a seasoned Product Manager with nearly a decade of experience building and scaling user-facing B2B products for global markets. He has a strong track record of taking products from zero to one and expanding them into new markets, with specialized expertise in customer journeys, international expansion, marketplaces, and fulfillment. Saurabh has spent six of his seven years in product management working across early-stage startups, giving him a builder's instinct for navigating ambiguity and driving outcomes. He is particularly focused on leveraging AI/ML to create impactful product experiences, working closely with technical teams and business stakeholders to connect vision with execution. Saurabh holds an MBA from the McCombs School of Business at UT Austin (Class of 2022) and a degree in Mechanical Engineering from IIT Kharagpur.

title: Sponsored by: Snowplow | The Missing Layer Between Your Lakehouse and Genie: Customer Context with Snowplow
abstract: Genie lets anyone ask data questions in plain English. But ask about customer intent, real-time behavior, or why conversions dropped, and most teams don’t have the data to work with. In this session, we'll show how Snowplow delivers a validated customer context layer directly into your Lakehouse, ie: every digital interaction, human and AI agent alike, governed through Unity Catalog. Pre-built semantic models plug into Genie's Knowledge Store so business users self-serve behavioral insights without the data team. No custom engineering. No modeling from scratch. We'll also unpack the flywheel: every interaction generates richer data, which sharpens Genie answers and agent decisions. Walk away with a practical architecture for turning Genie into the customer intelligence layer your teams actually trust.

speakers.name: Jonathan Malloy
speakers.bio: Jon Malloy is a Data Strategist at Snowplow where he is responsible for helping customers get the most value from their pipeline and derive meaningful insights. Prior to join Snowplow, Jon spent 4 years as a Technical Analyst in the US health care communications industry and 4 years as a Data Scientist in the US health care communications / finance industry. He holds as Master of Science in Business Analytics from Bentley University and resides in Boston, MA.

title: Sponsored by: Stardog | Implementing Agentic AI on Databricks with an Ontology-Driven Semantic Layer
abstract: Building reliable AI agents on Databricks requires more than pipelines and models—it demands a semantic foundation that encodes business meaning and context. In this session, we’ll show how to implement an ontology-powered semantic layer that integrates with Delta Lake and Unity Catalog to ground LLM-based agents. You’ll learn how to bootstrap ontologies, enable real-time semantic queries, and go beyond traditional RAG approaches. We’ll walk through patterns for evaluating agent performance against natural language queries across use cases like supply chain, drug discovery, and compliance. Leave with a practical architecture for deploying explainable, production-ready agentic AI on Databricks.

title: Sponsored by: Tealium | Unifying Data and Action: WGU, Databricks & Tealium Driving Real-Time Insights at Scale
abstract: Organizations today face a critical challenge: not just collecting data, but activating it in real time to drive meaningful outcomes. Using WGU as a practical example, we’ll examine how the WGU, Tealium and Databricks integration supports personalized student experiences, improves engagement, and enhances operational efficiency. This session explores how Tealium, Western Governors University (WGU), and Databricks can work together to create a modern, scalable data ecosystem. Tealium enables real-time data collection and audience orchestration, while Databricks provides a unified platform for data, AI, and analytics. Attendees will gain insight into architectural patterns, integration strategies, and real-world use cases that demonstrate how streaming data and AI-driven insights can be operationalized across the enterprise to deliver measurable impact

title: Sponsored by: Tech Mahindra Limited | From Data to Decisions - How We Gave Our AI a Business Brain
abstract: A practitioner session showing how grounding agentic AI in an ontology layer changed what was possible. Not a theory talk, a walk through of what broke first, why RAG wasn't enough, how existing Power BI logic became the foundation, and what the agents can actually do now. Real numbers, real workflows, real lessons. A session with a joint customer for architects and business leaders who are past the hype and need to know what good looks like.

speakers.name: Saurabh Jha
speakers.bio: Saurabh Jha is a seasoned business professional and digital & analytics evangelist with over two decades of experience. A passionate technocrat, he has built and scaled businesses from concept to high‑growth portfolios by leveraging innovative and emerging technologies. As Senior Vice President and Global Head of the Data & Analytics (D&A) Practice at Tech Mahindra, Saurabh is driving enterprise transformation across data, cloud and AI. His solutions and frameworks have earned recognition from leading analysts and customers worldwide. Previously, he held leadership roles at KPMG, Oracle and Mphasis, leading several large‑scale digital transformation initiatives.

title: Sponsored by: Tenarai | AI-Accelerated Real Time Supply Chain Operations on Databricks
abstract: Enterprises are reimagining supply chains by consolidating fragmented data into a unified Lakehouse, enabling real-time decisions, AI-driven forecasting, and end-to-end visibility from factory to last mile. In this session, we will showcase how we innovated with AI to accelerate migration, modernization, and transformation of supply chain operations for global customers. This solution addressed critical business challenges like demand volatility, misaligned inventory, shipment risks, and decision latency due to fragmented systems and batch-era architectures that significantly impact cost and performance of operations. Our real time and event-based solution is powered by Structured Streaming, Delta Lake, Unity Catalog, Agent Bricks and AI/BI Genie architecture to drive ~20+% faster operations, ~$15M+ cost savings, and ~60% reduction in manual effort leading to resilient supply chain operations at scale.

speakers.name: Sriraj Srinivasan
speakers.job_title: Senior Vice President | Data and AI Head

title: Sponsored by: Tiger Analytics | From Data Potential to Business Value: Scaling Commercial & Consumer Products at PepsiCo
abstract: "Data platforms create potential; Data products create value.” At PepsiCo, the Enterprise Data Foundation (EDF) anchors enterprise-wide data access, but real business impact comes from what gets built on top of it. In this session, Petr Gazaryan (PepsiCo) and Parag Shah (Tiger Analytics) share the strategic approach behind designing, scaling and operating a global portfolio of commercial and consumer data products. These span revenue management, store effectiveness, media mix modeling, and personalization, deployed across 25+ markets globally. The talk centers on critical decisions that matter at scale: architecting reusable data products across markets, managing downstream complexity on a shared platform, and designing for operational sustainability rather than one-off delivery. Attendees will leave with a strategic framework for moving beyond platform investments to a mature, scalable data product operating model, with lessons from one of the world’s largest CPG organizations

title: Sponsored by: Tredence | PepsiCo’s Enterprise Data Foundation Journey for Agentic AI Readiness
abstract: At PepsiCo, operating across 190+ countries, 6 sectors, and 1,500+ applications, data has long been fragmented across siloed, sector-specific lakes. As PepsiCo is preparing for the era of Agentic AI, the priority has shifted from simply managing data to making it truly enterprise-ready, through a global, unified data platform. In this session, discover how PepsiCo, in partnership with Tredence, is building a an Enterprise Data Foundation platform using Unity Catalog and a unified data lake to establish a single, trusted source of truth across all sectors. From breaking silos to standardizing governance at scale, Amlan will share the real challenges encountered along the way and how PepsiCo is addressing them to become truly ready for GenAI and Agentic AI. The session will also offer a glimpse into the use cases PepsiCo is unlocking in parallel to building this data foundation, to drive faster, smarter, and more connected decision-making across the enterprise.
description_raw: At PepsiCo, operating across 190+ countries, 6 sectors, and 1,500+ applications, data has long been fragmented across siloed, sector-specific lakes. As PepsiCo is preparing for the era of Agentic AI, the priority has shifted from simply managing data to making it truly enterprise-ready, through a global, unified data platform. In this session, discover how PepsiCo, in partnership with Tredence, is building a an Enterprise Data Foundation platform using Unity Catalog and a unified data lake to establish a single, trusted source of truth across all sectors. From breaking silos to standardizing governance at scale, Amlan will share the real challenges encountered along the way and how PepsiCo is addressing them to become truly ready for GenAI and Agentic AI. The session will also offer a glimpse into the use cases PepsiCo is unlocking in parallel to building this data foundation, to drive faster, smarter, and more connected decision-making across the enterprise.

speakers.name: Majaz Mohammed
speakers.bio: 14+ years of experience in supply chain process improvement and technology implementations. Specialties include IBP, Inventory Optimization, Sourcing and Order Fulfillment. Certified in Production and Inventory Management.

title: Sponsored by: Weil | Goldmines and Minefields: Building AI Systems for High-Stakes Work
abstract: Most organizations treat AI as a tooling upgrade. Weil treated it as a systems architecture problem — and rebuilt how legal work gets done. This session is for builders — executives, engineers, and product leaders — deploying AI where data is messy, stakes are high, and trust is non-negotiable. We’ll cover: • Structuring complex data (contracts, policies) for LLMs • A modular, multi-step “reasoning” architecture • Designing for privacy, security, and auditability • Rebuilding workflows into AI-native pipelines Professional services is both a goldmine (high-value, repeatable work) and a minefield (sensitive data, real liability). We’ll share what we’ve built — and what it means for deploying AI in real-world, high-stakes environments.

title: Sponsored by: West Monroe | Operational Intelligence at the Point of Decision with Clonal and Databricks
abstract: Organizations are investing in Databricks for analytics and AI, but struggle to turn insights into real-time action across CRM, service, and frontline workflows. This session introduces Clonal, an operational intelligence platform that activates Databricks data at the point of decision. Clonal creates a live operational model by unifying data from core systems and Databricks, processes events in milliseconds, and orchestrates actions across systems. While Databricks powers data engineering, analytics, and AI, Clonal translates those outputs into context-aware applications embedded directly into workflows. Together, they form a complementary architecture: Databricks as the system of intelligence, Clonal as the system of action. Attendees will learn how to close the gap between having great data and using it in every interaction—enabling real-time, consistent, and scalable execution powered by AI.

speakers.name: Jeremy Bruck
speakers.bio: Jeremy Bruck is a Partner at West Monroe, a global business and technology consulting firm, where he helps private equity firms and their portfolio companies unlock measurable value from data, analytics, and AI. He focuses on bridging the gap between emerging technologies and real business impact—turning data and AI investments into revenue growth, margin expansion, and operational efficiency. With deep expertise in data strategy and digital transformation, Jeremy advises executive teams, boards, CIOs, and CFOs on how to prioritize and scale initiatives that deliver tangible results. His work centers on moving organizations beyond experimentation to build durable capabilities grounded in strong data foundations, modern architectures, and clear operating models.

title: Sponsored by: Wisdom AI | Why Data Analysts are the Future of AI: The Rise of AI Context Engineers
abstract: AI analytics projects struggle to scale beyond demos because AI understands code but lacks critical business context—details like defining churn or explaining anomalies. Without this knowledge, AI produces wrong answers. The essential context is held by data analysts, who are currently underutilized. In this session, Kapil Chhabra introduces the AI Context Engineer, a vital new role merging analytics, data engineering, governance, and business strategy. Learn why this role is critical for building trusted AI on modern lakehouse platforms like Databricks. You will learn: why AI analytics projects fail; the four layers of context AI requires; how analysts become AI Context Engineers; and how governance improves trust in AI outputs. Success belongs to the companies that operationalize context.

speakers.name: Kapil Chhabra
speakers.bio: Kapil Chhabra is the Co-Founder and Chief Product Officer of WisdomAI, where he leads product vision and innovation for the company’s enterprise analytics platform. He brings more than 20 years of experience building category-defining products across data, analytics, cloud, and AI. Prior to WisdomAI, Kapil held leadership roles at Rubrik, where he led product organizations during its growth to $400M+ ARR. As Senior Director of Product Management, he oversaw initiatives across enterprise data protection and SaaS. He also led new database products scaling to $50M ARR in under three years. Before Rubrik, Kapil was a Senior Product Manager at AWS, launching major services like Amazon Kinesis Analytics. Earlier, he spent a decade at Akamai Technologies building award-winning analytics and media products. Kapil is also an active angel investor and advisor in the AI and cloud infrastructure space.

title: Sponsored by: You.com | Achieving State of the Art on Deep Search: Lessons for Agent Builders
abstract: AI agents need to go beyond simple lookups. They need to search deeply, synthesize across hundreds of sources, and verify facts across multi-step reasoning chains. But building a deep search system that's accurate, fast, and reliable at scale is a fundamentally different problem than traditional software systems. Saahil Jain, CTO of You.com, shares how the team built the Research API — ranked #1 on DeepSearchQA with 83.67% accuracy and 93.16% F1 — and what they learned along the way. The talk covers: why a robust but simple harness paired with high-quality search tools outperforms complex agent orchestration; the architectural choices behind pushing the accuracy-latency frontier across benchmarks from SimpleQA to DeepSearchQA; and why single-run eval numbers are misleading for agents, with practical techniques for measuring what actually matters. Built for builders on platforms like Databricks who want to add deep search to their agentic workflows.

speakers.name: Saahil Jain
speakers.bio: Saahil Jain is the CTO of You.com. He started at You.com in December 2020 as a Founding Engineer and most recently served as the Head of AI. Before You.com, Saahil was a graduate researcher in the Stanford Machine Learning Group under Professor Andrew Ng, where he researched topics related to deep learning and natural language processing (NLP) in resource-constrained domains like healthcare. His research work has been published in machine learning conferences such as EMNLP, NeurIPS Datasets & Benchmarks, and ACM-CHIL among others.

title: SQL Is Still Your Superpower: Building Production Ready Data Pipelines Using Only SQL
abstract: SQL didn’t get weaker, data platforms got better. In this session, we demonstrate how modern data engineering on Databricks can be delivered end-to-end using SQL alone on the Lakehouse, without dependency on Python or external frameworks, including SQL AI functions and Geospatial Functions. Designed for organisations with strong BI/SQL foundations, this session shows how to transition to production-grade data engineering with minimal upskilling. We showcase how pipelines are built using Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines via the Pipeline Editor, with ingestion, transformation, and data quality rules all defined declaratively in SQL. This demonstrates how SQL moves beyond querying into full pipeline engineering. Governance is implemented by design using Unity Catalog, providing fine-grained permissions, lineage, and auditability across the entire pipeline. We also showcase AI/BI Genie, configured using SQL-only approaches (metrics, dimensions, instructions, and evaluation queries), enabling natural language access to trusted data. This session reframes SQL as a first-class engineering language on the Lakehouse, capable of handling streaming, transformation, data quality, governance, and AI consumption, while unlocking existing talent and accelerating adoption without increasing complexity.

We showcase how pipelines are built using Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines via the Pipeline Editor, with ingestion, transformation, and data quality rules all defined declaratively in SQL. This demonstrates how SQL moves beyond querying into full pipeline engineering.

Governance is implemented by design using Unity Catalog, providing fine-grained permissions, lineage, and auditability across the entire pipeline. title: SQL Programming and Procedural Logic
abstract: In this hands-on course, you will explore core SQL programming and procedural logic skills within Databricks, focusing on how to build modular, maintainable, and production-ready analytics workflows. You’ll start by learning about key SQL constructs—such as Common Table Expressions, temporary views, and User Defined Functions—that make your SQL development cleaner and more reusable. The course then guides you through advanced SQL scripting techniques using compound statements, variables, control flow, conditionals, loops, and robust error handling to create structured and flexible SQL processes. Finally, you’ll discover how to orchestrate and modernize complex workflows with Lakeflow Jobs and migration strategies, transforming legacy tasks into modular, automated pipelines that leverage the full capabilities of the Databricks platform. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, including workspaces, SQL warehouses, and notebooks Experience with Databricks Lakehouse architecture, including Delta Lake and Unity Catalog Intermediate SQL experience, including stored procedures, dynamic queries, and data analysis in relational databases Familiarity with data pipeline orchestration and workflow management Working knowledge of data warehousing concepts, including ETL, schema design, data modeling, and BI reporting

  • Familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, including workspaces, SQL warehouses, and notebooks

  • Familiarity with data pipeline orchestration and workflow management
  • Working knowledge of data warehousing concepts, including ETL, schema design, data modeling, and BI reporting

title: SQM: From Fragmented Data Silos to Real-Time ML Operations with Delta Sharing
abstract: SQM set out to increase production efficiency by improving how raw materials are converted into finished product. But, it faced a classic data engineering challenge: critical operational data was trapped in silos (plant historians, lab systems, simulation tools) with no scalable way to unify it with enterprise data in Databricks. Operating decisions relied on operator experience rather than predictive models, leading to excess raw material use and inconsistent output. Instead of building custom ETL pipelines to move data across systems, SQM leveraged Delta Sharing to create a governed, real-time data backbone. AVEVA’s cloud-based industrial intelligence platform, CONNECT, seamlessly shared data with Databricks on Azure using zero-copy and no data movement. This enabled operational data to be unified and refined into consistent minute-level views, and used to develop machine learning and optimization models.

This event is a must-see for VCs, founders and those interested in the early-stage company ecosystem.

speakers.name: Josh Lillie
speakers.bio: Josh helps lead the Startups GTM business at Databricks where he works with early stage companies to adopt Databricks. Separately, Josh is an investor and GTM advisor in numerous early stage startups.

speakers.name: Phil Mizrahi
speakers.bio: Phil Mizrahi is CEO and co-founder of Linkup, search infrastructure for AI agents and autonomous systems. Linkup indexes the web at the level of atoms of information, the granularity AI agents actually need. Backed by Gradient, Linkup powers real-time web search and deep research for teams building production AI. Previously, Phil built Amundsen, the open-source search engine at Lyft.

speakers.name: Luyuan Fang
speakers.bio: Dr. Luyuan Fang is CEO and Founder of Curative AI, building AI-native solutions to transform healthcare revenue cycle management and clinical operations. The company reached cash-flow positivity in its first year without external funding. Previously, Luyuan was Chief AI Officer at Change Healthcare (acquired by Optum for $13.5B), leading AI at scale across the largest healthcare administrative network in the US, processing ~50% of US claims. She was also Chief AI and Data Officer at Prescryptive Health, landing AI in a network of 50,000+ pharmacies; and Founder of Expedia AI Labs, supporting all 14 Expedia Brands. Earlier, she held leadership roles at Microsoft Azure, Cisco, eBay, and AT&T. She holds a PH.D. in AI and 10 US patents.

title: Stateful Apps for Thousands of Users: Horizontal Scaling, Session Affinity, and Sharding in Databricks Apps
abstract: Databricks Apps make it easy to stand up a powerful internal app, but most teams don’t have a clear mental model for what happens when usage jumps from a handful of users to hundreds or thousands. In this advanced, under-the-hood session, we’ll walk through how Databricks Apps can remain stateful at scale: horizontal scaling across multiple app instances, session affinity to keep user context and in-memory state attached to the right replica, and sharding to distribute traffic and load safely across your fleet. By the end, you’ll have a practical architecture playbook and checklist you can apply to take an existing Databricks App—from LLM agents to analytics UIs—and harden it for real production scale without giving up the fast developer experience you already enjoy.

By the end, you’ll have a practical architecture playbook and checklist you can apply to take an existing Databricks App—from LLM agents to analytics UIs—and harden it for real production scale without giving up the fast developer experience you already enjoy.

title: Stop Arguing About Numbers: How Zalando Scales Governance With Unity Catalog and Metric Views
abstract: At Zalando’s scale—serving over 50M European customers via a decentralized microservices architecture—data governance quickly turns into a "Data Swamp." Domain teams shipped fast, but without a unified semantic layer, "Net Revenue" on a marketing dashboard rarely matched the finance report. We faced a familiar dilemma: centralize and create bottlenecks, or stay decentralized and lose trust in our numbers.This session will focus on our implementation of Unity Catalog, scaled across an organization with over 1,000 data engineers. This setup fosters team autonomy, enabling rapid data creation. We will also detail the governance mechanisms we've put in place to facilitate data sharing while maintaining minimal compliance risk.We’ll then go deep into how we use metric views to move business logic out of siloed BI tools and directly into the lakehouse. This metric-as-code approach ensures that whether users query via notebooks, dashboards or AI agents, they get the same trusted answer.

speakers.name: Mukrram Ur Rahman
speakers.bio: Mukrram is a Principal Software Engineer at Zalando Data Foundation and is passionate about building scalable, governed, and business-friendly data platforms. He works at the intersection of data engineering, governance, and AI-driven analytics, helping organizations make trusted data more accessible while maintaining strong control and reliability. His experience spans platform architecture, data standardization, governance frameworks, and AI-driven innovation, with a consistent focus on turning technical complexity into business value. In his free time, he enjoys solving problems and actively explores AI, both as a technology and as a tool for creating practical, high-impact solutions.

speakers.name: Fabian Halkivaha
speakers.bio: Long Time veteran in the Data + AI space at Zalando who built the first "Big Data" pipelines back in 2013 when Hadoop was still a buzz word. Nowadays, mostly focusing on Analytical tooling and semantic governance topics.

title: Stream into the Future: Mastering Stream processing for All Workloads
abstract: The data world isn't slowing down—it’s streaming faster than ever. And if you're still thinking batch-first, it's time to flip the script.Join us for an overview of new features in Apache Spark™ Structured Streaming. Learn how it is enabling AI workloads for Cursor, machine learning workflows in Coinbase, and complex real-time requirements of MakeMyTrip.If you are an advanced user, this session is for you: we will showcase advanced features like real-time mode, transform with state, and streaming from hybrid sources. If you like simplicity, this session is also for you: you will discover how Spark Declarative Pipelines’s constructs can simplify stream processing.

If you like simplicity, this session is also for you: you will discover how Spark Declarative Pipelines’s constructs can simplify stream processing.

speakers.name: Indrajit Roy
speakers.bio: Indrajit is passionate about making data processing simple and easy to use for everyone. At Databricks, he leads the teams the power Spark Structured Streaming and make materialized views magical. Indrajit used to lead the peta-byte scale and revenue critical Napa datawarehouse at Google. Indrajit started his career as a researcher at HP Labs, sprinkling machine learning over columnar store databases.

title: Streaming at Scale With Real-Time Mode: Sub-Second Train Telemetry Across the Netherlands
abstract: In this talk, we showcase how NS (Dutch Railways) built an end-to-end Databricks solution that processes train telemetry in real time, enabling live detection of carriage failures and improving overall reliability. The platform handles more than 100 billion data points per day with sub-second end-to-end latency.The session features a live demo of real train telemetry, giving the audience a direct, in‑room experience of real time.We explain how the solution was engineered using the new Real-Time Mode for Spark Structured Streaming. The entire stack is built in PySpark and SQL, so no microservices, no separate streaming framework, just Databricks.Throughout the session, we connect to other high velocity domains with cases like real time parcel rerouting, live airport baggage tracking and real-time signals feeding analytics and AI. Attendees leave with practical insights into bringing real time data into the modern data stack without the complexity of traditional streaming systems.

speakers.name: Anant Pingle
speakers.bio: Anant is a Sr. Specialist Solutions Engineer at Databricks, where he focuses on data engineering and warehousing. With a passion for streaming data, he helps organizations build robust, real-time data architectures. Before joining Databricks, he worked as a data engineer in the retail and insurance industries.

speakers.name: Wout de Ruiter
speakers.bio: Wout is a Data Architect at Capgemini with a strong focus on real time analytics. For almost three years, he has been working closely with Dutch Railways (NS), where his most recent project focuses on processing and analyzing live train telemetry at massive scale with an end-to-end Databricks solution. Wout enjoys working across the entire spectrum, from crafting data connectors to designing and delivering complete, production ready use cases. His approach centers on building durable platforms and reusable patterns that organizations can operate and evolve independently, turning real time data into a strategic, internal capability.

title: Streaming Data for Operational Apps: Building the Bridge From Analytics to Serving
abstract: Real‑time operational applications need instant query responses, but analytical platforms optimize for throughput over latency. Running separate operational and analytical databases leads to duplicate infrastructure, complex sync logic and data consistency issues. We needed sub‑second dashboards for factory operators to track machine performance in real time, while also enabling historical analysis and ML on the same data. Our previous platform stored only four days of raw data and lacked proper engineering practices. We built a streaming architecture on Databricks that serves operational queries via Lakebase Postgres and analytical workloads via Delta Lake from a single pipeline, eliminating the dual‑database problem. This session covers the architecture, our custom streaming sink, validation of the new platform alongside the legacy system, early mistakes and patterns for complex state logic, exactly‑once delivery, and measurable production outcomes.

speakers.name: Dmitry Ratushnyak
speakers.bio: Dmitry Ratushnyak is Global Data Engineering & Platform Lead at Lipton Teas and Infusions, where he drives the company’s data and AI platform strategy. With a background spanning Unilever and financial services, he specializes in building scalable data platforms and enabling real-world business impact through data. Dmitry focuses on turning complex architectures into practical, high-value solutions, helping organizations unlock insights and accelerate their data journey with modern technologies like Databricks.

title: Streaming NASA Data with Lakeflow and Agent Bricks: A Data Engineer's Guide to Databricks AI
abstract: This Data + AI Summit session walks you through a streaming application that analyzes live NASA space data using Lakeflow and Agent Bricks.Lakeflow is the foundation layer of every AI application. I explain how Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines (SDP) ingests from one of the very few public Kafka topics that exist and how it works in symbiosis with Agent Bricks to turn real-time NASA circulars about cosmic events into an AI-powered knowledge base with Databricks Apps UI.The AI stack covers AI Gateway, guardrails, Vector Search, agent evaluation, and MLflow tracing, all from a data engineer's perspective.The second part focuses on Time to Value. I used several Databricks AI tools: Genie Code, Claude Code, the AI Dev Kit, and others. Each capable of taking you from zero to a working application in days rather than months. I share the trade-offs and best practices learned.Grab the full application from GitHub afterward — and play with data from exploding supernovas.

title: Streaming the Agency: Databricks-Powered Contest Management, Scalable Dashboards and GenAI Chatbots
abstract: Insurance agents increasingly require fast, accurate and scalable insights to drive sales and service performance. However, legacy contest systems, performance-limited dashboards and rule-based chatbots create delays, manual effort and constrained scalability. This presentation showcases how Databricks’ advanced data engineering like live streaming and DLT that come with near real-time ETL enabled a modernized CMS, scalable dashboards and an intelligent GenAI chatbot. By replacing it with streaming pipelines, we improved contest flexibility, accelerated deployment and delivered timely progress updates. A new dashboard framework now supports 25,000 agents with 1,200 concurrent users. The GenAI chatbot automates content understanding, enabling accurate Q&A, product comparison and pre meeting preparation. The result: data delivered to frontline agents within five seconds, 50% faster contest setup and a more empowered, efficient distribution across organisation.

title: Streaming the Game: Scaling Real-Time Intelligence at DraftKings With Kafka, Databricks & Sigma
abstract: DraftKings set out to build a real-time analytics capability before the use cases were fully defined. Rather than waiting for perfect requirements, we invested in the streaming foundation first — allowing meaningful use cases to emerge from the platform itself.We assembled an initial solution to deliver second-level visibility into transactions, active users, and revenue. As stakeholders gained live insight, new needs surfaced — including real-time liability tracking and deeper financial monitoring — many not originally envisioned. As adoption expanded, we evolved the architecture to Kafka, Databricks Spark Declarative Pipelines and Real-Time Mode, as well as Sigma to improve scalability, flexibility, and governance. In this session, we will review the architectural evolution, key streaming design decisions, and how we blend real-time, historical, and forecast data into a financial intelligence system.Attendees will leave with practical lessons for designing streaming-first systems when requirements are evolving.

As adoption expanded, we evolved the architecture to Kafka, Databricks Spark Declarative Pipelines and Real-Time Mode, as well as Sigma to improve scalability, flexibility, and governance. speakers.name: Monika Hristova
speakers.bio: Monika Hristova is a Lead Software Architect at DraftKings with 18 years of experience designing complex, large-scale systems. Over the past decade, she has specialized in distributed, real-time data architectures using Apache Flink, Spark, and Databricks. She leads fraud detection and streaming initiatives, focusing on scalability, resilience, and performance. Her background spans multiple industries, where she delivered data platforms and ML pipelines. She is passionate about scalable system design and enjoys mountain biking, trail running, and reading.

speakers.name: Vinod Paidakula
speakers.bio: I’m a data leader with 17+ years of experience across data and analytics, currently leading Data Product Management and QA at DraftKings. I’m passionate about bringing product thinking into data—building scalable platforms centered around reusable capabilities and self-serve access. More recently, I’ve been driving GenAI adoption to transform how data teams operate and deliver. Outside of work, I’m an avid pickleball player.

title: Streaming to Lakehouse: How Launchmetrics Simplified Pipelines, Built Data Trust and Adopted Genie
abstract: At Launchmetrics, a leader in Brand Performance for fashion, luxury and beauty, our AWS real-time streaming architecture delivered low latency but created growing complexity, data inconsistencies and rising infrastructure costs.We shifted to a batch-first Databricks Lakehouse built on Delta Lake, using medallion architecture and Unity Catalog for governance. The impact exceeded expectations: fewer pipelines, predictable costs, safe historical recomputation, and stronger data reliability—restoring trust across product, commercial and leadership teams.Most importantly, this foundation enabled company-wide adoption of AI/BI Genie. Today, non-technical stakeholders query the lakehouse in natural language, reducing dependency on the data team and accelerating decisions.In this session, we’ll share how we evaluated real-time vs. batch trade-offs, executed the migration safely and designed a “Genie-ready” lakehouse that delivers measurable business value.

speakers.name: Pau Montero Pares
speakers.bio: Pau Montero Parés is a technology executive with more than 20 years of experience in technology leadership, digital transformation, and team management. He currently serves as Chief Technology Officer at Launchmetrics, where he leads the company’s technology strategy, product innovation, and engineering organization. Prior to Launchmetrics, Pau worked as Development and Systems Manager at iMente Global, building strong expertise in technical operations and R&D. He holds a background in computer engineering and a master’s degree in policy and marketing, combining strategic vision with deep technical knowledge. He is passionate about building high-performing teams, driving innovation, and scaling modern cloud-based and AI-powered platforms that support global growth.

speakers.name: Albert Vila
speakers.bio: Albert Vila is Vice President of Software Development at Launchmetrics, with a long tenure and deep expertise in software engineering and technology leadership. He began his journey at Launchmetrics in 2003 as a software developer building large-scale search systems and progressively advanced into team leadership and strategic technology roles, culminating in his current position leading the software development organisation. At Launchmetrics he focuses on driving technical excellence, innovation with modern data-driven solutions, and mentoring engineering teams to deliver high-impact software products. ￼ Throughout his career he has worked across engineering, R&D and technical management, engaging with complex technology challenges and fostering a culture of continuous improvement. His passion for optimisation and learning underpins his approach to building scalable systems and supporting team growth.

title: Supercharging Genie Performance with Metric Views
abstract: Databricks Genie allows users to talk to their data, but its answers are only as good as the semantic layer beneath it. The Data Platform team shares how we are "supercharging" our internal Genie deployment by feeding it highly structured Metric Views. We will discuss the art of curating data specifically for GenAI consumption, ensuring that natural language queries yield accurate, trustworthy SQL generation. If you want to deploy AI BI that actually works, you need to see how we structure our data for it.

title: Tech Industry Forum | Sponsored by Entrada
abstract: As the first wave of AI experimentation matures, leaders are facing a new bottleneck: the “Architectural Tax” of fragmented stacks. To move beyond universal foundation models and build production-grade AI applications, an interoperable foundation is essential. This foundation must unify governance, data and model choice to power high-frequency data and governed intelligence from a single location. This 90-minute Tech Industry Forum joins leaders from the top AI innovators to bridge the gap between AI ambition and operational reality. We will move past the hype to address the foundational challenges that now define market leadership: eliminating the latency between data and application, slashing the overhead of massive compute costs and establishing the governance frameworks required to scale intelligent systems with confidence. This session is designed for leaders who must act now to transition from experimental AI into a high-velocity, production-scale operation. After the session, connect with your peers during the exclusive Industry Forum Happy Hour. Reserve your seat today!

title: Tech Industry Session: How AI is Redefining the Way Apps are Built
abstract: The traditional blueprint for building applications is more than 20 years old and optimized for a fragmented, ETL-heavy architectures of the early cloud era. The rise of agentic AI has broken this model, forcing us to reimagine how we — or perhaps agents — will build applications for the next 20 years. Today's AI native applications are increasingly written by agents, require easy access to real-time context and AI generated insights from other agents that legacy cloud platforms cannot provide without an expensive "Architectural Tax."Join Akhil Gupta and engineering leaders from the leading tech companies as they discuss the shift from legacy stacks to an interoperable foundation. We’ll explore the "Lakebase" paradigm, where Lakebase, Databricks Apps and Agent Bricks provide the modern way to build AI-native applications. This session will move beyond the "vibe check," offering a candid look at how to eliminate silos and ship governed, production-grade AI applications at scale.Key topics include: Identifying and eliminating the "Architectural Tax" imposed by legacy cloud silos. Understanding why AI-native applications require an interoperable app foundation: Lakebase. How do you provide easy access to AI to these applications with Agent Bricks and Databricks Apps Learn how a unified governance layer for both your data and AI helps you move from experimental prototypes to production agents faster.
description_raw:
The traditional blueprint for building applications is more than 20 years old and optimized for a fragmented, ETL-heavy architectures of the early cloud era. The rise of agentic AI has broken this model, forcing us to reimagine how we — or perhaps agents — will build applications for the next 20 years. Today's AI native applications are increasingly written by agents, require easy access to real-time context and AI generated insights from other agents that legacy cloud platforms cannot provide without an expensive "Architectural Tax."Join Akhil Gupta and engineering leaders from the leading tech companies as they discuss the shift from legacy stacks to an interoperable foundation. We’ll explore the "Lakebase" paradigm, where Lakebase, Databricks Apps and Agent Bricks provide the modern way to build AI-native applications. This session will move beyond the "vibe check," offering a candid look at how to eliminate silos and ship governed, production-grade AI applications at scale.Key topics include:

  • Identifying and eliminating the "Architectural Tax" imposed by legacy cloud silos.
  • Understanding why AI-native applications require an interoperable app foundation: Lakebase.
  • How do you provide easy access to AI to these applications with Agent Bricks and Databricks Apps
  • Learn how a unified governance layer for both your data and AI helps you move from experimental prototypes to production agents faster.

speakers.bio: Jaya Upadhyay is Senior Director of Enterprise Data Platform at Zillow, where she is enabling Zillow's data to be AI-ready for consumer experiences and internal teams. She leads the company's data mesh transformation across a portfolio spanning API and MCP orchestration, real-time streaming, analytics and ML platforms, and end-to-end data governance. With nearly two decades of data engineering and data platform leadership across Boats Group, Expedia Group, and Groupon, she has built petabyte-scale platforms, customer data ecosystems, and the high-performing teams behind them.

title: Tech Industry Session: The High-Velocity Platform for Customer Apps
abstract: The hidden cost and engineering friction of maintaining fragmented infrastructure for customer-facing features slows your product velocity. As high-frequency data and intelligence become the core of the user experience, the traditional strategy of managing siloed operational and analytical stacks has shifted from a technical inconvenience to a strategic liability. For organizations that must ship faster while controlling infrastructure overhead, the path forward is a fundamental move toward consolidation. This tech industry session moves past the theory to provide a proven blueprint for architecting experiences built directly on a unified foundation. We will explore the shift from “integrated” to “built-on” and detail how to consolidate specialized databases, eliminate brittle data pipelines and use the lakehouse and Lakebase architectures to power user experiences at a fraction of the traditional engineering cost.

We build, own and operate our enterprise lakehouse for Databricks where all employees and all teams inside use Data and AI to solve problems and guide our decisions. Previously Bruce held technical leadership roles at Netflix, Twilio and StitchFix where he gained deep experience building planet scale systems in a cloud-native environment. Bruce also formed the Chaos Engineering team and led the initiative at Netflix to achieve ultimate resilience. Bruce has also shares his experience and insights serving on product advisory boards previously for DataDog, PagerDuty, currently for DataBricks.

title: Technical Deep Dive: Architecting Production-Ready Data & AI Apps
abstract: Organizations are moving beyond simple dashboards to interactive, secure Data & AI applications built directly where their data lives. This session provides a technical deep dive into Databricks Apps, exploring how to transform complex AI logic into production-ready business tools. We will cover the essential architectural choices for developers, including a comparison of Pythonic frameworks like Streamlit and Gradio versus full-stack JS/TS implementations. The discussion will focus on the end-to-end development lifecycle, teaching you how to master authentication via Service Principals and On-Behalf-Of (OBO) tokens, and how to implement robust production workflows using Databricks Asset Bundles (DABs). Additionally, we will share best practices for optimizing performance and scalability—such as using async operations—and ensuring enterprise-grade observability through diagnostic logging and audit trails

title: The 52x Multiplier: How Zepto Mastered AI Agent Evaluation at Scale
abstract: In quick commerce, delivery windows are measured in minutes, making AI reliability existential. At Zepto, scaling to 80,000 daily autonomous support tickets demanded more than “vibe-based” testing—it required treating evaluation as core infrastructure. This session unveils the comprehensive dual-loop framework (powered by MLFlow 3.0 and DSPy) that transforms operational risk into a competitive advantage. We achieved a verified 52X ROI on evaluation infrastructure, slashing support costs by 65% while boosting CSAT by 20.5%. By shifting from reactive fixes to real-time tracing, we compressed issue detection from 3 hours to 5 minutes and reduced social media escalations by 74%. We will deconstruct the technical blueprint for establishing “Evaluation as Infrastructure”—covering LLM-as-a-Judge calibration, strategic sampling, and automated feedback loops—enabling teams to deploy complex, multimodal AI agents with absolute confidence and control.

speakers.name: Gireesh Sreedhar K P
speakers.bio: Gireesh Sreedhar is an AI Systems Architect and Transformation Leader with 23+ years in technology and a decade building and scaling Data + AI systems for global enterprises. At Databricks, He partners with technology leaders and Engineer teams to turn ambitious AI strategy into production‑grade, agentic AI on the Lakehouse, with a sharp focus on MLflow‑driven MLOps and rigorous evaluation at million‑plus interaction scale. He has delivered enterprise AI agents that slash processing from hours to minutes, cut costs by up to 85%, and achieve 5–8x faster resolutions with double‑digit CSAT uplift in complex, highly regulated environments.

speakers.name: Deepak Dhankani
speakers.bio: Deepak Dhankani is a Data Science leader at Zepto, India's fastest growing quick commerce platform, where he heads a multidisciplinary team spanning Ads, Customer Delight, Fraud, and Pricing. With hands-on technical depth and strategic leadership, he drives end-to-end data science initiatives from building ad systems, Bayesian fraud detection models and agentic AI customer support systems to designing multi-agent frameworks.

title: The Agentic Lakehouse: Optiver’s AI Data Stack
abstract: AI-driven usage has accelerated sharply in recent months. A significant portion of our users now delegate Databricks work to agents rather than interacting with the platform directly. This shift increases the importance of the foundations beneath.This session introduces Optiver's Lakehouse AI Stack: a set of MCP servers, skills, and deployment tooling designed to provide agents with the context Databricks does not expose by default. We walk through a representative user journey, highlight where agents fall short today, and describe how each component addresses a specific gap.

title: The Alpha Advantage: Building the Intelligent Capital Markets Firm with Databricks
abstract: Markets look calm, but underneath, there is a rapid reshuffling of outcomes driven by technology, capital intensity, and AI. In this environment, the edge isn’t models— it’s proprietary data and the ability to operationalize it.With Databricks, firms like RBC Capital Markets, Ares Management, and LSEG are automating workflows, scaling AI, and turning data into a durable competitive advantage.

speakers.name: Adam Towne
speakers.bio: Adam Towne is a Director of Product at LSEG, where he leads Lipper, the firm's fund analytics business, and owns data distribution across Analytics. With over a decade building data and analytics products in financial services, he focuses on serving sophisticated institutional users — quants, portfolio managers, and analysts who treat data as critical infrastructure. His current work spans cloud delivery, enterprise data licensing, and applying AI to fund research workflows. His product philosophy centers on designing for power users — what he calls "optimizing for the 1,000th use, not the first." He holds a degree in engineering from Cornell University.

speakers.name: Mark Nigro
speakers.bio: Mark Nigro is the Head of Portfolio Research at Franklin Templeton, where he leads research efforts focused on evaluating investment managers, markets, and portfolio opportunities across asset classes. His work centers on creating unique, large-scale structured data sets and using AI to improve how investment insights are communicated and applied, turning market complexity into clear, actionable outcomes for investors. Mark has spent his 20-year career in asset management working with complex investment data across research, portfolio construction, fixed income, alternatives, and institutional and advisor-facing roles. Mark is a CFA and CAIA Charterholder and holds a B.S. in Finance from Canisius University.

title: The Data Gap in Women's Football & How FIFPro and Databricks Are Helping to Close It
abstract: As the FIFA World Cup kicks off in the USA days before this summit, billions will follow the men's game, but women footballers still fight for visibility and basic protections. FIFPro, representing 65,000+ professional footballers, is using data to change that.For over a decade, FIFPro has surveyed women footballers worldwide on working conditions, welfare and employment rights. But that data lived on laptops in fragmented spreadsheets with critical insights locked away.In this session, Blend shares how we built a data platform on Databricks to power evidence-based advocacy. We'll cover designing a medallion architecture for messy survey data spanning multiple tournament cycles, configuring AI/BI Genie so non-technical researchers can query data in natural language, and building Power BI dashboards that surface cross-confederation trends.When women's football data sits on laptops, it's easy to overlook. In a lakehouse, it's impossible to ignore.

title: The Databricks Big Book of AgentOps: The New DevOps Principles for AI Agents
abstract: The journey from traditional machine learning models to AI systems demands a new, specialized set of operational practices. This talk traces the evolution of core DevOps principles from the decade of MLOps to the Agents era, where we must plan for deploying and maintaining autonomous, multi-step AI agents that can reason, plan, and take actions in the real world using external tools.The talk will show how classic DevOps tenets like continuous deployment must be radically re-engineered to tackle core AgentOps challenges. Authors of The Databricks Big Book of Agent Ops will cover:Ensuring Maturity of Operational Controls: How critical operational controls must evolve to support multi-agent architectures.Distributed Tracing and Observability: Transforming single-step I/O logs into traces essential for debugging and explaining agent behavior.Governed Deployment and Rollback: Establishing AgentOps CI/CD pipelines that treat configurations (prompts, tools, routing logic) as code
