speakers.name: Jeanne Choo
speakers.bio: Jeanne Choo is the APJ ML Lead at Databricks. She started as AI Engineer #1 at AI Singapore, building their consulting arm, apprenticeship program and technical stack from scratch. Jeanne was then AI Lead at Bank of Singapore. Her best projects have been APAC-specific, for example, Japanese Search Query Understanding, Speech Recognition models for Singlish, and building NLP models and corpuses for Indonesian. Each of these projects faced similar MLOps challenges; to be at Databricks solving these issues is very exciting for Jeanne.

speakers.name: Kyra Wulffert
speakers.bio: Kyra Wulffert is an AI Specialist Architect at Databricks with a background spanning production ML, AI strategy, and agentic systems. She specialises in agentic architectures, evaluation frameworks, and production pipelines, having previously led data science teams shipping NLP, recommendation, and predictive systems. She has spoken at PyData London, ODSC Europe, and WiDS Stanford.

title: The Databridge Platform: Connecting Data Silos Seamlessly With Databricks
abstract: We introduce the Databridge Platform, a solution developed by Rakuten Group, Inc. to address the significant data management challenges inherent in their vast global ecosystem. Rakuten faces issues such as data and infrastructure silos, complex data access procedures and data duplication.Built on the Databricks ecosystem, the Databridge Platform leverages technologies like Unity Catalog, Data Access Management Service (DAMS) and AI/BI Genie. Its architecture prioritizes a single workspace for ease of setup while retaining flexibility for dedicated tenant workspaces. The platform's strategic roadmap includes a proof of concept by March 2026, a production release by June 2026, and a long-term goal of achieving 100% connected data warehouse federation by 2028, ultimately unlocking enhanced AI capabilities and driving cross-business growth through improved data utilization.

speakers.name: Akhmad Muqtafi
speakers.bio: Muqtafi Akhmad is a Senior Data Engineer at Rakuten, where he develops and maintains large-scale data pipeline platforms that ingest data from more than 70 business units and make it available in a centralized data lake. His work includes designing and building the platform, supporting data engineers in its use, and providing troubleshooting and operational support. Prior to Rakuten, he worked at Micron Technology as a Data Scientist developing end-to-end data solutions for high-volume manufacturing. In addition, earlier in his career as a software engineer at Traveloka he contributed to backend services and data ingestion platforms development.

title: The Evolution of Prompting: From Artisan Craft to Algorithmic Optimization
abstract: LLMs unlocked advanced capabilities for users of all technical abilities precisely because of their natural language interface. Building enterprise solutions involved prompt engineering - a process of trial and error. This talk explores how automated prompt optimization represents a paradigm shift in how we build high quality, maintainable enterprise solutions with LLMs. We'll examine the core limitations of hand-crafted prompting, then dive into how prompt optimizers solve these problems. The session will cover the current state of the art in prompt optimization, including the latest algorithms such as GEPA. We'll demonstrate measurable performance gains that prompt optimizers achieve over manual engineering - often discovering counterintuitive prompts that humans would never write but dramatically outperform our best handcrafted attempts. You'll leave understanding how prompt optimizers work and why they are essential for building high quality, maintainable GenAI solutions.

speakers.name: Hanna Farooq Moazam
speakers.bio: Hanna is a Solutions Architect at Databricks, based in London. While she specializes in data science and ML, she enjoys solving all kinds of complex problems with data. She is a strong proponent of and contributor to the open source community, developing open source solutions for governance and GenAI. Prior to Databricks, she was a Data & AI CSA at Microsoft. Hanna has a degree in Computer Science from Carnegie Mellon University.

title: The Full Databricks App Experience
abstract: What does a real Databricks App look like in production? In this session, we walk through an internal tool we use every day: an agentic Lakeflow generation system built entirely on the Databricks platform. Starting from raw Unity Catalog tables, the app uses foundational models orchestrated in a LangGraph loop to profile data, design pipelines, validate transformations, and generate fully deployable Lakeflow artefacts from Silver through to Gold.We’ll show how application state, specifications, and user preferences are managed in Lakebase, enabling a seamless, iterative build experience inside Databricks. Forget the quick toy POC Apps you've been seeing. This is a practical blueprint for building intelligent, stateful data applications natively on the Lakehouse.

speakers.name: Simon Whiteley
speakers.bio: Simon is the CTO of Advancing Analytics, a world-leading, forward-thinking Databricks consultancy, where he turns complex technology innovations into practical organisational guidance. With a career spent architecting data systems and shaping strategy, he brings a rare mix of deep technical expertise and big-picture vision. On stage and online, Simon shares insights on AI, Engineering and Analytics with clarity and energy making the future of tech both accessible and exciting. A trusted voice in the Databricks space and a prolific YouTuber, he’s driven by a passion for building solutions that are as impactful as they are intelligent, always keeping people, purpose, and progress at the core.

title: The Future of Cross-Company Collaboration on Databricks
abstract: For years, cross-company collaboration meant sharing files, tables, and dashboards. That era is ending. In this session, we explore the next era of collaboration — one where organizations share intelligence, not just data.Databricks is reimagining what it means to work across organizational boundaries. Instead of exchanging static assets, partners and customers will interact through AI-native experiences — governed, secure, and designed for a world where every company runs on agents. We'll paint the picture of a new ecosystem of tools and services that turns every data relationship into something dynamic, conversational, and instantly valuable.If you believe the future of B2B is agentic, this session will show you where it's all heading. You'll leave with a new mental model for what collaboration looks like when AI is the interface.

title: The Intelligent Healthcare System: How Agentic AI is Redefining Care Delivery and Coverage
abstract: The next wave of healthcare transformation won't be driven by data alone — it will be driven by AI that can reason, act and operate across the full continuum of care. In this session, we explore how leading health systems and health plans are moving beyond dashboards and static models to deploy agentic AI workflows built on Databricks — where Genie enables clinical and operational teams to query complex data in plain language, and Mosaic AI orchestrates multi-step agents that surface insights at the point of decision. From reducing prior authorization friction to accelerating discharge planning and closing care gaps, we'll examine how the convergence of unified data, governed AI and natural language interfaces is collapsing the distance between insight and action. Attendees will leave with a clear view of the architectural patterns enabling this shift, the governance guardrails that make agentic AI trustworthy in regulated environments, and a roadmap for what the intelligent health system looks like in practice.

Attendees will leave with a clear view of the architectural patterns enabling this shift, the governance guardrails that make agentic AI trustworthy in regulated environments, and a roadmap for what the intelligent health system looks like in practice.

speakers.name: Guneeti Sharma
speakers.bio: Guneeti Sharma is a Senior Principal in AI at Slalom and a leading voice in the rise of Agentic AI in healthcare. Her work focuses on building intelligent systems that go beyond copilots—actively orchestrating decisions, workflows, and outcomes. She has led enterprise-scale agentic AI platforms on Databricks, scaling dozens of use cases across care delivery and payer operations. Her work shows how multi-agent systems can operate as coordinated, goal-driven engines in regulated environments. Guneeti is passionate about redefining healthcare through intelligent systems—where AI Governance becomes a foundational layer for how care is delivered, decisions are made, and value is created.

speakers.name: Mark Tabor
speakers.bio: As a Senior Manager at Centene, Mark Tabor leads Enterprise Data Science & AI Platforms. He specializes in building robust MLOps frameworks that operationalize Data Science at scale within complex enterprise environments. Mark combines technical depth with a passion for science and technology to deliver future-ready, intelligence-driven solutions in the healthcare space.

speakers.name: Barrett Foat
speakers.bio: Barrett C. Foat is a senior AI and machine learning leader responsible for leading large-scale enterprise AI initiatives within Centene’s Data & Analytics organization. He currently oversees the development, deployment, and governance of machine learning and AI capabilities that support business decisioning and healthcare outcomes across the enterprise. He is recognized for aligning AI innovation with business strategy, enabling measurable ROI, and operationalizing AI at scale through disciplined engineering, MLOps, and governance practices.

title: The Lakehouse Behind a Modern CDP
abstract: Building a CDP isn’t just a UI and APIs—it’s a data platform problem at scale. This talk shows how Warner Bros. Discovery built the Databricks-powered “back of house” for Mosaic, supporting audience segmentation and activation across millions of subscribers. We’ll walk through the data plane that converts high-volume first-party events (client SDK + server events) into governed Delta tables using Spark Structured Streaming, then runs incremental batch pipelines to produce custom attributes and curated event views for segmentation across identity grains. We’ll also cover the audience evaluation engine: drag-and-drop audience definitions are stored as rule trees, compiled into Spark plans and evaluated at scale to generate deterministic audience outputs for downstream delivery. Key production patterns include table modeling, join/partition strategies, observability and separation between the metadata plane (API-driven config) and the Databricks execution plane.

title: The Modern Databricks Notebook: Tips, AI workflows, and Best Practices for Every Practitioner
abstract: Databricks Notebooks are the heartbeat of data and AI work, but most practitioners only scratch the surface of what they can do. Whether you are writing your first cell or building production-grade pipelines, this session is a practical guide to getting more out of the modern notebook experience.Topics include:- Core authoring productivity: side-by-side editing, tab groups, TOC and minimap, and smarter cell management- Running and exploring data with result table profiling, filter-to-SQL, and targeted SQL execution- AI in your notebook with AI functions for SQL and Python, plus Genie Code for agentic development- Engineering best practices with the notebook debugger, unit testing, and Serverless session restore- A live end-to-end workflow that brings everything together

speakers.name: Samantha Banchik
speakers.bio: Samantha Banchik is a Product Manager at Databricks, focusing on authoring with the Databricks Assistant. She holds a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science from the University of California, Berkeley. Samantha brings a strong background in software engineering and product management, with previous roles in developer tooling, cloud security, and autonomous vehicles.

title: The New Genie: From Data Q&A to Enterprise Agent
abstract: In this session, we'll introduce the new Genie, a unified, multi-agent AI that doesn't just answer questions about your data, it acts on it. We'll show how Genie connects enterprise knowledge stores and unstructured content to deliver richer insights, how it orchestrates across Genie Spaces and custom agents to complete complex work, and how it can take governed actions on your behalf, including automating recurring tasks and skills. Attendees will leave understanding why Genie is not just a data Q&A chatbot, but an enterprise AI that connects your people, your data, and your systems—and gets work done.

speakers.name: Elise Georis
speakers.bio: Elise Georis is on the product team at Databricks, focused on Genie. Previously, she oversaw product for Lakeflow Connect. She holds a computer science degree from Princeton University.

title: The Open Handshake: Scaling Multi-Cloud Delivery via Delta Sharing and Iceberg
abstract: Client demand is shifting. They don’t want our data; they want it in their platform. We solved the “Vendor Lock-in” crisis by building an Open Data Passport. This session deconstructs how we leveraged Delta Sharing and Apache Iceberg UniForm to deliver high-scale healthcare products to clients without moving a single byte of raw data. We’ll share the engineering “scars” of managing cross-cloud permissions and how we turned an interoperability headache into a competitive product advantage for our global ecosystem. Demo: A live cross-platform “handshake” between a Databricks source and a non-Databricks consumer with zero ETL. Takeaways: Architecting zero-copy delivery via Delta Sharing Managing multi-platform governance Building vendor-agnostic data products

speakers.name: Ravi Purohit
speakers.bio: Ravi is a builder at heart who finds his "zen" at the intersection of deep engineering and product craft. Currently leading high-scale Data Platforms at McKesson, he has spent 15+ years architecting global ecosystems serving 1Bn+ users across e-commerce, fintech, and healthcare spanning both B2C and B2B domains. Ravi specializes in the "un-breakable" stuff - mission-critical migrations, high-performance infrastructure and state of the art user experience. A seasoned speaker, he’s best known for sharing "scars from the trenches" and proving that even the most daunting technical challenges can be reshaped into elegant, scalable user experiences.

speakers.name: vinaya thimmappa
speakers.bio: Vinaya is a seasoned engineering leader currently serving as Director of Engineering at McKesson Compile India, where he drives technical strategy and builds high-performance software teams. With over two decades of experience across technology and product organizations, Vinaya has led engineering efforts at companies spanning startups and large enterprises. He writes and speaks about software engineering practices, agile frameworks, and tech leadership, and enjoys sharing insights on observability, coding philosophy, and team culture. Vinaya holds advanced business and technical credentials and is passionate about mentoring engineers and shaping scalable systems

title: The Right Model for the Job: A Practical Framework for AI
abstract: Not every problem needs the biggest model — and not every model works out of the box. With frontier models, small open models, and fine-tuning all on the table, the hardest question isn't "can we do AI?" It's "which model is actually right for this?"In this session, we walk through how to match models to use cases across real enterprise workloads. You'll see when a frontier model earns its cost, when a small open model wins on latency, and when fine-tuning turns a generic model into a specialist that knows your business. We'll put the tradeoffs in action — quality, cost, latency, control — using concrete examples across customer support, document processing, and analytics.You'll leave with a repeatable framework for choosing, customizing, and deploying the right model on Databricks every time — and the patterns to keep those decisions sharp as new models land.

speakers.name: Viktoria Semaan
speakers.bio: Viktoria Semaan is a Principal Technical Evangelist and AI Engineer with 15+ years of experience across cloud infrastructure, software engineering, and consulting. At Databricks, she leads developer engagement initiatives that help teams adopt next-generation AI technologies, with a focus on multi-agent systems, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and modern AI toolchains. Previously, she worked at AWS, where she supported global partners in delivering cloud-native solutions across IT modernization, manufacturing, and SaaS. She holds an MBA from UCLA Anderson and an MS in Information Systems & Technology. Recognized as an AI/ML Top Voice on LinkedIn, Viktoria is known for turning complex technical topics into accessible stories through demos, educational content, and keynotes. She actively mentors emerging builders and champions inclusive innovation in the AI space.

speakers.name: Nuno Castro
speakers.bio: Nuno is a Director of AI Forward Deployed Engineering at Databricks, leading a specialized team that builds and productionizes first-of-its-kind AI applications. With 20 years of AI/ML experience - 12 in commercial leadership -he excels at moving AI from experimentation to live production. Previously, he was Sr. Manager of Applied Science at AWS, delivering 150+ GenAI and Agentic AI projects. His background includes 8 years at Expedia Group directing global data science for Search and Personalization, and fighting fraud at Feedzai. Nuno holds a PhD in CS/ML and began his career in R&D at Nokia-Siemens.

title: The Talent Engineering Playbook : How Yanolja Built a High-Performance Data Culture
abstract: A modern data platform is just the foundation; success depends on the teams steering it. For Yanolja, Korea’s leading travel-tech unicorn, scaling innovation required a systematic talent strategy to match rapid technical growth.This session offers an inside look at enterprise-scale talent evolution. We deconstruct an "assess-and-architect" methodology, using data-driven diagnostics to bridge skill gaps. A key pillar was establishing an enterprise-wide data capability framework. This created a shared language for skills and maturity — acting as a cultural catalyst that aligns leadership, engineering and business teams.Partnering with Databricks, Yanolja redefined how human capital accelerates platform ROI. Discover how structural enablement became a tangible value driver. Learn how technical fluency correlates with platform returns and how leadership can pivot from managing systems to architecting a resilient, data-first culture.

title: The Upcoming Apache Spark™ 4.2: The Next Chapter in Unified Analytics
abstract: AI workloads demand real-time feature pipelines, multimodal data support, and seamless AI-assisted development. Apache Spark™ 4.2 brings these capabilities into the engine, unifying batch and streaming while optimizing them at the planner level.Metric views provide a first-class semantic layer to define measures once and ensure consistent results. Catalog-managed flows make incremental and streaming queries lifecycle-aware catalog objects. A language-agnostic UDF protocol enables portable user logic across Python and emerging Spark Connect languages. Enhanced LLM discoverability and local performance improvements, including Arrow-backed caching and shuffle-free execution, make development faster and more interactive.Apache Spark™ 4.2 evolves into a governed, incremental, and AI-native platform built for the demands of the AI age.

speakers.name: Xiao Li
speakers.bio: Xiao Li is an Engineering Director at Databricks, an Apache Spark Committer, and a PMC member. He has a deep interest in Spark and database engines. Previously, he was an IBM Master Inventor and an expert in asynchronous database replication and consistency verification. Xiao earned his Ph.D. from the University of Florida in 2011.

speakers.name: DB Tsai
speakers.bio: DB Tsai is an engineering leader at the Databricks Spark team. He is an Apache Spark Project Management Committee (PMC) Member and Committer, and he enjoys building teams with great cultures focusing on large scale distributed data infrastructure. Before his transition to a leadership role, he implemented several algorithms including Linear Regression and Binary/Multinomial Logistic Regression with Elastici-Net (L1/L2) regularization using LBFGS/OWL-QN optimizers in Apache Spark project.

title: The Write Way: Building Bi-Directional Data Pipelines in the Lakehouse
abstract: Ever wondered how to bridge the gap between analytical data and operational workflows without breaking your lakehouse architecture? What if business users could edit data directly in a web app while maintaining a single source of truth? Traditional lakehouses excel at analytics but struggle with user-driven updates, often creating silos or complex workarounds.There’s a better way—in this session we’ll cover a full writeback loop where lakehouse (Unity Catalog) syncs to PostgreSQL (Lakebase) for operational speed, a Databricks app offers an intuitive editing interface and Lakeflow jobs sync changes back every 5 minutes.The lakehouse stays the source of truth while users get flexibility. We’ll explore scenarios including form-based data entry, table editing and data quality workflows, all built with Dash and Databricks Asset Bundles. By the end, you'll have a clear blueprint for building bi-directional data flows that empower users without sacrificing governance or reliability.

speakers.name: Falek Miah
speakers.bio: Databricks Champion and Microsoft & Databricks certified consultant with over 15 years of experience in data, analytics, and Azure. Specialises in designing and delivering end-to-end data platforms using Azure, Databricks, and the Power Platform, with a strong focus on automation, DevOps, and scalable analytics. Works closely with engineers and stakeholders to turn complex data problems into practical solutions. An active member of the data community who enjoys sharing real-world lessons through talks, blogs and mentoring. Outside work, enjoys running, swimming, football, and learning new technologies.

title: Thinking Fast & Slow: How Databricks Built High-Speed and Deep Research Agents
abstract: In today's competitive agentic search landscape, two operating modes are mission-critical. The first is a low-latency, low-cost mode for consumer-facing scale. It must meet strict tail-latency budgets for real-time, high-throughput use cases at minimal per-query cost, without sacrificing quality.The second is a compute-intensive deep research mode enabling expert-level analysis—from financial due diligence and technology mapping to clinical review and manufacturing diagnostics. The system must plan multi-step retrieval, triangulate sources, and synthesize coherent answers.Using our Instructed Retriever and Aroll frameworks, we built a unified agentic harness supporting both modes in a single architecture. Our system sits at the Pareto frontier of cost, speed, and quality. Instant mode delivers single-digit second latency maintaining accuracy, while Thinking mode achieves SoTA-level performance across enterprise domains.

Instant mode delivers single-digit second latency maintaining accuracy, while Thinking mode achieves SoTA-level performance across enterprise domains.

title: Tips and Tricks for Postgres Observability at Scale
abstract: Running Postgres in production gets harder as your application grows. Logs pile up, metrics live in one tool, schema changes live in another, and when something breaks, you are stuck stitching it all together under pressure.This session shares practical tips and tricks for getting ahead of common Postgres operational challenges. We will cover how to spot configuration issues before they hit production, how to unblock application updates when schema changes go sideways, and how to quickly connect application failures back to the database changes that caused them.Along the way, we will show how bringing Postgres operational data together with modern AI tooling can take a lot of the manual work out of diagnostics, so you spend less time hunting for root causes and more time shipping.Whether you are running a handful of Postgres instances or managing them at scale, you will leave with concrete techniques you can apply to your own environment.

title: Transforming Governance Into a Scalable Data & AI Capability in the Data Mesh Era
abstract: As enterprises scale AI and data products across domains, traditional governance models fail to keep pace. Historical approaches to data ownership no longer flex to meet data product demands. Domain autonomy creates inconsistency. AI amplifies risk. This presentation presents a scalable operating model that integrates data literacy, enterprise governance, governance engineering and domain enablement into a single cohesive approach built on the modern data platform. Using a federated data mesh architecture powered by Databricks, this modern approach to data ownership transforms governance from documentation and oversight into a programmable, platform-embedded enterprise capability.The result: Faster domain onboarding Reduced regulatory risk Automated policy enforcement Measurable AI readiness

speakers.name: Sherri Adame
speakers.bio: Sherri is a dynamic data governance leader with a proven track record of delivering enterprise-scale strategies that drive compliance, reduce costs, and accelerate data delivery. Currently in GM, Sherri is leading enterprise data governance transformation embedding data governance into engineering with proactive processes. She worked in healthcare at Cigna, where they launched a metadata strategy cataloging over 25,000 attributes & implemented a reference data framework saving over $1M in 6 months. Sherri has lead development of personalized customer experiences for 100M+ users while meeting Health Data Interoperability standards. Sherri brings a pragmatic, results-driven approach to data strategy and governance.

title: Translating Nubank’s Data Lake Access Control Model Into Databricks Unity Catalog
abstract: Nubank implemented consistent, fine-grained access control across its multi-platform data environment by translating its internal data lake access control model into Databricks Unity Catalog permissions. This model uses centrally managed "Permission Groups" (based on geography, data domains and column sensitivity) to govern access to over 300K tables for 5K+ users, enforcing control at the table and column level. The presentation will detail the architectural implementation, the programmatic translation process using Unity Catalog APIs, and the application of read-time masking for sensitive columns. It will address practical challenges in modeling fine-grained permissions and operating at scale, providing attendees with a reusable architectural pattern and concrete lessons for designing scalable, enterprise-grade access control on Databricks.

speakers.name: Nikita Deshmukh
speakers.bio: I am a Staff Software Engineer on the Data Platform team, where I design and evolve the access control and governance architecture for the company’s Databricks-based analytics platform and AWS data lake. My work focuses on extending the Data Lake access control model for read and write permissions, enforcing column-level security and PII masking, and ensuring data subject rights deletion and broader data protection requirements are correctly implemented on the lake. I am particularly interested in pragmatic architectures that keep sensitive data safe without blocking real workloads. At the Data + AI Summit, i'll share hard-won lessons from building privacy-aware, access-controlled data lake platforms in a fast-moving fintech environment.

title: Travel & Hospitality: The Making of the Modern Hospitality Experience— Humans, Data, and the Age of Anticipation
abstract: The best hospitality has always been about anticipation— knowing what a guest needs before they ask. AI is now making that possible at scale, but only when it's built on unified, governed data. With customer information still fragmented across legacy systems and siloed tools, most brands can't recognize the same traveler across channels, let alone act on real-time context. The brands pulling ahead are the ones turning data into a moat and AI into a co-pilot for every guest-facing and operational moment.Hear from leading travel and hospitality brands on how they're doing exactly that with Databricks. You’ll leave with practical architectures for transforming fragmented data into anticipatory, human-centered experiences.

AI is now making that possible at scale, but only when it's built on unified, governed data. With customer information still fragmented across legacy systems and siloed tools, most brands can't recognize the same traveler across channels, let alone act on real-time context. The brands pulling ahead are the ones turning data into a moat and AI into a co-pilot for every guest-facing and operational moment.Hear from leading travel and hospitality brands on how they're doing exactly that with Databricks. You’ll leave with practical architectures for transforming fragmented data into anticipatory, human-centered experiences.

title: Turning Data Requests Into Self-Service: Building an AI-Powered Analytics Product Users Love
abstract: Ticketmaster's operations team was drowning in data requests. Every "how many fans showed up to buy tickets last week?" question cost engineering hours. We were spending over 8 weeks/year being human query engines. We needed to give users the power to answer their own questions.We built OMT Wizard: a conversational analytics tool that lets non-technical users ask questions in plain English and get instant answers. Powered by Databricks Genie, it launched to 500+ users and achieved high stickiness. The users who try it keep coming back. This session shares product lessons from building AI-powered internal tools — how we identified the right problem to solve, designed for trust and transparency, trained the AI on business logic, measured real adoption and turned skeptics into champions. You'll learn what worked — suggested questions, showing your work — what didn't — hiding complexity — and how to build AI products that solve real problems instead of looking for problems to solve.

This session shares product lessons from building AI-powered internal tools — how we identified the right problem to solve, designed for trust and transparency, trained the AI on business logic, measured real adoption and turned skeptics into champions. You'll learn what worked — suggested questions, showing your work — what didn't — hiding complexity — and how to build AI products that solve real problems instead of looking for problems to solve.

speakers.name: Brian Muehlenkamp
speakers.bio: Brian Muehlenkamp is a Product Manager at Ticketmaster, where he leads the Onsale Management Technology platform serving 400+ operators through high-demand onsales. He works AI-native — using and evaluating new tools continuously and letting that practice reshape how he discovers, designs, and ships. Energized by the craft of product management and motivated by a futurist view of where the discipline is headed, Brian has delivered a portfolio of cutting-edge, high-impact products that have moved the business forward.

title: Ultra Low Latency Streaming for Real-Time Feature Engineering
abstract: Feature freshness is critical for fraud-detection, ad tech, real-time recommendations, dynamic pricing, etc. Historically, ML engineers have had to maintain two separate systems for feature engineering, leveraging Spark and Spark Structure Streaming for batch/near real-time features, while building bespoke pipelines when they needed sub-second feature freshness. In this session, we'll learn about Real-Time Mode in Apache Spark Structured Streaming, the engine behind the Databricks Feature Store, and how companies like DraftKings are using it to power real-time features with millisecond-level freshness. You'll learn how to pair Real-Time Mode with Lakebase to build a state-of-the-art online feature store and leave with a practical blueprint for a unified feature engineering platform that eliminates logic spread across different systems and shifts the focus from infrastructure orchestration to semantic feature modeling.

speakers.name: Jerry Peng
speakers.bio: Jerry Peng is currently an Staff Software Engineer at Databricks extensively working Apache Spark Structured Streaming. Before joining Databricks, he was a Principal Software Engineer at Splunk working on streaming and messaging projects especially with Apache Pulsar and Apache Flink. Jerry is a committer and PMC member of Apache Pulsar, Apache Storm, and Apache Heron projects. Before Splunk, he worked at Streamlio (acquired by Splunk), Citadel, and Yahoo on distributed systems and stream processing. Jerry has been working in the area of distributed systems and stream processing since his days in grad school at the University of Illinois, Urbana-Champaign.

speakers.name: Abhay Bothra
speakers.bio: Abhay leads real-time streaming and feature engineering at Databricks. Prior to joining Databricks, he was the founder and CTO of Fennel AI and has spent almost 15 years building systems at scale.

title: Under the Hood: Scalable Ingestion Strategies for Databases, Applications, and File Sources
abstract: Data engineers spend far too much time maintaining fragile scripts for a fragmented landscape of databases, applications, and file sources. In this technical session, we’ll explore how Lakeflow Connect allows you to build robust pipelines without the operational burden of DIY infrastructure. We’ll dive into: Database ingestion: A look under the hood at efficient CDC for sources like Oracle, MySQL, and Postgres, alongside query-based ingestion for sources like Teradata, Redshift, and Synapse. Application and file ingestion: What’s new—and happening behind the scenes—across our newest connectors for customer 360, employee 360, financial analytics, and more. Cost and performance optimization: Technical best practices for networking, security, and minimizing impact on source systems—plus how to use new features to slash TCO and boost performance. We’ll wrap up with how to create your own application and database connectors. Stop managing infrastructure and start delivering data.

We’ll wrap up with how to create your own application and database connectors. Stop managing infrastructure and start delivering data.

title: Underwriting the Future: How Leading Insurers Turn Proprietary Data into Competitive Advantage with Databricks
abstract: Insurers today are caught between rising claims complexity, evolving risks, and policyholders who expect more— faster. The carriers pulling ahead aren't just building better models. They're winning on data: who owns it, who can access it, and how quickly they can act on it.In this session, you'll see how leading insurers use Databricks to break down data silos, operationalize AI at scale, and put natural language insights directly in the hands of underwriters, claims teams, and distributors— turning data strategy into smarter risk selection, faster settlements, and profitable growth that compounds.

title: Unified Governance for Enterprise Analytics and AI
abstract: We implemented a unified governance architecture for enterprise analytics leveraging Databricks Unity Catalog as the authoritative control plane. The platform designed with a configuration-driven, dynamic attribute-based access control (ABAC) model with fully enforced row-level security (RLS) and column-level security (CLS) ensuring precise, policy-driven data access across the organization — including hierarchical visibility for cost owners and business leaders aligned to their reporting structures. This design prevents governance drift, reduces operational complexity, centralizes policy management, and enables secure self-service analytics across domains. Governance policies extend natively to AI/BI dashboards and Databricks Genie, ensuring consistent, compliant data access without duplicating or redefining security frameworks. Impact Eliminated decentralized access control models across 40+ dashboards. Enabled secure self-service analytics for 300+ users across 20+ teams.

This design prevents governance drift, reduces operational complexity, centralizes policy management, and enables secure self-service analytics across domains. speakers.name: Anand Tirthgirikar
speakers.bio: Anand Tirthgirikar is a Staff Engineer at InMobi, where he leads large-scale data platform and governance initiatives powering enterprise analytics and AI workloads. He has driven multiple platform modernization efforts across open lakehouse architectures, data governance, cost optimization, observability, and self-serve analytics. Anand works at the intersection of distributed systems, metadata platforms, query engines, and AI-enabled data experiences, with a strong focus on building scalable and developer-friendly platforms. He is passionate about solving complex data infrastructure challenges and enabling organizations to build governed, efficient, and future-ready data ecosystems.

title: Unified SRE Observability: How Workday Democratizes Multi-Platform Insights With Databricks AI/BI Genie
abstract: As Workday’s infrastructure scaled across multi-cloud environments, the Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) teams faced a common hurdle: fragmented data silos. Monitoring reliability across Databricks, Snowflake, ServiceNow and FiveTran required pivoting between tools, leading to delayed insights and manual reporting toil. To solve this, Workday built a unified cross-platform KPI dashboard on Databricks, centralizing telemetry into a single pane of glass for platform adoption, job performance and ITSM health. This session explores the architecture behind this “one-stop shop”—leveraging third-party integrations for seamless ingestion and the power of Databricks AI/BI Genie. By embedding conversational AI into the SRE workflow, Workday enables stakeholders to “talk to their data”—getting instant, natural-language answers without SQL. Learn how this shift from static charts to AI-driven insights has accelerated incident response, improved governance and freed SREs to focus on engineering.

title: Unify Your Data + AI Ecosystem via Databricks Marketplace
abstract: The average enterprise depends on dozens of third-party data and AI providers. Yet discovering, evaluating, and operationalizing those products remains fragmented, slow, and siloed. In this lightning talk, we'll explore how Databricks Marketplace is becoming the central hub for organizations to discover, access, and manage third-party data, apps, and AI models. Whether you're a data leader looking to streamline your vendor ecosystem or a provider looking to grow your reach, this session covers the vision and roadmap.You'll learn:How Marketplace simplifies discovery and access to third-party data and AI productsStrategies for reducing procurement friction and accelerating time to valueHow providers can expand distribution by meeting customers where they work

title: Unifying Upstream Intelligence with Generative AI
abstract: PetroWizard & IntelliSense utilizes an agent-based generative AI architecture on Databricks to address PETRONAS's challenge of fragmented, largely unstructured upstream data (90% unstructured, across OSDU, PI, SQL, etc.). Engineers previously spent 3–5 days locating data. This solution, featuring a Central Orchestrator and four specialized agents (Subsurface, Surface, Sensor, Model Router), enables cross-domain reasoning and model-agnostic, governed data intelligence. Outcomes include reducing data discovery time to 30–45 seconds, achieving 90%+ query accuracy, high user satisfaction (4/5 across 3,000+ users) and full audit traceability, fundamentally transforming upstream decision-making.

speakers.name: Prashant Singh
speakers.bio: Prashant Singh is a Senior Solutions Architect at Databricks with 10+ years of experience in Data & AI. He specializes in Lakehouse architecture, AI-driven applications, and enterprise data strategy, working with organizations across Southeast Asia to build scalable and impactful data platforms.

title: Unity AI Gateway: How to Govern Multi-AI Access Across Your Company
abstract: As coding agents like GitHub Copilot, Cursor, and Claude Code become embedded in daily development workflows, enterprises face a new challenge: how to govern and audit AI-assisted code generation at scale. Without centralized visibility, organizations risk exposing sensitive data, losing track of usage, and failing to meet security and compliance requirements.In this session, we'll show how Databricks enables end-to-end governance for coding agents across the enterprise. You'll learn how to enforce fine-grained permissions, track usage and costs across teams, and capture complete audit trails of prompts, responses, and tool interactions. We'll also cover how to apply consistent policies across providers — so developers can move fast with coding agents while maintaining the control, visibility, and compliance production environments demand.You'll walk away with the patterns to unlock coding agents safely across your organization — without creating a new compliance liability.

speakers.bio: Bilal is a product manager on the Mosaic AI team at Databricks, where he leads efforts to scale real-time and batch model serving for both traditional ML and GenAI workloads. Prior to Databricks, he worked at GitHub and Microsoft, building developer tools. Bilal holds an MS and an MBA from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology.

title: Unity Catalog Implementation at Navy Federal Credit Union
abstract: We will summarize our successful integration of Unity Catalog (UC) at Navy Federal Credit Union (NFCU), resulting in $10M savings. Our initiative established UC as the central access point for data assets within the Enterprise Data Lake (EDL) and redesigned the enterprise ETL framework.Key outcomes include: Accelerated development cycles: Reduced pipeline build and deployment time by 40%, enabling faster time-to-market for data products. Performance optimization: Achieved a 50% reduction in overall runtime and a 40% decrease in operational costs through efficient resource utilization and optimized job configurations. We will explore the architecture and strategic use of access control in UC, providing actionable insights and best practices for improving data management efficiency.EY will present alongside Krishnakumar Subramanian, Cloud Data Modernization and Analytics Leader at Navy Federal.

speakers.bio: Ansu is a Data engineering leader with strengths across technology, delivery execution, communication, and people leadership. She focuses focus on continuous improvement using clear stakeholder alignment, and disciplined execution. Known as a trusted subject matter expert, she bridges legacy platforms and modern cloud technologies with a focus Databricks and Azure. She thrives in high‑impact leadership roles and brings strong technical acumen, sound decision‑making, and collaborative communication to deliver sustained organizational value.

title: Unlocking Antibody Space With Databricks Vector Search
abstract: Antibodies form the bedrock of human health, and the body’s ability to create what is estimated to be a quintillion unique variants creates an impressive scaling issue for their study. Molecules derived from antibodies are now the fastest growing therapeutic category, and our understanding of how to design and modify antibodies requires solutions that scale. Databricks Vector Search provides an engineered solution for scaling up similarity search to 1 billion embeddings per endpoint, and by embedding antibody sequences with the protein language model AMPLIFY we will describe how this system might be used with the Observed Antibody Space dataset. The potential throughput of this system, at ~30 queries returned per second, would demonstrate state-of-the-art search. Using this scalable approach, we would then be positioned to explore advantages that this embedded representation affords for metadata lookup and statistical analysis.

speakers.name: Peter Hawkins
speakers.bio: Peter Hawkins, PhD, is a Specialist Solutions Architect at Databricks, focusing on applying machine learning at scale to accelerate drug discovery in the life sciences. He began his academic career researching ultrafast quantum optics before becoming deeply interested in machine learning and its application to biology. He spent four years as a Principal Scientist at Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, where he led a small research team focussing on machine learning models for immune receptors. He earned his MSci (2012) and PhD (2016) from Imperial College London, performed postdoctoral research at the University of Marburg and led an ML research group at Regeneron Pharmaceuticals before joining Databricks in 2024.

speakers.name: Robert Vernon
speakers.bio: Robert Vernon, PhD, has a career focus on the study of protein sequence to function relationships including protein design, structure prediction, and the protein folding problem. He completed his PhD under David Baker at the University of Washington, did his post-doctoral work on intrinsically disordered proteins under Julie Forman-Kay at the Hospital for Sick Children in Toronto, and is currently the Director of Therapeutic Protein Design at Amgen.

title: Unlocking Cross-Engine Governance With Scan APIs
abstract: While companies have embraced the data lakehouse architecture and benefits it provides, maintaining data governance has become a much more complicated story. Many companies today implement cumbersome workarounds, such as processing and maintaining multiple copies of datasets, duplicating access policies in different systems, or worse bypassing access controls altogether. In this talk, we'll explore some of the current problems with data governance and the data lakehouse architecture and how Scan APIs can address some of those challenges.
description_raw:
While companies have embraced the data lakehouse architecture and benefits it provides, maintaining data governance has become a much more complicated story.

Many companies today implement cumbersome workarounds, such as processing and maintaining multiple copies of datasets, duplicating access policies in different systems, or worse bypassing access controls altogether.

In this talk, we'll explore some of the current problems with data governance and the data lakehouse architecture and how Scan APIs can address some of those challenges.

title: Unlocking Video Data at Scale: VLM Batch Inference with Ray on Databricks
abstract: Organisations sit on vast video archives (surveillance, manufacturing, inspections), yet lack scalable methods to extract insights. This session presents a production-ready architecture for distributed video analytics using Vision Language Models (VLMs) on Databricks.We'll walk through a three-stage accelerator: (1) video ingestion into Unity Catalog Volumes, (2) VLM registration with MLflow for reproducibility, and (3) distributed batch inference using Ray and VLLM with Qwen2.5-VL-32B. You'll see how Ray orchestrates GPU-accelerated inference across video datasets and how to extract structured entities from VLM outputs using Databricks AI Functions.Attendees will leave with:A reusable pattern for multi-modal video intelligence at scaleWorking code integrating Ray, VLLM, and Unity CatalogPrompt engineering techniques for video inputsCost and performance considerations for VLM workloadsWhether your use case is retail, manufacturing, or public safety—this pattern applies.

speakers.name: Samantha Wise
speakers.bio: Samantha Wise is a Senior Specialist Solutions Engineer in Data Science/Machine Learning at Databricks. With a MSc in Mathematics from the University of Bristol, Samantha has a profound passion for leveraging mathematics and science to address real-world challenges. She began her career as a data scientist at an asset management company, where she contributed significantly, including her role on the Coronavirus Research Taskforce. Samantha then transitioned to a marketing tech startup, focusing on Generative AI, before bringing her expertise to Databricks.

title: Using AI to Migrate Semantic Models and Dashboards to Databricks Metric Views and AI/BI
abstract: At HP, we are working to integrate more of our business intelligence and analytics directly into the Databricks platform. To minimize impacts on analytics teams and their partners across organizations, we needed a way to automate and accelerate migrations of legacy semantic models and dashboards into Databricks. We developed a scalable AI based approach to parse existing BI artifacts, translate semantic models into Metric Views, convert existing visualizations into Databricks Dashboards and validate the results. This presentation shares lessons learned across multiple migrations, including both our key challenges and successes.
description_raw:
At HP, we are working to integrate more of our business intelligence and analytics directly into the Databricks platform. To minimize impacts on analytics teams and their partners across organizations, we needed a way to automate and accelerate migrations of legacy semantic models and dashboards into Databricks.

We developed a scalable AI based approach to parse existing BI artifacts, translate semantic models into Metric Views, convert existing visualizations into Databricks Dashboards and validate the results. This presentation shares lessons learned across multiple migrations, including both our key challenges and successes.

speakers.name: Jeff Mabeck
speakers.bio: Jeff Mabeck is principal analytics architect at HP Inc., focused on large scale analytics platform strategy, data architecture, and modernization. His work spans data lake and BI platform convergence, standardization of analytics and governance patterns, and the design of scalable, privacy-aware data products and BI solutions. He collaborates closely with engineering, product, and privacy stakeholders to translate complex data into actionable insights that inform decision making and improve customer experiences.

speakers.name: Utkarsh Siddu
speakers.bio: A Principal Architect with over 11 years of experience advancing AI/ML from research to large-scale production, he has built and scaled intelligent systems across printing, additive manufacturing, and enterprise data platforms. His journey spans deep learning–based computer vision, real-time quality inspection systems, and distributed model training, to leading GenAI strategy, LLM Agents & agent frameworks, and knowledge platforms in production environments. He owns multi-quarter AI roadmaps and reference architectures, driving governance, reliability, and cost-performance rigor while building strong executive alignment. A multi-time summit presenter, patent holder, and recipient of the HP Amaze Award (top 1%), he is recognized for transforming complex AI innovation into secure, observable, and measurable business impact.

title: Using Databricks Geospatial Processing at Scale
abstract: Our team started on Geospatial journey with Databricks DBR 16.X, from legacy project migration and continued to expand by ingesting and processing three different geospatial datasets (HD USHR maps, public OSM and curated OSM (Overture)). We applied different optimization techniques to make spatial joins more performant and worked closely with Databricks Geospatial team in testing out incremental improvements in DBR, culminating in large scale performance increase on DBR 17.X bringing support to geospatial data types (GEOMETRY, GEOGRAPHY) as dedicated data types that could be stored in delta table. A significant optimization was observed in spatial joins, which could eliminate previously used optimization techniques, making data pipelines maintenance easier. All of these improvements enabled us to enrich internal datasets based on various road attributes from these datasets and use them in analytics, training and potential reinforcement learning applications where needed.

speakers.name: Filip Ilic
speakers.bio: Filip Ilic is a Senior Data Engineer working on large scale Geospatial analytics within the Autonomy group of Rivian.

title: Using Genie to Speed Traditional Machine Learning Tasks
abstract: Machine learning on Databricks is becoming accessible to every role — from business analysts who have never written code to seasoned data scientists building production pipelines. This session introduces two complementary capabilities that democratize both the consumption and authoring of ML workloads.Genie for ML brings predictions directly to non-technical users. The agent understands user intent, recognizes when a question calls for ML — forecasting, classification, anomaly detection, regression — and delegates to optimized SQL ML functions behind the scenes. No Python, no notebooks, no training. Genie Code for ML targets the other side: technical users building those workflows, acting as an expert ML copilot that guides even junior data scientists through complex end-to-end ML journeys.Walk away with the patterns to make ML accessible across your organization — without sacrificing the depth experts need.

title: Utilities: Modernizing the Grid with AI and Real-Time Data
abstract: Discover how forward-thinking utilities are reimagining grid management, customer experience, and renewable integration through advanced analytics and AI. EDP and Eversource discuss how they are using the Databricks to turn complex operational data into actionable intelligence by improving reliability, reducing costs, and driving the clean energy transition at scale.

Over his career at EDP, he has progressed from roles in technology and digital innovation into global leadership, driving large‑scale cloud, data and AI programs that support the company’s energy‑transition strategy. He is recognized externally as a leading data and AI executive, has contributed to international initiatives such as World Economic Forum work on AI in industry, and invests in his executive development through programs like INSEAD’s Advanced Management Programme. Before that Nelson was an executive at Microsoft

title: Vibe Investigating with Lakewatch and Genie
abstract: For decades, security investigations have required equal parts query-language incantations, instinct, and raw persistence. Lakewatch and Genie use AI to change the game, allowing analysts to investigate by simply asking questions in natural language, extracting patterns, pivoting, and getting results fast. In this session, we'll demonstrate "vibe investigating" using Genie against real-world security datasets. We'll uncover multi-stage attacks including prompt injection, credential theft, C2 beaconing, lateral movement, and a few more surprises! We'll show the full investigation arc: establishing baselines, detecting anomalies, pivoting across data sources, and building attack timelines. No query languages. Just questions, answers, and a faster path to results. Attendees will leave with practical patterns for using Lakewatch Genie, crafting effective investigation prompts, and supercharging their security investigations.
description_raw: For decades, security investigations have required equal parts query-language incantations, instinct, and raw persistence. Lakewatch and Genie use AI to change the game, allowing analysts to investigate by simply asking questions in natural language, extracting patterns, pivoting, and getting results fast. In this session, we'll demonstrate "vibe investigating" using Genie against real-world security datasets. We'll uncover multi-stage attacks including prompt injection, credential theft, C2 beaconing, lateral movement, and a few more surprises! We'll show the full investigation arc: establishing baselines, detecting anomalies, pivoting across data sources, and building attack timelines. No query languages. Just questions, answers, and a faster path to results. Attendees will leave with practical patterns for using Lakewatch Genie, crafting effective investigation prompts, and supercharging their security investigations.

title: Virtual Delta Tables: Building a Multimodal Lakehouse
abstract: Managing multimodal inference is one of the defining challenges facing AI teams. LLMs now consider documents, images, and videos, producing higher quality results and reducing time wasted with data prep. The lakehouse architecture solves part of the problem but many teams are struggling to incorporate an increasing variety of data into their workflow. Rather than ingesting, re-encoding, or modifying massive troves of existing data, multimodal teams are looking to work with original artifacts.This talk introduces Virtual Delta Tables and the associated open source code designed for multimodal inference. Virtual Delta Tables incorporate original data from different sources into a singular logical structure for ease of use by data scientists and ML engineers. By offering a Delta Lake interface the entire ecosystem from Databricks to DuckDB can be used in multimodal work. We'll highlight just how far you can push the lakehouse architecture in the rapidly changing AI data landscape!

speakers.name: Tyler Croy
speakers.bio: At Scribd Tyler created the delta-rs project and has presented a number of times on Scribd's large scale data and ML workloads combining Rust, Delta Lake, and the Databricks platform. He has written extensively on using Delta Lake from Python and Rust, joining the talented authors of the award-winning #1 best selling book "Delta Lake: The Definitive Guide", by contributing Chapter 6. Tyler is also a Databricks MVP and a generally tall guy.

title: Weaving Data Into the Financial Fabric: How Nasdaq Uses Databricks to Power the Innovation Economy
abstract: Nasdaq is the trusted fabric of the world’s financial system, and data is at the heart of everything we do. Learn how we’re using Databricks to drive insights, support decision-making and deliver industry-leading financial solutions. Using Delta Lake’s open formats and Unity Catalog, we’ve consolidated disparate data stores into a unified corporate data lakehouse to create a single source of truth for enterprise data. We’ll demo how we’re using AgentBricks and Databricks Apps to democratize access to data while maintaining oversight and governance.You’ll also discover how our Capital Access Platforms division has built its core data platform on Databricks. With exchange and index data, alternative datasets and institutional data for more than 110,000 public and private‑market strategies and funds covering over $90 trillion in institutional assets, the platform enables rapid product development, creates value from our data assets and forms the foundation of our AI strategy.

speakers.name: Angie Ruan
speakers.bio: Angie Ruan is the Divisional CTO for Nasdaq Capital Access Platforms. She is responsible for overseeing the division’s technology strategy, including engineering across Listings, Index, Data, Analytics, Investor Relations, and Governance Solutions. She rejoined Nasdaq after serving as SVP at Chime. Prior to Chime, Angie was SVP of Global Technology at Nasdaq, overseeing key Market Technology engineering. Angie has a strong track record of leading business transformations across American Express, PayPal, and eBay. She holds four technical patents, a Master of Science in Computer Science from UC Santa Barbara, and studied undergraduate in Computer Science at Tsinghua University of China.

title: What is Databricks Apps?
abstract: Databricks Apps is the fastest way to build, deploy, and govern production-grade applications directly on the Databricks Platform. In this foundational session, we’ll explore how Databricks Apps allows you to transform your data and AI/ML models into interactive, custom applications that your entire enterprise can use. Whether you are building AI-powered agents, real-time dashboards, or internal tools for business process automation, discover how we’ve simplified application development on Databricks.

In this foundational session, we’ll explore how Databricks Apps allows you to transform your data and AI/ML models into interactive, custom applications that your entire enterprise can use. Whether you are building AI-powered agents, real-time dashboards, or internal tools for business process automation, discover how we’ve simplified application development on Databricks.

title: What Worked, What Didn’t: Virgin Atlantic’s Flight to Trusted Generative BI
abstract: As decision-making at Virgin Atlantic sped up, our BI stack creaked. Leaders wanted answers in hours and questions increasingly cut across commercial, operational, and customer data. Like many teams, we turned to GenBI to close that gap. Our first attempt didn’t go well. Answers arrived faster, but confidence dropped. Without shared metric definitions, visible lineage, or governance designed in from the start, ambiguity scaled faster than trust. “Is this right?” became the default question again. In this session, we share how we rebuilt GenBI on trusted foundations using Databricks. We’ll show how Unity Catalog and UC Metrics Views anchor AI answers to signed-off metrics, how Databricks Genie enables users to interrogate and explain results, and why explainability became part of the user experience. Attendees will leave with practical lessons for moving to decision-grade GenBI, drawn from real production use cases and delivered as a visual, story-led case study.

As decision-making at Virgin Atlantic sped up, our BI stack creaked. Leaders wanted answers in hours and questions increasingly cut across commercial, operational, and customer data. Like many teams, we turned to GenBI to close that gap.

We’ll show how Unity Catalog and UC Metrics Views anchor AI answers to signed-off metrics, how Databricks Genie enables users to interrogate and explain results, and why explainability became part of the user experience.

Attendees will leave with practical lessons for moving to decision-grade GenBI, drawn from real production use cases and delivered as a visual, story-led case study.

speakers.name: Tom Barber
speakers.bio: With over 13 years of experience in data & analytics within the aviation industry working for two of the UK's most well-known airlines, Tom has led the development of data products, data platforms, analytics environments and evolved the airline's data governance and its data culture. Tom's latest focus has been in the world of Customer Analytics and the platforms that fuel them to unlock value for both the airline and the experience of its customers. Tom is lives on the South Coast of the UK and loves jumping on (and out of) planes in his spare time, he is also a DJ in his spare time!

title: What's New in Apache Spark™ 4.1
abstract: Apache Spark 4.1 continues the evolution of the world’s leading open-source data engine, accelerating developer productivity and making modern data engineering patterns first-class citizens. Spark 4.1 improvements reduce friction, improve performance, and expand what Spark can express natively.In this session you’ll see how Spark 4.1: Declarative APIs that let you define outcomes while Spark manages pipeline execution, dependencies, and failure handling Real-Time Mode for streaming, enabling continuous, low-latency processing Improved Python performance and ergonomics, including Arrow-native UDFs/UDTFs and better debugging Expanded SQL expressiveness, with GA SQL Scripting, VARIANT support for semi-structured data, recursive CTEs, and new functions Mature Spark Connect, including GA Spark ML support for Python clients and improved stability at scale This session is a must for teams building and operating data pipelines, analytics, and streaming workloads on Spark at scale.
description_raw:
Apache Spark 4.1 continues the evolution of the world’s leading open-source data engine, accelerating developer productivity and making modern data engineering patterns first-class citizens. Spark 4.1 improvements reduce friction, improve performance, and expand what Spark can express natively.In this session you’ll see how Spark 4.1:

  • Declarative APIs that let you define outcomes while Spark manages pipeline execution, dependencies, and failure handling
  • Real-Time Mode for streaming, enabling continuous, low-latency processing
  • Improved Python performance and ergonomics, including Arrow-native UDFs/UDTFs and better debugging
  • Expanded SQL expressiveness, with GA SQL Scripting, VARIANT support for semi-structured data, recursive CTEs, and new functions
  • Mature Spark Connect, including GA Spark ML support for Python clients and improved stability at scale

This session is a must for teams building and operating data pipelines, analytics, and streaming workloads on Spark at scale.

speakers.name: Daniel Tenedorio
speakers.bio: Daniel Tenedorio is a senior staff software engineer at Databricks, working on Spark Core and Spark SQL. He interacts with the Apache Spark™ open-source community to develop and review query processing features. Daniel has a breadth of experience in database systems development from building and launching features underpinning multiple major production services over time.

speakers.name: Wenchen Fan
speakers.bio: Wenchen Fan is a software engineer at Databricks, working on Spark Core and Spark SQL. He mainly focuses on the Apache Spark open-source community, leading the discussion and reviews of many features/fixes in Spark. He is a Spark committer and a Spark PMC member.

title: What’s New in Apache Spark™ 4.1?
abstract: Explore the exciting new features of Apache Spark 4.1 with major new capabilities for both data engineering and analytics workflows. We introduce Spark Declarative Pipelines (SDP) to let users define what their data pipelines should accomplish while Spark orchestrates execution, dependencies, parallelism and retries, an official Real-Time Mode in Structured Streaming for sub-second, continuous low-latency processing, and extensive improvements to the PySpark ecosystem with Arrow-native UDFs/UDTFs, Python Data Source filter pushdown, and better Python worker logging. The release also brings Spark Connect enhancements including GA support for Spark ML on the Python client and increased stability for large workloads, and expands SQL support with GA SQL Scripting, the VARIANT type with shredding optimizations, support for recursive CTEs, and new approximate data sketch functions. Spark 4.1 prioritizes higher-level abstractions, real-time performance, richer Python support, and more.

title: What's New in Databricks Apps
abstract: Databricks Apps has moved from “great for prototypes” to an enterprise app hosting platform you can trust for real mission critical applications and this session is your fast tour of what’s new. We’ll cover major improvements to the developer experience, upgrades that make each individual app more production-ready and reliable. and new admin and governance capabilities that let you group, govern, and operate thousands of apps across your organization. If you’re building or planning to build on Databricks Apps, this is the roadmap of how the platform is evolving to support you—from first app to fleets of them.

title: When Redis Isn’t the Answer: Serving Lakehouse Data at Scale
abstract: As our feature and operational use cases grew, maintaining separate Redis/DynamoDB stacks alongside the data lake added latency, skew and ops burden. At Superhuman (formerly Grammarly), we adopted Lakebase to serve low‑latency data directly from Databricks, simplifying our architecture while meeting strict SLAs. This session details our setup and results: how we modelled entities and keys, tuned Lakebase capacity and read replicas for high QPS and enforced freshness with Change Data Feed–driven syncs. We’ll show code and configs for provisioning online stores, publishing tables, and querying endpoints, plus before/after latency and maintenance metrics. Attendees will leave with reference patterns to consolidate online serving on Lakebase without sacrificing performance.

Attendees will leave with reference patterns to consolidate online serving on Lakebase without sacrificing performance.

speakers.name: Michael Kobelev
speakers.bio: Michael Kobelev is a Software Engineer at ML Infrastructure team at Superhuman, focused on making ML data fast and reliable at scale. He spent three years at Grammarly building the data systems behind features like personalized emails, and recently led the effort to replace Feast with Lakebase. Before ML infrastructure, he built backend systems for the payments team. Based in Berlin.

title: Who Goes There? Enterprise Auth and Identity Management on Databricks Apps
abstract: For an application to move from a prototype to a core business asset, it must meet the highest standards of enterprise security and compliance. This session explores the advanced security architecture of Databricks Apps, demonstrating how it inherits and extends the robust governance of the Data Intelligence Platform. We will move beyond basic access to explore sophisticated identity management patterns, including On-Behalf-Of (OBO) authentication for user-scoped actions and secure compute resource delegation. You will also learn how the platform is hardening the application lifecycle with features like automated secret rotation and dedicated network namespaces to ensure total isolation. Whether you are handling sensitive PII or building internal tools for thousands of users, this session provides the blueprint for building apps that are secure by design, fully audited, and ready for the most regulated environments.
description_raw:
For an application to move from a prototype to a core business asset, it must meet the highest standards of enterprise security and compliance. This session explores the advanced security architecture of Databricks Apps, demonstrating how it inherits and extends the robust governance of the Data Intelligence Platform.

We will move beyond basic access to explore sophisticated identity management patterns, including On-Behalf-Of (OBO) authentication for user-scoped actions and secure compute resource delegation. You will also learn how the platform is hardening the application lifecycle with features like automated secret rotation and dedicated network namespaces to ensure total isolation. Whether you are handling sensitive PII or building internal tools for thousands of users, this session provides the blueprint for building apps that are secure by design, fully audited, and ready for the most regulated environments.

speakers.name: Wenwen Xie
speakers.bio: Wenwen Xie is an experienced Machine Learning and Generative AI Solutions Architect at Databricks, based in Jersey City, New Jersey. At Databricks, Wenwen specializes in the design and deployment of advanced ML systems, focusing heavily on model evaluation and validation frameworks. She is particularly passionate about Evaluation-Driven Development, advocating strongly for integrating robust model evaluation as a foundational practice in ML system design. Prior to joining Databricks, Wenwen served briefly as a Data Scientist Lead Vice President at J.P. Morgan. Her earlier role as Senior Data Scientist and Machine Learning Engineer at LexisNexis significantly shaped her expertise in generative AI and natural language processing (NLP).

title: Who's Building Enterprise Apps Now? Scaling App Development Beyond the Data Team
abstract: The next wave of enterprise app development isn't coming from IT or data engineering alone — it's coming from everyone. Business users are building apps and agents. Analysts are shipping tools their teams actually use. And admins are left figuring out how to govern an explosion of applications they didn't deploy and can't fully see.In this session, we'll cover where enterprise app development is headed, what it means for the teams responsible for keeping things secure and cost-effective, and how Databricks Apps is evolving to give builders the freedom they need and admins the control they require. Tune in for the Apps roadmap, vision, and a look at what's coming soon.

title: Why Caresource Chose Databricks AI/BI Over Traditional BI for Regulatory Reporting
abstract: CareSource initially planned to migrate hundreds of state-mandated SQL Server Reporting Services (SSRS) reports to Power BI Paginated Reports. However, with a mature Databricks platform already in place, that approach would have required added data movement, duplicated governance logic, and new licensing costs. CareSource and West Monroe share how a Databricks AI/BI pilot reshaped the strategy. By keeping regulatory reporting in Databricks, CareSource eliminated downstream exports, improved reliability, and reduced runtimes from hours to minutes. We’ll review the scalable architecture, including a custom extract framework that generates state-specific files and automates secure SFTP delivery, plus a business-friendly interface that drove adoption among non-technical users. Attendees will gain practical guidance on building governed regulatory pipelines and knowing when AI/BI is a strong alternative to traditional BI tools.
description_raw:
CareSource initially planned to migrate hundreds of state-mandated SQL Server Reporting Services (SSRS) reports to Power BI Paginated Reports. However, with a mature Databricks platform already in place, that approach would have required added data movement, duplicated governance logic, and new licensing costs.

CareSource and West Monroe share how a Databricks AI/BI pilot reshaped the strategy. By keeping regulatory reporting in Databricks, CareSource eliminated downstream exports, improved reliability, and reduced runtimes from hours to minutes. We’ll review the scalable architecture, including a custom extract framework that generates state-specific files and automates secure SFTP delivery, plus a business-friendly interface that drove adoption among non-technical users. Attendees will gain practical guidance on building governed regulatory pipelines and knowing when AI/BI is a strong alternative to traditional BI tools.

title: Why It's Time to Take AI/BI Seriously
abstract: I'm a Power BI developer by trade, and for years I've refused to engage with other data visualization platforms. Every time I did, I just found it was lacking capabilities or had serious deficiencies compared to Power BI. Then 2025 hit and Databricks invested heavily in improving AI/BI, metric views and Genie. Now, AI/BI is ready to be a legitimate challenger in the Data Visualisation space.In this talk I will show you how you can use AI/BI, metric views and Genie to get the most out of your data. We will start from basics, building a metric view, using it to build a dashboard and then setting up a Genie space. I'll then walk you through my top tips on how you can get the most out of each of them. We'll talk about the types of work where AI/BI thrives and discuss where I see it going in its pursuit of being the next big data visualization platform.AI/BI is here and it's ready to be taken seriously.
