speakers.name: Ashley Warren
speakers.bio: Ash is a Senior Analytics Consultant at Advancing Analytics, who focuses on analytics engineering and data visualisation. In the past, I've led data teams at small and large organisations, helping unlock the power of data through storytelling that's underpinned by strong foundations. I started out my data career as a Power BI report developer, though in the last couple of years I find myself spending more of my time building in Databricks. Ash can regularly be found speaking at user groups, conferences and events, sharing what he's learnt back with the community.

title: Why Vector Stores Are Not Enough: Using Lakebase as a Durable Memory Layer for Autonomous Agents
abstract: We are told that Vector Databases are the 'Memory' for AI Agents. But they give you a probabilistic similarity score. In enterprise workflows, 'probably' isn't good enough. Agents need deterministic, transactional state. We will use Lakebase as a memory layer for agents, enabling them to remember actions, lock resources, and handle concurrency. We will build a 'Stateful Worker' agent live with: The 'Two-Phase Commit' Prompt: Forcing an LLM to lock a row in Lakebase before calling an external API, preventing hallucinated actions. Time Travel for Agents: Leveraging Lakebase Branching to create isolated 'simulation' environments where agents can test destructive actions (e.g., 'Delete User') in a sandbox before committing to production. Hybrid Recall: A pattern combining Vector Search for knowledge with Lakebase for state, proving you need both to build reliable agents. Attendees will leave with a resuable architecture to build high-performing and autonomous agents in production.
description_raw:
We are told that Vector Databases are the 'Memory' for AI Agents. But they give you a probabilistic similarity score. In enterprise workflows, 'probably' isn't good enough. Agents need deterministic, transactional state.

We will use Lakebase as a memory layer for agents, enabling them to remember actions, lock resources, and handle concurrency.

We will build a 'Stateful Worker' agent live with:

  • The 'Two-Phase Commit' Prompt: Forcing an LLM to lock a row in Lakebase before calling an external API, preventing hallucinated actions.
  • Time Travel for Agents: Leveraging Lakebase Branching to create isolated 'simulation' environments where agents can test destructive actions (e.g., 'Delete User') in a sandbox before committing to production.
  • Hybrid Recall: A pattern combining Vector Search for knowledge with Lakebase for state, proving you need both to build reliable agents.

Attendees will leave with a resuable architecture to build high-performing and autonomous agents in production.

speakers.name: Nam Nguyen
speakers.bio: Nam Nguyen is a Senior Solutions Engineer, and he is part of the field engineering team based in Paris, France. He has 7+ years of experience in data engineering working for big tech companies in France. At Databricks, Nam helps entreprise to navigate the technologies decision and accelerate the adoption cycle. Nam holds several Databricks and AWS certifications in data engineering and generative AI.

title: You Built That with Databricks Apps?! A Customer Showcase of What’s Possible
abstract: You’ve seen Databricks Apps used for simple dashboards and internal tools—but that’s just the starting line. In this breakout, we’ll showcase some of the most creative, high-impact apps real customers have built on Databricks, from agentic workflows and decision copilots to line-of-business apps that entire organizations now run on every day. We’ll walk through a curated set of customer stories, each answering three questions: What problem were they stuck on? What did they build on Databricks Apps? What changed in their business as a result? Along the way, you’ll see patterns for building AI assistants on top of your lakehouse, rich operational UIs that write back to Lakebase, governance-aware “self-service” data apps for business users, and more. If you’ve been thinking of Apps as “just a nicer way to host a notebook or dashboard,” this session is designed to flip that mental model. You’ll leave with concrete ideas, architectures, and inspiration to say, “Wait—we could build our version of that.

title: Your AI Strategy is Only as Good as Your People Strategy
abstract: As AI moves from experimentation to enterprise impact, success depends on workforce readiness, not just technology. Market leaders have upskilled over 50% of their employees and are redesigning workflows around AI, while 60% of organizations still struggle to generate value from their investments. In this session, Robin Sutara and Melanie Botha explore how to build an AI-enabled workforce by aligning culture, people, process and enablement with your data strategy. You'll learn how to assess your data and AI maturity, close critical skills gaps at scale through structured learning programs, and integrate AI agents into teams in ways that elevate human talent. Drawing on customer proof points and the Databricks data and AI maturity framework, this session provides a practical roadmap for treating upskilling as a core investment—so every project becomes a data and AI project and every employee contributes to AI-driven outcomes.

speakers.name: Robin Sutara
speakers.bio: From repairing Apache helicopters near the Korean DMZ to the corporate battlefield, Robin has demonstrated success in navigating the high stress, and sometimes combative, complexities of data-led transformations. She has consulted with hundreds of organizations on data strategy, data culture, organizational design, and operational efficiency. Robin has had an eclectic career in technical and business functions with more than two decades in tech companies, including Microsoft and Databricks. She has been recognized as a Top 20 Women in Data and Tech, DataIQ 100 Most Influential People in Data, and Tech leader of the Year.

speakers.name: Melanie Botha
speakers.bio: Melanie leverages 25+ years of experience to guide organizations in building data-driven cultures and scale initiatives. A trusted advisor to C-suite executives, she believes technological transformation requires organizational and cultural evolution. Her team empowers customers, partners, and internal stakeholders to maximize their data and AI potential. Previously, at AWS ANZ, she led programs that skilled over 200,000 individuals, including pioneering GenAI training for non-technical audiences. At Microsoft South Africa, she managed Consumer Channels Group and Marketing and Operations. Earlier roles spanned across Europe and Africa. Melanie is a qualified Art Therapist supporting individuals to overcome mental health challenges.

title: ZeroETL Was Just the Start: Operational Databases belong in the Medallion Architecture
abstract: The medallion architecture dictates how we typically think about data, but that framework has historically been limited to the data lifecycle strictly within the Lakehouse. Lakebase takes this a step further by expanding the architectural blueprint to redefine how data enters your Bronze layer and how it is consumed from Gold. Rather than extracting and moving data across system boundaries, your operational database natively acts as the Bronze foundation to immediately feed downstream analytics. On the other end, Lakebase closes the loop by directly serving those refined Gold datasets to production applications with operational low latency, eliminating the need to export data out of your ecosystem.
description_raw: The medallion architecture dictates how we typically think about data, but that framework has historically been limited to the data lifecycle strictly within the Lakehouse. Lakebase takes this a step further by expanding the architectural blueprint to redefine how data enters your Bronze layer and how it is consumed from Gold. Rather than extracting and moving data across system boundaries, your operational database natively acts as the Bronze foundation to immediately feed downstream analytics. On the other end, Lakebase closes the loop by directly serving those refined Gold datasets to production applications with operational low latency, eliminating the need to export data out of your ecosystem.
